\NormalTok{observed\_sum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(observed\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y) }\CommentTok{\# Total number of \textquotesingle{}right\textquotesingle{} choices in the actual observed data}

\NormalTok{ppc\_prior\_ggplot }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(draws\_gq, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ prior\_rep\_sum)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Draw the histogram of the model\textquotesingle{}s prior predictions (light red)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#ffbaba"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#ff5252"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{30}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Add the vertical line for the actual observed data (dark red)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ observed\_sum, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Labels and theming}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive Check: Total \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choices"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = histogram spans the full range; observed value (dashed) is not in the tails"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Total Number of \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choices (out of"}\NormalTok{, N\_trials, }\StringTok{"trials)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Frequency"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}

\NormalTok{ppc\_prior\_ggplot}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_prior_predictive_check-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Interpretation:} The histogram covers the full range of possible
trial counts, centred near 60 (chance). This is what we want: the prior
does not pre-commit the model to an extreme outcome before seeing any
data. If the histogram were piled up near 0 or 120, that would mean our
prior is effectively deciding the answer for us --- a broken prior. The
dashed line marks our observed data. It sits well inside the bulk of the
distribution, confirming the prior does not rule out the kind of data we
actually saw.

\section{Phase 3: Posterior Predictive Checks - Does the Fitted Model
Resemble the
Data?}\label{phase-3-posterior-predictive-checks---does-the-fitted-model-resemble-the-data}

\begin{quote}
\emph{Why check posterior predictions after fitting?} A model can
produce a beautiful, well-converged posterior and still be completely
wrong about the data. The posterior predictive check asks: if we used
our fitted model to generate new datasets, would those datasets look
like the one we actually observed? If not, the model's generative
structure is misspecified --- regardless of what the posteriors say.
\end{quote}

Now that we've examined the prior implications, let's perform the
posterior predictive check. The fundamental question of model adequacy
is: Conditional on the data we observed, can the inferred
data-generating process replicate the key structural features of our
data?

\subsection{The Generative Algorithm:}\label{the-generative-algorithm-1}

The algorithm mirrors the prior predictive check, but instead of
sampling from the prior, we condition on the learned posterior
\(p(\theta \mid y)\):

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Draw from the Posterior: For each MCMC sample \(s\), take the inferred
  parameter \(\theta^{(s)}\).
\item
  Simulate Replications: Generate a synthetic dataset
  \(y_{\text{rep}}^{(s)}\) of the exact same dimensions as the empirical
  data, using the data
  model:\[y_{\text{rep}}^{(s)} \sim \text{Bernoulli}(\theta^{(s)})\]
\item
  Evaluate Test Statistics: Calculate theoretically relevant summary
  statistics \(T(y_{\text{rep}}^{(s)})\) and compare them against the
  empirical statistic \(T(y)\).
\end{itemize}

We must interrogate these predictions critically. Our model assumes
independent, identically distributed (i.i.d.) Bernoulli trials. But what
if the participant exhibited sequential dependencies, such as a
``win-stay/lose-shift'' strategy? If so, our model might successfully
capture the overall choice proportion (the sum), but it will fail
catastrophically to reproduce the sequential structure (e.g., the
maximum run-length of identical choices).

\subsection{Performing the Posterior Predictive
Check:}\label{performing-the-posterior-predictive-check}

We extract the replicated datasets (y\_post\_rep) generated
simultaneously within the Stan generated quantities block. We will then
systematically compare the test statistic distributions
\(T(y_{\text{rep}})\) against our empirical ground truth \(T(y)\).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract posterior predictive simulations from the fitted object}
\CommentTok{\# This matrix has dimensions: [number\_of\_draws, number\_of\_trials]}
\NormalTok{y\_post\_rep }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"y\_post\_rep"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Calculate the test statistic T(y) for the *actual* empirical data}
\NormalTok{observed\_sum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(observed\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y) }

\CommentTok{\# Calculate the test statistic T(y\_rep) for *each* replicated dataset}
\CommentTok{\# We compute the row{-}wise sum across the posterior draws}
\NormalTok{post\_rep\_sums }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rowSums}\NormalTok{(y\_post\_rep)}

\CommentTok{\# Bundle into a tibble for strictly structured plotting}
\NormalTok{ppc\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Replicated\_Sum =}\NormalTok{ post\_rep\_sums}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Graphical Posterior Predictive Check using ggplot2 {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{ppc\_posterior\_ggplot }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ppc\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Replicated\_Sum)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{30}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ observed\_sum, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive Check: Total \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choices"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = observed value (dashed) sits inside the bulk of replicated datasets"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Total Number of \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choices (out of"}\NormalTok{, N\_trials, }\StringTok{"trials)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Frequency (Replicated Datasets)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add annotation for the empirical ground truth}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ observed\_sum, }\AttributeTok{y =} \ConstantTok{Inf}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Empirical data"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{vjust =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{(observed\_sum }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ N\_trials}\SpecialCharTok{/}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\FloatTok{1.1} \ControlFlowTok{else} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}

\NormalTok{ppc\_posterior\_ggplot}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_posterior_predictive_checks-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ppc\_overlay\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Replicated\_Sum =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(draws\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_rep\_sum, post\_rep\_sums),}
  \AttributeTok{Distribution =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{each =} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(post\_rep\_sums)),}
    \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Generate the overlay plot}
\NormalTok{ppc\_overlay\_ggplot }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ppc\_overlay\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Replicated\_Sum, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Distribution)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Use position = "identity" and alpha to ensure both distributions are visible}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{position =} \StringTok{"identity"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{30}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ observed\_sum, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#ffbaba"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior vs. Posterior Predictive: Narrowing of Predictions After Fitting"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Posterior (blue) should be much tighter than prior (red) and centred on observed data"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Total Number of \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choices (out of"}\NormalTok{, N\_trials, }\StringTok{"trials)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Frequency (Replicated Datasets)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add annotation for the empirical ground truth}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ observed\_sum, }\AttributeTok{y =} \ConstantTok{Inf}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Empirical data"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{vjust =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{(observed\_sum }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ N\_trials}\SpecialCharTok{/}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\FloatTok{1.1} \ControlFlowTok{else} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(ppc\_overlay\_ggplot)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_posterior_predictive_checks-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Example of checking another statistic (e.g., max run length) {-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Define a function to calculate the maximum run length in a vector}
\NormalTok{max\_run\_length }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) \{}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(x) }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0} \SpecialCharTok{||} \FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(x))) }\FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }
\NormalTok{  rle\_x }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rle}\NormalTok{(x) }\CommentTok{\# Run Length Encoding calculates consecutive identical choices}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(rle\_x}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{lengths) }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }
  \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(rle\_x}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{lengths) }
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Calculate the empirical test statistic T(y)}
\NormalTok{observed\_max\_run }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{max\_run\_length}\NormalTok{(observed\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y)}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Extract prior replicated matrices and compute T(y\_rep) for the prior}
\CommentTok{\# y\_prior\_rep is an S x N matrix generated from the prior distributions}
\NormalTok{y\_prior\_rep }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"y\_prior\_rep"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{prior\_max\_runs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(y\_prior\_rep, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, max\_run\_length)}

\CommentTok{\# 4. Extract posterior replicated matrices and compute T(y\_rep) for the posterior}
\CommentTok{\# y\_post\_rep is an S x N matrix generated from the posterior distributions}
\NormalTok{y\_post\_rep }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"y\_post\_rep"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{post\_max\_runs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(y\_post\_rep, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, max\_run\_length)}

\CommentTok{\# 5. Bundle into a strictly structured tibble for overlay plotting}
\NormalTok{ppc\_run\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Replicated\_Max\_Run =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(prior\_max\_runs, post\_max\_runs),}
  \AttributeTok{Distribution =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{each =} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(post\_max\_runs)),}
    \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# 6. Generate the overlay plot}
\NormalTok{p\_ppc\_max\_run }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ppc\_run\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Replicated\_Max\_Run, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Distribution)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{position =} \StringTok{"identity"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ observed\_max\_run, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#ffbaba"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive: Maximum Run Length of Consecutive Choices"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Tests sequential structure — a model assuming i.i.d. trials should still pass this"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Test Statistic T(y\_rep): Maximum Run Length of Consecutive Choices"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Frequency (Replicated Datasets)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add annotation for the empirical ground truth}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ observed\_max\_run, }\AttributeTok{y =} \ConstantTok{Inf}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Empirical T(y)"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{vjust =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_ppc\_max\_run)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_posterior_predictive_checks-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Interpretation:} Both test statistics --- the total count of
``right'' choices and the maximum run length --- fall comfortably inside
their posterior predictive distributions. This means the model passes on
two fronts: it captures the average tendency (how often the agent picks
``right'') \emph{and} the sequential texture (how long the agent stays
on the same choice).

The maximum run length test is the more important one here. Our model
assumes each trial is independent --- it has no memory of what came
before. If the data showed very long runs (suggesting a
win-stay/lose-shift strategy), the model would fail on that statistic
even while passing on the count. It does not fail. This confirms that an
i.i.d. Bernoulli model is a defensible description of this particular
dataset.

\textbf{So what?} We have passed two out of six phases. The model is
plausibly specified and fits the data it was trained on. Phase 4 now
asks whether the data were actually informative enough to move our
beliefs.

\section{Phase 4: Epistemic Updating (Prior-Posterior Update Checks) -
How Much Did the Data Change our
Beliefs?}\label{phase-4-epistemic-updating-prior-posterior-update-checks---how-much-did-the-data-change-our-beliefs}

\begin{quote}
\emph{Why plot the prior and posterior together?} Two things can go
wrong that only this plot reveals: (1) the posterior as uncertain
(broad) as the prior, or even more, meaning that it wasn't able to learn
from the data; (2) the posterior is far from the true value and narrow
--- meaning the model is confidently wrong. Neither failure is visible
from the posterior alone.
\end{quote}

This check visualizes the core engine of Bayesian inference: the formal
mathematical updating of our structural assumptions by the empirical
evidence. Conceptually, we are observing Bayes' theorem in action as we
transition from the prior \(p(\theta)\) to the posterior
\(p(\theta \mid y) \propto p(y \mid \theta) p(\theta)\).

By explicitly plotting the marginal prior distribution against the
marginal posterior distribution for our parameters, we must interrogate
two critical epistemic properties:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \textbf{Information Gain}: How aggressively did the likelihood
  \(p(y \mid \theta)\) compress the variance of the prior? If the
  posterior distribution is substantially narrower (higher precision)
  than the prior, the empirical data possessed high informational value
  and strongly constrained our parameter estimates.
\item
  \textbf{Prior Influence}: Did the central tendency of the posterior
  shift significantly away from the prior mean? If the posterior sort of
  mirrors the prior, we must ask a deep structural question: Did the
  data provide too little informational value, or was our prior so
  strong that it overwhelmed the likelihood?
\end{itemize}

We will inspect this transformation on both the unconstrained space
defined by our model (\(\theta_{\text{logit}} \in \mathbb{R}\)) and the
conceptually intuitive probability space (\(\theta \in [0, 1]\)).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract MCMC draws into a structured dataframe}
\NormalTok{draws\_gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{())}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Define the empirical ground truth for reference}
\NormalTok{true\_theta\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.8}
\NormalTok{true\_theta\_logit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{qlogis}\NormalTok{(true\_theta\_prob)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} The Computational (Unconstrained) Space {-}{-}{-}}

\NormalTok{update\_logit\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Parameter\_Value =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(draws\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta\_logit\_prior, draws\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta\_logit),}
  \AttributeTok{Distribution =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{each =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(draws\_gq)),}
    \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate the overlay density plot for the logit space}
\NormalTok{p\_update\_logit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(update\_logit\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Parameter\_Value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Distribution)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta\_logit, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#ffbaba"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior{-}Posterior Update: Log{-}Odds Scale"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = posterior (blue) narrower than prior (red), shifted toward true value (dashed)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(theta[logit] }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{" (Log{-}Odds)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Marginal Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta\_logit, }\AttributeTok{y =} \ConstantTok{Inf}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Ground Truth"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{vjust =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (true\_theta\_logit }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\FloatTok{1.1} \ControlFlowTok{else} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_update\_logit)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_prior_posterior_update-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} The Cognitive (Constrained) Space {-}{-}{-}}

\CommentTok{\# We use plogis() to push the unconstrained prior simulations through the inverse{-}link function.}
\NormalTok{update\_prob\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Parameter\_Value =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(draws\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta\_logit\_prior), draws\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta),}
  \AttributeTok{Distribution =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{each =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(draws\_gq)),}
    \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate the overlay density plot for the probability space}
\NormalTok{p\_update\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(update\_prob\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Parameter\_Value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Distribution)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta\_prob, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#ffbaba"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \CommentTok{\# Mathematically enforce the [0, 1] bounds}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior{-}Posterior Update: Probability Scale"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = posterior narrower and closer to true θ = 0.8 than the flat prior"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(theta }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{" (Probability of \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choice)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Marginal Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta\_prob, }\AttributeTok{y =} \ConstantTok{Inf}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Ground Truth"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{vjust =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (true\_theta\_prob }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\FloatTok{1.1} \ControlFlowTok{else} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_update\_prob)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_prior_posterior_update-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Interpretation:} The posterior (blue) is substantially narrower
than the prior (red) and has moved toward the true value of 0.8 (dashed
line). The data were informative: 120 trials were enough to meaningfully
constrain our estimate. If the posterior had been identical to the
prior, the data would have been uninformative for this parameter --- a
sign that the model structure or the experimental design is broken. If
the posterior were narrow but centred far from the true value, the model
would be confidently wrong. Neither failure occurred here. Phase 5 now
tests whether this conclusion is sensitive to our specific choice of
prior.

Now that we have a better feel for the model, it is high time to go
beyond one dataset and one prior, and be systematic about testing the
model across a wide range of parameter values and prior specifications.
We will first assess the sensitivity of our model to prior values to
then proceed onto parameter recovery.

\section{Phase 5: Prior Sensitivity Analysis - Do Priors Matter Too Too
Much?}\label{phase-5-prior-sensitivity-analysis---do-priors-matter-too-too-much}

\begin{quote}
\emph{Why test sensitivity to prior choice?} We chose
\texttt{Normal(0,\ 1.5)} for the logit-scale prior, but that was a
judgment call. If a researcher using \texttt{Normal(0,\ 0.5)} instead
gets a completely different posterior, we need to more carefully discuss
and motivate the assumptions. Sensitivity analysis quantifies exactly
how much the prior choice matters.
\end{quote}

The next validation check addresses a critical epistemic vulnerability:
Are our structural conclusions pathologically dependent on the specific
mathematical bounds of our chosen prior? If we had specified a tighter
regularizing prior or a completely diffuse one, would our estimate of
the cognitive parameter \(\theta\) change drastically? We must
explicitly investigate the robustness of our inference by perturbing the
prior space and observing the resulting posterior geometry.

\subsection{5a. Empirical Prior Perturbation (Manual Sensitivity
Check)}\label{a.-empirical-prior-perturbation-manual-sensitivity-check}

\textbf{The Mathematical Algorithm}:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \emph{Define a Perturbation Grid}: Specify a set of alternative,
  scientifically plausible prior distributions for the parameter of
  interest. For example, if our baseline is
  \(\theta_{\text{logit}} \sim \text{Normal}(0, 1.0)\), we might test a
  highly restrictive prior, \(\text{Normal}(0, 0.5)\), and a diffuse
  prior, \(\text{Normal}(0, 2.0)\).
\item
  \emph{Re-compute the Posterior}: Fit the generative model to the data
  under each alternative prior specification to obtain
  \(p(\theta \mid y, \text{prior}_k)\).
\item
  \emph{Evaluate Invariance}: Overlay and compare the resulting marginal
  posterior distributions.
\end{enumerate}

\textbf{Decision Rule}: If the marginal posteriors are mathematically
invariant (nearly identical) across the perturbation grid, our inference
is computationally robust. The likelihood function contains sufficient
information to overwhelm the prior constraints. Conversely, if the
posteriors shift drastically, our inference is fragile. A fragile model
demands that we either mathematically justify our exact prior
specification, acknowledge the severe uncertainty in our conclusions, or
collect a larger empirical sample. However, it's also important to
remember that in many contexts influential priors are a good choice: to
regularize inference, to incorporate strong domain knowledge and/or
assess the distance between our results and the previous literature,
etc. See this talk for more:
https://youtu.be/kMSrRd4f2As?si=-7bbx3wFWobgxebQ

\subsubsection{Computational
Implementation:}\label{computational-implementation}

To execute this efficiently, we have modified our Stan model to accept
the prior's mean and standard deviation directly via the data block.
This parameterization allows us to loop through our grid of alternative
\(\sigma\) values in R, iteratively updating the posterior without
forcing a costly C++ recompilation of the model at each step.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# We test 3 location parameters (means) and 10 scale parameters (SDs)}
\NormalTok{prior\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ tidyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }
  \AttributeTok{prior\_sigma =} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{3.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{length.out =} \DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Standardized filename variable}
\NormalTok{sensitivity\_draws\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir), }\StringTok{"ch07\_sensitivity\_draws.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Check if we need to re{-}run the sensitivity analysis (fixed variable name)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(sensitivity\_draws\_file)) \{}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Removed \textquotesingle{}seed\_val\textquotesingle{} from the function arguments}
\NormalTok{  fit\_and\_extract }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(prior\_mu, prior\_sigma) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Dynamically inject the perturbed parameters into the data list}
\NormalTok{    current\_data\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ observed\_data\_list}
\NormalTok{    current\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_theta\_mu }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu}
\NormalTok{    current\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_theta\_sigma }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ prior\_sigma }

    \CommentTok{\# Fit using the original compiled model}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ current\_data\_list,}
      \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{3000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{show\_exceptions =} \ConstantTok{FALSE} \CommentTok{\# Added to prevent C++ errors from polluting stdout}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
\NormalTok{    draws\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Return a strictly structured tibble, discarding the fragile fit object}
\NormalTok{    tibble}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{theta\_prob =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_mu =}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_sigma =}\NormalTok{ prior\_sigma}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# Establish the parallel backend }
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Execute the parallel mapping}
\NormalTok{  draws\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ furrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{future\_pmap}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    prior\_grid,}
\NormalTok{    fit\_and\_extract,}
    \AttributeTok{.options =}\NormalTok{ furrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stdout =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }
\NormalTok{  )}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Collapse the list of dataframes into a single tidy dataframe}
\NormalTok{  sensitivity\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(draws\_list)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Shut down the parallel backend to free up RAM}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{sequential)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Save the indestructible, pure data (fixed variable name)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(sensitivity\_draws, sensitivity\_draws\_file)}
  
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Fixed variable names to match what was saved}
\NormalTok{  sensitivity\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(sensitivity\_draws\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing perturbation grid from:"}\NormalTok{, sensitivity\_draws\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing perturbation grid from: /Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/simmodels/ch07_sensitivity_draws.rds 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{p\_sensitivity\_2d }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(sensitivity\_draws, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ theta\_prob, }\AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(prior\_sigma), }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ prior\_sigma)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{stat =} \StringTok{"density"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta\_prob, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu, }\AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{labeller}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prior\_mu =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Mean (Logit): "}\NormalTok{, x))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{name =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(Prior }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ sigma)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Epistemic Robustness: 2D Prior Perturbation Grid"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(theta }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{" (Probability scale)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Marginal Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{p\_sensitivity\_2d}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_manual_sensitivity_analysis-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{sensitivity\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sensitivity\_draws }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(prior\_mu, prior\_sigma) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{post\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob),}
    \CommentTok{\# The 95\% CrI (Outer Ribbon)}
    \AttributeTok{lower\_95 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{upper\_95 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{),}
    \CommentTok{\# The 68\% CrI (\textasciitilde{}1 Standard Deviation, Inner Ribbon)}
    \AttributeTok{lower\_68 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.16}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{upper\_68 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.84}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \CommentTok{\# Create strict, mathematically explicit facet labels}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_label =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      prior\_mu, }
      \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }
      \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior \textbackslash{}u03bc = {-}1.5 (Strong Left Bias)"}\NormalTok{, }
                 \StringTok{"Prior \textbackslash{}u03bc = 0 (Neutral)"}\NormalTok{, }
                 \StringTok{"Prior \textbackslash{}u03bc = 1.5 (Strong Right Bias)"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Generate the bounded trajectory plot with graded topological ribbons}
\NormalTok{p\_sensitivity\_trajectory }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(sensitivity\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ prior\_sigma, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ post\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# The generative ground truth is our invariant reference line}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta\_prob, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The 95\% Credible Interval (Outer Ribbon, Highly Transparent)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lower\_95, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ upper\_95, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu\_label), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The 68\% Credible Interval (\textasciitilde{}1 SD, Inner Ribbon, More Opaque)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lower\_68, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ upper\_68, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu\_label), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.35}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The posterior mean trajectory}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu\_label), }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu\_label), }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Structural faceting}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ prior\_mu\_label) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{begin =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{begin =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \CommentTok{\# Zoom to the scientifically relevant probability bounds}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Pedagogical labeling}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Epistemic Trajectory: Graded Posterior Estimates across Prior Perturbations"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Standard Deviation ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ sigma }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{") {-}\textgreater{} Increasing Diffuseness"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior Estimate of "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ theta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" (Mean, 68\%, and 95\% CrI)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{, }\CommentTok{\# Redundant with facet labels}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{),}
    \CommentTok{\# Add light y{-}grid lines to help the eye track the shrinkage}
    \AttributeTok{panel.grid.major.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray90"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{) }
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sensitivity\_trajectory)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_manual_sensitivity_analysis-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\subsection{5b. Sensitivity Analysis using
priorsense}\label{b.-sensitivity-analysis-using-priorsense}

Executing a computationally heavy 30-model grid is acceptable for a
single-parameter Bernoulli process, but what happens when we transition
to high-dimensional hierarchical structures in later chapter? The
computational cost of manual grid searches becomes prohibitive.

To solve this, we rely on the priorsense package, which elegantly
estimates prior sensitivity directly from the original model fit without
requiring a single MCMC re-run. It achieves this via power-scaling, a
mathematical technique that analytically down-weights alternatively the
likelihood and the prior to simulate how the posterior would deform
under different epistemic conditions. Conceptually, it evaluates the
perturbed posterior:

\[p(\theta \mid y) \propto p(y \mid \theta)^{\alpha} p(\theta)^{\beta}\]

where \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are scaling factors.

\subsubsection{Conceptual Idea:}\label{conceptual-idea}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  Baseline Fit: Fit the model once using your principled, generative
  priors.
\item
  Importance Sampling: Use priorsense::powerscale\_sensitivity to apply
  Pareto-smoothed importance sampling (PSIS). This analytically
  estimates the posterior geometry under perturbed scaling factors
  (\(\alpha \neq 1, \beta \neq 1\)).
\item
  Compute Sensitivity (\(\psi\)): The algorithm calculates specific
  diagnostic metrics, such as sensitivity\_psi. This metric quantifies
  the gradient of change: how much does the posterior mean shift
  (relative to its standard deviation) when the prior is perturbed?
\item
  Epistemic Diagnosis: High sensitivity values (e.g., \(\psi > 0.1\))
  indicate structural fragility. This suggests either that the prior is
  pathologically dominating the likelihood, or that the prior and
  likelihood are fundamentally in conflict (prior-data conflict).
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# We run prior sense}
\NormalTok{sensitivity\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_sensitivity}\NormalTok{(fit\_gq, }
    \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# we extract the variable of interest}
\NormalTok{theta\_metrics }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sensitivity\_summary }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{{-}{-}{-} Epistemic Diagnosis {-}{-}{-}}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

--- Epistemic Diagnosis ---
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(theta\_metrics)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Sensitivity based on cjs_dist
Prior selection: all priors
Likelihood selection: all data

 variable prior likelihood diagnosis
    theta 0.027      0.104         -
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Visualize the geometric deformation of the posterior}
\NormalTok{p\_priorsense\_dens }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_plot\_dens}\NormalTok{(fit\_gq, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Analytical Epistemic Fragility: Power{-}Scaled Density Deformation"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{p\_priorsense\_dens}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_priorsense_analysis-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\section{Phase 6: Computational
Calibration}\label{phase-6-computational-calibration}

\begin{quote}
\emph{Why run parameter recovery and SBC after the model already fits
the data?} Phases 2--5 tested the model's \emph{structure}. Phase 6
tests the \emph{engine}. Even a perfectly specified model produces
garbage if the HMC sampler cannot explore the posterior correctly. And
viceversa, testing the model across many parameter values is a waste of
computation if there are obvious issues with the model that could have
been solved looking at one simulation only. Parameter recovery (6a)
checks whether the engine finds the right answer at specific known
values. SBC (6b) checks whether the posterior \emph{intervals} are
calibrated across the entire prior space --- a 95\% CI should contain
the truth 95\% of the time, not 70\% or 99\%. These are different tests
that can fail independently.
\end{quote}

We must investigate two structural properties of our inference before we
can trust the posterior:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Data Adequacy (Parameter Recovery): Does our specific experimental
  design (e.g., \(N=120\) trials) contain enough informational density
  to shrink the posterior around the true parameter?
\item
  Algorithmic Unbiasedness (SBC): Is the Hamiltonian Monte Carlo engine
  mathematically well-calibrated across the entire prior space, or is it
  structurally overconfident or biased?
\end{itemize}

\subsection{6a. Parameter Recovery and Posterior
Shrinkage}\label{a.-parameter-recovery-and-posterior-shrinkage}

We will simulate empirical data across an escalating grid of trial
lengths (30, 60, 120, 240) for three distinct generative agents: a
neutral agent (\(\theta = 0.5\)), a weakly right-biased agent
(\(\theta = 0.7\)), and a strongly-biased agen (\(\theta = 0.9\)). We
then fit our Stan model to each dataset and track the changes in mean
and variance of the estimates.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{recovery\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ tidyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{N\_trials =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{30}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{60}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{120}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{240}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{true\_theta =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{recovery\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch07\_recovery\_draws.rds"}\NormalTok{)}


\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(recovery\_file)) \{}
  
  \CommentTok{\# 2. Define the isolated recovery pipeline}
\NormalTok{  run\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(N\_trials, true\_theta) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Generate synthetic empirical data}
\NormalTok{    sim\_y }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n =}\NormalTok{ N\_trials, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prob =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Structure the data block for Stan}
\NormalTok{    rec\_data\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{N\_trials =}\NormalTok{ N\_trials,}
      \AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ sim\_y,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_mu =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_sigma =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Fit the generative model}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ rec\_data\_list,}
      \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{2000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Extract structural draws immediately to avoid temporary file fragility}
\NormalTok{    draws\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{)}
    
\NormalTok{    tibble}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{trials =}\NormalTok{ N\_trials,}
      \AttributeTok{true\_theta =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta,}
      \AttributeTok{theta\_prob =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# 3. Establish the parallel backend and execute mapping}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  recovery\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ furrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{future\_pmap}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    recovery\_grid,}
\NormalTok{    run\_recovery,}
    \AttributeTok{.options =}\NormalTok{ furrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stdout =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{()}
    
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{sequential)}
  
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(recovery\_draws, recovery\_file)}
  
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  recovery\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(recovery\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing 2D recovery draws from:"}\NormalTok{, recovery\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing 2D recovery draws from: simmodels/ch07_recovery_draws.rds 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 4. Compress draws into rigorous posterior summaries}
\NormalTok{recovery\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_draws }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(trials, true\_theta) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{post\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob),}
    \AttributeTok{lower\_95 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{upper\_95 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(theta\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{facet\_label =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Generative Ground Truth: \textbackslash{}u03b8 = "}\NormalTok{, true\_theta)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# 5. Visualize the geometric shrinkage across the parameter space}

\NormalTok{p\_recovery\_2d }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(recovery\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(trials), }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ post\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Map the true value to the hline dynamically per facet}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_pointrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lower\_95, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ upper\_95, }\AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_theta)), }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ facet\_label) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Adjust palette range so colors remain distinct}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{begin =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Parameter Recovery: Posterior Mean vs. True θ Across N"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = points near dashed true{-}value line; intervals shrink as N increases"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Experimental Design (Number of Trials, N)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior Estimate of "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ theta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" (Mean \& 95\% CrI)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.35}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \CommentTok{\# Bound visually to the relevant domain}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{panel.grid.major.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray90"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_recovery\_2d)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_parameter_recovery_grid-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{p\_recovery\_2d\_alt }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(recovery\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_theta, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ post\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# The Identity Line: y = x (Estimate perfectly matches Ground Truth)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The Posterior Estimates and 95\% CrI}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_pointrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lower\_95, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ upper\_95, }\AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_theta)), }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Facet strictly by the Experimental Design (N trials)}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}
    \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ trials, }
    \AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{as\_labeller}\NormalTok{(}\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(x, }\StringTok{" Trials"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{begin =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Ensure the coordinate space is perfectly square to preserve the visual geometry of y=x}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_fixed}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Generative Ground Truth ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ theta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior Estimate ("} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{hat}\NormalTok{(theta) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" Mean \& 95\% CrI)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Parameter Recovery Identity Plot"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = points on the diagonal (y = x); bars are 95\% CrI. N grows left → right."}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{),}
    \CommentTok{\# Add a faint grid to help the eye assess the distance from the diagonal}
    \AttributeTok{panel.grid.major =} \FunctionTok{element\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray90"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_recovery\_2d\_alt)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_parameter_recovery_grid-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\emph{Interpretation}

By faceting across our experimental design (\(N\)) and plotting the
generative ground truth on the x-axis, we have constructed a parameter
recovery identity plot. Perfect inference dictates that our posterior
estimates (\(y\)) fall exactly on the true parameters (\(x\)), tracing
the dashed diagonal line (\(y=x\)).

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  Epistemic Evolution: Read the facets from left to right. At \(N=30\),
  the model is fundamentally underpowered. The 95\% Credible Intervals
  (vertical bars) are massive, and the mean estimates may drift away
  from the diagonal due to prior shrinkage. The likelihood is too weak
  to fully constrain the parameter space.
\item
  Asymptotic Consistency: As we scale to \(N=240\), the geometry snaps
  into focus. The estimates lock perfectly onto the diagonal identity
  line, and the uncertainty bounds collapse. We have mathematically
  proven that if we run an experiment with 120 to 240 trials, our
  cognitive architecture is very likely to identify the true underlying
  bias of the agent.
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Phase 6b. Simulation-Based Calibration Checks
(SBC)}\label{phase-6b.-simulation-based-calibration-checks-sbc}

This is the final crucible for our Simple Biased Agent. Parameter
recovery (Phase 6a) proved that the mean of our posterior is
asymptotically consistent. However, we want a more direct test of
whether the uncertainty of the model is well calibrated.

If our inference systematically generates posterior distributions that
are too narrow (overconfident) or too wide (underconfident), our 95\%
Credible Intervals are fictions. To formally investigate whether our
posterior geometry is calibrated across the entire prior space, we must
execute Simulation-Based Calibration Checks (SBC).

\subsubsection{The Mathematical Theorem of
SBC}\label{the-mathematical-theorem-of-sbc}

SBC relies on a fundamental self-consistency property of Bayesian
inference. If we:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  Draw a ground-truth parameter from the exact prior:
  \(\tilde{\theta} \sim p(\theta)\)
\item
  Simulate a dataset from the data model:
  \(\tilde{y} \sim p(y \mid \tilde{\theta})\)
\item
  Sample the posterior using our Stan engine:
  \(p(\theta \mid \tilde{y})\)
\end{enumerate}

Then the rank of the true parameter \(\tilde{\theta}\) within the
posterior draws must be strictly Uniformly distributed. If we repeat
this process 1,000 times, any deviation from uniformity mathematically
proves that our computational engine is structurally biased or our model
is misspecified.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{sbc\_results\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch07\_sbc\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Backend is defined OUTSIDE the cache conditional so it is always available —}
\CommentTok{\# both for Phase 6b itself and for the power analysis in the next section.}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{sbc\_backend }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  mod\_gq,}
  \AttributeTok{chains        =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup   =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{refresh       =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_file)) \{}

  \CommentTok{\# 1. Define the exact mathematical Generative Function}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_generator\_fn }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Draw from the structural prior (on the unconstrained space)}
\NormalTok{    theta\_logit\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{mean =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{sd =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    theta\_true       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(theta\_logit\_true)}

    \CommentTok{\# Simulate empirical reality (N = 120 trials)}
\NormalTok{    N\_trials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{120}
\NormalTok{    y\_sim    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(N\_trials, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prob =}\NormalTok{ theta\_true)}

    \CommentTok{\# Format strictly required by the SBC package}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{theta\_logit =}\NormalTok{ theta\_logit\_true, }\AttributeTok{theta =}\NormalTok{ theta\_true),}
      \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{N\_trials          =}\NormalTok{ N\_trials,}
        \AttributeTok{y                 =}\NormalTok{ y\_sim,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_mu    =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_sigma =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics   =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# Cast the raw function into a formal SBC generator object}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_generator\_obj }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(sbc\_generator\_fn)}

  \CommentTok{\# 2. Generate datasets and compute rank statistics}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_datasets }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(sbc\_generator\_obj, }\AttributeTok{n\_sims =} \DecValTok{200}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_results  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(sbc\_datasets, sbc\_backend, }\AttributeTok{keep\_fits =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{sequential)}

  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results, sbc\_results\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBC completed. Results saved to:"}\NormalTok{, sbc\_results\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing SBC results from:"}\NormalTok{, sbc\_results\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing SBC results from: simmodels/ch07_sbc_results.rds 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 4. Visualize the Calibration Geometry}

\CommentTok{\# Plot A: Rank Histogram}

\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_hist }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBC Rank Histogram: Algorithmic Calibration"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = bars flat within grey band. U{-}shape = overconfident. Hump = underconfident. Skew = biased."}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Plot B: Empirical Cumulative Distribution Function (ECDF)}

\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_ecdf }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBC ECDF: Deviation from Uniformity"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Healthy = blue line stays inside the grey ellipse at all points"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Combine plots  using patchwork}
\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_combined }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_sbc\_hist }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_sbc\_ecdf}
\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_combined}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_sbc_calibration-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{How to read these two plots:}

\emph{Rank histogram:} Under perfect calibration, every rank band is
equally likely, so the bars should be flat within the grey 95\%
confidence band.

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Shape
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Diagnosis
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Action
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
Flat (within grey band) & Calibrated ✓ & Proceed \\
U-shape (high at edges) & Overconfident --- posteriors too narrow &
Widen priors or reparameterise \\
Hump (high in centre) & Underconfident --- posteriors too wide & Tighten
priors \\
Skewed left or right & Directional bias in the sampler & Check model
code; try non-centred parameterisation \\
\end{longtable}

\emph{ECDF plot:} The blue line is the empirical CDF of the ranks; the
grey ellipse is the region consistent with uniformity. Any excursion
outside the ellipse at a given rank level is a calibration failure at
that part of the parameter space.

\textbf{So what?} A flat histogram and an in-bounds ECDF line mean that
when we report a 95\% credible interval, it actually contains the true
parameter 95\% of the time. This is the formal guarantee that makes
Bayesian inference meaningful.

\subsubsection{Parameter Recovery via
SBC}\label{parameter-recovery-via-sbc}

The rank histogram and ECDF confirm \emph{algorithmic calibration} ---
that the sampler is unbiased. But we have been handed something more for
free. The \texttt{sbc\_results\$stats} dataframe, which the SBC package
computes as a byproduct of the rank calculation, contains for every
simulated universe:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \texttt{simulated\_value}: the ground-truth \(\theta\) drawn from the
  prior --- the quantity we passed in \texttt{variables}.
\item
  \texttt{median}, \texttt{q5}, \texttt{q95}: the posterior median and
  the bounds of the 90\% credible interval from the fitted posterior.
\end{itemize}

This is precisely the data we need for a \textbf{systematic parameter
