recovery study}, and we already have it. No extra computation is
required. The crucial distinction from our grid recovery in Phase 6a is
architectural:

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 2\tabcolsep) * \real{0.5000}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 2\tabcolsep) * \real{0.5000}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Phase 6a (grid recovery)
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
SBC-based recovery
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
Fixed ground truths: \(\theta \in \{0.5, 0.7, 0.9\}\) & 200 values
spanning \(\text{Normal}(0, 1.5)\) on logit scale \\
Asks ``does it work at these specific values?'' & Asks ``does it work
everywhere the prior places mass?'' \\
Controlled, pedagogically interpretable & Exhaustive, scientifically
complete \\
\end{longtable}

The grid recovery shows the shrinkage structure around specific anchor
points with interpretable coordinates. The SBC recovery shows the full
recovery profile across the entire plausible parameter space (if we have
set our priors correctly :-)). I set up the first to give you a concrete
idea of what happens in the code, but the latter is more systematic and
rigorous.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract the lightweight stats dataframe — already computed as part of SBC above.}
\NormalTok{sbc\_stats }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats}

\CommentTok{\# We plot on the probability scale for interpretability, matching Phase 6a.}
\CommentTok{\# The \textquotesingle{}theta\textquotesingle{} variable was stored (on probability scale) alongside \textquotesingle{}theta\_logit\textquotesingle{}}
\CommentTok{\# in our generator\textquotesingle{}s variables list.}
\NormalTok{recovery\_sbc\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Identity plot: true value (x) vs. posterior median (y)}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Under perfect recovery, every point lies on the dashed y = x diagonal.}
\CommentTok{\# Scatter around the diagonal is unavoidable estimation uncertainty.}
\CommentTok{\# Systematic curvature (bowing above or below) would reveal directional bias.}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(recovery\_sbc\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ median)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \CommentTok{\# Perfect{-}recovery reference line}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
              \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \CommentTok{\# 90\% posterior credible intervals}
  \CommentTok{\# Under correct calibration, \textasciitilde{}90\% of bars should contain the true value —}
  \CommentTok{\# which we already verified via the rank histogram above.}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q5, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95),}
                \AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \CommentTok{\# Posterior median point estimates}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \FunctionTok{coord\_fixed}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Parameter Recovery via SBC: Simple Biased Agent"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"N = 200 simulated universes. True \textbackslash{}u03b8 drawn from the prior predictive.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Each point is one universe. Bars are 90\% posterior credible intervals."}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ theta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" (probability scale, drawn from prior)"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior Median"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sbc\_recovery)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_sbc_recovery_plot-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Quantitative recovery metrics}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Pearson r   : linear correlation between truth and estimate (1.0 = perfect)}
\CommentTok{\# RMSE        : average estimation error on the probability scale (0 = perfect)}
\CommentTok{\# coverage90  : empirical 90\% CI coverage — should be near 0.90 under calibration}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}

\NormalTok{recovery\_metrics\_sbc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_sbc\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Pearson\_r  =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value, median),}
    \AttributeTok{RMSE       =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{((simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ median)}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{coverage90 =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ q5 }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=}\NormalTok{ q95)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== SBC PARAMETER RECOVERY METRICS (theta, probability scale) ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== SBC PARAMETER RECOVERY METRICS (theta, probability scale) ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    recovery\_metrics\_sbc,}
    \AttributeTok{digits    =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{col.names =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Pearson r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"RMSE"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Empirical 90\% CI Coverage"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}


| Pearson r|  RMSE| Empirical 90% CI Coverage|
|---------:|-----:|-------------------------:|
|     0.988| 0.041|                     0.915|
\end{verbatim}

\subsubsection{A Precision (Pseudo-Power) Analysis via
SBC}\label{a-precision-pseudo-power-analysis-via-sbc}

Our Phase 6a grid recovery showed how precision evolves at three
specific \(\theta\) values as \(N\) grows. We can now generalise that
question systematically: across the \emph{full} prior space, at what
\(N\) does the model become reliable?

Because the SBC generator is a plain R function, we can pass different
\texttt{N\_trials} values and re-run the calibration+recovery pipeline
at each design point. The faceted identity plots below show the entire
prior-weighted recovery profile for \(N \in \{30, 60, 120, 240\}\),
directly comparable to the grid facets in Phase 6a but without the
restriction to three anchor values.

This is a Precision Analysis, the Bayesian analogue of a frequentist
power analysis, with three important distinctions:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{No asymptotic approximations.} We simulate from the exact
  model.
\item
  \textbf{No point null.} The ``true effect'' is drawn from the prior,
  characterising performance over the full realistic range.
\item
  \textbf{Precision, not rejection rate.} The outcome metric is credible
  interval width, not whether a \(p\)-value crosses a threshold.
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# precision{-}analysis generator: same logic as sbc\_generator\_fn above,}
\CommentTok{\# but parameterised on N\_trials so we can loop over the design grid.}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{make\_sbc\_precision\_generator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(N\_trials) \{}
  \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
\NormalTok{    theta\_logit\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{mean =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{sd =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    theta\_true       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(theta\_logit\_true)}
\NormalTok{    y\_sim            }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(N\_trials, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prob =}\NormalTok{ theta\_true)}

    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{theta\_logit =}\NormalTok{ theta\_logit\_true,}
        \AttributeTok{theta       =}\NormalTok{ theta\_true}
\NormalTok{      ),}
      \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{N\_trials          =}\NormalTok{ N\_trials,}
        \AttributeTok{y                 =}\NormalTok{ y\_sim,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_mu    =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_sigma =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics   =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Grid of experimental designs to evaluate}
\NormalTok{trial\_grid\_precision }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{30}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{60}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{120}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{240}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Accumulate recovery stats across all design conditions}
\NormalTok{precision\_stats }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ purrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(trial\_grid\_precision, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(n\_t) \{}

  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"  precision analysis: N\_trials = \%d ...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, n\_t))}

\NormalTok{  precision\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir,}
                          \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch07\_sbc\_precision\_n\%d.rds"}\NormalTok{, n\_t))}

  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(precision\_file)) \{}

\NormalTok{    gen\_precision }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{make\_sbc\_precision\_generator}\NormalTok{(n\_t))}

    \CommentTok{\# 200 datasets per condition — enough to characterise the recovery envelope}
\NormalTok{    dsets\_precision  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(gen\_precision, }\AttributeTok{n\_sims =} \DecValTok{200}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    result\_precision }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      dsets\_precision,}
\NormalTok{      sbc\_backend,   }\CommentTok{\# reuse the compiled backend from Phase 6b}
      \AttributeTok{keep\_fits =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{    )}
    \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{datasets =}\NormalTok{ dsets\_precision, }\AttributeTok{results =}\NormalTok{ result\_precision), precision\_file)}

\NormalTok{  \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    obj\_precision    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(precision\_file)}
\NormalTok{    result\_precision }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obj\_precision}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{results}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  result\_precision}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n\_trials =}\NormalTok{ n\_t)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
  precision analysis: N_trials = 30 ...
  precision analysis: N_trials = 60 ...
  precision analysis: N_trials = 120 ...
  precision analysis: N_trials = 240 ...
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Summary table: key precision metrics as a function of N}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{precision\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ precision\_stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(n\_trials) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{median\_CI\_width =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(q95 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ q5),}
    \AttributeTok{coverage90      =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ q5 }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=}\NormalTok{ q95),}
    \AttributeTok{RMSE            =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{((simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ median)}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups         =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== PRECISION ANALYSIS: RECOVERY QUALITY VS. N\_TRIALS ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== PRECISION ANALYSIS: RECOVERY QUALITY VS. N_TRIALS ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    precision\_summary,}
    \AttributeTok{digits    =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{col.names =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N Trials"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Median 90\% CI Width"}\NormalTok{,}
                  \StringTok{"Empirical 90\% Coverage"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"RMSE"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}


| N Trials| Median 90% CI Width| Empirical 90% Coverage|  RMSE|
|--------:|-------------------:|----------------------:|-----:|
|       30|               0.251|                  0.890| 0.079|
|       60|               0.183|                  0.835| 0.060|
|      120|               0.134|                  0.945| 0.031|
|      240|               0.095|                  0.930| 0.026|
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Faceted identity plot: one panel per trial count}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(precision\_stats, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ median)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
              \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q5, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95),}
                \AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.20}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.45}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_fixed}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N = "}\NormalTok{, n\_trials), }\AttributeTok{nrow =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Precision Analysis: Recovery Quality vs. Number of Trials"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"Each panel: 200 independent SBC universes. "}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Bars are 90\% credible intervals.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Tighter scatter around the diagonal = higher estimation precision."}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ theta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" (drawn from prior)"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior Median"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_sbc_precision_analysis-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Read the precision table as follows. The \textbf{median CI width} column
measures how precisely a typical experiment estimates \(\theta\) at a
given \(N\): a width of \(0.3\) means the 90\% credible interval spans
roughly \(0.3\) probability units, which may or may not be adequate
depending on the scientific question. The \textbf{empirical 90\%
coverage} column should remain near \(0.90\) at every \(N\) --- if
coverage collapses at small \(N\), the posterior is becoming
overconfident relative to the calibration guarantee we verified above.
The \textbf{RMSE} column captures average estimation error; halving it
requires roughly quadrupling \(N\), consistent with the classical
\(1/\sqrt{N}\) rate.

Comparing the faceted identity plots here to the pointrange plots in
Phase 6a, the two visualisations are complementary. Phase 6a shows the
shrinkage behaviour at three \emph{specific} anchor values with clearly
labelled coordinates --- pedagogically invaluable for understanding how
prior pull operates at the boundary (\(\theta = 0.9\)) versus at the
centre (\(\theta = 0.5\)). The SBC precision plots show the full
recovery \emph{envelope} across the prior's probability mass, making it
impossible to cherry-pick a favourable test point. Together they
constitute a complete characterisation of the model's identifiability as
a function of experimental design.

\subsection{Phase 6c: Posterior SBC}\label{phase-6c-posterior-sbc}

However, if you paid attention, you've realized that the calibration was
based on the prior. In other words, it's strongly bound by our
assumptions (and we should be careful about that). But what if the
posterior brings us to a slightly different region of the parameter
space? We can also check the calibration of the posterior by running SBC
with a different generator function that samples from the posterior
instead of the prior. This answers the question: ``Given the constraints
the actual data has placed on my parameters, is my model-sampler
combination still unbiased in this specific high-probability manifold?''

\subsubsection{The Mathematical Logic of
PSBC}\label{the-mathematical-logic-of-psbc}

The self-consistency property of Bayes' theorem extends to any valid
distribution. In PSBC, we treat our empirical posterior as a ``local
prior'':

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  Draw a ``true'' parameter \(\tilde{\theta}\) from the fitted
  posterior: \(\tilde{\theta} \sim p(\theta \mid y_{obs})\).
\item
  Simulate a new synthetic dataset \(\tilde{y}\) from the data model:
  \(\tilde{y} \sim p(y \mid \tilde{\theta})\).
\item
  Fit the model to this synthetic data to get a ``new'' posterior
  \(p(\theta \mid \tilde{y}, y_{obs})\).
\item
  Check Ranks: The rank of \(\tilde{\theta}\) within the new posterior
  must still be Uniform.
\end{enumerate}

If PSBC passes but Prior SBC fails, it means your model is only reliable
for data similar to what you observed. If PSBC fails, you cannot trust
the specific estimates for your current experiment, even if the model
works ``in general''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Extract posterior draws as the required \textquotesingle{}draws\_matrix\textquotesingle{} type}
\NormalTok{post\_draws\_theta\_raw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{as\_draws\_matrix}\NormalTok{(fit\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"theta\_logit"}\NormalTok{)))}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Sequential Data Generation}
\CommentTok{\# We manually draw \textquotesingle{}true\textquotesingle{} values from the posterior instead of a formula}
\NormalTok{generate\_psbc\_data\_theta }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n\_sims =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  indices }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(post\_draws\_theta\_raw), n\_sims, }\AttributeTok{replace =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  sampled\_vars }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ post\_draws\_theta\_raw[indices, , drop }\OtherTok{=} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{]}
  
\NormalTok{  gen\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{()}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_sims) \{}
\NormalTok{    true\_logit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(sampled\_vars[i, }\StringTok{"theta\_logit"}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{    true\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(true\_logit) }\CommentTok{\# Logistic transformation}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Simulate data based on these posterior{-}conditioned parameters }
\NormalTok{    y\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(observed\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_trials, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prob =}\NormalTok{ true\_prob)}
    
\NormalTok{    gen\_list[[i]] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{N\_trials          =}\NormalTok{ observed\_data\_list}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_trials,}
      \AttributeTok{y                 =}\NormalTok{ y\_sim,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_mu    =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_theta\_sigma =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics   =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}
  
  \CommentTok{\# PSBC requires parameters in \textquotesingle{}variables\textquotesingle{} and Stan data in \textquotesingle{}generated\textquotesingle{}}
  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{SBC\_datasets}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{variables =}\NormalTok{ sampled\_vars, }\AttributeTok{generated =}\NormalTok{ gen\_list))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{psbc\_results\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch07\_psbc\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(psbc\_results\_file)) \{}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_datasets\_theta }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_psbc\_data\_theta}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n\_sims =} \DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{sequential)}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_results\_theta }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(psbc\_datasets\_theta, sbc\_backend)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(psbc\_results\_theta, psbc\_results\_file)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_results\_theta }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(psbc\_results\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing PSBC results from:"}\NormalTok{, psbc\_results\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing PSBC results from: simmodels/ch07_psbc_results.rds 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Visualize Rank Histograms for local calibration}
\NormalTok{SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(psbc\_results\_theta, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \StringTok{"theta"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior SBC: Simple Biased Agent"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_posterior_sbc-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Once we have done all this work and we are satisfied it's time to either
move onto empirical data, or to increase the model complexity and make
sure that the new model also works. We'll leave empirical data for a
future chapter, so now let's move to a more complex model.

\section{Extending Checks to the Memory
Model}\label{extending-checks-to-the-memory-model}

The biased agent is a clean single-parameter model. Real cognitive
models are messier. This section applies the same six-phase workflow to
the memory model from Ch. 5 --- a model with two parameters
(\texttt{bias} and \texttt{beta}) where the choice probability on each
trial depends on the opponent's cumulative history.

The workflow will expose a genuine identifiability problem:
\texttt{bias} and \texttt{beta} are correlated in the posterior when the
opponent plays with a constant bias. We will diagnose why this happens,
show that it is a property of the \emph{experimental design} rather than
the model, and fix it by simulating data from a reversal learning
paradigm --- an opponent who switches preference halfway through the
session. The fixed dataset is then saved for use in Chapters 6 and 7.

This is the normal cycle of model development: build → validate →
discover failure → understand its cause → fix the design → validate
again.

When a model incorporates recursive memory --- the agent's internal
state at time \(t\) depends on time \(t-1\) --- the posterior geometry
becomes nonlinear and HMC engines can struggle if the priors are
misspecified or parameters are weakly identified. The checks below catch
all of this.

Before we can subject the Memory Model to our diagnostic battery, we
must remind ourself of its mathematical architecture and build its
isolated generative simulator in R. This simulator will serve as the
invariant engine for our Prior Predictives, Parameter Recovery, and SBC.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} The Social Memory Generative Engine {-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# }
\CommentTok{\#\textquotesingle{} Simulate a Social Memory Agent}
\CommentTok{\#\textquotesingle{} }
\CommentTok{\#\textquotesingle{} @param bias Numeric. Baseline log{-}odds of choosing Right.}
\CommentTok{\#\textquotesingle{} @param beta Numeric. Sensitivity to the opponent\textquotesingle{}s cumulative rate.}
\CommentTok{\#\textquotesingle{} @param opponent\_choices Integer Vector. The sequence of choices made by the \textquotesingle{}Other\textquotesingle{}.}
\CommentTok{\#\textquotesingle{} @return A tibble of the agent\textquotesingle{}s internal states and choices.}
\NormalTok{simulate\_memory }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(bias, beta, opponent\_choices) \{}
\NormalTok{  n\_trials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(opponent\_choices)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Calculate cumulative rate up to trial t{-}1 (Memory Input)}
  \CommentTok{\# We start with a neutral prior of 0.5}
\NormalTok{  opp\_rate }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(opponent\_choices) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(opponent\_choices)}
\NormalTok{  memory\_input }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{lag}\NormalTok{(opp\_rate, }\AttributeTok{default =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Enforce numerical stability for the logit transformation}
\NormalTok{  memory\_input }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{pmin}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(memory\_input, }\FloatTok{0.01}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.99}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Compute choice probabilities: logit(p) = bias + beta * logit(memory)}
  \CommentTok{\# We use qlogis() for the logit transformation}
\NormalTok{  theta\_probs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(bias }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ beta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{qlogis}\NormalTok{(memory\_input))}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Generate choices}
\NormalTok{  self\_choices }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(n\_trials, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta\_probs)}
  
\NormalTok{  tibble}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{trial =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_trials,}
    \AttributeTok{other\_choice =}\NormalTok{ opponent\_choices,}
    \AttributeTok{memory\_state =}\NormalTok{ memory\_input,}
    \AttributeTok{theta =}\NormalTok{ theta\_probs,}
    \AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ self\_choices}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{2. Defining the Stan Model with Generated
Quantities:}\label{defining-the-stan-model-with-generated-quantities}

Now, we define the Stan model for the memory agent. The memory state is
calculated internally within the transformed parameters block based on
the opponent's choices (other). Crucially, we include simulations in the
generated quantities block for both prior and posterior predictive
checks, and calculate log-likelihood and joint log prior for sensitivity
analyses.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Stan model for Memory Agent with Generated Quantities}
\NormalTok{stan\_memory\_gq\_code }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// ch07\_MemoryBernoulli\_GQ.stan}
\StringTok{// Social Memory Agent: Estimates bias and memory sensitivity.}

\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_trials;               // Number of trials}
\StringTok{  array[N\_trials] int y;               // Agent\textquotesingle{}s choices (0, 1)}
\StringTok{  array[N\_trials] int other;           // Opponent\textquotesingle{}s choices (0, 1)}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  // Dynamic Prior Controls}
\StringTok{  real prior\_bias\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_bias\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_beta\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_beta\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real bias; }
\StringTok{  real beta; }
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_trials] memory;}
\StringTok{  memory[1] = 0.5; // Neutral start}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  for (t in 2:N\_trials) \{}
\StringTok{    // Recursive update: running average of \textquotesingle{}other\textquotesingle{} choices}
\StringTok{    memory[t] = memory[t{-}1] + ((other[t{-}1] {-} memory[t{-}1]) / (t{-}1.0));}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    // Boundary clipping for numerical stability of the logit link}
\StringTok{    memory[t] = fmax(0.01, fmin(0.99, memory[t]));}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Principled regularizing priors}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(bias | prior\_bias\_mu, prior\_bias\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(beta | prior\_beta\_mu, prior\_beta\_sigma);}

\StringTok{  // Likelihood function}
\StringTok{  // We use the vectorized bernoulli\_logit for computational efficiency}
\StringTok{  target += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y | bias + beta * logit(memory));}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  // {-}{-}{-} Prior Predictive Checks {-}{-}{-}}
\StringTok{  real bias\_prior = normal\_rng(prior\_bias\_mu, prior\_bias\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  real beta\_prior = normal\_rng(prior\_beta\_mu, prior\_beta\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  // {-}{-}{-} Conditional Diagnostics {-}{-}{-}}
\StringTok{  array[N\_trials] int y\_prior\_rep;}
\StringTok{  int prior\_sum;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_trials] int y\_post\_rep;}
\StringTok{  int post\_sum;}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_trials] log\_lik;}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (t in 1:N\_trials) \{}
\StringTok{      y\_prior\_rep[t] = bernoulli\_logit\_rng(bias\_prior + beta\_prior * logit(memory[t]));}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{    // Summary Statistic: Cumulative Choice Rate}
\StringTok{    prior\_sum = sum(y\_prior\_rep);}

\StringTok{    // {-}{-}{-} Posterior Predictive Checks {-}{-}{-}}
\StringTok{    for (t in 1:N\_trials) \{}
\StringTok{      y\_post\_rep[t] = bernoulli\_logit\_rng(bias + beta * logit(memory[t]));}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{    post\_sum = sum(y\_post\_rep);}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    for (t in 1:N\_trials) \{}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[t] = bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y[t] | bias + beta * logit(memory[t]));}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}

\StringTok{  // 2. Total Log{-}Prior (for Sensitivity/Update analysis)}
\StringTok{  real lprior = normal\_lpdf(bias | prior\_bias\_mu, prior\_bias\_sigma) + }
\StringTok{                normal\_lpdf(beta | prior\_beta\_mu, prior\_beta\_sigma);}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}
\NormalTok{stan\_file\_mem\_gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_model\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch07\_Memory\_GQ.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_memory\_gq\_code, stan\_file\_mem\_gq)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Fitting and Generative
Validation}\label{fitting-and-generative-validation}

We execute the fit using the data generated by our invariant R
simulator. Notice we keep the priors relatively narrow to prevent the
``Beta'' parameter (memory weight) from creating pathological
deterministic choices during the warmup phase.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{trials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{120}
\NormalTok{opponent\_choices }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(trials, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\CommentTok{\# Opponent prefers \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} 80\% of the time}

\NormalTok{d\_memory\_obs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_memory}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{bias =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{beta =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{opponent\_choices =}\NormalTok{ opponent\_choices}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Prepare the data list for Stan}
\NormalTok{observed\_data\_list\_mem }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{N\_trials =}\NormalTok{ N\_trials,}
  \AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ d\_memory\_obs}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice,}
  \AttributeTok{other =}\NormalTok{ d\_memory\_obs}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{other\_choice,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_mu =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_sigma =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_beta\_mu =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prior\_beta\_sigma =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics =} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{model\_file\_mem\_gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch07\_Memory\_GQ.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Fit the model}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(model\_file\_mem\_gq)) \{}
\NormalTok{  mod\_mem\_gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_mem\_gq)}

\NormalTok{  fit\_mem\_gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_mem\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ observed\_data\_list\_mem,}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, parallel}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{detectCores}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{2000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,     }\CommentTok{\# Increased for the nonlinear memory geometry}
    \AttributeTok{max\_treedepth =} \DecValTok{12}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \CommentTok{\#fit\_mem\_gq$save\_object(file = model\_file\_mem\_gq)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mem\_gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(model\_file\_mem\_gq)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Running MCMC with 4 chains, at most 8 in parallel...

Chain 1 finished in 0.4 seconds.
Chain 2 finished in 0.4 seconds.
Chain 3 finished in 0.4 seconds.
Chain 4 finished in 0.4 seconds.

All 4 chains finished successfully.
Mean chain execution time: 0.4 seconds.
Total execution time: 0.6 seconds.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Visualizing the Cognitive State {-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Let\textquotesingle{}s observe how the model \textquotesingle{}thinks\textquotesingle{} the memory state evolved}

\NormalTok{plot\_memory\_trace }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ d\_memory\_obs }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ memory\_state)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ other\_choice), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"The Memory Trace: Tracked Opponent Choice Rate"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(plot\_memory\_trace)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/05-fit-memory-model-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The transition from a static bias to a dynamic memory sensitivity
(\(\beta\)) introduces nonlinearities in the posterior. Our first step
is to verify that the NUTS sampler navigated this geometry without
pathology.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{fit\_mem\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"beta"}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 2 x 10
  variable  mean median    sd   mad     q5   q95  rhat ess_bulk ess_tail
  <chr>    <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
1 bias     0.291  0.294 0.384 0.382 -0.346 0.926  1.00    1850.    2522.
2 beta     0.375  0.370 0.334 0.330 -0.174 0.933  1.00    1845.    2543.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{diagnostic\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mem\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{()}

\CommentTok{\# 1. Check for Divergent Transitions (Zero Tolerance)}
\NormalTok{divergences }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(diagnostic\_summary}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{num\_divergent)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (divergences }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{stop}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"CRITICAL FAILURE:"}\NormalTok{, divergences, }\StringTok{"divergences detected. The posterior geometry is pathological. Reparameterization required. Do not interpret these results."}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Check E{-}BFMI (Energy{-}Bayesian Fraction of Missing Information)}
\CommentTok{\# Threshold is typically 0.3. Values below this indicate poor exploration.}
\NormalTok{min\_ebfmi }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(diagnostic\_summary}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ebfmi)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (min\_ebfmi }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{warning}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"WARNING: Low E{-}BFMI detected (Min ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(min\_ebfmi, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }
                \StringTok{"). The sampler may struggle to explore the tails of the posterior."}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Check for Convergence (Rhat \textless{} 1.01)}
\NormalTok{max\_rhat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(fit\_summary}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{rhat, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (max\_rhat }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.01}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{stop}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"CRITICAL FAILURE: Chains did not converge. Max Rhat ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(max\_rhat, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 4. Check Effective Sample Size (ESS \textgreater{} 400 for both bulk and tail)}
\NormalTok{min\_ess\_bulk }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(fit\_summary}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ess\_bulk, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{min\_ess\_tail }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(fit\_summary}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ess\_tail, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (min\_ess\_bulk }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \DecValTok{400} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ min\_ess\_tail }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \DecValTok{400}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{warning}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"WARNING: Insufficient Effective Sample Size. Autocorrelation is too high. Run longer chains."}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsubsection{Prior and Posterior Predictive
Check:}\label{prior-and-posterior-predictive-check}

We now compare what the model expected before seeing the data versus
what it ``learned'' after seeing the agent's choices. We will use the
cumulative choice rate as our primary summary statistic.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Extract Prior and Posterior Replications}
\NormalTok{draws\_mem }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_mem\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{())}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Wrangle for Comparison}
\NormalTok{predictive\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Value =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(draws\_mem}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_sum, draws\_mem}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{post\_sum),}
  \AttributeTok{Type =} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{each =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(draws\_mem))}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Visualize Predictive Overlap}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(predictive\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Type)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(observed\_data\_list\_mem}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{h), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior Predictive"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"gray70"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior Predictive"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Predictive Check: Total \textquotesingle{}Right\textquotesingle{} Choices"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Cumulative Count of Choices"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/05-memory-predictive-checks-1.pdf}
\end{center}

We must investigate how our findings are shaped by our priors. We'll
first run a prior-posterior update check and then use the analytical
priorsense power-scaling to check both bias and beta.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{true\_vals }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Parameter =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Memory Strength (Beta)"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{Value =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{) }\CommentTok{\# true\_bias\_mem and true\_beta\_mem}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Wrangle draws for comparison}
\NormalTok{update\_long }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ draws\_mem }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(bias, beta, bias\_prior, beta\_prior) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param\_raw"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"Value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Distribution =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_detect}\NormalTok{(param\_raw, }\StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
                          \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{Parameter =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_detect}\NormalTok{(param\_raw, }\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"Bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Memory Strength (Beta)"}\NormalTok{),}
                       \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Memory Strength (Beta)"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Plot with Ground Truth markers}
\NormalTok{p\_mem\_update }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(update\_long, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Distribution)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ true\_vals, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ Value), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{Parameter, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#ffbaba"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Posterior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Epistemic Update: Parameter Convergence"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Dashed line represents the true generative parameter"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Parameter Value"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_mem\_update)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/05 -prior-sensitivity-memory-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{sensitivity\_mem }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_sensitivity}\NormalTok{(fit\_mem\_gq, }\AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"beta"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_plot\_dens}\NormalTok{(fit\_mem\_gq, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"beta"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/05 -prior-sensitivity-memory-2.pdf}
\end{center}

While the marginal updates show that we recovered the parameters, we
must also inspect the joint posterior distribution. If Bias and Beta are
highly correlated, our experimental design may not be strong enough to
pull these two cognitive strategies apart.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Phase 10b: Parameter Correlation \& Joint Posterior {-}{-}{-}}

\CommentTok{\# 1. Extract the posterior draws (excluding prior simulations)}
\NormalTok{post\_draws\_mem }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ draws\_mem }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(bias, beta)}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient}
\NormalTok{post\_cor }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(post\_draws\_mem}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{bias, post\_draws\_mem}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{beta)}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Visualize the Joint Posterior Surface}
\NormalTok{p\_joint\_posterior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(post\_draws\_mem, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ bias, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ beta)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add a 2D density surface to see the "shape" of the uncertainty}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(level)), }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"polygon"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add the individual MCMC points (thinned for clarity)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"skyblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add the ground truth marker (Bias=0, Beta=0.9)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stroke =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{guide =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Joint Posterior: Bias vs. Memory Strength"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior Correlation (r) = "}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(post\_cor, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Baseline Bias ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ bias }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Memory Strength ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ beta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =} \FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Ground Truth"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_joint\_posterior)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/ch06_memory_correlation-1.pdf}
\end{center}

This is a problem. How can we make this correlation disappear? We have
at least two options: 1. Play with the priors, make them stricter 2.
Increase the amount of data

As we kinda like the priors, we will now explicitly manipulate the
experimental design (\(N \in \{30, 60, 120, 240, 480\}\)). We want to
observe the ``cigar'' shaped posterior mathematically decouple into a
tight, independent sphere.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Phase 12b: Asymptotic Identifiability \& Covariance Collapse {-}{-}{-}}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Initiating Asymptotic Identifiability Grid...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Initiating Asymptotic Identifiability Grid...
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Define the experimental bounds}
\NormalTok{trial\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{30}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{60}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{120}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{240}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{480}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{true\_bias\_mem }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.0}
\NormalTok{true\_beta\_mem }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.9}

\NormalTok{cov\_collapse\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch07\_cov\_collapse\_draws.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(cov\_collapse\_file)) \{}
  
  \CommentTok{\# 2. Define the isolated recovery pipeline}
\NormalTok{  run\_cov\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(n\_trials) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Simulate the opponent (80\% Right preference)}
\NormalTok{    opp\_choices }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(n\_trials, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Simulate our agent using the invariant generator}
\NormalTok{    sim\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_memory}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{bias =}\NormalTok{ true\_bias\_mem, }
      \AttributeTok{beta =}\NormalTok{ true\_beta\_mem, }
      \AttributeTok{opponent\_choices =}\NormalTok{ opp\_choices}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Structure the data block for Stan}
\NormalTok{    rec\_data\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{n =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials,}
      \AttributeTok{h =}\NormalTok{ sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice,}
      \AttributeTok{other =}\NormalTok{ sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{other\_choice,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_m =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_s =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_beta\_m =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prior\_beta\_s =} \FloatTok{1.0}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Fit the generative model}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_mem\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ rec\_data\_list,}
      \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\CommentTok{\# Prevent core oversubscription}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{2000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Extract strictly the parameters of interest}
\NormalTok{    draws\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"beta"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{)}
    
\NormalTok{    tibble}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{trials =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials,}
      \AttributeTok{bias =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{bias,}
      \AttributeTok{beta =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{beta}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# 3. Execute mapping using parallelization}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  cov\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ furrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{future\_map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    trial\_grid, }
\NormalTok{    run\_cov\_recovery, }
    \AttributeTok{.options =}\NormalTok{ furrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stdout =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{sequential)}
  
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(cov\_draws, cov\_collapse\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Covariance collapse grid completed. Draws saved to:"}\NormalTok{, cov\_collapse\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  cov\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(cov\_collapse\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing covariance draws from:"}\NormalTok{, cov\_collapse\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing covariance draws from: simmodels/ch07_cov_collapse_draws.rds 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 4. Compute the correlation coefficient (r) for each trial count}
\NormalTok{cor\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cov\_draws }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(trials) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{r\_val =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(bias, beta),}
    \AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"r = "}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(r\_val, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \CommentTok{\# Create a highly structured facet label}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{facet\_label =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(trials, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ trial\_grid, }\AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N = "}\NormalTok{, trial\_grid)))}

\CommentTok{\# Merge facet labels back into the main draws for ggplot}
\NormalTok{cov\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cov\_draws }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(cor\_summary }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(trials, facet\_label), }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"trials"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# 5. Visualize the geometric collapse of the covariance}

\NormalTok{p\_cov\_collapse }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(cov\_draws, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ bias, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ beta)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Draw the density topology}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(level)), }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"polygon"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The invariant generative ground truth}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_bias\_mem, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_beta\_mem, }
             \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stroke =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Add the correlation text dynamically to each facet}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ cor\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ label), }
            \AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{inherit.aes =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ facet\_label, }\AttributeTok{nrow =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{guide =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{3.0}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{xlab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Baseline Bias ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ bias }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ylab}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Memory Strength ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ beta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"NA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{panel.grid.major =} \FunctionTok{element\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray90"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_cov\_collapse)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/05-memory-covariance-collapse-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Diagnosis:} The correlation between \texttt{bias} and
\texttt{beta} is not a numerical accident --- it is a structural
consequence of the experimental design. When the opponent plays with a
\emph{constant} bias (e.g.~always 80\% right), both parameters can
explain the agent's rightward tendency equally well:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  A high \texttt{beta} with neutral \texttt{bias} says ``the agent
  tracks the opponent's pattern and follows it.''
\item
  A high \texttt{bias} with low \texttt{beta} says ``the agent just
  always prefers right.''
\end{itemize}

Both stories are consistent with the data when the opponent never
changes. The model cannot tell them apart because the data provide no
discriminating evidence. Adding more trials of the same constant-bias
opponent does reduce the correlation somewhat (as the covariance
collapse grid showed), but not enough to make the parameters cleanly
identifiable.

\textbf{The fix --- reversal learning:} If the opponent \emph{switches}
preference halfway through the session (e.g.~80\% right for the first
120 trials, then 20\% right for the next 120), a pure-bias agent and a
memory agent make completely different predictions in the second half.
The pure-bias agent continues right; the memory agent flips. This
contrast is what discriminates \texttt{bias} from \texttt{beta}. We call
this a \textbf{reversal learning} paradigm.

We now simulate this design, fit the same memory model, and verify that
the \texttt{bias}/\texttt{beta} correlation collapses. We also save the
reversal dataset for use in Chapters 6 and 7.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{42}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{n\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{240}  \CommentTok{\# 120 trials per phase}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} 1. Simulate opponent with preference reversal {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{opponent\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{120}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{),  }\CommentTok{\# Phase 1: strongly right{-}biased}
  \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{120}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{)   }\CommentTok{\# Phase 2: reversal — strongly left{-}biased}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} 2. Simulate agent (true bias = 0, true beta = 0.9) {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{d\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_memory}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{bias             =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{beta             =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{opponent\_choices =}\NormalTok{ opponent\_reversal}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} 3. Visualise the reversal to confirm the design {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{p\_reversal\_design }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{trial         =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_reversal,}
  \AttributeTok{opponent\_rate =} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(opponent\_reversal) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(opponent\_reversal),}
  \AttributeTok{memory\_state  =}\NormalTok{ d\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{memory\_state}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(opponent\_rate, memory\_state),}
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"series"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{series =} \FunctionTok{recode}\NormalTok{(series,}
    \AttributeTok{opponent\_rate =} \StringTok{"Opponent cumulative rate"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{memory\_state  =} \StringTok{"Agent memory state"}
\NormalTok{  )) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ series)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \DecValTok{120}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{annotate}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"text"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =} \DecValTok{121}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Reversal"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
           \AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title  =} \StringTok{"Reversal Learning Design"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Opponent switches from 80\% right to 20\% right at trial 120"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Rate / Memory"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"top"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_reversal\_design)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{05-ModelQualityChecks_files/figure-pdf/05-reversal-learning-fix-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} 4. Fit the memory model to the reversal data {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{data\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{n            =}\NormalTok{ n\_reversal,}
  \AttributeTok{h            =}\NormalTok{ d\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice,}
  \AttributeTok{other        =}\NormalTok{ d\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{other\_choice,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_m =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_s =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_beta\_m =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{prior\_beta\_s =} \FloatTok{1.0}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{model\_file\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{file.path}\NormalTok{(stan\_results\_dir, }\StringTok{"ch06\_Memory\_Reversal.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(model\_file\_reversal)) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# mod\_mem\_gq was compiled earlier in this chapter}
\NormalTok{  fit\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_mem\_gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ data\_reversal,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =}\NormalTok{ parallel}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{detectCores}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{2000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{max\_treedepth   =} \DecValTok{12}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{file =}\NormalTok{ model\_file\_reversal)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(model\_file\_reversal)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} 5. Joint posterior: confirm bias/beta correlation collapses {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{draws\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"beta"}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{cor\_reversal   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(draws\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{bias, draws\_reversal}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{beta)}

\NormalTok{p\_joint\_reversal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(draws\_reversal, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ bias, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ beta)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(level)), }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"polygon"}\NormalTok{,}
                  \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stroke =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{guide =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Joint Posterior After Reversal Learning Design"}\NormalTok{,}
