\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sbc\_recovery)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_cp_execution-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 4. Quantify the Recovery (Correlation and RMSE)}
\NormalTok{recovery\_metrics }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(variable) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Pearson\_r =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value, median),}
    \AttributeTok{Spearman\_rho =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value, median, }\AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"spearman"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{RMSE =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{((simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ median)}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== PARAMETER RECOVERY QUANTIFICATION ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== PARAMETER RECOVERY QUANTIFICATION ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(recovery\_metrics, }\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}


|variable | Pearson_r| Spearman_rho|  RMSE|
|:--------|---------:|------------:|-----:|
|mu_theta |      0.99|        0.989| 0.212|
\end{verbatim}

Let's explore the warnings we got.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract diagnostics now so they are available for the targeted refit below}
\NormalTok{diags\_cp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{backend\_diagnostics}
\NormalTok{cp\_fail\_rate }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(diags\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{n\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{100}

\CommentTok{\# Divergence count distribution across all 200 universes}
\NormalTok{diags\_cp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{binwidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"CP Divergence Counts Across 200 SBC Universes"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.0f\%\% of universes produced at least one divergence. }
\StringTok{Even a single divergence invalidates the geometric guarantee of NUTS."}\NormalTok{, cp\_fail\_rate),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Number of Divergent Transitions"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Number of SBC Universes"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_cp_divergence_viz-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Locate the divergence{-}prone region: do they concentrate at small sigma\_theta?}
\NormalTok{divergence\_profile }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(sbc\_datasets\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variables) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    diags\_cp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(sim\_id, n\_divergent),}
    \AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"sim\_id"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{had\_divergence =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(divergence\_profile, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ sigma\_theta, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Clean"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Divergent"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{name =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"CP Divergences Concentrate at Small True sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"The funnel geometry only manifests when population variance is small."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ sigma[theta]),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Number of Divergent Transitions"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_cp_divergence_viz-2.pdf}

\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(divergence\_profile, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ mu\_theta, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ sigma\_theta, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Sampler Status"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Healthy"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Diverged"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"The Anatomy of Failure: Where does CP break?"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True Population Mean (mu\_theta)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"True Population Variance (sigma\_theta)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_cp_divergence_viz-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{hard\_universe\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_fit\_cp\_hard.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{hard\_universe\_idx }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ divergence\_profile }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(had\_divergence) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{slice\_min}\NormalTok{(sigma\_theta, }\AttributeTok{n =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{pull}\NormalTok{(sim\_id)}

\NormalTok{hard\_stan\_data   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_datasets\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{generated[[hard\_universe\_idx]]}
\CommentTok{\# Ensure compatibility with updated Stan model if cache is stale}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\StringTok{"prior\_mu\_theta\_mu"} \SpecialCharTok{\%in\%} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(hard\_stan\_data)) \{}
\NormalTok{  hard\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_mu\_theta\_mu        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  hard\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{1.5}
\NormalTok{  hard\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{  hard\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{run\_diagnostics          }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{\}}
\NormalTok{hard\_true\_sigma  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ divergence\_profile}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sigma\_theta[hard\_universe\_idx]}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"Selected universe \%d: true sigma\_theta = \%.3f}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{  hard\_universe\_idx, hard\_true\_sigma}
\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Selected universe 198: true sigma_theta = 0.016
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(hard\_universe\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_cp\_hard }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_biased\_ml\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ hard\_stan\_data,}
    \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{999}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{init            =}\NormalTok{ init\_cp\_list,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_cp\_hard}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(hard\_universe\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_cp\_hard }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(hard\_universe\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 1. Generate the pairs plot }
\NormalTok{p\_funnel }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mcmc\_pairs}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  fit\_cp\_hard}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(),}
  \AttributeTok{pars     =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"theta\_logit[1]"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{np       =} \FunctionTok{nuts\_params}\NormalTok{(fit\_cp\_hard),}
  \AttributeTok{np\_style =} \FunctionTok{pairs\_style\_np}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{div\_color =} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{div\_alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{div\_size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{diag\_fun     =} \StringTok{"dens"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{off\_diag\_fun =} \StringTok{"hex"}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{wrap\_elements}\NormalTok{(p\_funnel) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{plot\_annotation}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Neal\textquotesingle{}s Funnel: CP Geometry at True sigma\_theta = \%.3f"}\NormalTok{, hard\_true\_sigma),}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red points are divergent transitions crashing at the funnel neck."}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_cp_hard_universe_fit-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Here we see a funnel. As sigma goes to zero, theta\_logit{[}1{]} can
only have one value: the value of the population (mu\_theta). The
curvature becomes infinitely steep, and the NUTS sampler fails to
explore this region, resulting in divergent transitions (red points).
This is the geometric pathology of the Centered Parameterization.

\subsection{Non-Centered Parameterization (NCP) as the
Solution}\label{non-centered-parameterization-ncp-as-the-solution}

\begin{quote}
\emph{Why does a coordinate transform fix a geometry problem?} Neal's
Funnel is not a flaw in the model --- it is a flaw in the \emph{space
the sampler must navigate}. In the CP, individual parameters
\(\theta_j\) live in a space whose width is controlled by
\(\sigma_\theta\): as \(\sigma_\theta
\to 0\) the space collapses. In the NCP, we sample standardized offsets
\(z_j \sim N(0,1)\) whose geometry is always the same --- the funnel
disappears because the sampler no longer navigates the narrowing space
directly. The parameters \(\theta_j\) are reconstructed \emph{after}
sampling, not during it.
\end{quote}

We have reached a critical juncture in our Bayesian workflow. We showed
computationally (via SBC and diagnostic pairs plots) that the Centered
Parameterization (CP) creates a pathological geometry---Neal's
Funnel---that makes it difficult for the NUTS sampler to efficiently
traverse the posterior space when data is sparse or population variance
is small. When the model is misbehaving, sometimes we need to reconsider
our theory and/or our data. Yet, in this case, we can do something to
the math underlying the model to topologically transform the posterior
space the model is sampling: a coordinate transformation known as the
Non-Centered Parameterization (NCP).

\subsubsection{The Geometry of the
Transformation}\label{the-geometry-of-the-transformation}

In the Centered Parameterization, the hierarchical dependency is
explicit and
direct:\[\theta_{\text{logit}, j} \sim \text{Normal}(\mu_\theta, \sigma_\theta)\]
This forces the sampler to navigate a space where the permissible bounds
of \(\theta\) are dynamically restricted by \(\sigma\), creating the
funnel. In the Non-Centered Parameterization, we decouple this
dependency, in a way that is not dissimilar from rescaling your
predictors - a common procedure you probably learned in your statistical
course to help model convergence and interpretation.

We introduce an auxiliary, standardized latent variable \(z_j\) for each
agent. We force the sampler to explore this \(z\)-space, which is simply
a perfectly behaved Normal distribution:\[z_j \sim \text{Normal}(0, 1)\]
Then, we deterministically shift and scale \(z_j\) to reconstruct our
target cognitive
parameter:\[\theta_{\text{logit}, j} = \mu_\theta + z_j \times \sigma_\theta\]

Mathematically, this is identical to the CP
(\(z \sim N(0,1) \implies \mu + z\sigma \sim N(\mu, \sigma)\)). However,
the computational geometry is entirely transformed and when the sampler
traverses it, the problems it meets are likely different.

\subsection{Stan Model: Multilevel Biased Agent
(NCP)}\label{stan-model-multilevel-biased-agent-ncp}

Here is the NCP version of the model. Note the changes in the
parameters, transformed parameters, and model blocks compared to the CP
version.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{stan\_biased\_ml\_ncp\_code }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Multilevel Biased Agent Model (Non{-}Centered Parameterization)}
\StringTok{// Estimates individual biases drawn from a population distribution.}

\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;                 // Total number of observations}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_subjects;              // Number of agents}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_subjects\textgreater{} subj\_id; // Agent ID}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;     // Observed choices}
\StringTok{  real prior\_mu\_theta\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  // Population{-}level parameters}
\StringTok{  real mu\_theta;                     // Population mean bias (logit scale)}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} sigma\_theta;         // Population SD of bias (logit scale)}

\StringTok{  // Individual{-}level parameters (standardized deviations, z{-}scores of the agents)}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] z\_theta;        // Non{-}centered individual effects}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  // Deterministic reconstruction of the cognitive parameters}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] theta\_logit = mu\_theta + z\_theta * sigma\_theta;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Priors for population{-}level parameters}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(mu\_theta | prior\_mu\_theta\_mu, prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += exponential\_lpdf(sigma\_theta | prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  /// Individual level prior (NON{-}CENTERED)}
\StringTok{  target += std\_normal\_lpdf(z\_theta);}

\StringTok{  // Likelihood}
\StringTok{  target += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y | theta\_logit[subj\_id]);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  // Transform hyperparameters back to the probability (scale)}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} mu\_theta\_prob = inv\_logit(mu\_theta);}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] theta\_prob = inv\_logit(theta\_logit);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  // Initialize containers for trial{-}level metrics}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_post\_rep;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_prior\_rep;}

\StringTok{  // {-}{-}{-} Prior Log{-}Density  {-}{-}{-}}
\StringTok{  real lprior;}
\StringTok{  lprior = normal\_lpdf(mu\_theta | prior\_mu\_theta\_mu, prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma) + }
\StringTok{           exponential\_lpdf(sigma\_theta | prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda) + }
\StringTok{           std\_normal\_lpdf(z\_theta);}

\StringTok{  // {-}{-}{-} Prior Predictive Checks: Generative Baseline {-}{-}{-}}
\StringTok{  real mu\_theta\_prior = normal\_rng(prior\_mu\_theta\_mu, prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  real sigma\_theta\_prior = exponential\_rng(prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda);}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    // 2. Draw individual agent parameters from the non{-}centered prior}
\StringTok{    vector[N\_subjects] theta\_logit\_prior;}
\StringTok{    for (j in 1:N\_subjects) \{}
\StringTok{      real z\_theta\_prior = normal\_rng(0, 1); }
\StringTok{      theta\_logit\_prior[j] = mu\_theta\_prior + z\_theta\_prior * sigma\_theta\_prior;}
\StringTok{    \}}

\StringTok{    // {-}{-}{-} Trial{-}Level Computations {-}{-}{-}}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y[i] | theta\_logit[subj\_id[i]]);}
\StringTok{      y\_post\_rep[i] = bernoulli\_logit\_rng(theta\_logit[subj\_id[i]]);}
\StringTok{      y\_prior\_rep[i] = bernoulli\_logit\_rng(theta\_logit\_prior[subj\_id[i]]);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}"}

\CommentTok{\# Write Stan code to file}
\NormalTok{stan\_file\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_multilevel\_biased\_ncp.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_biased\_ml\_ncp\_code, stan\_file\_ncp)}

\NormalTok{mod\_biased\_ml\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_ncp)}

\NormalTok{fit\_ncp\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_fit\_ncp.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Extract N\_subjects safely}
\NormalTok{  N\_subj\_val }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_subjects}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The dumb list (4 chains)}
\NormalTok{  init\_ncp\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mu\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sigma\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{z\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(N\_subj\_val, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mu\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sigma\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{z\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(N\_subj\_val, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mu\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sigma\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{z\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(N\_subj\_val, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mu\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sigma\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{z\_theta =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(N\_subj\_val, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{    fit\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_biased\_ml\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered,}
      \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{init            =}\NormalTok{ init\_ncp\_list,}
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    fit\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp\_path)}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{quote}
\textbf{Why we skip the full diagnostic battery here:} The NCP model is
not our production model for this dataset --- it is a \emph{pedagogical
fix}. We introduced the CP model specifically to watch it fail via SBC,
and the NCP to demonstrate the geometric solution. Running the full
six-phase battery (prior predictive, posterior predictive,
prior-posterior update, sensitivity, recovery, SBC) on both
parameterizations would be instructive but redundant for this chapter's
purpose. When you apply the NCP to your \emph{own} data, run all six
phases. Here we focus on the two diagnostics that directly reveal the
CP's failure and the NCP's fix: sampling efficiency and the SBC rank
histograms.
\end{quote}

\begin{tcolorbox}[enhanced jigsaw, toprule=.15mm, toptitle=1mm, bottomtitle=1mm, leftrule=.75mm, rightrule=.15mm, colframe=quarto-callout-tip-color-frame, left=2mm, colback=white, titlerule=0mm, coltitle=black, colbacktitle=quarto-callout-tip-color!10!white, opacityback=0, title=\textcolor{quarto-callout-tip-color}{\faLightbulb}\hspace{0.5em}{Stan Efficiency Rule 3 --- Subject Indexing: \texttt{subj\_id}
vs.~Contiguous Blocks}, arc=.35mm, opacitybacktitle=0.6, breakable, bottomrule=.15mm]

The models in this chapter index subject membership via an integer
array: \texttt{array{[}N\_total{]}\ int\ subj\_id}. On each trial row,
\texttt{subj\_id{[}i{]}} says which subject that row belongs to. This
\textbf{scatter-index} pattern is simple and works perfectly for models
where trial order does not matter --- like the biased-agent model here,
where the likelihood is
\texttt{bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y\ \textbar{}\ theta{[}subj\_id{]})}.

However, from Chapter 9 onward, all models are \textbf{path-dependent}:
the predicted probability on trial \(t\) is a function of everything
that happened on trials \(1, \ldots, t-1\) within the same subject. For
those models, we need to process each subject's trials in strict
sequence, which means we must know exactly where each subject's block of
rows starts and ends in the data array.

The \textbf{contiguous-block} pattern achieves this:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Sort data by subject, then by trial within subject}
\NormalTok{data\_sorted }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ data }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(subject\_id, trial)}
\NormalTok{bounds }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ data\_sorted }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{row =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(subject\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{subj\_start =} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(row), }\AttributeTok{subj\_end =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(row))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{data}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \DataTypeTok{array}\NormalTok{[N\_subjects] }\DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ subj\_start;  }\CommentTok{// first row index for each subject}
  \DataTypeTok{array}\NormalTok{[N\_subjects] }\DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ subj\_end;    }\CommentTok{// last row index for each subject}
\NormalTok{\}}
\KeywordTok{model}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (j }\ControlFlowTok{in} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{:N\_subjects) \{}
    \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in}\NormalTok{ subj\_start[j]:subj\_end[j]) \{}
      \CommentTok{// process trials in order — history is correct}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

This layout also enables the \textbf{\texttt{reduce\_sum} parallelism}
introduced in Chapter 11: because each subject's trials form a
self-contained contiguous block, independent subject loops can be
dispatched to separate CPU threads without any shared mutable state.

We keep \texttt{subj\_id} here for simplicity. You will see
\texttt{subj\_start/subj\_end} throughout Chapters 9--11.

\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Timing, ESS, and Pathfinder warm{-}start comparison for CP vs NCP}

\CommentTok{\# ── 1. Wall{-}clock and ESS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{time\_cp  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{time}\NormalTok{()}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total}
\NormalTok{time\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{time}\NormalTok{()}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total}

\NormalTok{ess\_cp  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{summarise\_draws}\NormalTok{(fit\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{)),  }\StringTok{"ess\_bulk"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ess\_cp  =}\NormalTok{ ess\_bulk)}
\NormalTok{ess\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{summarise\_draws}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{)), }\StringTok{"ess\_bulk"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ess\_ncp =}\NormalTok{ ess\_bulk)}

\NormalTok{efficiency\_comparison }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ess\_cp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(ess\_ncp, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"variable"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{ess\_per\_sec\_cp  =}\NormalTok{ ess\_cp  }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ time\_cp,}
    \AttributeTok{ess\_per\_sec\_ncp =}\NormalTok{ ess\_ncp }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ time\_ncp}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== COMPUTATIONAL SPEED (WALL{-}CLOCK) ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== COMPUTATIONAL SPEED (WALL-CLOCK) ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"CP  Total Time:"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(time\_cp,  }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"seconds}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
CP  Total Time: 4.22 seconds
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"NCP Total Time:"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(time\_ncp, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"seconds}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
NCP Total Time: 6.23 seconds
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Speedup Factor:"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(time\_cp }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ time\_ncp, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"x}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Speedup Factor: 0.68 x
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== SAMPLING EFFICIENCY (ESS / SECOND) ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== SAMPLING EFFICIENCY (ESS / SECOND) ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(efficiency\_comparison, }\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}


|variable    | ess_cp| ess_ncp| ess_per_sec_cp| ess_per_sec_ncp|
|:-----------|------:|-------:|--------------:|---------------:|
|mu_theta    | 4407.2|   603.3|         1044.8|            96.8|
|sigma_theta | 3472.6|   600.7|          823.2|            96.4|
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# ── 2. Pathfinder warm{-}start timing for NCP ───────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{fit\_ncp\_pf\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_fit\_ncp\_pathfinder\_init.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp\_pf\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  pf\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_biased\_ml\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
                                  \AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{42}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  init\_for\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf\_ncp)) pf\_ncp }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ init\_ncp\_list}

\NormalTok{  t\_pf\_start }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{proc.time}\NormalTok{()[}\StringTok{"elapsed"}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{  fit\_ncp\_pf }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_biased\_ml\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered,}
    \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{124}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,        }\CommentTok{\# shorter warmup thanks to Pathfinder init}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{init            =}\NormalTok{ init\_for\_ncp,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  time\_pf\_total }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{proc.time}\NormalTok{()[}\StringTok{"elapsed"}\NormalTok{] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ t\_pf\_start) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
                   \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf\_ncp)) pf\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{time}\NormalTok{()}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total }\ControlFlowTok{else} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  fit\_ncp\_pf}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp\_pf\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_ncp\_pf }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp\_pf\_path)}
\NormalTok{  time\_pf\_total }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_ncp\_pf}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{time}\NormalTok{()}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total   }\CommentTok{\# approximate (Pathfinder time not stored)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{timing\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ tibble}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tribble}\NormalTok{(}
  \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{Method,                       }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Time (s)}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{,  }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Warmup iter}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"CP (standard)"}\NormalTok{,                }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(time\_cp, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),    }\DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{L,}
  \StringTok{"NCP (standard init)"}\NormalTok{,          }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(time\_ncp, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),   }\DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{L,}
  \StringTok{"NCP (Pathfinder warm{-}start)"}\NormalTok{,  }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(time\_pf\_total, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{L}
\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(timing\_summary, }\AttributeTok{caption =} \StringTok{"Wall{-}clock time: CP vs NCP, with and without Pathfinder warm{-}start"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}lrr@{}}
\caption{Wall-clock time: CP vs NCP, with and without Pathfinder
warm-start}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
Method & Time (s) & Warmup iter \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
Method & Time (s) & Warmup iter \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
CP (standard) & 4.2 & 1000 \\
NCP (standard init) & 6.2 & 1000 \\
NCP (Pathfinder warm-start) & 5.0 & 500 \\
\end{longtable}

Here we see that the NCP model is sometimes slightly faster, sometimes
slightly slower than CP in raw wall-clock time, but always less
\emph{sampling-efficient} (lower ESS/second). This is the trade-off of
NCP: it eliminates the pathological geometry that causes SBC failures in
CP, at the cost of slightly less efficient sampling in data-rich
regimes. The Pathfinder warm-start partially recovers that efficiency by
cutting warmup iterations.

Yet, NCP might still end up exploring the space better. Let's see how it
fares on the SBC assessment that was giving issues to CP.

We will generate 200 random universes from the priors, fit the NCP model
to each, and plot the rank histograms.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{sbc\_ncp\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_sbc\_ncp.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{backend\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  mod\_biased\_ml\_ncp,}
  \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(sbc\_ncp\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Add keep\_fits = FALSE right here}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_results\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{datasets =}\NormalTok{ sbc\_datasets\_cp, }
    \AttributeTok{backend =}\NormalTok{ backend\_ncp, }
    \AttributeTok{keep\_fits =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}
  
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_ncp, sbc\_ncp\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_results\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(sbc\_ncp\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_ncp, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"SBC Rank Histograms: Non{-}Centered Parameterization"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_ncp_execution-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{sbc\_stats\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Filter for the population{-}level parameters you care about}
\CommentTok{\# (e.g., mu\_alpha, mu\_beta, rho, or even sigma[1])}
\NormalTok{recovery\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_stats\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{\%in\%} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Create the Parameter Recovery Identity Plot}
\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(recovery\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ median)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# The Identity Line (Perfect Recovery)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The Posterior Estimates with 90\% Credible Intervals}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q5, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95), }\AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Facet by parameter}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{variable, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Parameter Recovery (Extracted Directly from SBC)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Points represent the posterior median. Bars are 90\% Credible Intervals."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True Generative Value (Drawn from Prior)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior Median Estimate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sbc\_recovery)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_ncp_execution-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 4. Quantify the Recovery (Correlation and RMSE)}
\NormalTok{recovery\_metrics }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(variable) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Pearson\_r =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value, median),}
    \AttributeTok{Spearman\_rho =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value, median, }\AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"spearman"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{RMSE =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{((simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ median)}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== PARAMETER RECOVERY QUANTIFICATION ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== PARAMETER RECOVERY QUANTIFICATION ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(recovery\_metrics, }\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}


|variable    | Pearson_r| Spearman_rho|  RMSE|
|:-----------|---------:|------------:|-----:|
|mu_theta    |     0.990|        0.989| 0.212|
|sigma_theta |     0.986|        0.982| 0.191|
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract diagnostics now so they are available for the targeted refit below}
\NormalTok{diags\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{backend\_diagnostics}
\NormalTok{ncp\_fail\_rate }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(diags\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{n\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{100}

\CommentTok{\# Divergence count distribution across all 200 universes}
\NormalTok{diags\_ncp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{binwidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"NCP Divergence Counts Across 200 SBC Universes"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.0f\%\% of universes produced at least one divergence. }
\StringTok{Even a single divergence invalidates the geometric guarantee of NUTS."}\NormalTok{, cp\_fail\_rate),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Number of Divergent Transitions"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Number of SBC Universes"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_ncp_execution-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Locate the divergence{-}prone region: do they concentrate at small sigma\_theta?}
\NormalTok{divergence\_profile }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(sbc\_datasets\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variables) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    diags\_ncp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(sim\_id, n\_divergent),}
    \AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"sim\_id"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{had\_divergence =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(divergence\_profile, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ sigma\_theta, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Clean"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Divergent"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{name =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"NCP Divergences Concentrate at Small True sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"The funnel geometry only manifests when population variance is small."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ sigma[theta]),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Number of Divergent Transitions"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(divergence\_profile, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ mu\_theta, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ sigma\_theta, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Sampler Status"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"darkred"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Healthy"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Diverged"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"The Anatomy of Failure: Where does NCP break?"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True Population Mean (mu\_theta)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"True Population Variance (sigma\_theta)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_sbc_ncp_execution-5.pdf}
\end{center}

\subsubsection{Posterior Simulation-Based Calibration Checks: Probing
Local
Geometry}\label{posterior-simulation-based-calibration-checks-probing-local-geometry}

Prior SBC tested our sampler across the entire prior space --- it asked
whether NUTS is unbiased when the true parameters could be anything the
prior assigns non-negligible mass to. This is a global guarantee, and it
is necessary. But it is not sufficient. A sampler can be globally
unbiased yet struggle precisely in the regions that matter most: the
posterior neighborhoods that the actual observed data force us into.
When the likelihood is highly informative --- as it typically is once we
have a few hundred trials per agent --- the posterior concentrates
sharply in a small corner of the prior space. The sampler must now take
large numbers of tiny, precise steps in a geometrically restricted
region. Prior SBC never directly stresses this regime, because most of
its simulated universes place the true parameter somewhere across the
full prior, and only a fraction happen to fall in the data-rich
posterior neighborhood.

Posterior SBC (PSBC) is a complementary local diagnostic that directly
targets this regime. The logic inverts the generative direction. Instead
of drawing true parameters from the prior, we draw them from the
\emph{fitted posterior} --- a distribution that has already been
conditioned on our actual data and therefore concentrates in precisely
the region the sampler will need to navigate in practice. Concretely: we
sample a single draw \(\tilde{\theta}^{(s)}\) from the posterior
(\(\theta \mid y_{\text{obs}}\)), treat it as a ground truth, generate a
synthetic dataset
\(\tilde{y}^{(s)} \sim p(y \mid \tilde{\theta}^{(s)})\) from the data
model, fit the model to \(\tilde{y}^{(s)}\), and record where
\(\tilde{\theta}^{(s)}\) falls in the resulting posterior. If the
sampler is locally well-calibrated, these ranks are again uniform ---
but now over the concentrated geometry of the posterior, not the diffuse
geometry of the prior. A U-shaped rank histogram here means the sampler
is overconfident \emph{specifically when the data are informative},
which is exactly the failure mode we need to detect. Implementing this
requires us to write a \emph{factory function}: a function that closes
over a fitted model object and a data structure, and returns a new
generator function that the \texttt{SBC} package can call repeatedly to
produce fresh \(({\tilde{\theta}}, \tilde{y})\) pairs. If this pattern
is unfamiliar, read it as a function that \emph{builds} generators
rather than \emph{being} one ---
\texttt{make\_psbc\_generator(fit\_cp,\ stan\_data\_centered)} does not
itself generate a dataset; it returns a self-contained callable that
will do so every time the SBC machinery invokes it.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Define a robust factory function for the PSBC generator}
\NormalTok{make\_psbc\_generator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, original\_data) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Extract the full posterior array as a flat data frame}
\NormalTok{  draws\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{())}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Pre{-}compute the exact column names to guarantee flawless extraction}
\NormalTok{  theta\_cols }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta\_logit["}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_subjects, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  y\_cols }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"y\_post\_rep["}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_total, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The generator function required by the SBC package}
  \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Step A: Randomly sample one specific "state of the world" from the posterior}
\NormalTok{    idx }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(draws\_df)), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Step B: Package the true parameters for this specific simulation}
\NormalTok{    variables }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{mu\_theta =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mu\_theta[idx]),}
      \AttributeTok{sigma\_theta =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sigma\_theta[idx]),}
      \AttributeTok{theta\_logit =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(draws\_df[idx, theta\_cols])}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Step C: Package the dataset generated by this specific state of the world}
\NormalTok{    generated }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{N\_total                  =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_total,}
      \AttributeTok{N\_subjects               =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_subjects,}
      \AttributeTok{subj\_id                  =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{subj\_id,}
      \AttributeTok{y                        =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(draws\_df[idx, y\_cols]),}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_theta\_mu        =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_mu\_theta\_mu,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma     =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_mu\_theta\_sigma,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_sigma\_theta\_lambda,}
      \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics          =}\NormalTok{ original\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{run\_diagnostics}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{variables =}\NormalTok{ variables, }\AttributeTok{generated =}\NormalTok{ generated)}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{psbc\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_psbc.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(psbc\_path)) \{}
  
\NormalTok{  gen\_psbc\_cp  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{make\_psbc\_generator}\NormalTok{(fit\_cp,  stan\_data\_centered))}
\NormalTok{  gen\_psbc\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{make\_psbc\_generator}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp, stan\_data\_centered))}
  
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{8675309}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_data\_cp  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(gen\_psbc\_cp,  }\DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_data\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(gen\_psbc\_ncp, }\DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  backend\_cp\_psbc  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(mod\_biased\_ml\_cp,  }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  backend\_ncp\_psbc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(mod\_biased\_ml\_ncp, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(future}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_res\_cp  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(psbc\_data\_cp,  backend\_cp\_psbc)}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_res\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(psbc\_data\_ncp, backend\_ncp\_psbc)}
  
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{res\_cp  =}\NormalTok{ psbc\_res\_cp,}
      \AttributeTok{res\_ncp =}\NormalTok{ psbc\_res\_ncp}
\NormalTok{    ),}
\NormalTok{    psbc\_path}
\NormalTok{  )}
  
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_obj    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(psbc\_path)}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_res\_cp  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ psbc\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{res\_cp}
\NormalTok{  psbc\_res\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ psbc\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{res\_ncp}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(psbc\_res\_cp,  }\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"PSBC Rank Histograms: Centered Parameterization"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_psbc_execution-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(psbc\_res\_ncp, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"PSBC Rank Histograms: Non{-}Centered Parameterization"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_psbc_execution-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Extract the lightweight stats dataframe from the SBC object}
\NormalTok{sbc\_stats\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ psbc\_res\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Filter for the population{-}level parameters you care about}
\CommentTok{\# (e.g., mu\_alpha, mu\_beta, rho, or even sigma[1])}
\NormalTok{recovery\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_stats\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{\%in\%} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma\_theta"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Create the Parameter Recovery Identity Plot}
\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(recovery\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ median)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# The Identity Line (Perfect Recovery)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# The Posterior Estimates with 90\% Credible Intervals}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q5, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95), }\AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Facet by parameter}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{variable, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Parameter Recovery (Extracted Directly from SBC)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Points represent the posterior median. Bars are 90\% Credible Intervals."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True Generative Value (Drawn from Prior)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior Median Estimate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sbc\_recovery)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{06-MultilevelModels_files/figure-pdf/ch07_psbc_execution-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 4. Quantify the Recovery (Correlation and RMSE)}
\NormalTok{recovery\_metrics }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(variable) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Pearson\_r =} \FunctionTok{cor}\NormalTok{(simulated\_value, median),}
    \AttributeTok{RMSE =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{((simulated\_value }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ median)}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== PARAMETER RECOVERY QUANTIFICATION ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\end{verbatim}

\NormalTok{diags\_psbc\_ncp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ psbc\_res\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{backend\_diagnostics}

\NormalTok{psbc\_cp\_fail\_rate  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(diags\_psbc\_cp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{n\_divergent  }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{100}
\NormalTok{psbc\_ncp\_fail\_rate }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(diags\_psbc\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{n\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{100}

NCP Failure Rate: 0 %
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Comparing CP and NCP: Global Fragility vs.~Local
Efficiency}\label{comparing-cp-and-ncp-global-fragility-vs.-local-efficiency}

The CP did not seem to perform too bad, but it broke down when doing
SBC. We therefore reparameterized the model by decoupling the sampling
of individual and population level parameters (from CP to NCP). This
fundamentally altered how the Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (NUTS) algorithm
explores the posterior manifold.

We saw that NCP took slightly longer to run and was less efficient in
sampling (\textless{} ESS). We then ran extensive SBC both sampling the
simulations from the priors (global calibration) and sampling the
simulations from the fitted posteriors (local calibration).

By comparing global calibration (Prior SBC) against local calibration
(Posterior SBC), we have mathematically mapped the boundary conditions
of these parameterizations.

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Integration Stability and Global Fragility (Divergences): In CP,
  sparse data forces the sampler into the infinite curvature of Neal's
  Funnel. As proven by our Prior SBC failure rates, CP is globally
  fragile; it regularly diverged across the broad generative prior space
  whenever the true population variance (\(\sigma_\theta\)) was small.
  NCP, conversely, decouples the parameters. The sampler explores a
  standard, isotropic Gaussian space (\(z \sim \text{Normal}(0, 1)\))
  where the curvature is constant, completely immunizing the model
  against Neal's Funnel and dropping global divergences to zero.
\item
  Sampling Efficiency and Local Vulnerability (ESS and Speed): Because
  the NCP geometry is smooth and well-behaved in data-sparse regimes,
  the Hamiltonian integrator can take massive, efficient step sizes,
  causing the Effective Sample Size (ESS) to skyrocket. However, as our
  Posterior SBC proved, NCP is locally vulnerable to the Data Funnel (or
  ``Reverse Funnel''). In the specific neighborhood of our simulated
  data, the likelihood was highly informative and tightly constrained
  the posterior. CP navigated this sharp likelihood peak effortlessly.
  NCP, however, was forced to blindly explore a broad standard normal
  prior, only to find that the data violently rejected almost all of it,
  causing its ESS to plummet.
\item
  Stationarity (\(\hat{R}\)): While both models can achieve
  \(\hat{R} < 1.01\) under ideal local conditions, NCP achieves it
  reliably across the entire prior space, ensuring all Markov chains mix
  seamlessly regardless of the underlying data-generating universe.
\item
  The Geometric Trade-off: When CP Wins Given the global safety of NCP,
  it is tempting to conclude that we should always use the Non-Centered
  Parameterization. This is mathematically incorrect. The choice between
  CP and NCP is a strict geometric trade-off governed by the density of
  your data.

  ** The Data-Poor Regime (Use NCP): When trial counts (\(N_j\)) are low
  or population variance (\(\sigma_\theta\)) is small, the prior
  dominates the likelihood. This creates Neal's Funnel in the CP space.
  NCP thrives here because it decouples the parameters, allowing the
  sampler to easily and safely explore the prior geometry.

  ** The Data-Rich Regime (Use CP): Consider a scenario where every
  agent completes \(10,000\) trials. The likelihood becomes massively
  informative, crushing the uncertainty around
  \(\theta_{\text{logit}, j}\) into a tiny, tight peak.If we use CP, the
  sampler effortlessly navigates this tight, data-driven likelihood
  space. If we use NCP, the sampler attempts to explore the broad
  \(z \sim \text{Normal}(0,1)\) prior, only to find that almost the
  entire space is immediately rejected by the highly restrictive
  likelihood. This makes NCP highly inefficient, resulting in massive
  autocorrelation, low ESS, and high computational time.
\end{itemize}

In other words, there is no universal panacea in cognitive modeling. We
do not engage in blind ``tinkering'' to get our models to fit. Instead,
we adhere to the Iterative Bayesian Workflow: we fit the model, deploy a
strict diagnostic battery to identify the exact geometric failure
(prior-dominated funnels vs.~data-dominated funnels), and apply the
mathematically appropriate parameterization.

Because typical cognitive tasks (like our Matching Pennies game) involve
relatively sparse data (\(N_j \approx 100\)) and substantial
population-level shrinkage, we will utilize the Non-Centered
Parameterization as our default architecture as we proceed to construct
the more complex Memory and WSLS agents.

\section{The Epistemic Showdown: Visualizing
Shrinkage}\label{the-epistemic-showdown-visualizing-shrinkage}

We have theorized about the dangers of ignoring population structure
(Complete Pooling) and the dangers of ignoring noise in small samples
(No Pooling). In a rigorous Bayesian workflow, we do not rely on
intuition; we compute the alternatives and visualize the geometric
differences.We will quickly define and fit the two extreme models using
our strictly typed stan\_data\_centered. The complete and no-pooling
models are included here solely as visual contrasts --- they serve as
the ``wrong answer'' endpoints that make the partial pooling solution
legible. A full diagnostic battery on these two models would be
redundant: complete pooling is trivially identifiable (one parameter,
well-conditioned), and no-pooling is structurally identical to J
independent Ch. 6 models we already validated. The NCP model, which
actually matters, passed its full battery above.

\subsection{The Complete Pooling Model
(Underfitting)}\label{the-complete-pooling-model-underfitting}

This model assumes all agents are identical clones. The population
variance is forced to exactly \(0\). We estimate a single, universal
bias parameter.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{stan\_full\_pooling\_code }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real theta\_logit; // A single parameter for the entire universe}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(theta\_logit | 0, 1.5);}
\StringTok{  target += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y | theta\_logit);}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}
\NormalTok{stan\_file\_full }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_biased\_full\_pooling.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_full\_pooling\_code, stan\_file\_full)}
\NormalTok{mod\_full }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_full)}

\NormalTok{fit\_full\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_fit\_full\_pool.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_full\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_full }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_full}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{N\_total =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_total, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y),}
    \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_full}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_full\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_full }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(fit\_full\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{full\_pool\_est }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise\_draws}\NormalTok{(fit\_full}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta\_logit"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"median"}\NormalTok{)}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{median}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{The No Pooling Model
(Overfitting)}\label{the-no-pooling-model-overfitting}

This model assumes every agent is a completely isolated island. The
population variance is implicitly assumed to be \(\infty\). We estimate
an independent parameter for each agent, equipped only with a static,
weakly informative prior.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{stan\_no\_pooling\_code }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_subjects;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_subjects\textgreater{} subj\_id;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] theta\_logit; // N\_subjects completely independent parameters}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Static prior: No hierarchical learning}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(theta\_logit | 0, 1.5);}
\StringTok{  target += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(y | theta\_logit[subj\_id]);}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}
\NormalTok{stan\_file\_no }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_biased\_no\_pooling.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_no\_pooling\_code, stan\_file\_no)}
\NormalTok{mod\_no }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_no)}

\CommentTok{\# Fit the No Pooling model}
\NormalTok{fit\_no\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch07\_fit\_no\_pool.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_no\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_no }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_no}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_centered,}
    \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_no}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_no\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_no }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(fit\_no\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{no\_pool\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise\_draws}\NormalTok{(fit\_no}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta\_logit"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"median"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(variable, }\StringTok{"(?\textless{}=}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{[)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+(?=}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{])"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{theta\_no\_pool =}\NormalTok{ median)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{The Geometry of Shrinkage}\label{the-geometry-of-shrinkage}

We now have three distinct sets of inferences: * No Pooling: The raw,
isolated estimates (highly vulnerable to the noise of low \(N_j\)). *
Complete Pooling: The rigid, universal average (blind to genuine
individual differences). * Partial Pooling: Our hierarchical NCP model
estimates (the precision-weighted Bayesian compromise).

To truly evaluate the epistemological value of these models, we must
plot them not just against each other, but against the True Generative
Parameters (the hidden reality we simulated in R). We will also extract
the naive arithmetic mean of the No Pooling estimates to contrast it
with the precision-weighted Partial Pooling mean.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Extract the Partial Pooling Population and Individual Estimates (from the NCP model)}
\NormalTok{mu\_partial\_pool\_est }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise\_draws}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu\_theta"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"median"}\NormalTok{)}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{median}

\NormalTok{partial\_pool\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise\_draws}\NormalTok{(fit\_ncp}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta\_logit"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"median"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(variable, }\StringTok{"(?\textless{}=}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{[)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+(?=}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{])"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{theta\_partial\_pool =}\NormalTok{ median)}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Calculate the No Pooling Mean (simple arithmetic average of the isolated estimates)}
\NormalTok{no\_pool\_mean\_est }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(no\_pool\_summary}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta\_no\_pool)}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Calculate the True Generative Mean (The actual center of our simulated universe)}
\NormalTok{true\_mean\_est }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(true\_agent\_params}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{theta\_logit)}

\CommentTok{\# 4. Combine into a single dataframe, including the TRUE generative parameters}
\NormalTok{comparison\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ no\_pool\_summary }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, theta\_no\_pool) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(}
