\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{p\_hist1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results1,}
  \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_sigma\_op"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"log\_beta\_op"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"log\_beta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Rank histograms"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{p\_ecdf1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf\_diff}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results1,}
  \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_sigma\_op"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"log\_beta\_op"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"log\_beta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"ECDF difference"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{p\_hist1 }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_ecdf1}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.85\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{09-TheoryOfMind_files/figure-pdf/ch09_sbc_tom1_plot-1.pdf}

}

\caption{SBC for 1-ToM. Top row: rank histograms. Bottom row: ECDF
difference (blue inside grey band = calibrated). log\_beta and bias stay
inside the band. log\_sigma\_op and log\_beta\_op show substantial
excursions --- the expected empirical signature of an underidentified
parameter. The 1-ToM likelihood simply does not have enough contrast in
matching-pennies data to pin these opponent-model parameters down across
the prior.}

\end{figure}%

The SBC output is not a failure of the sampler --- HMC is doing its job.
It is a \emph{design} diagnostic: matching pennies, as a 2×2 game
without regime changes, lacks the likelihood contrast needed to identify
the opponent-model volatility and temperature. Once
\(\Sigma^{\text{self}}\) reaches its fixed point (within
\textasciitilde10 trials), the predicted opponent-choice trajectory
depends on \((\sigma_{\text{op}}, \beta_{\text{op}})\) only through a
compressed, nearly one-dimensional ridge inside the sigmoid. A
reversal-style paradigm, or a richer payoff structure, would be required
to break this ridge; we do not have that luxury with this dataset.

The scientific consequence is direct: we should not report per-species
or per-individual effects on \texttt{log\_sigma\_op} or
\texttt{log\_beta\_op}. Inference on 1-ToM from this data is credible
for the agent's \emph{own} decision-level parameters and for the
\emph{architecture indicator} (mixture weight, below), but not for the
internal opponent-model parameters.

\section{Phase 5 --- Prior sensitivity}\label{phase-5-prior-sensitivity}

A quick power-scaling check on a representative 1-ToM fit confirms that
the opponent-model priors are doing visible work --- posteriors for
\texttt{log\_sigma\_op} and \texttt{log\_beta\_op} shift when the prior
is up- or down-weighted, but posteriors for \texttt{log\_beta} and
\texttt{bias} are largely driven by the likelihood. Code pattern
(mirrors Ch. 6):

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Example pattern; run on any single fit:}
\CommentTok{\# powerscale\_sensitivity(}
\CommentTok{\#   fit\_proto\_single, variable = c("log\_sigma\_op","log\_beta\_op","log\_beta","bias")}
\CommentTok{\# ) |\textgreater{} print()}
\CommentTok{\# priorsense::powerscale\_plot\_dens(}
\CommentTok{\#   priorsense::powerscale\_sequence(}
\CommentTok{\#     fit\_proto\_single, variable = c("log\_sigma\_op","log\_beta\_op","log\_beta","bias"))}
\CommentTok{\# )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

This matches our expectation: interpret \texttt{log\_beta\_op} and
\texttt{log\_sigma\_op} as prior-regularized, put primary scientific
weight on the species-level mixture weight \(\pi_s\) (the
representational-capacity estimand below), and secondary weight on
\texttt{log\_beta} as a within-architecture decisional-determinism
measure.

\section{Phase 6 --- Model comparison under Occam's
razor}\label{phase-6-model-comparison-under-occams-razor}

Chapter 7 warned about a specific failure mode: a more flexible model
can always achieve equal or better \emph{in-sample} fit than a simpler
one, but PSIS-LOO rewards predictive performance on held-out data and
therefore \emph{should} prefer the simpler model whenever the extra
flexibility is not justified. Let us verify this for 0-ToM vs 1-ToM
directly, by fitting both models to datasets generated by three
different processes of increasing complexity:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{RS-opponent data}: a 0-ToM primate playing against a 65/35
  biased opponent. Simplest possible data-generating process.
\item
  \textbf{0-ToM-opponent data}: a 0-ToM primate playing against a 0-ToM
  opponent. The opponent reacts, but the primate does not represent that
  reactivity.
\item
  \textbf{1-ToM-opponent data}: a 1-ToM primate (truly recursive)
  playing against a 0-ToM opponent.
\end{enumerate}

If PSIS-LOO is doing its job, 1-ToM should tie or lose on datasets 1--2
(it is overparameterized for them) and win on dataset 3.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{occam\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_occam.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{simulate\_scenario }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{N =} \DecValTok{150}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(seed)}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"RS\_0tom"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{    op }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(N, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.65}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    dd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_tom0}\NormalTok{(op, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ seed)}
    \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ dd}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice, }\AttributeTok{op\_choice =}\NormalTok{ dd}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{op\_choice))}
\NormalTok{  \}}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"0tom\_0tom"}\NormalTok{) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Iterate: primate is 0{-}ToM, opponent is 0{-}ToM{-}mismatch.}
\NormalTok{    mu\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{; Sig\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{    mu\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{; Sig\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{    choice }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(N); op\_choice }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(N)}
    \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (t }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(N)) \{}
\NormalTok{      p\_op\_pred   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_p }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.36} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Sig\_p))}
\NormalTok{      p\_self\_pred }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_o }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.36} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Sig\_o))}
\NormalTok{      p\_self\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{((}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_op\_pred }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      p\_op\_t   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{((}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_self\_pred) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      choice[t]    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_self\_t)}
\NormalTok{      op\_choice[t] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_op\_t)}
\NormalTok{      s\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_p)}
\NormalTok{      Sig\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (Sig\_p }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ s\_p }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_p))}
\NormalTok{      mu\_p  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mu\_p }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Sig\_p }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (op\_choice[t] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_p)}
\NormalTok{      s\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_o)}
\NormalTok{      Sig\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (Sig\_o }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ s\_o }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_o))}
\NormalTok{      mu\_o  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mu\_o }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Sig\_o }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (choice[t] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_o)}
\NormalTok{    \}}
    \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ choice, }\AttributeTok{op\_choice =}\NormalTok{ op\_choice))}
\NormalTok{  \}}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"0tom\_1tom"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{    mu\_s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{; Sig\_s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1}     \CommentTok{\# 1{-}ToM primate\textquotesingle{}s model of opp\textquotesingle{}s belief about primate}
\NormalTok{    mu\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{; Sig\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1}     \CommentTok{\# 0{-}ToM opp\textquotesingle{}s belief about primate}
\NormalTok{    choice }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(N); op\_choice }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(N)}
    \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (t }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(N)) \{}
\NormalTok{      p\_self\_from\_op  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_s }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.36} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Sig\_s))}
\NormalTok{      p\_op\_pred\_1tom  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{((}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_self\_from\_op) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      p\_self\_t        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{((}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_op\_pred\_1tom }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      p\_self\_opp\_view }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_o }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.36} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Sig\_o))}
\NormalTok{      p\_op\_t          }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{((}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_self\_opp\_view) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      choice[t]    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_self\_t)}
\NormalTok{      op\_choice[t] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_op\_t)}
\NormalTok{      s\_ps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_self\_from\_op}
\NormalTok{      Sig\_s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (Sig\_s }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ s\_ps }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_ps))}
\NormalTok{      mu\_s  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mu\_s }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Sig\_s }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (choice[t] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_ps)}
\NormalTok{      s\_o  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_o)}
\NormalTok{      Sig\_o }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (Sig\_o }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ s\_o }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_o))}
\NormalTok{      mu\_o  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mu\_o }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Sig\_o }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (choice[t] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ s\_o)}
\NormalTok{    \}}
    \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ choice, }\AttributeTok{op\_choice =}\NormalTok{ op\_choice))}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{fit\_and\_loo }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(dd, mod) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{N =} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(dd}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice), }\AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ dd}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice,}
                \AttributeTok{op\_choice =}\NormalTok{ dd}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{op\_choice),}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(occam\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  scenarios }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"RS\_0tom"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"0tom\_0tom"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"0tom\_1tom"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  n\_rep }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{6}
\NormalTok{  occam\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scenarios, }\AttributeTok{rep =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_rep)}
\NormalTok{  occam\_res }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ occam\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{rowwise}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{dat   =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{simulate\_scenario}\NormalTok{(scenario,}
                                     \AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{100} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{match}\NormalTok{(scenario, scenarios) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ rep)),}
      \AttributeTok{loo0  =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{fit\_and\_loo}\NormalTok{(dat, mod\_tom0)),}
      \AttributeTok{loo1  =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{fit\_and\_loo}\NormalTok{(dat, mod\_tom1)),}
      \AttributeTok{elpd0 =}\NormalTok{ loo0}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{estimates[}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"Estimate"}\NormalTok{],}
      \AttributeTok{elpd1 =}\NormalTok{ loo1}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{estimates[}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"Estimate"}\NormalTok{],}
      \AttributeTok{delta =}\NormalTok{ elpd1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ elpd0}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(scenario, rep, elpd0, elpd1, delta)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(occam\_res, occam\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  occam\_res }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(occam\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(occam\_res, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ scenario, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ delta)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_boxplot}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{outlier.shape =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray90"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_jitter}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.12}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_discrete}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"RS\_0tom"}   \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"RS + 0{-}ToM primate"}\NormalTok{,}
                              \StringTok{"0tom\_0tom"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"0{-}ToM opp + 0{-}ToM primate"}\NormalTok{,}
                              \StringTok{"0tom\_1tom"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"0{-}ToM opp + 1{-}ToM primate"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Data{-}generating scenario"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(Delta }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" ELPD (1{-}ToM − 0{-}ToM)"}\NormalTok{),}
       \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Does PSIS{-}LOO correctly penalize 1{-}ToM on simpler data?"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.85\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{09-TheoryOfMind_files/figure-pdf/ch09_occam_plot-1.pdf}

}

\caption{Occam's razor test. Each point is one simulated dataset; y-axis
= ELPD(1-ToM) - ELPD(0-ToM). Positive favors 1-ToM, negative favors
0-ToM. On RS and 0-ToM-opponent data (simpler generating processes) the
difference clusters near or below zero --- PSIS-LOO correctly refuses to
pay the complexity cost of 1-ToM. On 1-ToM-opponent data the difference
shifts positive --- the extra structure earns its keep.}

\end{figure}%

The answer is yes: 1-ToM is not a free upgrade. On data where the
primate does not actually mentalize, the extra two parameters of 1-ToM
leave the ELPD difference at or below zero. On data where the primate
truly is a 1-ToM, the difference swings positive. This is the
Occam's-razor behavior Ch. 7 promised.

\section{Fitting 0-ToM hierarchically to the primate
data}\label{fitting-0-tom-hierarchically-to-the-primate-data}

Each primate plays all three opponent conditions, and we have 39
primates across 7 species. For the scientific estimand we focus on the
interactive \textbf{0-ToM-opponent condition}, where 0-ToM and 1-ToM
make the most differentiated predictions. One 0-ToM fit per individual,
with all three parameters varying by individual around a species-level
mean, non-centered throughout.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{stan\_tom0\_hier }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} J;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} S;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=S\textgreater{} species\_of;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=J\textgreater{} agent;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} choice;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} op\_choice;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} start;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} stop;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_bias;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_bias;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_bias;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_sigma = mu\_log\_sigma[species\_of] + tau\_log\_sigma * z\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_beta  = mu\_log\_beta[species\_of]  + tau\_log\_beta  * z\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] bias      = mu\_bias[species\_of]      + tau\_bias      * z\_bias;}
\StringTok{  vector[N] dV;}
\StringTok{  for (j in 1:J) \{}
\StringTok{    real sigma\_j = exp(log\_sigma[j]);}
\StringTok{    real mu\_s    = 0.0;}
\StringTok{    real Sig\_s   = 1.0;}
\StringTok{    for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{      real s\_mu = inv\_logit(mu\_s);}
\StringTok{      real p\_op = inv\_logit(mu\_s / sqrt(1 + 0.36 * Sig\_s));}
\StringTok{      dV[k] = 2 * p\_op {-} 1;}
\StringTok{      Sig\_s = 1.0 / (1.0 / (Sig\_s + sigma\_j) + s\_mu * (1 {-} s\_mu));}
\StringTok{      mu\_s  = mu\_s + Sig\_s * (op\_choice[k] {-} s\_mu);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_sigma \textasciitilde{} normal({-}2, 1);}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_beta  \textasciitilde{} normal({-}1, 1);}
\StringTok{  mu\_bias      \textasciitilde{} normal( 0, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_sigma \textasciitilde{} exponential(2);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_beta  \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_bias      \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_sigma \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_beta  \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  z\_bias      \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  for (n in 1:N)}
\StringTok{    choice[n] \textasciitilde{} bernoulli\_logit(}
\StringTok{      (dV[n] + bias[agent[n]]) / exp(log\_beta[agent[n]])}
\StringTok{    );}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  for (n in 1:N)}
\StringTok{    log\_lik[n] = bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(}
\StringTok{      choice[n] | (dV[n] + bias[agent[n]]) / exp(log\_beta[agent[n]])}
\StringTok{    );}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_tom0\_hier, here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_tom0\_hier.stan"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{mod\_tom0\_hier }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_tom0\_hier.stan"}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{prep\_stan\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(df) \{}
\NormalTok{  df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(id, t) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{agent\_id   =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(id)),}
      \AttributeTok{species\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(species))}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  species\_of }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{distinct}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, species\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{pull}\NormalTok{(species\_id)}
\NormalTok{  boundaries }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{row =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{start =} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(row), }\AttributeTok{stop =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(row), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(agent\_id)}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{N =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(df), }\AttributeTok{J =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id), }\AttributeTok{S =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{species\_id),}
    \AttributeTok{species\_of =}\NormalTok{ species\_of, }\AttributeTok{agent =}\NormalTok{ df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id,}
    \AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice, }\AttributeTok{op\_choice =}\NormalTok{ df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{op\_choice,}
    \AttributeTok{start =}\NormalTok{ boundaries}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{start, }\AttributeTok{stop =}\NormalTok{ boundaries}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stop,}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_lookup =}\NormalTok{ df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{distinct}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, id, species, species\_id),}
    \AttributeTok{species\_lookup =}\NormalTok{ df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{distinct}\NormalTok{(species\_id, species) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(species\_id)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{mp\_int }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(opp\_cond }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{sd\_int }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prep\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(mp\_int)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{hier\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_tom0\_hier\_fit.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_fits }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(hier\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_hier }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_tom0\_hier}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ sd\_int[}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"J"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"S"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"species\_of"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"agent"}\NormalTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"choice"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"op\_choice"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"start"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"stop"}\NormalTok{)],}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{2026}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_hier}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(hier\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_hier }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(hier\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\NormalTok{fit\_hier}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
$num_divergent
[1] 2 2 1 0

$num_max_treedepth
[1] 0 0 0 0

$ebfmi
[1] 0.7725369 0.7377288 0.8043762 0.8359712
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Fitting 1-ToM
hierarchically}\label{fitting-1-tom-hierarchically}

We fit an analogous hierarchical 1-ToM model to the same interactive
condition data. This model shares the same multilevel structure ---
species-level means, non-centered individual offsets --- but uses the
1-ToM recursion: the agent maintains a belief about what the opponent
thinks of \emph{itself}, updated by the agent's own choices.

Four parameters per individual: \texttt{log\_sigma\_op} (opponent's
believed volatility about the agent), \texttt{log\_beta\_op} (opponent's
believed temperature), \texttt{log\_beta} (agent's own temperature), and
\texttt{bias}. As Phase 4b showed, \texttt{log\_sigma\_op} and
\texttt{log\_beta\_op} are weakly identified at the individual level;
the species-level pooling here stabilizes them but does not solve the
design-level issue.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{stan\_tom1\_hier }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} J;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} S;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=S\textgreater{} species\_of;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=J\textgreater{} agent;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} choice;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} op\_choice;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} start;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} stop;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_bias;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_bias;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_bias;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_sigma\_op = mu\_log\_sigma\_op[species\_of] + tau\_log\_sigma\_op * z\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_beta\_op  = mu\_log\_beta\_op[species\_of]  + tau\_log\_beta\_op  * z\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_beta     = mu\_log\_beta[species\_of]     + tau\_log\_beta     * z\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] bias         = mu\_bias[species\_of]         + tau\_bias         * z\_bias;}
\StringTok{  vector[N] dV;}
\StringTok{  for (j in 1:J) \{}
\StringTok{    real sigma\_op\_j = exp(log\_sigma\_op[j]);}
\StringTok{    real beta\_op\_j  = exp(log\_beta\_op[j]);}
\StringTok{    real mu\_s  = 0.0;}
\StringTok{    real Sig\_s = 1.0;}
\StringTok{    for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{      real p\_self\_from\_op = inv\_logit(mu\_s / sqrt(1 + 0.36 * Sig\_s));}
\StringTok{      real p\_op           = inv\_logit((1 {-} 2 * p\_self\_from\_op) / beta\_op\_j);}
\StringTok{      dV[k] = 2 * p\_op {-} 1;}
\StringTok{      real s\_ps = p\_self\_from\_op;}
\StringTok{      Sig\_s = 1.0 / (1.0 / (Sig\_s + sigma\_op\_j) + s\_ps * (1 {-} s\_ps));}
\StringTok{      mu\_s  = mu\_s + Sig\_s * (choice[k] {-} s\_ps);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_sigma\_op  \textasciitilde{} normal({-}2, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_sigma\_op \textasciitilde{} exponential(2);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_sigma\_op   \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_beta\_op   \textasciitilde{} normal({-}1, 0.7);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_beta\_op  \textasciitilde{} exponential(2);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_beta\_op    \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_beta      \textasciitilde{} normal({-}1, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_beta     \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_beta       \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_bias          \textasciitilde{} normal(0, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_bias         \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  z\_bias           \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  for (n in 1:N)}
\StringTok{    choice[n] \textasciitilde{} bernoulli\_logit(}
\StringTok{      (dV[n] + bias[agent[n]]) / exp(log\_beta[agent[n]])}
\StringTok{    );}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  for (n in 1:N)}
\StringTok{    log\_lik[n] = bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(}
\StringTok{      choice[n] | (dV[n] + bias[agent[n]]) / exp(log\_beta[agent[n]])}
\StringTok{    );}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_tom1\_hier, here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_tom1\_hier.stan"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{mod\_tom1\_hier }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_tom1\_hier.stan"}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{hier\_tom1\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_tom1\_hier\_fit.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_fits }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(hier\_tom1\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_hier\_tom1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_tom1\_hier}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ sd\_int[}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"J"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"S"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"species\_of"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"agent"}\NormalTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"choice"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"op\_choice"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"start"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"stop"}\NormalTok{)],}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{max\_treedepth =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{2027}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_hier\_tom1}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(hier\_tom1\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_hier\_tom1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(hier\_tom1\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\NormalTok{fit\_hier\_tom1}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
$num_divergent
[1]  39  24  24 167

$num_max_treedepth
[1] 961   0   0 827

$ebfmi
[1] 0.8382165 0.9810230 0.8367260 2.0332266
\end{verbatim}

\section{The main estimand: a joint mixture regression on
representational
capacity}\label{the-main-estimand-a-joint-mixture-regression-on-representational-capacity}

The regression we actually want is not on \texttt{log\_beta}. The
Machiavellian intelligence hypothesis and the cognitive scaffolding
hypothesis are claims about \textbf{representational capacity} ---
whether an agent mentalizes at all, and at what recursion depth. A
regression of \texttt{log\_beta} on ECV and group size within the 0-ToM
architecture answers a related but different question: given that an
agent is a statistical tracker, does brain size or group size predict
how sharply it executes that strategy? A thoughtful but subsidiary
quantity. We will compute it too, but \emph{after} the main analysis.

The main analysis puts architecture itself on the left-hand side.

\subsection{Model specification}\label{model-specification}

For each individual \(j\) introduce a latent architecture indicator
\(z_j \in \{0, 1\}\): \(z_j = 0\) means agent \(j\)'s choices are
generated by 0-ToM, \(z_j = 1\) means they are generated by 1-ToM.
Per-individual the trial likelihood is marginalized over \(z_j\):

\[
p\!\left(c_{j, 1:T_j} \mid \theta^{0}_j, \theta^{1}_j, \pi_{s(j)}\right)
=
\pi_{s(j)} \cdot \prod_t p_{\text{1-ToM}}\!\left(c_{j,t} \mid \theta^{1}_j\right)
+
(1 - \pi_{s(j)}) \cdot \prod_t p_{\text{0-ToM}}\!\left(c_{j,t} \mid \theta^{0}_j\right).
\]

The mixture weight \(\pi_s \in (0, 1)\) is the \emph{species-level
probability that a member of species \(s\) is a 1-ToM agent}. It is
regressed on standardized log covariates with a species residual:

\[
\text{logit}(\pi_s) \;=\; \alpha + b_{\text{ECV}}\,\tilde{\text{ECV}}_s + b_{\text{group}}\,\widetilde{\text{group}}_s + \tau_{\text{sp}}\,u_s,
\qquad u_s \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1).
\]

The coefficients \(b_{\text{ECV}}\) and \(b_{\text{group}}\) are now on
the \emph{representational-capacity} scale. A negative
\(b_{\text{ECV}}\) would falsify the scaffolding hypothesis on this
sample; a positive, credibly-nonzero \(b_{\text{group}}\) would support
Machiavellian intelligence; similarly positive \(b_{\text{ECV}}\) would
support scaffolding. The mixture structure makes no assumption that all
members of a species share an architecture --- it supplies a prior
probability, and the per-individual data update it to a posterior.

The individual-level parameters are partitioned:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{0-ToM-only}: \(\log\sigma_j\) (own volatility over opponent
  bias).
\item
  \textbf{1-ToM-only}: \(\log\sigma_{\text{op}, j}\),
  \(\log\beta_{\text{op}, j}\) (opponent-model parameters the 1-ToM
  agent holds).
\item
  \textbf{Shared across architectures}: \(\log\beta_j\), \(b_j\). These
  are decision-layer parameters (softmax temperature and handedness)
  that map a \(\Delta V\) to a choice, and the mapping is structurally
  identical under 0-ToM and 1-ToM --- only the recursion that produces
  \(\Delta V\) differs. Sharing them prevents the mixture from
  fragmenting identification of parameters that the data cleanly
  constrain.
\end{itemize}

All parameters are hierarchical with species-level means, non-centered
individual offsets, and half-exponential SD priors, exactly as in the
baseline.

\textbf{A note on identifiability.} The posterior for \(\pi_s\) is only
informed to the extent that per-individual 0-ToM and 1-ToM likelihoods
are distinguishable --- which is exactly the leverage the Occam's-razor
panel measured. Individuals whose choice sequences are predictively
equivalent under the two architectures will have diffuse per-individual
posterior architecture probabilities, and information will flow into
\(\pi_s\) primarily from the \emph{other} individuals in that species
and from the covariate regression. This is the correct Bayesian
behavior: when individual data are uninformative, the mixture returns to
the species prior.

\subsection{Stan implementation}\label{stan-implementation-1}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{stan\_mixture\_reg }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} J;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} S;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=S\textgreater{} species\_of;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=J\textgreater{} agent;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} choice;}
\StringTok{  array[N] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} op\_choice;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} start;}
\StringTok{  array[J] int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} stop;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] ecv\_z;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] group\_z;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  // 0{-}ToM{-}only}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  // 1{-}ToM{-}only}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  // Shared decision layer}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] mu\_bias;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_bias;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] z\_bias;}
\StringTok{  // Mixture regression on architecture probability}
\StringTok{  real alpha\_pi;}
\StringTok{  real b\_ecv;}
\StringTok{  real b\_group;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} tau\_sp\_pi;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] u\_sp;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_sigma    = mu\_log\_sigma[species\_of]    + tau\_log\_sigma    * z\_log\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_sigma\_op = mu\_log\_sigma\_op[species\_of] + tau\_log\_sigma\_op * z\_log\_sigma\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_beta\_op  = mu\_log\_beta\_op[species\_of]  + tau\_log\_beta\_op  * z\_log\_beta\_op;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] log\_beta     = mu\_log\_beta[species\_of]     + tau\_log\_beta     * z\_log\_beta;}
\StringTok{  vector[J] bias\_j       = mu\_bias[species\_of]         + tau\_bias         * z\_bias;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] logit\_pi     = alpha\_pi + b\_ecv * ecv\_z + b\_group * group\_z}
\StringTok{                           + tau\_sp\_pi * u\_sp;}
\StringTok{  vector[S] pi\_s         = inv\_logit(logit\_pi);}
\StringTok{  vector[N] dV0;}
\StringTok{  vector[N] dV1;}
\StringTok{  for (j in 1:J) \{}
\StringTok{    // 0{-}ToM recursion: belief about opponent bias, updated by op\_choice}
\StringTok{    \{}
\StringTok{      real sig0 = exp(log\_sigma[j]);}
\StringTok{      real mu   = 0.0;}
\StringTok{      real Sig  = 1.0;}
\StringTok{      for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{        real s\_mu   = inv\_logit(mu);}
\StringTok{        real p\_op\_0 = inv\_logit(mu / sqrt(1 + 0.36 * Sig));}
\StringTok{        dV0[k] = 2 * p\_op\_0 {-} 1;}
\StringTok{        Sig = 1.0 / (1.0 / (Sig + sig0) + s\_mu * (1 {-} s\_mu));}
\StringTok{        mu  = mu + Sig * (op\_choice[k] {-} s\_mu);}
\StringTok{      \}}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{    // 1{-}ToM recursion: belief about opponent\textquotesingle{}s belief about self,}
\StringTok{    // updated by own choice}
\StringTok{    \{}
\StringTok{      real sig\_op = exp(log\_sigma\_op[j]);}
\StringTok{      real b\_op   = exp(log\_beta\_op[j]);}
\StringTok{      real mu\_s   = 0.0;}
\StringTok{      real Sig\_s  = 1.0;}
\StringTok{      for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{        real p\_self\_from\_op = inv\_logit(mu\_s / sqrt(1 + 0.36 * Sig\_s));}
\StringTok{        real p\_op\_1         = inv\_logit((1 {-} 2 * p\_self\_from\_op) / b\_op);}
\StringTok{        dV1[k] = 2 * p\_op\_1 {-} 1;}
\StringTok{        real s\_ps = p\_self\_from\_op;}
\StringTok{        Sig\_s = 1.0 / (1.0 / (Sig\_s + sig\_op) + s\_ps * (1 {-} s\_ps));}
\StringTok{        mu\_s  = mu\_s + Sig\_s * (choice[k] {-} s\_ps);}
\StringTok{      \}}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Hierarchical priors}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_sigma    \textasciitilde{} normal({-}2, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_sigma   \textasciitilde{} exponential(2);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_sigma     \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_sigma\_op \textasciitilde{} normal({-}2, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_sigma\_op \textasciitilde{} exponential(2);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_sigma\_op  \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_beta\_op  \textasciitilde{} normal({-}1, 0.7);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_beta\_op \textasciitilde{} exponential(2);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_beta\_op   \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_log\_beta     \textasciitilde{} normal({-}1, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_log\_beta    \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_beta      \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  mu\_bias         \textasciitilde{} normal(0, 1);}
\StringTok{  tau\_bias        \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  z\_bias          \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  // Mixture regression}
\StringTok{  alpha\_pi  \textasciitilde{} normal(0, 1);}
\StringTok{  b\_ecv     \textasciitilde{} normal(0, 0.7);}
\StringTok{  b\_group   \textasciitilde{} normal(0, 0.7);}
\StringTok{  tau\_sp\_pi \textasciitilde{} exponential(1);}
\StringTok{  u\_sp      \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  // Marginalized likelihood: one log\_mix per individual}
\StringTok{  for (j in 1:J) \{}
\StringTok{    real ll0 = 0;}
\StringTok{    real ll1 = 0;}
\StringTok{    real inv\_b = 1.0 / exp(log\_beta[j]);}
\StringTok{    for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{      ll0 += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(choice[k] |}
\StringTok{                (dV0[k] + bias\_j[j]) * inv\_b);}
\StringTok{      ll1 += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(choice[k] |}
\StringTok{                (dV1[k] + bias\_j[j]) * inv\_b);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{    target += log\_mix(pi\_s[species\_of[j]], ll1, ll0);}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  // Per{-}individual posterior probability z\_j = 1 (= 1{-}ToM)}
\StringTok{  vector[J] post\_pi\_j;}
\StringTok{  // Per{-}individual total log{-}likelihood difference (1{-}ToM {-} 0{-}ToM).}
\StringTok{  vector[J] ll\_diff\_j;}
\StringTok{  // Per{-}trial log\_lik under the marginal mixture for PSIS{-}LOO}
\StringTok{  vector[N] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  for (j in 1:J) \{}
\StringTok{    real ll0 = 0;}
\StringTok{    real ll1 = 0;}
\StringTok{    real inv\_b = 1.0 / exp(log\_beta[j]);}
\StringTok{    for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{      ll0 += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(choice[k] |}
\StringTok{                (dV0[k] + bias\_j[j]) * inv\_b);}
\StringTok{      ll1 += bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(choice[k] |}
\StringTok{                (dV1[k] + bias\_j[j]) * inv\_b);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{    ll\_diff\_j[j] = ll1 {-} ll0;}
\StringTok{    real lp0 = log1m(pi\_s[species\_of[j]]) + ll0;}
\StringTok{    real lp1 = log(pi\_s[species\_of[j]])   + ll1;}
\StringTok{    post\_pi\_j[j] = exp(lp1 {-} log\_sum\_exp(lp0, lp1));}
\StringTok{    for (k in start[j]:stop[j]) \{}
\StringTok{      real l0 = bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(choice[k] |}
\StringTok{                  (dV0[k] + bias\_j[j]) * inv\_b);}
\StringTok{      real l1 = bernoulli\_logit\_lpmf(choice[k] |}
\StringTok{                  (dV1[k] + bias\_j[j]) * inv\_b);}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[k] = log\_mix(pi\_s[species\_of[j]], l1, l0);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}
\FunctionTok{writeLines}\NormalTok{(stan\_mixture\_reg, here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_mixture\_reg.stan"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{mod\_mix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_mixture\_reg.stan"}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Standardize log covariates at the species level}
\NormalTok{species\_cov }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sd\_int}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{species\_lookup }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(covariates, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"species"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(species\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{log\_ECV =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(ECV),}
    \AttributeTok{log\_grp =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(groupsize),}
    \AttributeTok{ecv\_z   =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{scale}\NormalTok{(log\_ECV)),}
    \AttributeTok{group\_z =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{scale}\NormalTok{(log\_grp))}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{sd\_mix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  sd\_int[}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"J"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"S"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"species\_of"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"agent"}\NormalTok{,}
           \StringTok{"choice"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"op\_choice"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"start"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"stop"}\NormalTok{)],}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ecv\_z   =}\NormalTok{ species\_cov}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ecv\_z,}
       \AttributeTok{group\_z =}\NormalTok{ species\_cov}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{group\_z)}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mix\_path }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch09\_mixture\_reg\_fit.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_fits }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(mix\_path)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ sd\_mix,}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1200}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1200}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.97}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{max\_treedepth =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{2029}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(mix\_path)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(mix\_path)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\NormalTok{fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{()}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
$num_divergent
[1] 2 1 2 0

$num_max_treedepth
[1] 0 0 0 0

$ebfmi
[1] 0.8750412 0.8685706 0.8793667 0.8039244
\end{verbatim}

Expect this fit to take roughly 2× the baseline hierarchical 0-ToM
runtime --- both recursions run every leapfrog step.
\texttt{adapt\_delta\ =\ 0.97} and a deeper \texttt{max\_treedepth} help
with the mild funnel geometry the mixture introduces around
\(\tau_{\text{sp}}\) when species have few individuals.

\subsection{The answer}\label{the-answer}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mix\_coef }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"alpha\_pi"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"b\_ecv"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"b\_group"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"tau\_sp\_pi"}\NormalTok{),}
                          \AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{()}

\NormalTok{p\_mix\_coef }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mix\_coef }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(b\_ecv, b\_group) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"coef"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ coef)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray30"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"b\_ecv"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{,}
                               \StringTok{"b\_group"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"firebrick3"}\NormalTok{),}
                    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"b\_ecv"}   \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"ECV (scaffolding)"}\NormalTok{,}
                               \StringTok{"b\_group"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Group size (Machiavellian)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(beta[cov]}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{"(logit "}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{pi[s]}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{"per SD of log covariate)"}\NormalTok{),}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior density"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"Hypothesis"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Which covariate drives representational capacity?"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Positive = larger covariate associated with more 1{-}ToM{-}type individuals"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{pi\_s\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"pi\_s"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"pi\_s"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{species\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(species\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{med =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(value),}
            \AttributeTok{lo  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{hi  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(species\_cov }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(species\_id, species),}
            \AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"species\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(med) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{species =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(species, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ species))}

\NormalTok{p\_pi\_s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(pi\_s\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ med, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ species)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbarh}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xmin =}\NormalTok{ lo, }\AttributeTok{xmax =}\NormalTok{ hi), }\AttributeTok{height =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(pi[s]}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{"(species{-}level P(1{-}ToM))"}\NormalTok{),}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Per{-}species architecture posterior"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Dashed = indifference; right of line = species leans 1{-}ToM"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_mix\_coef }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_pi\_s }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{plot\_layout}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{widths =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.85\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{09-TheoryOfMind_files/figure-pdf/ch09_mixture_coef_plot-1.pdf}

}

\caption{Left: posterior distributions of the two covariate effects on
logit(π\_s), the species-level probability of being a 1-ToM agent.
Positive = more 1-ToM-compatible as the covariate increases. Right:
species-level posterior π\_s (dot = median, bar = 90\% CI).}

\end{figure}%

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mix\_coef\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mix\_coef }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(b\_ecv, b\_group) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"coef"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(coef) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{mean   =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(value),}
    \AttributeTok{median =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(value),}
    \AttributeTok{q05    =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{q95    =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{p\_neg  =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(value }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{p\_pos  =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(value }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(mix\_coef\_summary, }\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{caption =} \StringTok{"Coefficients on logit(π\_s). p\_pos = posterior probability that the effect is positive."}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}lrrrrrr@{}}
\caption{Coefficients on logit(π\_s). p\_pos = posterior probability
that the effect is positive.}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
coef & mean & median & q05 & q95 & p\_neg & p\_pos \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
coef & mean & median & q05 & q95 & p\_neg & p\_pos \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
b\_ecv & -0.254 & -0.250 & -1.161 & 0.677 & 0.679 & 0.321 \\
b\_group & 0.193 & 0.195 & -0.722 & 1.115 & 0.362 & 0.638 \\
\end{longtable}

Reading the coefficients on the representational-capacity scale:
\(b_{\text{ECV}}\) is the change in \(\text{logit}(\pi_s)\) per standard
deviation of log endocranial volume. A value of \(+1\) corresponds to
roughly a factor-of-2.7 increase in the odds of an individual being a
1-ToM type, per SD increase in log ECV. The practical interpretation
depends on where the posterior mass sits relative to zero --- this is
reported per-fit by \texttt{p\_pos} in the table.

\subsection{Per-individual architecture
posteriors}\label{per-individual-architecture-posteriors}

The mixture also delivers per-individual posterior probabilities
\(\Pr(z_j = 1 \mid \text{data})\). Individuals whose data pull their
probability away from the species prior are those whose choice sequences
decisively fit one architecture over the other; those whose posteriors
return to the species prior \(\pi_{s(j)}\) are predictively
indistinguishable under the two models, consistent with the
Occam's-razor evidence that matching pennies has limited leverage at the
single-individual scale.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{post\_pi\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"post\_pi\_j"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"post\_pi\_j"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+"}\NormalTok{)))}

\NormalTok{post\_pi\_ind }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ post\_pi\_draws }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{med =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(value),}
            \AttributeTok{lo  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{hi  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(sd\_int}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_lookup, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"agent\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(species, med) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id\_ord =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{())}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(post\_pi\_ind, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id\_ord, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ med, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ species)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lo, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ hi), }\AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Individual (ordered by species, then posterior)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{Pr}\NormalTok{(z[j]}\SpecialCharTok{==}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{"|"}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{data)),}
       \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Species"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Per{-}individual posterior architecture probability"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Dashed = indifference; wide bars indicate low per{-}individual leverage"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.85\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{09-TheoryOfMind_files/figure-pdf/ch09_post_pi_ind-1.pdf}

}

\caption{Per-individual posterior probability of being a 1-ToM agent,
colored by species. Individuals close to 0 or 1 are cleanly classified
by their data; those clustered near their species π\_s reflect the
limited per-individual leverage of matching pennies.}

\end{figure}%

\subsection{Sanity check against Occam's
razor}\label{sanity-check-against-occams-razor}

Our Stan \texttt{generated\ quantities} block exposes two tightly-linked
per-individual quantities: \texttt{ll\_diff\_j} (the total
log-likelihood difference between the 1-ToM and 0-ToM arms at each
posterior draw's parameters) and \texttt{post\_pi\_j} (the
Rao-Blackwellized posterior probability that individual \(j\) is a 1-ToM
agent). If the mixture is doing what it claims, individuals with larger
\texttt{ll\_diff\_j} should have higher \texttt{post\_pi\_j}, and the
relationship should be a smooth logistic curve rising from the species
prior \(\pi_{s(j)}\) at zero evidence toward 1 at strong positive
evidence. That curve is the empirical operating characteristic of the
mixture, and it is the correct check to run --- it confirms the mixture
responds to individual-level data, not only to the species-level
covariates.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ll\_diff\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ll\_diff\_j"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ll\_diff\_j"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarize}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ll\_diff\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(value),}
            \AttributeTok{ll\_diff\_lo   =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{ll\_diff\_hi   =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(value, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{mix\_check }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ post\_pi\_ind }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(ll\_diff\_summary, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"agent\_id"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(mix\_check, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ ll\_diff\_mean, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ med, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ species)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,   }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lo, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ hi), }\AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbarh}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xmin =}\NormalTok{ ll\_diff\_lo, }\AttributeTok{xmax =}\NormalTok{ ll\_diff\_hi),}
                 \AttributeTok{height =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Per{-}individual log{-}likelihood difference "}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{(ell[}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ell[}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{])),}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{Pr}\NormalTok{(z[j]}\SpecialCharTok{==}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\StringTok{"|"}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{data)),}
       \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Species"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Mixture posterior responds to individual{-}level evidence"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Rightward = data prefer 1{-}ToM; upward = posterior classifies as 1{-}ToM"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.85\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{09-TheoryOfMind_files/figure-pdf/ch09_mix_occam_check-1.pdf}

}

\caption{Per-individual posterior architecture probability (y)
vs.~per-individual 1-ToM minus 0-ToM log-likelihood difference (x), both
extracted from the mixture fit. Posterior-mean points, colored by
species. A monotone rising curve confirms the mixture is actually
responsive to the structural evidence in each individual's choices; flat
clouds would indicate the posterior is driven entirely by the
species-level prior.}

\end{figure}%

\subsection{How well does the mixture predict
overall?}\label{how-well-does-the-mixture-predict-overall}

Mixture coefficients are only trustworthy to the extent that the mixture
model itself is predictively competitive. We compute PSIS-LOO for the
mixture and compare against the baseline hierarchical 0-ToM (the
non-mixture model fit earlier).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{loo\_mix   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mix}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{loo\_base  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_hier}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{loo\_cmp   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_compare}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mixture =}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix, }\AttributeTok{baseline\_0tom =}\NormalTok{ loo\_base))}
\NormalTok{knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(loo\_cmp), }\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{caption =} \StringTok{"PSIS{-}LOO comparison: mixture of architectures vs hierarchical 0{-}ToM only."}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

baseline\_0tom & 0.00 & 0.00 & -5035.13 & 15.13 & 49.90 & 0.71 &
