  \FunctionTok{geom\_bar}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{position =} \StringTok{"identity"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(prop))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Standard Binomial (Fixed θ)"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{, }
                               \StringTok{"Beta{-}Binomial (Uncertain θ)"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4575b4"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{breaks =} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, n\_choices, }\AttributeTok{by =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 2: Choices out of \%d Trials"}\NormalTok{, n\_choices),}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Notice the wider spread (fatter tails) of the Beta{-}Binomial"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Number of \textquotesingle{}Blue\textquotesingle{} choices"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Probability"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fill =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"top"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Combine plots}
\NormalTok{p\_theta }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_spread }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{plot\_annotation}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"How Uncertainty Propagates: Beta{-}Binomial vs Binomial"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"When an agent makes multiple choices, uncertainty about the true rate creates a wider distribution of outcomes."}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_betabinomial_two_step_demo-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The left panel shows that even in a relatively informative scenario (11
total observations), there is genuine uncertainty about \(\theta\). The
right panel shows that this uncertainty propagates into choices: the
agent does not produce a deterministic .54 blue rate but a
\emph{distribution} of outcomes whose spread exceeds what a Binomial
with \(p = .54\) would produce.

\subsection{Predictions Under Equal
Weights}\label{predictions-under-equal-weights}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Calculate SBA predictions for all evidence combinations}
\NormalTok{sim\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ evidence\_combinations }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2, total1, total2) \{}
\NormalTok{    result }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{betaBinomialModel}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, blue1, total1, blue2, total2)}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{blue1         =}\NormalTok{ blue1,}
      \AttributeTok{blue2         =}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
      \AttributeTok{expected\_rate =}\NormalTok{ result}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{expected\_rate,}
      \AttributeTok{ci\_lower      =}\NormalTok{ result}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ci\_lower,}
      \AttributeTok{ci\_upper      =}\NormalTok{ result}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ci\_upper}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{semantic\_colors }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"Clear Red"}  \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"Maybe Red"}  \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#fdae61"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"Maybe Blue"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#abd9e9"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"Clear Blue"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4575b4"}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(sim\_data,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ blue1, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ expected\_rate,}
           \AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence, }\AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ ci\_lower, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ ci\_upper,}
                  \AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{breaks =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =}\NormalTok{ semantic\_colors) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =}\NormalTok{ semantic\_colors) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Simple Bayesian Agent: Evidence Integration"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Expected proportion with 95\% credible intervals | Weights fixed at (1, 1)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x        =} \StringTok{"Blue marbles in direct sample (out of 8)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y        =} \StringTok{"P(next marble = blue)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{colour   =} \StringTok{"Social evidence"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fill     =} \StringTok{"Social evidence"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_sba_predictions-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Observe that the lines are strictly parallel. Because the total
pseudo-count denominator is fixed, social evidence exerts a constant,
additive shift on the expected probability, regardless of direct
evidence strength.

\subsection{Beta-Binomial
Overdispersion}\label{beta-binomial-overdispersion}

As we have seen above, the BetaBinomial has greater variance than a
Binomial with the same mean. This excess variance is called
overdispersion, let's visualize it.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# We predict N=10 future draws. This allows the excess variance }
\CommentTok{\# from overdispersion to manifest as positive integers.}
\NormalTok{N\_predict }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{10}

\NormalTok{overdispersion\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{blue1 =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{blue2 =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{alpha\_p   =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
    \AttributeTok{beta\_p    =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{8} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{3} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
    \AttributeTok{theta\_hat =}\NormalTok{ alpha\_p }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (alpha\_p }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ beta\_p),}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Expected Binomial variance for N draws (Baseline)}
    \AttributeTok{var\_binom =}\NormalTok{ N\_predict }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ theta\_hat }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ theta\_hat),}
    
    \CommentTok{\# BetaBinomial variance for N draws (Includes belief uncertainty)}
    \CommentTok{\# The multiplier is [1 + (N {-} 1) / (alpha + beta + 1)]}
    \AttributeTok{var\_bb    =}\NormalTok{ var\_binom }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (N\_predict }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (alpha\_p }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ beta\_p }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
    
    \CommentTok{\# ABSOLUTE EXCESS VARIANCE: }
    \CommentTok{\# Var(BetaBinomial) {-} Var(Binomial)}
    \CommentTok{\# This yields a positive curve that peaks at maximum ambiguity}
    \AttributeTok{excess\_variance =}\NormalTok{ var\_bb }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ var\_binom,}
    
    \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(overdispersion\_data,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ blue1, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ excess\_variance,}
           \AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence, }\AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# A baseline at 0 to ground the visual}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray70"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set1"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{breaks =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Labels updated to reflect the N=10 prediction framing}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Beta{-}Binomial Overdispersion Across Evidence Combinations"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"Excess variance = Var(BetaBinomial) − Var(Binomial) for N=10 predicted draws."}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Highest when evidence is ambiguous (near a 50/50 probability split)."}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{x        =} \StringTok{"Blue marbles in direct sample (out of 8)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y        =} \StringTok{"Excess Variance}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(Driven by belief uncertainty)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{colour   =} \StringTok{"Social evidence"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{plot.title      =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{14}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{axis.title.y    =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{margin =} \FunctionTok{margin}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{r =} \DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{axis.title.x    =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{margin =} \FunctionTok{margin}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{t =} \DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_betabinomial_overdispersion-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Overdispersion is greatest when evidence is ambiguous (near a 50/50
probability split, such as 4 direct blue marbles). At this point, the
Beta posterior is widest and \(\theta\) is least constrained, driving
maximum excess variance in the predicted draws. This has a concrete
implication for model checking: if real participants produce choices
that are \emph{more} variable than a Binomial with \(p = \hat{\theta}\)
would predict, that is not a misspecification --- it is exactly what the
BetaBinomial predicts. If choices are \emph{less} variable (more
deterministic than the model allows), the LOO-PIT will show a
hump-shaped histogram, signalling that a threshold decision rule rather
than probabilistic sampling better describes the data.

\subsection{Comparison with Log-Odds
Integration}\label{comparison-with-log-odds-integration}

An alternative framing is to convert evidence to log-odds rather than
pseudo-counts. Here we compare the two approaches:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Log{-}Odds Integration Function {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{SimpleBayes\_f }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(bias, Source1, Source2) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Using base R equivalents for portability:}
\NormalTok{  logit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(p) }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(p }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p))}
\NormalTok{  inv\_logit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{x))}
  
\NormalTok{  outcome }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{inv\_logit}\NormalTok{(bias }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{logit}\NormalTok{(Source1) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{logit}\NormalTok{(Source2))}
  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(outcome)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Data Preparation {-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# We\textquotesingle{}ll use a bias of 0 (neutral) and convert counts to proportions for the logit model}
\NormalTok{comparison\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{blue1 =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{blue2 =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \CommentTok{\# Beta{-}Binomial Expected Rate (Pseudocounts)}
    \AttributeTok{p\_beta =}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{8} \SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{),}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Log{-}Odds Expected Rate (Using the function)}
    \CommentTok{\# We add a tiny epsilon to avoid logit(0) or logit(1)}
    \AttributeTok{prop1 =}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{prop2 =}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{p\_logit =} \FunctionTok{map2\_dbl}\NormalTok{(prop1, prop2, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\FunctionTok{SimpleBayes\_f}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, .x, .y)),}
    
    \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }
                             \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(p\_beta, p\_logit), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"Model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"p\_blue"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Plotting {-}{-}{-}}
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(comparison\_data, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ blue1, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ p\_blue, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{Model, }\AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{as\_labeller}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"p\_beta"}  \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Beta{-}Binomial (Counts/Accumulation)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"p\_logit"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Log{-}Odds (Linear Summation)"}
\NormalTok{  ))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =}\NormalTok{ semantic\_colors) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{breaks =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Comparison of Evidence Integration Scales"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Log{-}odds models (right) produce steeper \textquotesingle{}S{-}curves\textquotesingle{} than count{-}based models (left)."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Blue marbles in direct sample (out of 8)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"P(choose blue)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Social Evidence"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_classic}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_logodds_comparison-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The key difference: log-odds integration produces steeper ``S-curves''
--- the model is more confident near the extremes. Count-based
(Beta-Binomial) integration is more conservative, maintaining higher
uncertainty even with unbalanced evidence.

\section{Two Generative Models for Evidence
Integration}\label{two-generative-models-for-evidence-integration}

Now that we understand the Beta-Binomial better, we can use it to build
two different agents integrating direct and social evidence. The agents
differ in exactly one respect: whether the source weights are fixed at
the ``rational'' levels (taking the sources at face value) or they can
vary (e.g.~weighing direct experience more than social experience).

\subsection{The Simple Bayesian Agent
(SBA)}\label{the-simple-bayesian-agent-sba}

The SBA fixes the weights at \(w_d = w_s = 1\) --- every observation
counts at face value, regardless of its source (but remember that social
evidence in our design counts for less as it can have at most 3 balls of
a given color). With Jeffreys prior pseudo-counts:

\[\alpha_{\text{post}} = 0.5 + k_1 + k_2\]
\[\beta_{\text{post}}  = 0.5 + (n_1 - k_1) + (n_2 - k_2)\]

The SBA has \textbf{zero free parameters}. It is a fully deterministic
belief-update rule --- there is nothing to estimate from the data. We
can still fit it (in the sense of running it through Stan to get
posterior predictives and log-likelihoods) and check whether it predicts
behaviour.

\subsection{The Weighted Bayesian Agent
(WBA)}\label{the-weighted-bayesian-agent-wba}

But what if people don't weight both sources equally? Some individuals
might trust their direct experience more; others might be more socially
influenced. The WBA promotes the weights to free parameters:

\[\alpha_{\text{post}} = 0.5 + w_d \cdot k_1 + w_s \cdot k_2\]
\[\beta_{\text{post}}  = 0.5 + w_d \cdot (n_1 - k_1) + w_s \cdot (n_2 - k_2)\]

The WBA has \textbf{two free parameters}: \(w_d\) and \(w_s\). Note that
the SBA is nested inside the WBA at exactly \(w_d = w_s = 1\) ---
anything the SBA can predict, the WBA can predict too. The empirical
question is whether the extra flexibility is needed \emph{and} whether
the data can pin down where in \((w_d, w_s)\) space each participant
lives.

\subsection{Three Scenarios that Distinguish the
Models}\label{three-scenarios-that-distinguish-the-models}

Let's design three datasets --- each generated from a different agent
--- that we'll use throughout the next few sections. We will fit
\emph{both} the SBA and the WBA to all three datasets and see which
model captures which:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Self-Focused:} \(w_d = 1.5\), \(w_s = 0.5\) (trusts own
  observations more)
\item
  \textbf{Balanced:} \(w_d = 1.0\), \(w_s = 1.0\) (the SBA's generating
  process)
\item
  \textbf{Socially-Influenced:} \(w_d = 0.5\), \(w_s = 2.0\) (strongly
  socially influenced)
\end{enumerate}

In the Balanced dataset SBA and WBA coincide. Or in other words, only
the Balanced dataset is generable by SBA, while WBA can generate all
three scenarios. We expect the SBA to fit the Balanced scenario and
\emph{misfit} the other two systematically --- a basic model fitting
test. The WBA, in principle, can fit all three.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{agents }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{agent\_type    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Self{-}Focused"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially{-}Influenced"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{weight\_direct =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{weight\_social =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate simulated data for all three agent types}
\NormalTok{simulation\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ agents }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_type, weight\_direct, weight\_social) \{}
    \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(weight\_direct, weight\_social,}
\NormalTok{                             evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_type =}\NormalTok{ agent\_type)}
\NormalTok{  \}) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_type =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      agent\_type,}
      \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Self{-}Focused"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially{-}Influenced"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Summarize for plotting}
\NormalTok{plot\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ simulation\_results }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_type, blue1, social\_evidence) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{theoretical\_p =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(expected\_rate),}
    \AttributeTok{empirical\_p   =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(choice),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Visualize}
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(plot\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ blue1, }\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence, }\AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ theoretical\_p), }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ empirical\_p), }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ empirical\_p), }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey30"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ agent\_type) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =}\NormalTok{ semantic\_colors) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{breaks =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Weighted Bayesian Agents: Three Scenarios"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Solid faded = Internal Belief | Dashed = Observed Choice Proportions (N=5 per condition)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Blue marbles in direct sample (out of 8)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"P(choose blue)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"Social evidence"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
        \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_three_scenarios-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Notice how the scenarios differ:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \textbf{Self-Focused:} Lines are spread apart by direct evidence;
  social evidence has less effect
\item
  \textbf{Balanced:} Equal spacing from both sources
\item
  \textbf{Socially-Influenced:} Lines are bunched together; social
  evidence dominates.
\end{itemize}

The conceptual setup is now in place: we have two nested models and
three datasets, only one of which the SBA can possibly capture. Round 1
puts both models in Stan and finds out what happens.

\section{Round 1: Fitting SBA and
WBA}\label{round-1-fitting-sba-and-wba}

\subsection{Stan code: SBA}\label{stan-code-sba}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}usetikzlibrary}\NormalTok{\{bayesnet\}}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}begin}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
  \CommentTok{\% Nodes}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const] (alpha0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const, right=1cm of alpha0] (beta0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below left=1.5cm and 0.5cm of alpha0] (blue1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.5cm of blue1] (total1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below right=1.5cm and 0.5cm of beta0] (blue2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.5cm of blue2] (total2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=3.5cm of alpha0] (alphapost) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=3.5cm of beta0] (betapost) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1.5cm of alphapost, xshift=0.75cm] (choice) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$y\_i$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Edges}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alpha0\} \{alphapost\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{beta0\} \{betapost\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{blue1, total1, blue2, total2\} \{alphapost, betapost\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alphapost, betapost\} \{choice\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Plate}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{p1\} \{(blue1)(total1)(blue2)(total2)(alphapost)(betapost)(choice)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$N$}\NormalTok{\};}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}end}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.6\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/sba-dag-1.pdf}

}

\caption{DAG for the Simple Bayesian Agent}

\end{figure}%

The generative relationships for the SBA are: \[
\begin{aligned}
\alpha_{post,i} &= \alpha_0 + k_{1,i} + k_{2,i} \\
\beta_{post,i}  &= \beta_0 + (n_{1,i} - k_{1,i}) + (n_{2,i} - k_{2,i}) \\
y_i &\sim \text{Beta-Binomial}(1, \alpha_{post,i}, \beta_{post,i})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{SimpleAgent\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Simple Bayesian Agent (SBA).}
\StringTok{// No free parameters — evidence is counted at face value.}
\StringTok{// Jeffreys prior pseudo{-}counts: alpha0 = beta0 = 0.5.}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total2;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Vectorized likelihood with fixed weights = 1}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] alpha\_post = 0.5 + to\_vector(blue1) + to\_vector(blue2);}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] beta\_post  = 0.5 + (to\_vector(total1) {-} to\_vector(blue1))}
\StringTok{                                   + (to\_vector(total2) {-} to\_vector(blue2));}
\StringTok{                             }
\StringTok{  target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      real alpha\_post = 0.5 + blue1[i] + blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real beta\_post  = 0.5 + (total1[i] {-} blue1[i]) + (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}

\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(SimpleAgent\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir      =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_simple\_agent.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_simple_agent.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Stan code: WBA (raw
parameterisation)}\label{stan-code-wba-raw-parameterisation}

We start with the intuitive parameterisation using \(w_d\) and \(w_s\)
directly:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}usetikzlibrary}\NormalTok{\{bayesnet\}}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}begin}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
  \CommentTok{\% Fixed Prior}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const] (alpha0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const, right=1.5cm of alpha0] (beta0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Latent Parameters (Direct Weights)}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, below=1.2cm of alpha0] (wd) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$w\_d$}\NormalTok{\}; }
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, below=1.2cm of beta0] (ws) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$w\_s$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Inputs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=1cm of wd] (k1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=0.3cm of k1] (n1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=1cm of ws] (k2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.3cm of k2] (n2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Beliefs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2.5cm of wd] (ap) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2.5cm of ws] (bp) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Action}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1.2cm of ap, xshift=1.25cm] (y) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$y\_i$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Edges}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alpha0, beta0\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{wd, k1, n1, ws, k2, n2\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{ap, bp\} \{y\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{p1\} \{(k1)(n1)(k2)(n2)(ap)(bp)(y)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$N$}\NormalTok{\};}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}end}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.6\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/wba0-dag-1.pdf}

}

\caption{DAG for the unreparameterized Weighted Bayesian Agent}

\end{figure}%

The generative relationships for the unreparameterized WBA are: \[
\begin{aligned}
w_d &\sim \text{Lognormal}(0, 0.5) \\
w_s &\sim \text{Lognormal}(0, 0.5) \\
\alpha_{post,i} &= \alpha_0 + w_d k_{1,i} + w_s k_{2,i} \\
\beta_{post,i}  &= \beta_0 + w_d(n_{1,i} - k_{1,i}) + w_s(n_{2,i} - k_{2,i}) \\
y_i &\sim \text{Beta-Binomial}(1, \alpha_{post,i}, \beta_{post,i})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{WeightedAgentRaw\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Weighted Bayesian Agent (raw parameterisation).}
\StringTok{// weight\_direct, weight\_social \textgreater{} 0: independent weights on direct and social evidence.}
\StringTok{// Jeffreys prior pseudo{-}counts (0.5) consistent with SBA.}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total2;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  real prior\_wd\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_wd\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_ws\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_ws\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} weight\_direct;  // w\_d}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} weight\_social;  // w\_s}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Priors: lognormal centered on 1 (evidence taken at face value)}
\StringTok{  target += lognormal\_lpdf(weight\_direct | prior\_wd\_mu, prior\_wd\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += lognormal\_lpdf(weight\_social | prior\_ws\_mu, prior\_ws\_sigma);}

\StringTok{  // Vectorized likelihood}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] alpha\_post = 0.5 + weight\_direct * to\_vector(blue1)}
\StringTok{                                   + weight\_social * to\_vector(blue2);}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] beta\_post  = 0.5 + weight\_direct * (to\_vector(total1) {-} to\_vector(blue1))}
\StringTok{                                   + weight\_social * (to\_vector(total2) {-} to\_vector(blue2));}
\StringTok{                             }
\StringTok{  target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      real alpha\_post = 0.5 + weight\_direct * blue1[i] + weight\_social * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real beta\_post  = 0.5 + weight\_direct * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i]) }
\StringTok{                           + weight\_social * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}

\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(WeightedAgentRaw\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir      =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_weighted\_agent\_raw.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_weighted_agent_raw.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Fitting the three
scenarios}\label{fitting-the-three-scenarios}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_simple       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_simple\_agent.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{mod\_weighted\_raw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_weighted\_agent\_raw.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{sampler\_args }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsubsection{Preparing data}\label{preparing-data}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# True values for the three WBA scenarios}
\NormalTok{true\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Self\_Focused        =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{Balanced            =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{Socially\_Influenced =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate simulated data for each scenario}
\NormalTok{stan\_data\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(true\_wba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(params) \{}
\NormalTok{  sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    params}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{wd, params}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ws,}
\NormalTok{    evidence\_combinations,}
    \AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{total1 =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{L, }\AttributeTok{total2 =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{L)}

\NormalTok{  d }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(sim)}
  \CommentTok{\# Add priors for the weighted model}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_wd\_mu    }\OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_wd\_sigma }\OtherTok{=} \FloatTok{0.5}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_ws\_mu    }\OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_ws\_sigma }\OtherTok{=} \FloatTok{0.5}
\NormalTok{  d}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Fitting the Simple Model}\label{fitting-the-simple-model}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{fit\_sba\_paths }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{,}
  \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch10\_fit\_sba\_"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba), }\StringTok{".rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{set\_names}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba))}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_sba\_paths))) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(data, scenario) \{}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_simple}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data          =}\NormalTok{ data,}
      \AttributeTok{chains        =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup   =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{fixed\_param   =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh       =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_sba\_paths[[scenario]])}
\NormalTok{    fit}
\NormalTok{  \})}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(fit\_sba\_paths, readRDS)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Fitting the Weighted
Model}\label{fitting-the-weighted-model}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{fit\_wba\_raw\_paths }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ here}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{,}
  \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch10\_fit\_wba\_raw\_"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba), }\StringTok{".rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{set\_names}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba))}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba\_raw\_paths))) \{}
\NormalTok{  seeds }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{set\_names}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{101}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{103}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba))}

\NormalTok{  fit\_wba\_raw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(data, scenario) \{}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      mod\_weighted\_raw}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sample,}
      \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ data, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ seeds[[scenario]]), sampler\_args)}
\NormalTok{    )}
    \CommentTok{\# $save\_object() internalises the CSV draws so the file is}
    \CommentTok{\# self{-}contained. Plain saveRDS() would store dangling pointers}
    \CommentTok{\# to temporary CSVs that no longer exist in a new session.}
\NormalTok{    fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba\_raw\_paths[[scenario]])}
\NormalTok{    fit}
\NormalTok{  \})}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_wba\_raw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba\_raw\_paths, readRDS)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\CommentTok{\# Check diagnostics}
\NormalTok{check\_diagnostics }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, label) \{}
\NormalTok{  diag\_smry }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{  min\_ebfmi }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(diag\_smry}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ebfmi)}
  
\NormalTok{  smry }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(rhat))}
\NormalTok{  n\_fail }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ smry }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(rhat }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.01} \SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ ess\_bulk }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \DecValTok{400} \SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ ess\_tail }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \DecValTok{400}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{()}
  
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%{-}25s | Divs: \%3d | Min E{-}BFMI: \%.2f | Failures: \%d}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, }
\NormalTok{              label, }\FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(diag\_smry}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{num\_divergent), min\_ebfmi, n\_fail))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{=== Weighted Bayesian Agent (Raw Parameterisation) ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

=== Weighted Bayesian Agent (Raw Parameterisation) ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{iwalk}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba\_raw, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{check\_diagnostics}\NormalTok{(.x, }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"WBA Raw"}\NormalTok{, .y)))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
WBA Raw Self_Focused      | Divs:   0 | Min E-BFMI: 1.02 | Failures: 0
WBA Raw Balanced          | Divs:   0 | Min E-BFMI: 0.97 | Failures: 0
WBA Raw Socially_Influenced | Divs:   0 | Min E-BFMI: 0.97 | Failures: 0
\end{verbatim}

SBA has no parameters to fit, so it makes no sense to check sampling
diagnostics. The samplers are healthy for WBA models on all three
datasets --- no divergences, good E-BFMI, \(\hat{R}\) and ESS within
bounds. So we move on to predictive checks.

\section{Prior and Posterior Predictive
Checks}\label{prior-and-posterior-predictive-checks}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Generalized Extraction Function for Posteriors}
\NormalTok{extract\_predictive\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, stan\_data, scenario\_name, }\AttributeTok{target\_var =} \StringTok{"posterior\_pred"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  obs\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{obs\_id =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N),}
    \AttributeTok{blue1  =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1,}
    \AttributeTok{blue2  =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2,}
    \AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(target\_var)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.chain, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.iteration) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
\NormalTok{    tidyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{cols =}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(target\_var), }
      \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"obs\_id"}\NormalTok{, }
      \AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"pred"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{obs\_id =}\NormalTok{ readr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{parse\_number}\NormalTok{(obs\_id))}

\NormalTok{  obs\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_obs =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(choice), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  pred\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pred\_samples }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(obs\_df, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"obs\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw, blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_draw =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pred), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{p\_pred =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(p\_draw),}
      \AttributeTok{lower  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(p\_draw, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{upper  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(p\_draw, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(obs\_summary, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"blue1"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"blue2"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{        blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ),}
      \AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ scenario\_name}
\NormalTok{    )}
  
  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(pred\_summary)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Standardized Plotting Function}
\NormalTok{plot\_predictive\_check }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(plot\_data, title, subtitle) \{}
\NormalTok{  semantic\_colors }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}  \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}  \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#fdae61"}\NormalTok{,}
                       \StringTok{"Maybe Blue"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#abd9e9"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4575b4"}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(plot\_data, }
         \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ blue1, }\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ social\_evidence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_linerange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lower, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ upper),}
                   \AttributeTok{position =} \FunctionTok{position\_dodge}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{), }
                   \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ p\_pred), }
              \AttributeTok{position =} \FunctionTok{position\_dodge}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{), }
              \AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ p\_obs), }
               \AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{21}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stroke =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
               \AttributeTok{position =} \FunctionTok{position\_dodge}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =}\NormalTok{ semantic\_colors) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =}\NormalTok{ semantic\_colors) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{breaks =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =}\NormalTok{ title,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =}\NormalTok{ subtitle,}
      \AttributeTok{x        =} \StringTok{"Blue marbles in direct sample"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{y        =} \StringTok{"P(choose blue)"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{colour   =} \StringTok{"Social evidence"}\NormalTok{, }
      \AttributeTok{fill     =} \StringTok{"Social evidence"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{theme\_bw}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Extract predictions for the SBA}
\CommentTok{\# Extract SBA posterior predictive checks across all three scenarios}
\NormalTok{sba\_post\_ppc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(fit\_sba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, scenario) \{}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_predictive\_data}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{fit =}\NormalTok{ fit,}
    \AttributeTok{stan\_data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_wba[[scenario]],}
    \AttributeTok{scenario\_name =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{target\_var =} \StringTok{"posterior\_pred"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}

\CommentTok{\# Plot SBA across all three datasets}
\FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  sba\_post\_ppc,}
  \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Posterior Predictive Checks: Simple Bayesian Agent"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"SBA fits the Balanced dataset but misses the other two systematically"}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_posterior_predictive-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Custom R function to generate Prior predictions for the raw model}
\NormalTok{generate\_wba\_raw\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(stan\_data, scenario\_name, }\AttributeTok{n\_draws =} \DecValTok{4000}\NormalTok{) \{}
  
\NormalTok{  obs\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{obs\_id =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N),}
    \AttributeTok{blue1  =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1,}
    \AttributeTok{total1 =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total1,}
    \AttributeTok{blue2  =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2,}
    \AttributeTok{total2 =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total2,}
    \AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \CommentTok{\# 1. Draw parameters from the priors defined in the raw Stan model}
\NormalTok{  wd\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_draws, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ws\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_draws, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# 2. Simulate choices across all draws and observations}
\NormalTok{  pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{.draw =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_draws, }\AttributeTok{obs\_id =}\NormalTok{ obs\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(obs\_df, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"obs\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{wd =}\NormalTok{ wd\_prior[.draw],}
      \AttributeTok{ws =}\NormalTok{ ws\_prior[.draw],}
      \AttributeTok{alpha\_post =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ wd }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ws }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
      \AttributeTok{beta\_post  =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ wd }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ws }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
      
      \CommentTok{\# Probability matching: sample belief, then sample choice}
      \AttributeTok{theta =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), alpha\_post, beta\_post),}
      \AttributeTok{pred  =} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}
\NormalTok{    )}

  \CommentTok{\# 3. Summarize in the exact same format as the posterior function}
\NormalTok{  obs\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_obs =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(choice), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  pred\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pred\_samples }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw, blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_draw =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pred), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{p\_pred =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(p\_draw),}
      \AttributeTok{lower  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(p\_draw, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{upper  =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(p\_draw, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(obs\_summary, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"blue1"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"blue2"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{        blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ),}
      \AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ scenario\_name}
\NormalTok{    )}

  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(pred\_summary)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Extract PRIOR predictive checks by simulating in R}
\NormalTok{wba\_raw\_prior\_ppc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{generate\_wba\_raw\_prior}\NormalTok{(.x, .y))}

\CommentTok{\# Plot PRIOR}
\FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  wba\_raw\_prior\_ppc,}
  \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Prior Predictive Checks: WBA (Raw)"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Lines/Bars = Predicted P(Blue) from Prior alone ± 95\% CI | Circles = Observed"}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_posterior_predictive-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract POSTERIOR predictive checks for WBA Raw}
\NormalTok{wba\_raw\_post\_ppc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba\_raw, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, scenario) \{}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_predictive\_data}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{fit =}\NormalTok{ fit, }
    \AttributeTok{stan\_data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_wba[[scenario]], }
    \AttributeTok{scenario\_name =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{target\_var =} \StringTok{"posterior\_pred"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}

\CommentTok{\# Plot POSTERIOR}
\FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  wba\_raw\_post\_ppc,}
  \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Posterior Predictive Checks: WBA (Raw)"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Lines/Bars = Predicted P(Blue) after fitting ± 95\% CI | Circles = Observed"}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_posterior_predictive-3.pdf}
\end{center}

Note that for SBA prior and posterior predictive checks are the same (so
we only draw them once), as there is no fitted parameter. The SBA
reproduces the Balanced dataset because that \emph{is} the SBA's
generating process. But for the Self-Focused and Socially-Influenced
datasets the predicted lines diverge from the observed proportions ---
the SBA cannot bend its weights, so it cannot capture either bias. This
is a clear \textbf{model misspecification} failure: the predictive check
shows that SBA is clearly the wrong model for the data.

The WBA's posterior predictive checks look broad but well-centered on
every dataset. Where the SBA failed structurally, the WBA appears to fit
adequately So we seem to have our winner! However, as we know from
previous chapters, we should be rigorous and systematically test WBA's
ability to fit the data and recover the parameter values across a wide
range of parameter values.

\subsection{Parameter recovery: WBA
collapses}\label{parameter-recovery-wba-collapses}

Before we go into SBC, let's check if WBA can actually recover the true
parameter values in these three scenarios:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract posteriors and compare to true values}
\NormalTok{wba\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba\_raw, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, name) \{}
  \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(weight\_direct, weight\_social) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ name)}
\NormalTok{\})}

\CommentTok{\# True values for plotting}
\NormalTok{wba\_true\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(true\_wba, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{(.x), }\AttributeTok{.id =} \StringTok{"Scenario"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(wd, ws), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"parameter"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"true\_value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{parameter =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(parameter }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"wd"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Plot: weight\_direct recovery}
\NormalTok{p\_wd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ wba\_recovery }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Balanced"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Self\_Focused"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Scenario, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ wba\_true\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(parameter }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{), }
             \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_value, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show.legend =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                                \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }
                                \StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                                 \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }
                                 \StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Weight on Direct Evidence (w\_d)"}\NormalTok{, }
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Dashed = true generating value"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Plot: weight\_social recovery  }
\NormalTok{p\_ws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ wba\_recovery }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Balanced"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Self\_Focused"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Scenario, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ wba\_true\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(parameter }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{), }
             \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_value, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show.legend =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                                \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }
                                \StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                                 \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }
                                 \StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Weight on Social Evidence (w\_s)"}\NormalTok{, }
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Dashed = true generating value"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_wd }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_ws }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{plot\_annotation}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"⚠️ Parameter Recovery: WBA (Raw Parameterisation)"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_parameter_recovery_raw-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Something is wrong!} The posteriors are extremely wide and don't
center on the true values. The recovery is inadequate --- especially for
the social weight.

\subsection{Diagnosing the ridge}\label{diagnosing-the-ridge}

Let's visualize the joint posterior to understand what's happening:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract joint posterior draws}
\NormalTok{post\_draws\_self }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  fit\_wba\_raw}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{()}

\CommentTok{\# Generate implied prior for comparison}
\NormalTok{wba\_prior\_joint }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{weight\_direct =} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{5000}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{weight\_social =} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{5000}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Prior contour (grey dashed)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ wba\_prior\_joint, }
                  \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social), }
                  \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey60"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Posterior scatter (red to signify pathology)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ post\_draws\_self, }
             \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social), }
             \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Posterior contour}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ post\_draws\_self, }
                  \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social), }
                  \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#a50026"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# True value crosshair}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{), }
             \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{stroke =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"The Posterior Ridge: A Severe Identifiability Problem"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Self{-}Focused Agent (true: w\_d=1.5, w\_s=0.5). Grey = prior contours."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Weight on Direct (w\_d)"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Weight on Social (w\_s)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_posterior_ridge-1.pdf}
\end{center}

In the plot we see the prior for the two weights (grey dashed contours)
and the posterior (red points and contours). The true value is marked
with a dashed crosshair. From this plot we can identify a potential
source for the poor recovery: the posterior is not a tight blob around
the true value, but rather a long diagonal ridge. If we move along this
ridge, we can see that the likelihood remains high --- the model cannot
