distinguish between different combinations of \(w_d\) and \(w_s\) that
produce the same relative weighting of direct vs social evidence. Why is
that? \(w_d = 2, w_s = 2\) produces the \textbf{same average decisions}
as \(w_d = 1, w_s = 1\) --- the absolute scale affects only the
\emph{variance} of choices, which is hard to pin down from binary
outcomes. If you were expecting the ridge to follow the line
\(w_d = w_s\) (where the agent is balanced), that would be wrong --- the
ridge actually follows the line where the \emph{ratio} of weights is
constant, given the inferred preferences of the agent. This specific
Self-Focused agent was simulated with the true parameters \(w_d = 1.5\)
and \(w_s = 0.5\). Therefore, the ridge in that plot follows the
constant ratio of those true values: \(1.5 / 0.5 = 3\). The ridge
follows the line \(w_d = 3 w_s\).

Maybe we just need more trials? Let's check:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Set up a grid of simulations across our desired sample sizes}
\NormalTok{n\_sims\_per\_size }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{50}   \CommentTok{\# Increased from 20 to 50}
\NormalTok{sample\_sizes    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{180}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{360}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{720}\NormalTok{) }\CommentTok{\# Doubling the sample size}

\NormalTok{sim\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{sample\_size =}\NormalTok{ sample\_sizes,}
  \AttributeTok{sim\_id      =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_sims\_per\_size}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \CommentTok{\# Draw true parameters from the exact same Lognormal(0, 0.5) prior}
    \AttributeTok{true\_wd =} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{true\_ws =} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Helper function to generate exactly N trials }
\NormalTok{generate\_random\_trials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(wd, ws, n\_trials) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Sample combinations with replacement to hit the exact trial count}
\NormalTok{  ev\_sample }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ evidence\_combinations }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{slice\_sample}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials, }\AttributeTok{replace =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Generate choices using your existing function}
  \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(wd, ws, ev\_sample, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{total1 =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{L, }\AttributeTok{total2 =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{L)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Running \%d total parameter recovery simulations...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(sim\_grid)))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Running 150 total parameter recovery simulations...
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Run fits in parallel (using your existing multicore plan)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_n\_investigation.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
\NormalTok{  recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{future\_pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(sim\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sample\_size, sim\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_id, sim\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{true\_wd, sim\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{true\_ws),}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(n\_trials, id, wd, ws) \{}
      
      \CommentTok{\# 1. Generate data and prep for Stan}
\NormalTok{      df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_random\_trials}\NormalTok{(wd, ws, n\_trials)}
\NormalTok{      stan\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(df)}
      
      \CommentTok{\# 2. Fit the raw model (using reduced iterations for speed )}
\NormalTok{      fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_weighted\_raw}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data, }
        \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }
        \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{save\_warmup =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{output\_dir =} \StringTok{"models/temp"}
\NormalTok{      )}
      
      \CommentTok{\# 3. Extract posteriors}
\NormalTok{      draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{)))}
      
      \CommentTok{\# 4. Summarize recovery}
      \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{sample\_size =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials,}
        \AttributeTok{sim\_id      =}\NormalTok{ id,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_wd     =}\NormalTok{ wd,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_ws     =}\NormalTok{ ws,}
        \AttributeTok{est\_wd      =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weight\_direct),}
        \AttributeTok{lower\_wd    =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weight\_direct, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
        \AttributeTok{upper\_wd    =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weight\_direct, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
        \AttributeTok{est\_ws      =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weight\_social),}
        \AttributeTok{lower\_ws    =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weight\_social, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
        \AttributeTok{upper\_ws    =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weight\_social, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \},}
    \AttributeTok{.options =} \FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(recovery\_results, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_n\_investigation.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_n\_investigation.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Reshape data for plotting}
\NormalTok{plot\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_results }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{ends\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"wd"}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{ends\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ws"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{".value"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"parameter"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{names\_pattern =} \StringTok{"(.*)\_(w[ds])"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{parameter =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(parameter }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"wd"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Weight Direct (w\_d)"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Weight Social (w\_s)"}\NormalTok{),}
    \CommentTok{\# Force the factor levels so the facets order correctly top{-}to{-}bottom}
    \AttributeTok{sample\_size\_label =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N ="}\NormalTok{, sample\_size), }
                               \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N = 180"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"N = 360"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"N = 720"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Plot True vs Estimated values}
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(plot\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ est, }\AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(sample\_size))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Lowered alpha slightly to accommodate the higher density of points}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ lower, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ upper), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_smooth}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{method=}\NormalTok{lm, }\AttributeTok{se=}\ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_grid}\NormalTok{(sample\_size\_label }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ parameter) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Does More Data Help? NO!"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Even at N=720, recovery remains terrible due to the posterior ridge."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True Parameter Value"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior Median"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Sample Size"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{panel.border =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey80"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_trial_number_investigation-1.pdf}
\end{center}

While there is a positive slope between true and inferred parameter
values, and it slightly increase with trials, the overall recovery is
not ideal. This is not a statistical power problem --- it's a
\textbf{fundamental identifiability problem} in the parameterisation.

\section{Round 2: PBA and Reparameterised
WBA}\label{round-2-pba-and-reparameterised-wba}

The problem is structural: the likelihood depends on \(w_d\) and \(w_s\)
only through their \emph{ratio} and not (much) their \emph{sum}. We have
two options for fixing this, and they correspond to two different
cognitive commitments.

\textbf{Option 1 --- fix the scale.} If we believe attention has a
limited budget, the total weight \(w_d + w_s\) could be fixed at 1, and
the only thing left to estimate is how that budget is allocated. This
gives us the \textbf{Proportional Bayesian Agent (PBA)}:

\[\alpha_{\text{post}} = 0.5 + p \cdot k_1 + (1-p) \cdot k_2\]

with \(p \in (0,1)\). One free parameter, no identifiability problem by
construction.

\textbf{Option 2 --- keep the scale, but reparameterise.} If we don't
want to assume a fixed attention budget, we can keep two parameters but
rotate them into orthogonal directions:

\[\rho = \frac{w_d}{w_d + w_s} \in (0,1) \qquad \kappa = w_d + w_s > 0\]

Now \(\rho\) answers ``which source is trusted more?'' ---
well-identified from conflict trials --- and \(\kappa\) answers ``how
much evidence is inferred to be there overall?'' --- harder to pin down
but at least living in its own dimension with its own prior. The
original weights are reconstructed as \(w_d = \rho\kappa\) and
\(w_s = (1-\rho)\kappa\). We call this the \textbf{reparameterised WBA}.

The two solutions make different scientific claims (fixed vs.~free total
attention) and we'll fit both.

\section{Stan Implementation: The
Solutions}\label{stan-implementation-the-solutions}

\subsection{Proportional Bayesian Agent
(PBA)}\label{proportional-bayesian-agent-pba}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}usetikzlibrary}\NormalTok{\{bayesnet\}}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}begin}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
  \CommentTok{\% Fixed Prior}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const] (alpha0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const, right=1cm of alpha0] (beta0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Allocation Parameter}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, below=0.8cm of alpha0, xshift=0.5cm] (p) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$p$}\NormalTok{\}; }

  \CommentTok{\% Inputs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below left=1.2cm and 0.8cm of p] (k1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=0.3cm of k1] (n1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below right=1.2cm and 0.8cm of p] (k2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.3cm of k2] (n2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Beliefs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2.5cm of p, xshift={-}0.7cm] (ap) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2.5cm of p, xshift=0.7cm] (bp) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Action}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1cm of ap, xshift=0.7cm] (y) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$y\_i$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Edges}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alpha0, beta0\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{p, k1, n1, k2, n2\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{ap, bp\} \{y\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{p1\} \{(k1)(n1)(k2)(n2)(ap)(bp)(y)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$N$}\NormalTok{\};}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}end}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.6\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/pba-dag-1.pdf}

}

\caption{DAG for the Proportional Bayesian Agent}

\end{figure}%

The generative relationships for the PBA are: \[
\begin{aligned}
p &\sim \text{Beta}(2, 2) \\
\alpha_{post,i} &= \alpha_0 + p k_{1,i} + (1-p) k_{2,i} \\
\beta_{post,i}  &= \beta_0 + p(n_{1,i} - k_{1,i}) + (1-p)(n_{2,i} - k_{2,i}) \\
y_i &\sim \text{Beta-Binomial}(1, \alpha_{post,i}, \beta_{post,i})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ProportionalAgent\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Proportional Bayesian Agent (PBA).}
\StringTok{// p in [0,1] allocates the unit evidence budget between direct and social.}
\StringTok{// p = 0.5 approximates balanced weighting; p {-}\textgreater{} 1 ignores social; p {-}\textgreater{} 0 ignores direct.}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total2;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  real prior\_p\_alpha;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_p\_beta;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} p;  // Allocation to direct evidence}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // prior}
\StringTok{  target += beta\_lpdf(p | prior\_p\_alpha, prior\_p\_beta);}

\StringTok{  // Vectorized likelihood}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] alpha\_post = 0.5 + p * to\_vector(blue1) + (1.0 {-} p) * to\_vector(blue2);}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] beta\_post  = 0.5 + p * (to\_vector(total1) {-} to\_vector(blue1))}
\StringTok{                                   + (1.0 {-} p) * (to\_vector(total2) {-} to\_vector(blue2));}
\StringTok{                             }
\StringTok{  target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}
\StringTok{  real lprior = beta\_lpdf(p | prior\_p\_alpha, prior\_p\_beta);}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      real alpha\_post = 0.5 + p * blue1[i] + (1.0 {-} p) * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real beta\_post  = 0.5 + p * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i]) }
\StringTok{                           + (1.0 {-} p) * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}

\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(ProportionalAgent\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_proportional\_agent.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_proportional_agent.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Weighted Bayesian Agent
(Reparameterised)}\label{weighted-bayesian-agent-reparameterised}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}usetikzlibrary}\NormalTok{\{bayesnet\}}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}begin}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
  \CommentTok{\% Fixed Prior}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const] (alpha0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const, right=1cm of alpha0] (beta0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Latent Parameters}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, below=0.8cm of alpha0] (rho) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}rho}\SpecialStringTok{$}\NormalTok{\}; }
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, below=0.8cm of beta0] (kappa) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}kappa}\SpecialStringTok{$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Transformed Weights}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=1cm of rho, xshift={-}0.5cm] (wd) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$w\_d$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=1cm of kappa, xshift=0.5cm] (ws) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$w\_s$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Inputs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=1cm of wd] (k1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=0.3cm of k1] (n1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=1cm of ws] (k2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.3cm of k2] (n2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Beliefs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2cm of wd] (ap) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2cm of ws] (bp) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Action}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1.2cm of ap, xshift=1cm] (y) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$y\_i$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Edges}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alpha0, beta0\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{rho, kappa\} \{wd, ws\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{wd, k1, n1, ws, k2, n2\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{ap, bp\} \{y\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{p1\} \{(k1)(n1)(k2)(n2)(ap)(bp)(y)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$N$}\NormalTok{\};}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}end}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.6\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/wba-dag-1.pdf}

}

\caption{DAG for the Weighted Bayesian Agent (Reparameterised)}

\end{figure}%

The generative relationships for the reparameterized WBA are: \[
\begin{aligned}
\rho &\sim \text{Beta}(2, 2) \\
\kappa &\sim \text{Lognormal}(\log(2), 0.5) \\
w_d &= \rho \cdot \kappa \\
w_s &= (1 - \rho) \cdot \kappa \\
\alpha_{post,i} &= \alpha_0 + w_d k_{1,i} + w_s k_{2,i} \\
\beta_{post,i}  &= \beta_0 + w_d(n_{1,i} - k_{1,i}) + w_s(n_{2,i} - k_{2,i}) \\
y_i &\sim \text{Beta-Binomial}(1, \alpha_{post,i}, \beta_{post,i})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{WeightedAgent\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Weighted Bayesian Agent (reparameterised).}
\StringTok{// rho in (0,1): relative weight of direct vs social evidence.}
\StringTok{// kappa \textgreater{} 0: total evidence scaling (w\_d + w\_s).}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} total2;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  real prior\_rho\_alpha;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_rho\_beta;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_kappa\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_kappa\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} rho;    // relative weight: w\_d / (w\_d + w\_s)}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}          kappa;  // total weight: w\_d + w\_s}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} weight\_direct = rho * kappa;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} weight\_social = (1.0 {-} rho) * kappa;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // rho: weakly centred on equal weighting}
\StringTok{  target += beta\_lpdf(rho | prior\_rho\_alpha, prior\_rho\_beta);}
\StringTok{  // kappa: lognormal centered on 2 (SBA equivalent)}
\StringTok{  target += lognormal\_lpdf(kappa | prior\_kappa\_mu, prior\_kappa\_sigma);}

\StringTok{  // Vectorized likelihood}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] alpha\_post = 0.5 + weight\_direct * to\_vector(blue1)}
\StringTok{                                   + weight\_social * to\_vector(blue2);}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] beta\_post  = 0.5 + weight\_direct * (to\_vector(total1) {-} to\_vector(blue1))}
\StringTok{                                   + weight\_social * (to\_vector(total2) {-} to\_vector(blue2));}
\StringTok{                             }
\StringTok{  target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}
\StringTok{  real lprior = beta\_lpdf(rho | prior\_rho\_alpha, prior\_rho\_beta) + }
\StringTok{                lognormal\_lpdf(kappa | prior\_kappa\_mu, prior\_kappa\_sigma);}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      real alpha\_post = 0.5 + weight\_direct * blue1[i] + weight\_social * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real beta\_post  = 0.5 + weight\_direct * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i]) }
\StringTok{                           + weight\_social * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}

\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(WeightedAgent\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_weighted\_agent.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_weighted_agent.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\section{Fitting the Reparameterised
Models}\label{fitting-the-reparameterised-models}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_proportional }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_proportional\_agent.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{mod\_weighted     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_weighted\_agent.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Prepare PBA Data}\label{prepare-pba-data}

The PBA uses normalized weights that sum to 1, so we need to convert our
scenarios:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# For PBA, p represents w\_d / (w\_d + w\_s)}
\CommentTok{\# Self{-}Focused: 1.5 / 2.0 = 0.75}
\CommentTok{\# Balanced: 1.0 / 2.0 = 0.5  }
\CommentTok{\# Socially{-}Influenced: 0.5 / 2.5 = 0.2}

\NormalTok{true\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Self\_Focused        =} \FloatTok{0.75}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{Balanced            =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{Socially\_Influenced =} \FloatTok{0.2}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate simulated data for each scenario}
\NormalTok{stan\_data\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(true\_pba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(p) \{}
\NormalTok{  sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(p, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p, evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{total1 =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{L, }\AttributeTok{total2 =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{L)}
  
\NormalTok{  d }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(sim)}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_p\_alpha }\OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_p\_beta  }\OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{  d}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Prepare WBA Reparameterised
Data}\label{prepare-wba-reparameterised-data}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{true\_wba\_reparam }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{Self\_Focused        =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rho =} \FloatTok{0.75}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{kappa =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{Balanced            =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rho =} \FloatTok{0.50}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{kappa =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{Socially\_Influenced =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rho =} \FloatTok{0.20}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{kappa =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate simulated data for each scenario}
\NormalTok{stan\_data\_wba\_reparam }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(true\_wba\_reparam, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(params) \{}
\NormalTok{  sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(params}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{wd, params}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ws,}
\NormalTok{                                  evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{total1 =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{L, }\AttributeTok{total2 =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{L)}

\NormalTok{  d }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(sim)}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_rho\_alpha     }\OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_rho\_beta      }\OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_kappa\_mu      }\OtherTok{=} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prior\_kappa\_sigma   }\OtherTok{=} \FloatTok{0.5}
\NormalTok{  d}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Fit All Models}\label{fit-all-models}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_pba\_\%s.rds"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_pba))))) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Fit PBA {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{  fit\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map2}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_pba, }\DecValTok{201}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{203}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(data, seed) \{}
    \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(mod\_proportional}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sample,}
            \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ data, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ seed), sampler\_args))}
\NormalTok{  \})}
  \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(fit\_pba) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_pba)}
  \FunctionTok{iwalk}\NormalTok{(fit\_pba, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ .x}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_pba\_\%s.rds"}\NormalTok{, .y)}
\NormalTok{  ))}

  \CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} Fit WBA reparameterised {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{  fit\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map2}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba\_reparam, }\DecValTok{204}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{206}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(data, seed) \{}
    \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(mod\_weighted}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sample,}
            \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ data, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ seed), sampler\_args))}
\NormalTok{  \})}
  \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba\_reparam)}
  \FunctionTok{iwalk}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ .x}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_wba\_\%s.rds"}\NormalTok{, .y)}
\NormalTok{  ))}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_pba),}
    \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_pba\_\%s.rds"}\NormalTok{, .x))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{set\_names}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_pba))}

\NormalTok{  fit\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba\_reparam),}
    \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_wba\_\%s.rds"}\NormalTok{, .x))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{set\_names}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_wba\_reparam))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Check diagnostics}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{=== Proportional Bayesian Agent ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Quick predictive checks}\label{quick-predictive-checks}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Helper function to generate and combine Prior vs. Posterior checks dynamically}
\NormalTok{generate\_side\_by\_side\_ppc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit\_list, data\_list, model\_title) \{}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Map over all three scenarios (Self\_Focused, Balanced, Socially\_Influenced)}
\NormalTok{  plot\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap}\NormalTok{(fit\_list, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, scenario) \{}
    
    \CommentTok{\# 1. Extract and plot Prior Predictive}
\NormalTok{    prior\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{extract\_predictive\_data}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{fit =}\NormalTok{ fit,}
      \AttributeTok{stan\_data =}\NormalTok{ data\_list[[scenario]],}
      \AttributeTok{scenario\_name =} \StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{target\_var =} \StringTok{"prior\_pred"}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
\NormalTok{    p\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      prior\_data,}
      \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(model\_title, }\StringTok{"{-}"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{str\_replace\_all}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\StringTok{"\_"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{" "}\NormalTok{)),}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Prior Predictive Check"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{) }\CommentTok{\# Hide individual legends to save space}
    
    \CommentTok{\# 2. Extract and plot Posterior Predictive}
\NormalTok{    post\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{extract\_predictive\_data}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{fit =}\NormalTok{ fit,}
      \AttributeTok{stan\_data =}\NormalTok{ data\_list[[scenario]],}
      \AttributeTok{scenario\_name =} \StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{target\_var =} \StringTok{"posterior\_pred"}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
\NormalTok{    p\_post }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      post\_data,}
      \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(model\_title, }\StringTok{"{-}"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{str\_replace\_all}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\StringTok{"\_"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{" "}\NormalTok{)),}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Posterior Predictive Check"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{axis.title.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{()) }\CommentTok{\# Share Y axis visually with the prior plot}
    
    \CommentTok{\# 3. Combine side{-}by{-}side}
    \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(p\_prior }\SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ p\_post)}
\NormalTok{  \})}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Stack the three scenarios vertically and collect the legend at the very bottom}
\NormalTok{  combined\_all }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{wrap\_plots}\NormalTok{(plot\_list, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
    \FunctionTok{plot\_layout}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{guides =} \StringTok{"collect"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\&} 
    \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(combined\_all)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== Proportional Bayesian Agent (PBA) Predictive Checks ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== Proportional Bayesian Agent (PBA) Predictive Checks ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{pba\_combined }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_side\_by\_side\_ppc}\NormalTok{(fit\_pba, stan\_data\_pba, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(pba\_combined)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_reparam_ppc-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{=== Weighted Bayesian Agent (WBA) Predictive Checks ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

=== Weighted Bayesian Agent (WBA) Predictive Checks ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Note: WBA uses stan\_data\_wba\_reparam}
\NormalTok{wba\_combined }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_side\_by\_side\_ppc}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba, stan\_data\_wba\_reparam, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(wba\_combined)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_reparam_ppc-2.pdf}
\end{center}

Predictive checks look good for both models, with the posterior
predictions (right column) closely matching the observed data (black
points) across all scenarios. The priors (left column) are more diffuse,
as expected, but still cover the range of observed outcomes. This gives
us confidence that the models are fitting the data well and that we can
proceed to examine parameter recovery.

\section{Parameter Recovery: The Solution
Works!}\label{parameter-recovery-the-solution-works}

\subsection{PBA Recovery}\label{pba-recovery}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{pba\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{4000}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{Scenario =} \StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{pba\_posteriors }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(fit\_pba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, name) \{}
  \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"p"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(p) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ name)}
\NormalTok{\})}

\NormalTok{pba\_true\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{enframe}\NormalTok{(true\_pba, }\AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Scenario"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =} \StringTok{"true\_value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{unnest}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(true\_value))}

\FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(pba\_prior, pba\_posteriors) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Scenario, }
                           \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Self\_Focused"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ p, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Scenario, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ pba\_true\_df, }
             \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_value, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show.legend =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{inherit.aes =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"grey80"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                               \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                                \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"✓ PBA Parameter Recovery"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"p (Allocation to direct evidence)"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_pba_recovery-1.pdf}
\end{center}

This is not perfect, but pretty good, perhaps overregularized by the
prior (pulled towards the center compared to true values).

\subsection{WBA Reparameterised
Recovery}\label{wba-reparameterised-recovery}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{wba\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{rho =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{4000}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }
  \AttributeTok{kappa =} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{4000}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{), }
  \AttributeTok{Scenario =} \StringTok{"Prior"}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{wba\_posteriors }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, name) \{}
  \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(rho, kappa) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ name)}
\NormalTok{\})}

\NormalTok{wba\_true\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(true\_wba\_reparam, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{(.x[}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{)]), }\AttributeTok{.id =} \StringTok{"Scenario"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(rho, kappa), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"parameter"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"true\_value"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(wba\_prior, wba\_posteriors) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(rho, kappa), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"parameter"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Scenario, }
                           \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Self\_Focused"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Scenario, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ wba\_true\_df, }
             \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ true\_value, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Scenario), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show.legend =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{inherit.aes =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ parameter, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"grey80"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                               \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Prior"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Balanced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }
                                \StringTok{"Self\_Focused"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#e41a1c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Socially\_Influenced"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377eb8"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"✓ WBA Parameter Recovery"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"rho (relative weight) recovers ok; kappa (total scale) has wider uncertainty as expected"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Parameter Value"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_wba_reparam_recovery-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Rho is pretty good. Kappa not as good. So the problem of exactly how
much evidence is counted from the sources doesn't seem to be solvable
via parameterization. This would call for a change in the experimental
paradigm: we do not simply want to know the choices of the participants,
but also their confidence in the choice, or alternatively their reaction
time (which is an indirect measure of confidence). This would give us
more information to pin down the total scale of evidence weighting, and
not just the relative weighting. But for now we proceed as is.

\subsection{Comparing the Two
Parameterisations}\label{comparing-the-two-parameterisations}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Joint posterior in reparameterised space}
\NormalTok{p\_good }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ rho, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ kappa)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#4575b4"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#313695"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FloatTok{0.75}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Reparameterised (ρ, κ): Clean!"}\NormalTok{, }
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Nearly orthogonal posterior"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"ρ (relative weight)"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"κ (total scale)"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Joint posterior in original space}
\NormalTok{p\_bad }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density\_2d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#a50026"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Original (w\_d, w\_s): Ridge!"}\NormalTok{, }
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Strong correlation → poor recovery"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"w\_d"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"w\_s"}\NormalTok{) }

\NormalTok{p\_bad }\SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ p\_good }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{plot\_annotation}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"The Power of Reparameterisation"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Same model, different parameterisation, vastly different posterior geometry"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
