\end{center}

\subsection{Prior sensitivity}\label{prior-sensitivity-2}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\DocumentationTok{\#\# Prior Sensitivity}
\NormalTok{sens\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_sensitivity}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  fit\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused,}
  \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(sens\_wba)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Sensitivity based on cjs_dist
Prior selection: all priors
Likelihood selection: all data

 variable prior likelihood                                diagnosis
      rho 0.033      0.094                                        -
    kappa 0.313      0.012 potential strong prior / weak likelihood
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ps\_seq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_sequence}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  fit\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused,}
  \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_plot\_dens}\NormalTok{(ps\_seq) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Prior Sensitivity: WBA (Self{-}Focused)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Does scaling the prior/likelihood shift the posteriors?"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_prior_sensitivity-1.pdf}
\end{center}

ρ shows low sensitivity to prior (well-identified from conflict trials),
tho' it could use more data. κ shows moderate sensitivity (harder to pin
down from binary outcomes), consistent with the identifiability analysis
above, and it's not helped by more data (the problem is deeper). All in
all the prior sensitivity analysis confirms our previous analysis (but
it couldn't have replaced it), and tells us that more data would help ρ.
We'll see later whether multilevel modeling can help with that.

\subsection{Loo-pit}\label{loo-pit-1}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\DocumentationTok{\#\# LOO{-}PIT Calibration (Randomised for Discrete Outcomes)}
\CommentTok{\# For Bernoulli outcomes, standard LOO{-}PIT is discrete and requires}
\CommentTok{\# randomisation. We use the log{-}likelihood to reconstruct P(y=1|y\_\{{-}i\}).}
\CommentTok{\# Note: for a BetaBinomial(1, alpha, beta), P(y=1) = alpha / (alpha + beta).}
\CommentTok{\# The LOO predictive probability is approximated via PSIS.}

\NormalTok{loo\_wba\_self }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fit\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Extract LOO predictive probabilities for y=1}
\CommentTok{\# For Bernoulli: p\_loo = exp(elpd\_loo) when y=1, }
\CommentTok{\#                        and 1 {-} exp(elpd\_loo) when y=0 is more complex.}
\CommentTok{\# The proper way: use the posterior predictive draws directly.}
\NormalTok{pp\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"posterior\_pred"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{y\_obs    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_wba\_reparam}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Self\_Focused}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice}

\CommentTok{\# Compute randomised PIT using posterior predictive samples}
\NormalTok{pit\_vals }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(y\_obs), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{  p\_leq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pp\_draws[, i] }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=}\NormalTok{ y\_obs[i])}
\NormalTok{  p\_lt  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pp\_draws[, i] }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ y\_obs[i])}
  \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_lt, p\_leq)}
\NormalTok{\})}

\NormalTok{bayesplot}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{ppc\_pit\_ecdf}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ y\_obs, }\AttributeTok{pit =}\NormalTok{ pit\_vals) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"LOO{-}PIT: WBA (Self{-}Focused)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Flat = well{-}calibrated | Hump = model too variable | U = model too deterministic"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_loo_pit-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The results of this analysis vary a bit from run to run. Most of the
time the calibration looks fine. Sometimes, participants seem more
deterministic than probability matching predicts, as indicated by the
LOO-PIT showing a hump. This generally would indicate that a softmax
observation model might be better at capturing the data. However, this
is simulated data from the beta-binomial, so we know we are right in
using a beta-binomial and we will keep doing so. With empirical data we
wouldn't be as sure :-) In any case, remember: all diagnostics are part
of a critical evaluation, not deterministic rules!

\section{Simulation-Based Calibration Checks
(SBC)}\label{simulation-based-calibration-checks-sbc-2}

Now that we have roughly working models (we can identify the ratio
pretty decently), we validate them systematically with SBC. This
provides a global certificate that our posteriors are well-calibrated
across the entire prior space.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_sbc\_datasets }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{500}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_sbc\_wba\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_sbc\_pba\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)))) \{}
  
\NormalTok{  fixed\_ev\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ evidence\_combinations }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{slice}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{()), }\AttributeTok{times =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{))}

  \CommentTok{\# ── WBA SBC generator ──────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_generator\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(ev) \{}
\NormalTok{      N }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ev)}
\NormalTok{      rho\_true   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      kappa\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      wd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ rho\_true }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa\_true}
\NormalTok{      ws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ rho\_true) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa\_true}

\NormalTok{      alpha\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ wd }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ws }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2}
\NormalTok{      beta\_p  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ wd }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
\NormalTok{                       ws }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2)}
      
\NormalTok{      p\_blue  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(N, alpha\_p, beta\_p)}
\NormalTok{      choices }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(N, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_blue)}

      \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rho =}\NormalTok{ rho\_true, }\AttributeTok{kappa =}\NormalTok{ kappa\_true),}
        \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{N =}\NormalTok{ N, }\AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ choices,}
          \AttributeTok{blue1 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1, }\AttributeTok{total1 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total1,}
          \AttributeTok{blue2 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2, }\AttributeTok{total2 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total2}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \},}
    \AttributeTok{ev =}\NormalTok{ fixed\_ev\_grid}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_datasets\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(sbc\_generator\_wba, n\_sbc\_datasets)}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_backend\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_weighted,}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_results\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(sbc\_datasets\_wba, sbc\_backend\_wba, }\AttributeTok{keep\_fits =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_wba, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_sbc\_wba\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# ── PBA SBC generator ──────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_generator\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(ev) \{}
\NormalTok{      N }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ev)}
\NormalTok{      p\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
      
\NormalTok{      alpha\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_true }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_true) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2}
\NormalTok{      beta\_p  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_true }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
\NormalTok{                       (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_true) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2)}
                       
\NormalTok{      theta   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(N, alpha\_p, beta\_p)}
\NormalTok{      choices }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(N, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}

      \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p =}\NormalTok{ p\_true),}
        \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{N =}\NormalTok{ N, }\AttributeTok{choice =}\NormalTok{ choices,}
          \AttributeTok{blue1 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1, }\AttributeTok{total1 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total1,}
          \AttributeTok{blue2 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2, }\AttributeTok{total2 =}\NormalTok{ ev}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total2}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \},}
    \AttributeTok{ev =}\NormalTok{ fixed\_ev\_grid}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_datasets\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(sbc\_generator\_pba, n\_sbc\_datasets)}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_backend\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_proportional,}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_results\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(sbc\_datasets\_pba, sbc\_backend\_pba, }\AttributeTok{keep\_fits =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_pba, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_sbc\_pba\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_results\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_sbc\_wba\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  sbc\_results\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_sbc\_pba\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Plot SBC results}
\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf\_diff}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_wba, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"SBC Calibration: WBA (ρ, κ)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N = "}\NormalTok{, n\_sbc\_datasets, }\StringTok{" datasets. Flat = well{-}calibrated."}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ SBC}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf\_diff}\NormalTok{(sbc\_results\_pba, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \StringTok{"p"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"SBC Calibration: PBA"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"N = "}\NormalTok{, n\_sbc\_datasets, }\StringTok{" datasets."}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_wba }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_sbc\_pba}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_sbc-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The movements are not exactly a brownian motion as we'd expect, but they
stay well within the blue boundaries, so this doesn't give us any clear
signal of miscalibration for either model.

\subsection{Global Parameter Recovery from
SBC}\label{global-parameter-recovery-from-sbc}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{wba\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{\%in\%} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model =} \StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{pba\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"p"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model =} \StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{sbc\_recovery\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(wba\_recovery, pba\_recovery)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(sbc\_recovery\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ median)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q5, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{width =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#4575b4"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(model }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ variable, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Global Parameter Recovery Across SBC Universes"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Each point = one simulated dataset. Error bars = 90\% CI."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True (simulated) value"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior median"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"gray95"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_sbc_recovery-1.pdf}
\end{center}

As we could expect from our test on three scenarios, the rho parameters
in either model are nicely identified, while the kappa is a bit hopeless
:-)

\section{Case Studies in Model Comparison: Nested Models and Occam's
Razor}\label{case-studies-in-model-comparison-nested-models-and-occams-razor}

Before running a massive simulation to build a model comparison pipeline
and see if it works, let's zoom in on the mechanics of \emph{why} models
win or lose in our specific case.

Our three candidate models are mathematically nested. The Simple
Bayesian Agent (SBA) is a special case of the Proportional Bayesian
Agent (PBA) where \(p = 0.5\). In turn, the PBA is a special case of the
Weighted Bayesian Agent (WBA) where the total evidence scale
\(\kappa = 1.0\). Because of this nesting, the most complex model (WBA)
can perfectly mirror the behavior of the simpler models depending on its
parameters.

We expect a specific directionality in our ``errors'': if the true
data-generating process (DGP) is complex (WBA) but its parameters mimic
a simpler model, the procedure \emph{should} misclassify the DGP and
declare the simpler model the winner. This is Occam's Razor in action:
penalizing unnecessary complexity.

To prove this, we will simulate three specific datasets, all generated
by the WBA, but with parameters deliberately chosen to test these
boundaries:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{The SBA-Mimic (Balanced):} WBA with \(\rho = 0.5\) and
  \(\kappa = 2.0\) (\(w_d = 1.0, w_s = 1.0\)).
\item
  \textbf{The PBA-Mimic (Proportional):} WBA with \(\rho = 0.8\) and
  \(\kappa = 1.0\) (\(w_d = 0.8, w_s = 0.2\)).
\item
  \textbf{The Distinct WBA (Socially Influenced):} WBA with
  \(\rho = 0.2\) and \(\kappa = 2.5\) (\(w_d = 0.5, w_s = 2.0\)).
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Helper: fit SBA, PBA, WBA to a single dataset}
\NormalTok{fit\_all\_models }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(stan\_data, ...) \{}
\NormalTok{  sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_simple}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fixed\_param =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, ...}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_proportional}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, ...}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_weighted}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, ...}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SBA =}\NormalTok{ sba, }\AttributeTok{PBA =}\NormalTok{ pba, }\AttributeTok{WBA =}\NormalTok{ wba)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_scenario\_case\_studies.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2026}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# 1. Generate three datasets (all True Model = WBA with specific params)}
\NormalTok{  df\_sba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  df\_pba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  df\_distinct  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  stan\_data\_sba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(df\_sba\_mimic)}
\NormalTok{  stan\_data\_pba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(df\_pba\_mimic)}
\NormalTok{  stan\_data\_distinct  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(df\_distinct)}

  \CommentTok{\# 2. Fit all three models to all three datasets (9 fits total)}
\NormalTok{  fits\_sba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{fit\_all\_models}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_sba\_mimic)}
\NormalTok{  fits\_pba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{fit\_all\_models}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_pba\_mimic)}
\NormalTok{  fits\_distinct  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{fit\_all\_models}\NormalTok{(stan\_data\_distinct)}

  \CommentTok{\# 3. Pre{-}compute LOO (avoids needing the raw fit objects later)}
\NormalTok{  extract\_loos }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fits) \{}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{SBA =} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fits}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)),}
      \AttributeTok{PBA =} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fits}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{PBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)),}
      \AttributeTok{WBA =} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fits}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{WBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{  loos\_sba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{extract\_loos}\NormalTok{(fits\_sba\_mimic)}
\NormalTok{  loos\_pba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{extract\_loos}\NormalTok{(fits\_pba\_mimic)}
\NormalTok{  loos\_distinct  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{extract\_loos}\NormalTok{(fits\_distinct)}

  \CommentTok{\# 4. Save everything we need downstream: data frames + LOO objects.}
  \CommentTok{\#    We do NOT save the fit objects themselves — the LOO objects and}
  \CommentTok{\#    data frames are all the downstream chunks use.}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{df\_sba\_mimic =}\NormalTok{ df\_sba\_mimic,}
    \AttributeTok{df\_pba\_mimic =}\NormalTok{ df\_pba\_mimic,}
    \AttributeTok{df\_distinct  =}\NormalTok{ df\_distinct,}
    \AttributeTok{loos\_sba\_mimic =}\NormalTok{ loos\_sba\_mimic,}
    \AttributeTok{loos\_pba\_mimic =}\NormalTok{ loos\_pba\_mimic,}
    \AttributeTok{loos\_distinct  =}\NormalTok{ loos\_distinct}
\NormalTok{  ), }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_scenario\_case\_studies.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  cache }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_scenario\_case\_studies.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  df\_sba\_mimic   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cache}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{df\_sba\_mimic}
\NormalTok{  df\_pba\_mimic   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cache}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{df\_pba\_mimic}
\NormalTok{  df\_distinct    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cache}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{df\_distinct}
\NormalTok{  loos\_sba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cache}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{loos\_sba\_mimic}
\NormalTok{  loos\_pba\_mimic }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cache}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{loos\_pba\_mimic}
\NormalTok{  loos\_distinct  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cache}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{loos\_distinct}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Now, let's look at the stacking weights and LOO comparisons for these
three scenarios.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Print comparison summaries using the pre{-}computed LOO objects}
\NormalTok{print\_scenario }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(loos, name, true\_params) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{=== \%s ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{True DGP: WBA \%s}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, name, true\_params))}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_compare}\NormalTok{(loos))}
\NormalTok{  w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_model\_weights}\NormalTok{(loos)}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(w, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{print\_scenario}\NormalTok{(loos\_sba\_mimic, }\StringTok{"Scenario 1: The SBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{,  }\StringTok{"(rho=0.5, kappa=2.0)"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

WBA -0.9       0.4   
PBA -0.9       0.7   
Method: stacking
------
    weight
SBA 1.000 
PBA 0.000 
WBA 0.000

\FunctionTok{print\_scenario}\NormalTok{(loos\_pba\_mimic, }\StringTok{"Scenario 2: The PBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{,  }\StringTok{"(rho=0.8, kappa=1.0)"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

------

SBA 0.012 
PBA 0.618 
WBA 0.369 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Create the dataframe directly from the console output}
\NormalTok{extract\_loo\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(loos, scenario\_name) \{}
\NormalTok{  comp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{(loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_compare}\NormalTok{(loos), }\AttributeTok{rownames =} \StringTok{"Model"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Model, elpd\_diff, se\_diff)}
\NormalTok{  w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model =} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_model\_weights}\NormalTok{(loos)), }\AttributeTok{weight =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_model\_weights}\NormalTok{(loos)))}
\NormalTok{  comp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(w, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"Model"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ scenario\_name)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Create the summary dataframe dynamically without hardcoding values}
\NormalTok{scenario\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_loo\_summary}\NormalTok{(loos\_sba\_mimic, }\StringTok{"1. SBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_loo\_summary}\NormalTok{(loos\_pba\_mimic, }\StringTok{"2. PBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_loo\_summary}\NormalTok{(loos\_distinct, }\StringTok{"3. Distinct WBA"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \CommentTok{\# Lock in the factor levels for consistent left{-}to{-}right plotting}
    \AttributeTok{Model =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"1. SBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"2. PBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"3. Distinct WBA"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Plot 1: ELPD Differences with Standard Error}
\NormalTok{p\_elpd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(scenario\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Model)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_pointrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ se\_diff, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ se\_diff), }
                  \AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"ELPD Differences (with Standard Error)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Zero indicates the best{-}fitting model in that scenario."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"ELPD Difference"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Plot 2: Bayesian Stacking Weights}
\NormalTok{p\_weight }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(scenario\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Model)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_col}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{percent}\NormalTok{(weight, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)), }
            \AttributeTok{vjust =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Bayesian Stacking Weights"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"The procedure perfectly penalizes complexity, shifting all weight to the simplest adequate model."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Stacking Weight"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{()) }\CommentTok{\# Hide strips on the bottom plot for a cleaner look}

\CommentTok{\# Combine with patchwork}
\NormalTok{p\_elpd }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_weight }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{plot\_annotation}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model Recovery: The Occam\textquotesingle{}s Razor Effect"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{theme =} \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_scenario_comparison_weights-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Notice the clear directionality of the classification:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  In the SBA-mimic scenario, the WBA generates the data, but it is
  effectively functioning as the 0-parameter SBA model. Both the PBA and
  WBA fit the data equally well, but LOO accurately applies a complexity
  penalty. The SBA ``wins.''
\item
  In the PBA-mimic scenario, the WBA parameters (\(\kappa = 1.0\)) mean
  the agent is trading off evidence exactly as the 1-parameter PBA
  assumes. The PBA easily beats the SBA, but it also beats the true
  generating WBA, because the WBA's extra scale parameter isn't needed.
\item
  In Scenario 3, the agent has a high total evidence sensitivity
  (\(\kappa = 2.5\)) and heavily overweights social evidence
  (\(\rho = 0.2\)). The SBA cannot flex to capture this. However, both
  the PBA and the WBA get substantial weight, with PBA winning.
\end{itemize}

This is not ideal, but neither a failure of model comparison. It tells
us that the WBA could still be likely, but it is not a better model than
PBA. This is likely because WBA is more complex (one parameter more),
but the it has issues in reconstructing the extra parameter (kappa), We
should be aware of these issues.

\subsection{Visualizing Pointwise ELPD: Where the Advantage
Lies}\label{visualizing-pointwise-elpd-where-the-advantage-lies}

To truly understand this, we can plot the pointwise ELPD differences. We
will compare the true generating model (WBA) against the simpler model
that ``won'' in the mimicking scenarios, and against the PBA in the
distinct scenario.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{pw\_1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{obs       =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{180}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{elpd\_diff =}\NormalTok{ loos\_sba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{WBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[,}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ loos\_sba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[,}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{Scenario  =} \StringTok{"1. SBA{-}Mimic}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(True WBA vs. SBA)"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{blue1 =}\NormalTok{ df\_sba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1, }\AttributeTok{blue2 =}\NormalTok{ df\_sba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{pw\_2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{obs       =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{180}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{elpd\_diff =}\NormalTok{ loos\_pba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{WBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[,}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ loos\_pba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{PBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[,}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{Scenario  =} \StringTok{"2. PBA{-}Mimic}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(True WBA vs. PBA)"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{blue1 =}\NormalTok{ df\_pba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1, }\AttributeTok{blue2 =}\NormalTok{ df\_pba\_mimic}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{pw\_3 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{obs       =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{180}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{elpd\_diff =}\NormalTok{ loos\_distinct}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{WBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[,}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{] }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ loos\_distinct}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{PBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[,}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{Scenario  =} \StringTok{"3. Distinct WBA}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(True WBA vs. PBA)"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{blue1 =}\NormalTok{ df\_distinct}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1, }\AttributeTok{blue2 =}\NormalTok{ df\_distinct}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{plot\_pw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(pw\_1, pw\_2, pw\_3) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{prop\_direct =}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{/} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prop\_social =}\NormalTok{ blue2 }\SpecialCharTok{/} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{evidence\_conflict =} \FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(prop\_direct }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ prop\_social)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(plot\_pw, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ evidence\_conflict, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_smooth}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"gam"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{formula =}\NormalTok{ y }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{s}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{bs =} \StringTok{"cs"}\NormalTok{), }
              \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"firebrick"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Favors Simpler Model"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Favors True WBA"}\NormalTok{), }
    \AttributeTok{name =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Pointwise ELPD Differences: True DGP vs. Simpler Alternatives"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"When the WBA mimics simpler models (Plots 1 \& 2), the simpler models win by a constant, small margin (complexity penalty)."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Evidence Conflict |prop\_direct {-} prop\_social|"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"ELPD Difference (True WBA {-} Simpler Model)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_scenario_pointwise_elpd-1.pdf}
\end{center}

This plot provides some insight on how complexity penalties play out on
a trial-by-trial basis:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  In Scenario 1 (SBA-Mimic): The simpler model's advantage actively
  increases as evidence conflict grows, pulling the trend line
  progressively downward (favoring the SBA). When the true agent is
  perfectly balanced, the WBA's extra flexibility actively hurts its
  predictive performance the most when the evidence sources strongly
  disagree.
\item
  In Scenario 2 (PBA-Mimic): The overall trend line is flat and sits
  essentially at zero, reflecting a small, constant complexity penalty
  for the WBA across all conflict levels. However, the scatter shows
  that while the models often tie, there are several specific
  trials---especially at lower conflict---where the points dip sharply
  into the red. This indicates that the WBA's unnecessary extra
  parameter occasionally causes it to make noticeably worse,
  over-dispersed predictions compared to the tightly constrained PBA.
\item
  In Scenario 3 (Distinct WBA): Contrary to the expectation that the WBA
  would generate massive predictive advantages, the overall trend line
  remains stubbornly flat. As you noted, the WBA does win by small
  margins on a large number of trials across the board (the cluster of
  blue dots). However, these numerous small victories are completely
  offset by a handful of severe misses (the deep red dots), particularly
  at lower conflict levels. The WBA's added flexibility allows it to
  occasionally make overconfident, incorrect predictions. The heavy ELPD
  penalty from those few large misses drags the average down,
  neutralizing the WBA's expected advantage.
\end{itemize}

With this nested directionality in mind, we can now confidently
interpret the confusion matrix generated by our full model recovery
procedure.

\section{Model Recovery: Scaling Up}\label{model-recovery-scaling-up}

We now scale up our case studies to a full model recovery simulation.
Because the models are nested, we know Occam's razor will dominate: if a
dataset generated by the WBA has parameters that mimic the PBA or SBA,
the procedure \emph{should} appropriately select the simpler model.

For each of our three structural generating processes (SBA, PBA, WBA),
we simulate 50 synthetic datasets, cross-fit all three candidate models,
and use stacking weights to observe how often the procedure correctly
penalizes complexity and recovers the simplest adequate model.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_sims }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{50}
\FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =}\NormalTok{ parallel}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{detectCores}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_model\_recovery.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}

  \CommentTok{\# Generate datasets from each model type}
\NormalTok{  sim\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_sims, }\AttributeTok{true\_model =} \StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{wd =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{),}

    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_sims, }\AttributeTok{true\_model =} \StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{,}
           \AttributeTok{p =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(n\_sims, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{wd =}\NormalTok{ p, }\AttributeTok{ws =} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p),}

    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{sim\_id =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_sims, }\AttributeTok{true\_model =} \StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{,}
           \AttributeTok{rho =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(n\_sims, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
           \AttributeTok{kappa =} \FunctionTok{rlnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_sims, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{wd =}\NormalTok{ rho }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa, }\AttributeTok{ws =}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ rho) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  recovery\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sim\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{agent\_data =} \FunctionTok{pmap}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(wd, ws), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(wd, ws) \{}
        \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(wd, ws, evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_samples =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      \})}
\NormalTok{    )}

  
\NormalTok{  results\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{()}

  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(recovery\_data))) \{}

\NormalTok{    id       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_id[[i]]}
\NormalTok{    true\_mod }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{true\_model[[i]]}
\NormalTok{    df       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_data[[i]]}
\NormalTok{    true\_wd  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{wd[[i]]}
\NormalTok{    true\_ws  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ws[[i]]}

\NormalTok{    stan\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(df)}

    \CommentTok{\# Fit all three models}
\NormalTok{    fit\_s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_simple}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data,}
                               \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
                               \AttributeTok{fixed\_param =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
                               \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{save\_warmup =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
                               \AttributeTok{output\_dir =} \StringTok{"models/temp"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{    fit\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_proportional}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data,}
                                     \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
                                     \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
                                     \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{save\_warmup =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
                                     \AttributeTok{output\_dir =} \StringTok{"models/temp"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{    fit\_w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_weighted}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data,}
                                 \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
                                 \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
                                 \AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{save\_warmup =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
                                 \AttributeTok{output\_dir =} \StringTok{"models/temp"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{    loo\_s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{    loo\_p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_p}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{    loo\_w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_w}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{()}

\NormalTok{    weights }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo\_model\_weights}\NormalTok{(}
      \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SBA =}\NormalTok{ loo\_s, }\AttributeTok{PBA =}\NormalTok{ loo\_p, }\AttributeTok{WBA =}\NormalTok{ loo\_w),}
      \AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"stacking"}
\NormalTok{    )}

    \CommentTok{\# Extract posterior means of effective weights (wd, ws) from each model}
    \CommentTok{\# SBA: deterministic (1, 1)}
\NormalTok{    pba\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_p}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    wba\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_w}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{    results\_list[[i]] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{sim\_id     =}\NormalTok{ id,}
      \AttributeTok{true\_model =}\NormalTok{ true\_mod,}
      \AttributeTok{true\_wd    =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd,}
      \AttributeTok{true\_ws    =}\NormalTok{ true\_ws,}
      \CommentTok{\# Stacking weights}
      \AttributeTok{weight\_SBA =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(weights[}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{]),}
      \AttributeTok{weight\_PBA =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(weights[}\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{]),}
      \AttributeTok{weight\_WBA =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(weights[}\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{]),}
      \CommentTok{\# Posterior mean effective weights per candidate model}
      \AttributeTok{sba\_wd =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{sba\_ws =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{pba\_wd =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pba\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{p),}
      \AttributeTok{pba\_ws =} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pba\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{p),}
      \AttributeTok{wba\_wd =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(wba\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{rho }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ wba\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{kappa),}
      \AttributeTok{wba\_ws =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{((}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ wba\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{rho) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ wba\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{kappa)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(results\_list)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(recovery\_results, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_model\_recovery.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_model\_recovery.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Confusion Matrix}\label{confusion-matrix}

The probabilistic confusion matrix shows, for each true generating
model, the average stacking weight assigned to each candidate. A perfect
recovery procedure would place all weight on the diagonal.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{binary\_matrix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_results }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \CommentTok{\# 1. Reshape to make finding the maximum weight per simulation easier}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"predicted\_model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"weight"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{predicted\_model =} \FunctionTok{str\_remove}\NormalTok{(predicted\_model, }\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  
  \CommentTok{\# 2. Keep only the winning model for each simulation}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(sim\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{slice\_max}\NormalTok{(weight, }\AttributeTok{n =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{with\_ties =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  
  \CommentTok{\# 3. Count the wins and calculate proportions per true model}
  \FunctionTok{count}\NormalTok{(true\_model, predicted\_model) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(true\_model) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{proportion =}\NormalTok{ n }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(n)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  
  \CommentTok{\# 4. Ensure we have all combinations factored correctly (handles 0\% scenarios)}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{true\_model =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{predicted\_model =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(predicted\_model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{complete}\NormalTok{(true\_model, predicted\_model, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{proportion =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Optional: View it as a standard table }
\NormalTok{binary\_matrix }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{n) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ predicted\_model, }\AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ proportion) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{caption =} \StringTok{"Binary Confusion Matrix: Highest Weight Win Rate"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}lrrr@{}}
\caption{Binary Confusion Matrix: Highest Weight Win
Rate}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
true\_model & SBA & PBA & WBA \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
true\_model & SBA & PBA & WBA \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
SBA & 0.95 & 0.05 & 0.00 \\
PBA & 0.36 & 0.64 & 0.00 \\
WBA & 0.53 & 0.18 & 0.29 \\
\end{longtable}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{binary\_matrix }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ predicted\_model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ proportion)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_tile}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{percent}\NormalTok{(proportion, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
            \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{direction =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_discrete}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{limits =}\NormalTok{ rev) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \CommentTok{\# Keeps SBA at the top left}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model Recovery: Binary Classification"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Winning Candidate Model (Max Weight)"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"True Generating Model"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"Win Rate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{panel.grid =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_model_recovery_confusion-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{confusion\_matrix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_results }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(true\_model) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Recovered\_SBA =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(weight\_SBA),}
    \AttributeTok{Recovered\_PBA =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(weight\_PBA),}
    \AttributeTok{Recovered\_WBA =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(weight\_WBA),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{true\_model =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(true\_model)}

\NormalTok{knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(confusion\_matrix, }\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{caption =} \StringTok{"Probabilistic Confusion Matrix: Average Stacking Weights"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}lrrr@{}}
\caption{Probabilistic Confusion Matrix: Average Stacking
Weights}\tabularnewline

true\_model & Recovered\_SBA & Recovered\_PBA & Recovered\_WBA \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead

true\_model & Recovered\_SBA & Recovered\_PBA & Recovered\_WBA \\

PBA & 0.34 & 0.52 & 0.15 \\
WBA & 0.39 & 0.21 & 0.39 \\
