\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{confusion\_matrix }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovered"}\NormalTok{),}
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"fitted\_model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"weight"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{fitted\_model =} \FunctionTok{str\_remove}\NormalTok{(fitted\_model, }\StringTok{"Recovered\_"}\NormalTok{),}
    \CommentTok{\# FIX 1: Lock in the horizontal order by converting to a factor}
    \AttributeTok{fitted\_model =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(fitted\_model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ fitted\_model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ weight)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_tile}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{percent}\NormalTok{(weight, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
            \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{direction =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# FIX 2: Reverse the vertical axis so "SBA" is at the top left}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_discrete}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{limits =}\NormalTok{ rev) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model Recovery: Predictive Weight Allocation"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Candidate Model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"True Generating Model"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"Avg. Weight"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{panel.grid =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_model_recovery_confusion-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\subsection{Model Calibration}\label{model-calibration}

A well-calibrated model comparison procedure should assign a stacking
weight of, say, 0.7 to a model that turns out to be the true generator
roughly 70\% of the time. We assess this by binning stacking weights for
each candidate and computing the empirical frequency with which that
candidate was, in fact, the true model. Points on the diagonal indicate
perfect calibration; a curve bowed above the diagonal signals
underconfidence (the procedure ``hedges'' too much), while a curve below
signals overconfidence.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Reshape to long: one row per (simulation × candidate model)}
\NormalTok{calibration\_long }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_results }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"candidate"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"stacking\_weight"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{candidate =} \FunctionTok{str\_remove}\NormalTok{(candidate, }\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{is\_true    =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(candidate }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ true\_model)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Bin stacking weights and compute empirical frequency}
\NormalTok{n\_bins }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{10}
\NormalTok{calibration\_binned }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ calibration\_long }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{bin =} \FunctionTok{cut}\NormalTok{(stacking\_weight,}
                   \AttributeTok{breaks =} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{length.out =}\NormalTok{ n\_bins }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
                   \AttributeTok{include.lowest =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(candidate, bin) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{bin\_mid    =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(stacking\_weight),}
    \AttributeTok{observed   =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(is\_true),}
    \AttributeTok{N\_datasets =} \FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups    =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{SE    =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{((observed }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ observed)) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ N\_datasets),}
    \AttributeTok{Lower =} \FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, observed }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FloatTok{1.96} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ SE),}
    \AttributeTok{Upper =} \FunctionTok{pmin}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, observed }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{1.96} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ SE)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(calibration\_binned, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ bin\_mid, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ observed, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ candidate)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ Lower, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ Upper, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ candidate),}
              \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model Comparison Calibration"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Ribbons show 95\% CI; wider ribbons reflect fewer datasets in that weight bin."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Mean Stacking Weight (Binned)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Empirical Frequency (True Generating Model)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Candidate"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"Candidate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_model_recovery_calibration-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The line goes up, but it's deviations from the diagonal are worrying
(and resonate with the not ideal confusion matrix). We should probably
run more simulations to make sure, but now we want to move on looking
more closely to misclassifications .

\subsection{Parameter Recovery Across
Models}\label{parameter-recovery-across-models}

Even when the ``wrong'' model wins, its parameters may still capture the
effective behavior of the true generating process. To check this, we
compute a \textbf{model-averaged estimate} of the effective evidence
weights (\(w_d\), \(w_s\)) by weighting each candidate's posterior means
by its stacking weight. If a wrongly-selected model is still
behaviorally faithful, these averaged estimates should track the true
values closely.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Determine the winning model, enforce factor levels, and extract ITS specific fitted parameters}
\NormalTok{param\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ recovery\_results }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \CommentTok{\# Identify the winner based on the highest weight}
    \AttributeTok{winner =} \FunctionTok{case\_when}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      weight\_SBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ weight\_PBA }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ weight\_SBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ weight\_WBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{      weight\_PBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ weight\_SBA }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ weight\_PBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ weight\_WBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{,}
      \ConstantTok{TRUE} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{"WBA"}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \CommentTok{\# Enforce factor levels for consistent plotting order (SBA {-}\textgreater{} PBA {-}\textgreater{} WBA)}
    \AttributeTok{winner =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(winner, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{true\_model =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{correct =}\NormalTok{ winner }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ true\_model,}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Extract the actual parameters estimated by the WINNING model}
    \AttributeTok{win\_wd =} \FunctionTok{case\_when}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      winner }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"SBA"} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ sba\_wd, }\CommentTok{\# Will likely be NA/0 if SBA is 0{-}parameter}
\NormalTok{      winner }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"PBA"} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ pba\_wd,}
\NormalTok{      winner }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"WBA"} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ wba\_wd}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{win\_ws =} \FunctionTok{case\_when}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      winner }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"SBA"} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ sba\_ws,}
\NormalTok{      winner }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"PBA"} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ pba\_ws,}
\NormalTok{      winner }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"WBA"} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ wba\_ws}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# VISUALIZATION 1: Winning Model Parameter Recovery}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}

\NormalTok{p\_wd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(param\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ win\_wd)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ correct), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_smooth}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{method=}\NormalTok{lm, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ true\_model), }\AttributeTok{se =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{),}
                     \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Correct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Wrong"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovery of "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[d]),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[d]),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Winning Model "} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{hat}\NormalTok{(w)[d]),}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"True Model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \StringTok{"Recovery"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{p\_ws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(param\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_ws, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ win\_ws)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ correct), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_smooth}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{method =}\NormalTok{ lm, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ true\_model), }\AttributeTok{se =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{),}
                     \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Correct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Wrong"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovery of "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[s]),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[s]),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Winning Model "} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{hat}\NormalTok{(w)[s]),}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"True Model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \StringTok{"Recovery"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# View Combined Recovery Plot}
\NormalTok{p\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_wd }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_ws }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{plot\_layout}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{guides =} \StringTok{"collect"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\&}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{p\_recovery}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_model_recovery_parameters-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# VISUALIZATION 2: True vs. Fitted Parameter Spaces}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}

\CommentTok{\# Plot A: True Parameter Space (Where do misclassifications happen?)}
\NormalTok{p\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(param\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_ws)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray60"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ winner, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ correct), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{as\_labeller}\NormalTok{(}\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True:"}\NormalTok{, x))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{guide =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set1"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"A. Data Generating Space"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Which true parameter regions cause the wrong model to win?"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[d]),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[s]),}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Winning Model"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Plot B: Fitted Parameter Space (How do models mimic each other?)}
\NormalTok{p\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(param\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ win\_wd, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ win\_ws)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray60"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ correct), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ winner, }\AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{as\_labeller}\NormalTok{(}\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Winner:"}\NormalTok{, x))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Dark2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"B. Fitted Parameter Space"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"What parameters did the winning model actually estimate?"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Fitted "} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{hat}\NormalTok{(w)[d]),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Fitted "} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{hat}\NormalTok{(w)[s]),}
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"True Model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \StringTok{"Recovery"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# View Combined Spaces Plot}
\NormalTok{p\_true }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{param\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ param\_recovery }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{true\_rho   =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (true\_wd }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ true\_ws),}
    \AttributeTok{true\_kappa =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ true\_ws,}
    \AttributeTok{win\_rho    =}\NormalTok{ win\_wd  }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (win\_wd }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ win\_ws),}
    \AttributeTok{win\_kappa  =}\NormalTok{ win\_wd  }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ win\_ws}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{p\_rho   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(param\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(true\_rho, win\_rho)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ correct), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_equal}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{p\_kappa }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(param\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(true\_kappa, win\_kappa)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ true\_model, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ correct), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_log10}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_log10}\NormalTok{()}

\NormalTok{p\_rho }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_kappa}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# VISUALIZATION 3: Parameter Shifting (Misclassifications)}
\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}

\CommentTok{\# Filter out correct recoveries to focus purely on the mapping of misclassifications}
\NormalTok{misclassifications }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ param\_recovery }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(correct }\SpecialCharTok{==} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Create the Vector Shift Plot}
\NormalTok{p\_shift }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(misclassifications) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"gray60"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Draw arrows starting at the True params and pointing to the Fitted params}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_segment}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_ws, }\AttributeTok{xend =}\NormalTok{ win\_wd, }\AttributeTok{yend =}\NormalTok{ win\_ws, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ winner),}
    \AttributeTok{arrow =} \FunctionTok{arrow}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{length =} \FunctionTok{unit}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"cm"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{type =} \StringTok{"closed"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Add a distinct black dot at the starting point (True parameters)}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_ws), }
    \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{16}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Grid layout: Rows = True generating model, Columns = The wrong model that won}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_grid}\NormalTok{(true\_model }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ winner, }\AttributeTok{drop =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{labeller}\NormalTok{(}
               \AttributeTok{true\_model =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True:"}\NormalTok{, x),}
               \AttributeTok{winner =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Winner:"}\NormalTok{, x)}
\NormalTok{             )) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Match the colors you used previously}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#E41A1C"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#377EB8"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4DAF4A"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{drop =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Parameter Shifting During Model Misclassification"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Arrows trace from the True Parameters (black dots) to the Compensatory Fitted Parameters of the wrong model"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Weight Delay ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[d] }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Weight Social ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[s] }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_bw}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{) }

\CommentTok{\# View Shift Plot}
\NormalTok{p\_shift}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_model_recovery_parameters-5.pdf}
\end{center}

To thoroughly evaluate the reliability of our models, we conduct a
three-stage parameter recovery and identifiability analysis. This
pipeline not only tests whether we can recover the true generating
parameters, but also maps the exact conditions under which models
misclassify and how they compensate when they do. N.B. It's a work in
progress, so I'm sure there are better ways, and I will hopefully
develop better plots in the next course iterations.

\subsubsection{1. Winning Model Parameter
Recovery}\label{winning-model-parameter-recovery}

We first assess the accuracy of our parameter estimates by evaluating
the actual weights inferred by whichever model ``won'' the selection
process. The first pair of plots displays the recovery of the direct
evidence weight (\(w_d\)) and the social weight (\(w_s\)).

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{How to read it}: The x-axis represents the true data-generating
  parameter, while the y-axis represents the estimate generated by the
  winning model (\(\hat{w}\)). The dashed diagonal line indicates
  perfect recovery.
\item
  \emph{Interpretation}: Points falling tightly along the diagonal
  demonstrate robust parameter recovery. We distinguish between correct
  model recoveries (circles) and misclassifications (crosses). By
  plotting the parameters of the winning model, we can observe exactly
  what psychological weights would be reported in practice if we
  selected the best-fitting architecture for a given dataset. The dotted
  trend lines further reveal whether specific true models systematically
  under- or overestimate these weights across the parameter space.
\item
  \emph{Visual Evidence}: The plots show that correct PBA recoveries
  (orange circles) track the diagonal tightly within their bounded \(0\)
  to \(1\) constraint. However, the WBA model (blue dotted trend line)
  systematically underestimates both \(w_d\) and \(w_s\) at higher true
  values (\(> 1.5\)), demonstrating a distinct shrinkage effect.
  Additionally, the constraints of misclassification are highly visible:
  when the PBA incorrectly wins for a true WBA (blue crosses), it
  severely truncates the higher true weight estimates down to values
  \(\leq 1\) to force the \(w_d + w_s = 1\) rule.
\end{itemize}

\subsubsection{2. Mapping Model Identifiability (True vs.~Fitted
Space)}\label{mapping-model-identifiability-true-vs.-fitted-space}

Next, we investigate the geographical boundaries of model selection
accuracy. Rather than just asking if a model failed, we ask where it
failed.

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Plot A (Data Generating Space): This plot is faceted by the True
  Model. It illustrates which specific regions of the true parameter
  space cause the wrong model to win. For instance, we can observe if
  misclassifications cluster near the reference diagonal (where
  \(w_d \approx w_s\)), indicating that the models struggle to
  differentiate the underlying strategies when the weights are balanced.
  Visual Evidence: Looking at the ``True: WBA'' panel,
  misclassifications are highly systematic. The WBA generates data that
  causes the PBA to falsely win (blue crosses) almost exclusively when
  the true WBA parameters fall close to the \(w_d + w_s = 1\) line.
  Similarly, the SBA falsely wins (red crosses) when the true WBA
  parameters cluster tightly near \((1,1)\).
\item
  Plot B (Fitted Parameter Space): This plot is faceted by the Winning
  Model. It reveals the ``imitation space''---the specific parameter
  ranges a model leverages when it inappropriately wins. By comparing
  these two spaces, we can see if models are relying on extreme or
  restricted parameter values to mimic a different generating
  architecture. Visual Evidence: Plot B starkly visualizes the rigid
  architectural constraints of the simpler models. Whenever the SBA
  wins, all parameter estimates collapse precisely to the \((1, 1)\)
  coordinate. Whenever the PBA wins, all estimates are strictly forced
  onto the diagonal \(w_d + w_s = 1\) boundary line, fundamentally
  stripping away absolute scale regardless of where the true parameters
  originated.
\end{itemize}

\subsubsection{3. Structural Mimicry and Compensatory
Shifts}\label{structural-mimicry-and-compensatory-shifts}

To evaluate whether model misclassifications are behaviorally benign, we
isolate the failed recovery trials and trace exactly how the parameter
estimates shift when the wrong model is selected.

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{How to read it}: In this grid, rows represent the true
  generating model and columns represent the incorrectly selected
  ``winning'' model. Each arrow represents a single misclassified
  simulation: the black dot marks the true generating parameters, while
  the arrowhead points to the values estimated by the wrong winning
  model.
\item
  \emph{Interpretation}: We can directly observe structural mimicry. If
  the arrows are very short, or if they reliably project toward a
  specific parameter ratio, it indicates that the misidentification is
  relatively benign; the winning model achieves its higher fit by tuning
  its parameters to functionally approximate the true data-generating
  weights. Conversely, long, erratic arrows reveal regions where the
  wrong model fundamentally distorts the psychological interpretation of
  the data, pulling the estimates far from their true origins to force a
  statistical fit.
\item
  Loss of Scale, Preservation of Ratio (e.g., True WBA \(\rightarrow\)
  Winner PBA): The blue arrows project scattered true WBA parameters
  directly down or up onto the \(w_d + w_s = 1\) line. This strips away
  the total evidence scale but generally preserves the relative
  weighting ratio (the slopes), serving as a benign functional
  approximation.
\item
  Inflation of Scale (e.g., True PBA \(\rightarrow\) Winner WBA): The
  green arrows shoot outward from the PBA's natural boundary. To mimic
  the PBA, the WBA maintains the correct relative ratio (following the
  same trajectory) but unnecessarily inflates the total evidence scale.
\item
  Severe Distortion (e.g., True WBA \(\rightarrow\) Winner SBA): The red
  arrows collapse widely dispersed true WBA parameters entirely into the
  fixed \((1, 1)\) coordinate. This indicates a complete loss of both
  relative weighting and absolute scale information, severely distorting
  the psychological profile of the simulated agent.
\end{itemize}

\section{Summary: What We Learned}\label{summary-what-we-learned}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{The SBA is a baseline} with zero free parameters --- it takes
  all evidence at face value.
\item
  \textbf{The WBA with raw \((w_d, w_s)\) parameterisation has
  identifiability problems.} Even without divergences and with good
  diagnostics, parameter recovery fails because:

  \begin{itemize}
  \tightlist
  \item
    \(w_d = 2, w_s = 2\) produces the same \emph{average} decisions as
    \(w_d = 1, w_s = 1\)
  \item
    Only the \emph{ratio} matters for predicting choices; the
    \emph{scale} mainly affects variance
  \end{itemize}
\item
  \textbf{More data doesn't help} --- this is a structural
  identifiability issue, not a power issue.
\item
  \textbf{Reparameterisation solves the problem:}

  \begin{itemize}
  \tightlist
  \item
    The \textbf{PBA} (\(p\)) fixes the scale and estimates only relative
    allocation
  \item
    The \textbf{WBA} (\(\rho\), \(\kappa\)) separates relative weight
    from total scale
  \end{itemize}
\item
  \textbf{Always run parameter recovery checks} before trusting your
  model, even when standard diagnostics look clean.
\end{enumerate}

\section{Multilevel Extension}\label{multilevel-extension}

In real experiments, we observe many participants, each with their own
weighting tendencies. Multilevel models capture these individual
differences while sharing statistical strength across the population.
This is the same partial-pooling logic developed in Chapter 7.

A single-agent model answers: \emph{how does this person weight direct
vs.~social evidence?} A multilevel model answers the population-level
question: \emph{how do people in general weight evidence, and how much
do they vary?} The hierarchical structure also regularises extreme
individual estimates via partial pooling --- agents with fewer
informative trials are pulled toward the population mean, exactly as we
saw in Chapter 7's matching-pennies analysis.

\section{Simulating a Multi-Agent
Population}\label{simulating-a-multi-agent-population}

We simulate 20 agents, each with their own evidence-weighting tendency,
drawn from a shared population distribution. The population is
parameterised on transformed scales: \(\text{logit}(\rho)\) and
\(\log(\kappa)\) follow a bivariate normal. This ensures
\(\rho \in (0, 1)\) and \(\kappa > 0\) on the natural scale, preserves
the identifiable reparameterisation from the single-agent section, and
allows correlation between relative allocation and total scaling.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_agents          }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{20}
\NormalTok{n\_trials\_per\_comb }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{2}   \CommentTok{\# repetitions of each evidence combination per agent}

\CommentTok{\# Population parameters (on transformed scales)}
\NormalTok{pop\_logit\_rho\_mean }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.0}    \CommentTok{\# logit(0.5) → balanced on average}
\NormalTok{pop\_logit\_rho\_sd   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.6}    \CommentTok{\# moderate individual variation in allocation}
\NormalTok{pop\_log\_kappa\_mean }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\CommentTok{\# kappa ≈ 2 on average (SBA{-}like total scaling)}
\NormalTok{pop\_log\_kappa\_sd   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.3}    \CommentTok{\# moderate variation in total evidence use}
\NormalTok{pop\_correlation    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.3}   \CommentTok{\# mild tradeoff: high rho → slightly lower kappa}

\CommentTok{\# Construct the bivariate{-}normal covariance matrix}
\NormalTok{Sigma\_pop }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  pop\_logit\_rho\_sd}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{  pop\_correlation }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ pop\_logit\_rho\_sd }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ pop\_log\_kappa\_sd,}
\NormalTok{  pop\_correlation }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ pop\_logit\_rho\_sd }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ pop\_log\_kappa\_sd,}
\NormalTok{  pop\_log\_kappa\_sd}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nrow =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Draw individual parameters from the population}
\NormalTok{raw\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ MASS}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mvrnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_agents, }
                           \AttributeTok{mu    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(pop\_logit\_rho\_mean, pop\_log\_kappa\_mean),}
                           \AttributeTok{Sigma =}\NormalTok{ Sigma\_pop)}

\NormalTok{agent\_params\_ml }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{agent\_id    =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_agents),}
  \AttributeTok{logit\_rho   =}\NormalTok{ raw\_draws[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{log\_kappa   =}\NormalTok{ raw\_draws[, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{rho         =} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(logit\_rho),}
  \AttributeTok{kappa       =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(log\_kappa),}
  \AttributeTok{weight\_direct =}\NormalTok{ rho }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa,}
  \AttributeTok{weight\_social =}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ rho) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate trial{-}level data for all agents}
\NormalTok{ml\_sim\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ agent\_params\_ml }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, logit\_rho, log\_kappa, rho, kappa,}
\NormalTok{                    weight\_direct, weight\_social) \{}
    \FunctionTok{generate\_agent\_decisions}\NormalTok{(weight\_direct, weight\_social,}
\NormalTok{                             evidence\_combinations,}
                             \AttributeTok{n\_samples =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials\_per\_comb) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{agent\_id  =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_rho  =}\NormalTok{ rho,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_kappa =}\NormalTok{ kappa,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_wd   =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_ws   =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{  \})}

\NormalTok{stan\_data\_ml }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{N          =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ml\_sim\_data),}
  \AttributeTok{J          =}\NormalTok{ n\_agents,}
  \AttributeTok{agent\_id   =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id,}
  \AttributeTok{choice     =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice,}
  \AttributeTok{N\_total    =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ml\_sim\_data),}
  \AttributeTok{N\_subjects =}\NormalTok{ n\_agents,}
  \AttributeTok{subj\_id    =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id,}
  \AttributeTok{y          =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{choice,}
  \AttributeTok{blue1      =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1,}
  \AttributeTok{total1     =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total1,}
  \AttributeTok{blue2      =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2,}
  \AttributeTok{total2     =}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{total2,}
  
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_p\_mu             =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_p\_sigma          =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_sigma\_p\_lambda      =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_logit\_p\_mu       =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_logit\_p\_sigma    =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_sigma\_logit\_p\_lambda =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_logit\_rho\_mu     =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_logit\_rho\_sigma  =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_log\_kappa\_mu     =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_mu\_log\_kappa\_sigma  =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_sigma\_lambda        =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_Omega\_shape         =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics           =} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Agents: \%d | Trials per agent: \%d | Total observations: \%d}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{            n\_agents, }\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(evidence\_combinations) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ n\_trials\_per\_comb, stan\_data\_ml}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N\_total))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Agents: 20 | Trials per agent: 72 | Total observations: 1440
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Calculate the true population means on the natural scale for our reference lines}
\NormalTok{pop\_rho\_true   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(pop\_logit\_rho\_mean)}
\NormalTok{pop\_kappa\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(pop\_log\_kappa\_mean)}
\NormalTok{pop\_wd\_mean    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pop\_rho\_true }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ pop\_kappa\_true}
\NormalTok{pop\_ws\_mean    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ pop\_rho\_true) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ pop\_kappa\_true}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(agent\_params\_ml, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight\_social)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add dashed lines for the population means}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ pop\_wd\_mean, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"blue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =}\NormalTok{ pop\_ws\_mean, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"blue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Add the individual agents}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id), }\AttributeTok{nudge\_x =} \FloatTok{0.04}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{nudge\_y =} \FloatTok{0.02}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Distribution of Simulated Agent Parameters"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Each point represents one agent; dashed lines show population means"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Weight for Direct Evidence ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[d] }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Weight for Social Evidence ("} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[s] }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{14}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{axis.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_multilevel_sim_setup-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Each agent sees \(36 \times 2 = 72\) trials, giving us
\(20 \times 72 = 1440\) total observations --- a realistic experimental
dataset size.

\subsection{Multilevel Simple Bayesian Agent
(Stan)}\label{multilevel-simple-bayesian-agent-stan}

The ML-SBA has zero free parameters per agent. It applies strict
Bayesian integration identically across all agents. This is the null
model: individual differences in evidence weighting do not exist.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}usetikzlibrary}\NormalTok{\{bayesnet\}}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}begin}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
  \CommentTok{\% Constants}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const] (alpha0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[const, right=1cm of alpha0] (beta0) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_0$}\NormalTok{\};}
  
  \CommentTok{\% Trial level}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below left=1.5cm and 0.5cm of alpha0] (blue1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.3cm of blue1] (total1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below right=1.5cm and 0.5cm of beta0] (blue2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=0.3cm of blue2] (total2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2.5cm of alpha0] (alphapost) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=2.5cm of beta0] (betapost) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1cm of alphapost, xshift=0.75cm] (choice) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$y\_i$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Edges}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alpha0\} \{alphapost\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{beta0\} \{betapost\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{blue1, total1, blue2, total2\} \{alphapost, betapost\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{alphapost, betapost\} \{choice\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Plates}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{trial\} \{(blue1)(total1)(blue2)(total2)(alphapost)(betapost)(choice)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$N$}\NormalTok{ trials\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{agent\} \{(trial)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$J$}\NormalTok{ agents\};}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}end}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.55\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ml-sba-dag-1.pdf}

}

\caption{DAG for the Multilevel Simple Bayesian Agent}

\end{figure}%

The generative relationships for the ML-SBA are identical across agents:
\[
\begin{aligned}
\alpha_{post,ij} &= \alpha_0 + k_{1,ij} + k_{2,ij} \\
\beta_{post,ij}  &= \beta_0 + (n_{1,ij} - k_{1,ij}) + (n_{2,ij} - k_{2,ij}) \\
y_{ij} &\sim \text{Beta-Binomial}(1, \alpha_{post,ij}, \beta_{post,ij})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{MultilevelSBA\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Multilevel Simple Bayesian Agent}
\StringTok{// Zero free parameters per agent. Strict integration applied uniformly.}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_subjects;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_subjects\textgreater{} subj\_id;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}  y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            total2;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed data \{}
\StringTok{  real alpha0 = 0.5;}
\StringTok{  real beta0  = 0.5;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] alpha\_post = alpha0 + to\_vector(blue1) + to\_vector(blue2);}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] beta\_post  = beta0  + (to\_vector(total1) {-} to\_vector(blue1)) +}
\StringTok{                                        (to\_vector(total2) {-} to\_vector(blue2));}
\StringTok{  target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      real alpha\_post = alpha0 + blue1[i] + blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real beta\_post  = beta0 + (total1[i] {-} blue1[i]) + (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(MultilevelSBA\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_multilevel\_sba.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_multilevel_sba.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_ml\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_multilevel\_sba.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Multilevel Proportional Bayesian Agent
(Stan)}\label{multilevel-proportional-bayesian-agent-stan}

The ML-PBA estimates a single parameter per agent --- the evidence
allocation \(p_j\) --- with a population distribution on the logit
scale. This is the intermediate-complexity model: it captures individual
differences in \emph{relative} weighting but not in total evidence
scaling.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{MultilevelPBA\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Multilevel Proportional Bayesian Agent}
\StringTok{// Population distribution on logit(p).}
\StringTok{// Non{-}centred parameterisation for individual p\_j.}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_subjects;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_subjects\textgreater{} subj\_id;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}  y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            total2;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  real prior\_mu\_logit\_p\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_mu\_logit\_p\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_sigma\_logit\_p\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real mu\_logit\_p;             // population mean on logit scale}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} sigma\_logit\_p; // population SD on logit scale}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] z\_p;      // NCP deviates}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] p;}
\StringTok{  for (j in 1:N\_subjects)}
\StringTok{    p[j] = inv\_logit(mu\_logit\_p + sigma\_logit\_p * z\_p[j]);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(mu\_logit\_p    | prior\_mu\_logit\_p\_mu, prior\_mu\_logit\_p\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += exponential\_lpdf(sigma\_logit\_p | prior\_sigma\_logit\_p\_lambda);}
\StringTok{  target += std\_normal\_lpdf(z\_p);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{    int j = subj\_id[i];}
\StringTok{    real alpha\_post = 0.5 + p[j] * blue1[i] + (1.0 {-} p[j]) * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{    real beta\_post  = 0.5 + p[j] * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i])}
\StringTok{                          + (1.0 {-} p[j]) * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}
\StringTok{    target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  real pop\_p = inv\_logit(mu\_logit\_p);}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      int j = subj\_id[i];}
\StringTok{      real alpha\_post = 0.5 + p[j] * blue1[i] + (1.0 {-} p[j]) * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real beta\_post  = 0.5 + p[j] * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i])}
\StringTok{                            + (1.0 {-} p[j]) * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(MultilevelPBA\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_multilevel\_pba.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_multilevel_pba.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_ml\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_multilevel\_pba.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Multilevel Weighted Bayesian Agent
(Stan)}\label{multilevel-weighted-bayesian-agent-stan}

The multilevel WBA implements the reparameterised \((\rho_j, \kappa_j)\)
parameterisation for each agent, with a bivariate population
distribution on the \((\text{logit}(\rho), \log(\kappa))\) scale. This
preserves the identifiability gains from the single-agent analysis while
allowing partial pooling.

The key design choices mirror Chapter 7:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{Non-centred parameterisation (NCP):} Individual deviations are
  expressed as \(z\)-scores to avoid the funnel geometry.
\item
  \textbf{Transformed-scale parameterisation:} We work on
  \((\text{logit}(\rho), \log(\kappa))\) to ensure correct support
  without boundary constraints.
\item
  \textbf{LKJ prior on the correlation:} With \(\eta = 2\), the prior is
  unimodal at zero and weakly regularises against extreme correlations.
  A negative correlation would mean agents who allocate more to direct
  evidence tend to use less total evidence.
\end{itemize}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}usetikzlibrary}\NormalTok{\{bayesnet\}}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}begin}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
  \CommentTok{\% Population parameters}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent] (mu) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}mu}\SpecialStringTok{$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, right=1.5cm of mu] (sigma) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}sigma}\SpecialStringTok{$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, right=1.5cm of sigma] (Omega) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}Omega}\SpecialStringTok{$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Individual parameters}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[latent, below=1.5cm of sigma] (indiv) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}rho}\SpecialStringTok{\_j, }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}kappa}\SpecialStringTok{\_j$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=1cm of indiv, xshift={-}1.5cm] (wd) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$w\_\{d,j\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=1cm of indiv, xshift=1.5cm] (ws) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$w\_\{s,j\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Trial level inputs}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1.5cm of wd, xshift={-}1.5cm] (k1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, right=0.3cm of k1] (n1) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{1,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1.5cm of ws, xshift=1.5cm] (k2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$k\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, left=0.3cm of k2] (n2) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$n\_\{2,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Posterior parameters (shifted further down to prevent overlap)}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=3cm of wd] (ap) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}alpha}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[det, below=3cm of ws] (bp) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}beta}\SpecialStringTok{\_\{post,i\}$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Output}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}node}\NormalTok{[obs, below=1.5cm of ap, xshift=1.5cm] (y) \{}\SpecialStringTok{$y\_i$}\NormalTok{\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Edges}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{mu, sigma, Omega\} \{indiv\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{indiv\} \{wd, ws\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{wd, k1, n1, ws, k2, n2\} \{ap, bp\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}edge}\NormalTok{ \{ap, bp\} \{y\};}

  \CommentTok{\% Plates (Nested)}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{trial\} \{(k1)(n1)(k2)(n2)(ap)(bp)(y)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$N$}\NormalTok{ trials\};}
  \FunctionTok{\textbackslash{}plate}\NormalTok{ \{agent\} \{(indiv)(wd)(ws)(trial)\} \{}\SpecialStringTok{$J$}\NormalTok{ agents\};}
\KeywordTok{\textbackslash{}end}\NormalTok{\{}\ExtensionTok{tikzpicture}\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{figure}[H]

{\centering \includegraphics[width=0.65\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ml-wba-dag-1.pdf}

}

\caption{DAG for the Multilevel Weighted Bayesian Agent}

\end{figure}%

The generative relationships for the ML-WBA are: \[
\begin{aligned}
\begin{pmatrix} \text{logit}(\rho_j) \\ \log(\kappa_j) \end{pmatrix} &\sim \text{MVN}(\boldsymbol{\mu}, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}) \\
w_{d,j} &= \rho_j \cdot \kappa_j \\
w_{s,j} &= (1 - \rho_j) \cdot \kappa_j \\
\alpha_{post,ij} &= \alpha_0 + w_{d,j} k_{1,ij} + w_{s,j} k_{2,ij} \\
\beta_{post,ij}  &= \beta_0 + w_{d,j}(n_{1,ij} - k_{1,ij}) + w_{s,j}(n_{2,ij} - k_{2,ij}) \\
y_{ij} &\sim \text{Beta-Binomial}(1, \alpha_{post,ij}, \beta_{post,ij})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{MultilevelWBA\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Multilevel Weighted Bayesian Agent (reparameterised)}
\StringTok{// Population distribution on (logit(rho), log(kappa)).}
\StringTok{// Non{-}centred parameterisation with correlated random effects.}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;                                // total observations}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_subjects;                             // number of agents}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_subjects\textgreater{} subj\_id;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}  y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            blue1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            total1;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            blue2;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}            total2;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  real prior\_mu\_logit\_rho\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_mu\_logit\_rho\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_mu\_log\_kappa\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_mu\_log\_kappa\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_sigma\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_Omega\_shape;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[2] mu;                              // population means: (logit\_rho, log\_kappa)}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}[2] sigma;                  // population SDs}
\StringTok{  cholesky\_factor\_corr[2] L\_Omega;           // Cholesky factor of correlation matrix}
\StringTok{  matrix[2, N\_subjects] z;                   // standard normal deviates (NCP)}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  // Reconstruct individual parameters on natural scale}
\StringTok{  matrix[2, N\_subjects] theta\_raw = diag\_pre\_multiply(sigma, L\_Omega) * z;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] rho;}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] kappa;}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] weight\_direct;}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] weight\_social;}

\StringTok{  for (j in 1:N\_subjects) \{}
\StringTok{    rho[j]           = inv\_logit(mu[1] + theta\_raw[1, j]);}
\StringTok{    kappa[j]         = exp(mu[2] + theta\_raw[2, j]);}
\StringTok{    weight\_direct[j] = rho[j] * kappa[j];}
\StringTok{    weight\_social[j] = (1.0 {-} rho[j]) * kappa[j];}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Population priors}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(mu[1] | prior\_mu\_logit\_rho\_mu, prior\_mu\_logit\_rho\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(mu[2] | prior\_mu\_log\_kappa\_mu, prior\_mu\_log\_kappa\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += exponential\_lpdf(sigma | prior\_sigma\_lambda);}
\StringTok{  target += lkj\_corr\_cholesky\_lpdf(L\_Omega | prior\_Omega\_shape);}
\StringTok{  target += std\_normal\_lpdf(to\_vector(z));}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  // Likelihood (vectorised per trial)}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{    int j = subj\_id[i];}
\StringTok{    real alpha\_post = 0.5}
\StringTok{                    + weight\_direct[j] * blue1[i]}
\StringTok{                    + weight\_social[j] * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{    real beta\_post  = 0.5}
\StringTok{                    + weight\_direct[j] * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i])}
\StringTok{                    + weight\_social[j] * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}
\StringTok{    target += beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, alpha\_post, beta\_post);}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  // Population summaries on natural scale}
\StringTok{  real pop\_rho   = inv\_logit(mu[1]);}
\StringTok{  real pop\_kappa = exp(mu[2]);}
\StringTok{  matrix[2, 2] Omega = multiply\_lower\_tri\_self\_transpose(L\_Omega);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int y\_rep;}

\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      int j = subj\_id[i];}
\StringTok{      real a = 0.5 + weight\_direct[j] * blue1[i] + weight\_social[j] * blue2[i];}
\StringTok{      real b = 0.5 + weight\_direct[j] * (total1[i] {-} blue1[i]) }
\StringTok{                   + weight\_social[j] * (total2[i] {-} blue2[i]);}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = beta\_binomial\_lpmf(y[i] | 1, a, b);}
\StringTok{      y\_rep[i]   = beta\_binomial\_rng(1, a, b);}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(MultilevelWBA\_stan,}
                \AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch10\_multilevel\_wba.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch10_multilevel_wba.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_ml\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan/ch10\_multilevel\_wba.stan"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Fitting the Multilevel
Models}\label{fitting-the-multilevel-models}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{fit\_ml\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_sba.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_ml,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_sba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_sba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\NormalTok{fit\_ml\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_pba.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_ml,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_pba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_pba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{fit\_ml\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_wba.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data\_ml,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_wba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_ml\_wba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
