\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{bayesplot}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mcmc\_trace}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  fit\_ml\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"mu[2]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma[2]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Omega[1,2]"}\NormalTok{)),}
  \AttributeTok{facet\_args =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Trace plots: ML{-}WBA — population parameters + correlation"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_ml_trace_plots-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The trace plots should show the ``hairy caterpillar'' pattern for all
WBA population-level parameters (remember SBA has no parameter). Pay
particular attention to \(\sigma\): if either component pushes toward
zero, the NCP may struggle (the reverse funnel problem from Chapter 7).
If the SDs are well-identified away from zero, NCP is the right choice.

\subsection{Reporting the Estimated
Correlation}\label{reporting-the-estimated-correlation}

The posterior for \texttt{Omega{[}1,2{]}} --- the correlation between
\(\text{logit}(\rho)\) and \(\log(\kappa)\) across individuals ---
answers the cognitive question: \emph{do agents who allocate more
attention to direct evidence also use more or less total evidence?}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{draws\_ml }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_ml\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{())}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(draws\_ml, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Omega[1,2]}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{60}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#756bb1"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ pop\_correlation, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"\#d73027"}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Posterior: Correlation between logit(ρ) and log(κ)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Red dashed = true generating value (\%.1f). Dotted = independence."}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{                       pop\_correlation),}
    \AttributeTok{x        =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(Omega[}\StringTok{"ρ, κ"}\NormalTok{]),}
    \AttributeTok{y        =} \StringTok{"Posterior count"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_correlation_posterior-1.pdf}
\end{center}

A negative correlation would suggest a tradeoff: agents who rely more on
direct evidence (high \(\rho\)) tend to scale total evidence less (low
\(\kappa\)). A correlation near zero indicates independence. The
posterior uncertainty around this estimate is moving its mass towards
the true value, but such a wide posterior means the experiment lacked
power to resolve the correlation structure.

\subsection{Population-Level Prior and Posterior Predictive
Checks}\label{population-level-prior-and-posterior-predictive-checks}

Just as we did for the single-agent models, we want to visualize what
our multilevel models believe before seeing the data, and what they
learned after.

Since we will have to run our slower multilevel code through e.g.SBC, we
kept the generated quantities leaner than usual: we didn't include
population-level prior or posterior predictions in the generated
quantities blocks (though we kept trial-level posterior predictions for
existing agents via pred\_choice). Instead, we can simulate these
hierarchical, population-level predictions dynamically in R. This is an
essential skill: by drawing from our population hyper-priors (\(\mu\)
and \(\sigma\)), sampling new individual agents, and pushing those
through the Beta-Binomial observation model, we can perfectly
reconstruct the model's population-level expectations.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 1. Custom function to simulate Multilevel Prior/Posterior Predictions in R}
\NormalTok{generate\_ml\_ppc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(model\_name, fit, data\_df, }\AttributeTok{is\_prior =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{n\_draws =} \DecValTok{400}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Prep data}
\NormalTok{  obs\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ data\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{obs\_id =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(obs\_id, agent\_id, blue1, total1, blue2, total2, choice)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Create base grid of draws x observations}
\NormalTok{  base\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{.draw =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{n\_draws, }\AttributeTok{obs\_id =}\NormalTok{ obs\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(obs\_df, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"obs\_id"}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (model\_name }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"ML{-}SBA"}\NormalTok{) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# SBA has no parameters, prior and posterior are identical}
\NormalTok{    pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{alpha\_p =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
        \AttributeTok{beta\_p  =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
        \AttributeTok{theta   =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), alpha\_p, beta\_p),}
        \AttributeTok{pred    =} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}
\NormalTok{      )}
      
\NormalTok{  \} }\ControlFlowTok{else} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (model\_name }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"ML{-}PBA"}\NormalTok{) \{}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (is\_prior) \{}
\NormalTok{      pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mu\_p =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sig\_p =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw, agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_j =} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_p }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ sig\_p }\SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{alpha\_p =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_j) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
          \AttributeTok{beta\_p  =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_j) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
          \AttributeTok{theta   =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), alpha\_p, beta\_p),}
          \AttributeTok{pred    =} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{    \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{      draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"p"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
        \FunctionTok{sample\_n}\NormalTok{(n\_draws, }\AttributeTok{replace =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{.draw =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(.draw, }\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"p["}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
        \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.draw, }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"p\_j"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id =}\NormalTok{ readr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{parse\_number}\NormalTok{(param)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{param)}
        
\NormalTok{      pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(draws, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{".draw"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"agent\_id"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{alpha\_p =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_j) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
          \AttributeTok{beta\_p  =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_j) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
          \AttributeTok{theta   =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), alpha\_p, beta\_p),}
          \AttributeTok{pred    =} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{    \}}
    
\NormalTok{  \} }\ControlFlowTok{else} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (model\_name }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"ML{-}WBA"}\NormalTok{) \{}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (is\_prior) \{}
\NormalTok{      pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{mu\_1 =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{mu\_2 =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
          \AttributeTok{sig\_1 =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sig\_2 =} \FunctionTok{rexp}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
          \AttributeTok{omega =} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{L21 =}\NormalTok{ omega, }\AttributeTok{L22 =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ omega}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{        ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw, agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{z1 =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{z2 =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
          \AttributeTok{rho\_j =} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(mu\_1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ sig\_1 }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ z1),}
          \AttributeTok{kappa\_j =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(mu\_2 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ sig\_2 }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (L21 }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ z1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ L22 }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ z2)),}
          \AttributeTok{wd\_j =}\NormalTok{ rho\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa\_j, }\AttributeTok{ws\_j =}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ rho\_j) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa\_j}
\NormalTok{        ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{alpha\_p =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ wd\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ws\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
          \AttributeTok{beta\_p  =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ wd\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ws\_j }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
          \AttributeTok{theta   =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), alpha\_p, beta\_p),}
          \AttributeTok{pred    =} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{    \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{      draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{sample\_n}\NormalTok{(n\_draws, }\AttributeTok{replace =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{.draw =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(.draw, }\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.draw, }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"val"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{agent\_id =}\NormalTok{ readr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{parse\_number}\NormalTok{(param), }
          \AttributeTok{param\_type =} \FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"weight\_[a{-}z]+"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{        ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{param) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ param\_type, }\AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ val)}
        
\NormalTok{      pred\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_grid }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(draws, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{".draw"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"agent\_id"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
        \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{alpha\_p =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ weight\_social }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ blue2,}
          \AttributeTok{beta\_p  =} \FloatTok{0.5} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ weight\_direct }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ weight\_social }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (total2 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2),}
          \AttributeTok{theta   =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), alpha\_p, beta\_p),}
          \AttributeTok{pred    =} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, theta)}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Summarize (Averaging over all agents to get population{-}level PPC)}
\NormalTok{  obs\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_obs =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(choice), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  pred\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pred\_samples }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(.draw, blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{p\_draw =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(pred), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(blue1, blue2) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{p\_pred =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(p\_draw), }
      \AttributeTok{lower =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(p\_draw, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{), }
      \AttributeTok{upper =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(p\_draw, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{), }
      \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(obs\_summary, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"blue1"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"blue2"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{social\_evidence =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(blue2, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \DecValTok{0}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Clear Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Red"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Maybe Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Clear Blue"}\NormalTok{)),}
      \AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(is\_prior, }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(model\_name, }\StringTok{"{-} Prior"}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(model\_name, }\StringTok{"{-} Posterior"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(pred\_summary)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 2. Wrapper to plot them side{-}by{-}side}
\NormalTok{build\_ml\_ppc\_row }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(model\_name, fit) \{}
\NormalTok{  prior\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_ml\_ppc}\NormalTok{(model\_name, fit, ml\_sim\_data, }\AttributeTok{is\_prior =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  p\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(prior\_data, }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(model\_name, }\StringTok{"Prior"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"Population Prior Predictive Check"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
    \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  post\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_ml\_ppc}\NormalTok{(model\_name, fit, ml\_sim\_data, }\AttributeTok{is\_prior =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  p\_post }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plot\_predictive\_check}\NormalTok{(post\_data, }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(model\_name, }\StringTok{"Posterior"}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"Population Posterior Predictive Check"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
    \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{axis.title.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}
    
  \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(p\_prior }\SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ p\_post)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# 3. Generate and combine}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Generating Multilevel Predictive Checks (this takes a few seconds)...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Generating Multilevel Predictive Checks (this takes a few seconds)...
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{p\_ml\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_ml\_ppc\_row}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ML{-}SBA"}\NormalTok{, fit\_ml\_sba)}
\NormalTok{p\_ml\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_ml\_ppc\_row}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ML{-}PBA"}\NormalTok{, fit\_ml\_pba)}
\NormalTok{p\_ml\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_ml\_ppc\_row}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ML{-}WBA"}\NormalTok{, fit\_ml\_wba)}

\NormalTok{(p\_ml\_sba }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_ml\_pba }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_ml\_wba) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{plot\_layout}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{guides =} \StringTok{"collect"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\&} 
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_multilevel_ppc-1.pdf}
\end{center}

It might look as if the models haven't updated their predictions after
seeing the data. That's indeed true for SBA. However, PBA and WBA have.
The reason for the changes being so minimal is that the simulated
parameters are indeed close to SBA (a balanced population level
behavior). So we need to rather look at individual agents. We could run
predictive checks at the agent level, but for now we move to parameter
recovery to see how well the models estimated the true parameters for
each agent, and whether the multilevel WBA shows appropriate shrinkage
toward the population mean.

\section{Parameter Recovery: Single-shot Multilevel
Model}\label{parameter-recovery-single-shot-multilevel-model}

Parameter recovery at the multilevel level must check two things: (1)
population-level parameters are recovered, and (2) individual-level
parameters are recovered and show appropriate partial pooling ---
extreme agents are pulled toward the population mean.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# 0. Obtain UNPOOLED estimates via fast optimization (MAP) — used here and in the next chunk}
\NormalTok{unpooled\_ests }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(ml\_sim\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(id) \{}
\NormalTok{  agent\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_sim\_data }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(agent\_id }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ id)}
\NormalTok{  agent\_stan\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prepare\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(agent\_df)}

\NormalTok{  opt }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_weighted}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{optimize}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ agent\_stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}

  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_id       =}\NormalTok{ id,}
    \AttributeTok{unpooled\_rho   =}\NormalTok{ opt}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{mle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{unpooled\_kappa =}\NormalTok{ opt}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{mle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"kappa"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{unpooled\_wd    =}\NormalTok{ opt}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{mle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{unpooled\_ws    =}\NormalTok{ opt}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{mle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_social"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}
