\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Build summary table}
\NormalTok{extract\_ml\_loo }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(res, name) \{}
\NormalTok{  comp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{(res}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{comparison, }\AttributeTok{rownames =} \StringTok{"Model"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Model, elpd\_diff, se\_diff)}
\NormalTok{  w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model =} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(res}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weights),}
              \AttributeTok{weight =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(res}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weights))}
\NormalTok{  comp }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(w, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"Model"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Scenario =}\NormalTok{ name)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{ml\_scenario\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_ml\_loo}\NormalTok{(res\_scen1, }\StringTok{"1. SBA{-}Mimic"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_ml\_loo}\NormalTok{(res\_scen2, }\StringTok{"2. PBA Population"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{extract\_ml\_loo}\NormalTok{(res\_scen3, }\StringTok{"3. Full WBA"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Model    =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{Scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Scenario)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# ELPD differences}
\NormalTok{p\_ml\_elpd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenario\_summary,}
                     \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff, }\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ Model)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_pointrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ se\_diff,}
                       \AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ se\_diff),}
                  \AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Multilevel ELPD Differences"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"ELPD Difference"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Stacking weights}
\NormalTok{p\_ml\_weight }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenario\_summary,}
                       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ weight, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ Model)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_col}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{percent}\NormalTok{(weight, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
            \AttributeTok{vjust =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Multilevel Stacking Weights"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Stacking Weight"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}

\NormalTok{p\_ml\_elpd }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_ml\_weight }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{plot\_annotation}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Multilevel Model Recovery: Scenario Case Studies"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{theme =} \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_ml_scenario_comparison-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The expected pattern mirrors the single-agent case. In Scenario 1, the
SBA-mimic population has no individual variation in weights, so the
ML-SBA (zero parameters) should win or tie with the ML-PBA and ML-WBA
--- complexity is penalised. In Scenario 2, the PBA population requires
individual variation in \(\rho\) but not in \(\kappa\), so the ML-PBA
should win. In Scenario 3, the full WBA population needs both \(\rho\)
and \(\kappa\) variation, and the ML-WBA should dominate.

\subsection{LOO Pointwise Diagnostics Across
Scenarios}\label{loo-pointwise-diagnostics-across-scenarios}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{make\_ml\_pw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(loos, stan\_data, scenario\_name, }\AttributeTok{ref\_model =} \StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  elpd\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loos}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{WBA}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[, }\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{  elpd\_ref }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loos[[ref\_model]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[, }\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{]}
  
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{obs       =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N),}
    \AttributeTok{blue1     =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue1,}
    \AttributeTok{blue2     =}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{blue2,}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_diff =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_wba }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ elpd\_ref,}
    \AttributeTok{Scenario  =}\NormalTok{ scenario\_name}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{evidence\_conflict =} \FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(blue1 }\SpecialCharTok{/} \DecValTok{8} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ blue2 }\SpecialCharTok{/} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{ml\_pw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{make\_ml\_pw}\NormalTok{(res\_scen1}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{loos, scen1\_data,}
             \StringTok{"1. SBA{-}Mimic}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(ML{-}WBA vs ML{-}SBA)"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{make\_ml\_pw}\NormalTok{(res\_scen2}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{loos, scen2\_data,}
             \StringTok{"2. PBA Population}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(ML{-}WBA vs ML{-}PBA)"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{make\_ml\_pw}\NormalTok{(res\_scen3}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{loos, scen3\_data,}
             \StringTok{"3. Full WBA}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(ML{-}WBA vs ML{-}PBA)"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ml\_pw, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ evidence\_conflict, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_smooth}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"gam"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{formula =}\NormalTok{ y }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{s}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{bs =} \StringTok{"cs"}\NormalTok{),}
              \AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"midnightblue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ Scenario) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"firebrick"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"steelblue"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Favours simpler model"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Favours ML{-}WBA"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{name   =} \ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Multilevel Pointwise ELPD: Where Does Complexity Help?"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"In mimicking scenarios (left), the WBA loses by a constant complexity penalty.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{In the full WBA population (right), advantage emerges on high{-}conflict trials."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Evidence Conflict |prop\_direct − prop\_social|"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"ELPD Difference"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_ml_scenario_pointwise-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\subsection{Full Model Recovery
Simulation}\label{full-model-recovery-simulation}

We now scale up to a full model recovery simulation at the multilevel
level. For each of the three generating models (ML-SBA, ML-PBA, ML-WBA),
we generate multiple synthetic populations, cross-fit all three
candidates, and examine the confusion matrix and calibration.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_ml\_sims }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{30}  \CommentTok{\# Per generating model}
\NormalTok{J\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{12}  \CommentTok{\# Agents per simulated population}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_ml\_model\_recovery.rds"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
  
\NormalTok{  ml\_recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{  counter }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}
  
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (true\_model }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
    \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (sim\_id }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_ml\_sims)) \{}
\NormalTok{      counter }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ counter }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}
      
      \CommentTok{\# Generate population parameters}
      \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (true\_model }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{        rhos   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, J\_recovery)}
\NormalTok{        kappas }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, J\_recovery)}
\NormalTok{      \} }\ControlFlowTok{else} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (true\_model }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{        rhos   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(J\_recovery, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{        kappas }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{, J\_recovery)}
\NormalTok{      \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{        draws\_raw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ MASS}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mvrnorm}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{          J\_recovery,}
          \AttributeTok{mu    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)),}
          \AttributeTok{Sigma =} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{        rhos   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(draws\_raw[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{        kappas }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(draws\_raw[, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{      \}}
      
\NormalTok{      sim\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_ml\_data}\NormalTok{(rhos, kappas, evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_reps =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
      
      \CommentTok{\# Fit all three models (reduced iterations for speed)}
\NormalTok{      f\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ sim\_stan, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{      f\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ sim\_stan, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{      f\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ sim\_stan, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show\_messages =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{      )}
      
      \CommentTok{\# Compute LOO and stacking weights}
\NormalTok{      l\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(f\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      l\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(f\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      l\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(f\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}
      
\NormalTok{      w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
        \FunctionTok{loo\_model\_weights}\NormalTok{(}
          \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SBA =}\NormalTok{ l\_sba, }\AttributeTok{PBA =}\NormalTok{ l\_pba, }\AttributeTok{WBA =}\NormalTok{ l\_wba),}
          \AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"stacking"}
\NormalTok{        ),}
        \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SBA =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{/}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{PBA =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{/}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{WBA =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{/}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      )}
      
\NormalTok{      ml\_recovery\_results[[counter]] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{sim\_id     =}\NormalTok{ sim\_id,}
        \AttributeTok{true\_model =}\NormalTok{ true\_model,}
        \AttributeTok{weight\_SBA =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(w[}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{]),}
        \AttributeTok{weight\_PBA =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(w[}\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{]),}
        \AttributeTok{weight\_WBA =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(w[}\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{]),}
        \AttributeTok{mean\_true\_rho   =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(rhos),}
        \AttributeTok{sd\_true\_rho     =} \FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(rhos),}
        \AttributeTok{mean\_true\_kappa =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(kappas),}
        \AttributeTok{sd\_true\_kappa   =} \FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(kappas)}
\NormalTok{      )}
      
      \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (counter }\SpecialCharTok{\%\%} \DecValTok{10} \SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) \{}
        \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"  Completed \%d / \%d simulations}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, counter, }\DecValTok{3} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ n\_ml\_sims))}
\NormalTok{      \}}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}
  
\NormalTok{  ml\_recovery\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(ml\_recovery\_results)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(ml\_recovery\_df, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_ml\_model\_recovery.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  ml\_recovery\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_ml\_model\_recovery.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\NormalTok{ml\_binary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_recovery\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{),}
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"predicted\_model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"weight"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{predicted\_model =} \FunctionTok{str\_remove}\NormalTok{(predicted\_model, }\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(sim\_id, true\_model) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{slice\_max}\NormalTok{(weight, }\AttributeTok{n =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{with\_ties =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}

  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}

\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}

\NormalTok{ml\_binary }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{n) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_ml_probabilistic_confusion-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The expected pattern: the diagonal should dominate. Because the models
are nested (SBA \(\subset\) PBA \(\subset\) WBA), Occam's razor should
produce \emph{asymmetric} off-diagonal errors --- the WBA should
sometimes be classified as PBA or SBA when its parameters mimic simpler
models, but the SBA should rarely be classified as WBA when extra
parameters are unnecessary. This asymmetry is a \emph{feature} of the
comparison pipeline, not a bug.

\subsection{Model Comparison
Calibration}\label{model-comparison-calibration}

A well-calibrated comparison procedure assigns a stacking weight of,
say, 0.7 to a model that turns out to be the true generator roughly 70\%
of the time. We assess this by binning stacking weights and computing
the empirical frequency with which the candidate was the true model.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ml\_cal\_long }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_recovery\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{),}
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"candidate"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"stacking\_weight"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{candidate =} \FunctionTok{str\_remove}\NormalTok{(candidate, }\StringTok{"weight\_"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{is\_true   =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(candidate }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ true\_model)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{n\_bins }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{8}
\NormalTok{ml\_cal\_binned }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_cal\_long }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{bin =} \FunctionTok{cut}\NormalTok{(stacking\_weight,}
                   \AttributeTok{breaks =} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{length.out =}\NormalTok{ n\_bins }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
                   \AttributeTok{include.lowest =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(candidate, bin) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{bin\_mid    =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(stacking\_weight),}
    \AttributeTok{observed   =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(is\_true),}
    \AttributeTok{N\_datasets =} \FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups    =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{SE    =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{((observed }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ observed)) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(N\_datasets, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{Lower =} \FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, observed }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FloatTok{1.96} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ SE),}
    \AttributeTok{Upper =} \FunctionTok{pmin}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, observed }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{1.96} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ SE)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ml\_cal\_binned, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ bin\_mid, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ observed, }\AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ candidate)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ Lower, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ Upper, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ candidate),}
              \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_brewer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{palette =} \StringTok{"Set2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{labels =}\NormalTok{ scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\NormalTok{percent, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Multilevel Model Comparison Calibration"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Points on the diagonal = well{-}calibrated stacking weights.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Ribbons = 95\% CI; wider ribbons reflect fewer datasets in that bin."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Mean Stacking Weight (Binned)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Empirical Frequency (True Generating Model)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"Candidate"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"Candidate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_ml_model_calibration-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Points on the diagonal indicate perfect calibration: a stacking weight
of 0.7 means the model is the true generator 70\% of the time. A curve
bowed above the diagonal signals underconfidence (the procedure hedges
too much); a curve below signals overconfidence. The multilevel
comparison has additional sources of uncertainty relative to the
single-agent case --- partial pooling, correlation estimation, and
funnel geometry --- so some additional calibration noise is expected.

\section{Summary: What the Multilevel Pipeline
Adds}\label{summary-what-the-multilevel-pipeline-adds}

The multilevel extension answers the \emph{population-level} question:
how do people in general weight evidence, and how much do they vary? By
using the reparameterised \((\rho_j, \kappa_j)\) parameterisation ---
not the raw \((w_d, w_s)\) shown to be unidentifiable in the
single-agent case --- we preserve the identifiability gains while
gaining partial pooling.

The full pipeline mirrors the single-agent workflow:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Model quality:} Diagnostics, trace plots, and summary
  statistics confirmed clean sampling for all three multilevel models.
\item
  \textbf{Parameter recovery:} Individual \(\rho\) values recover well
  (identified from conflict trials); \(\kappa\) is noisier but partially
  regularised by the hierarchical prior. Partial pooling pulls extreme
  agents toward the population mean, as expected.
\item
  \textbf{SBC certification:} The multilevel SBC established that the
  Stan sampler is globally unbiased for the ML-WBA and ML-PBA population
  parameters --- a critical check given the more complex posterior
  geometry.
\item
  \textbf{Model comparison:} The three-way LOO comparison with pointwise
  ELPD visualizations showed \emph{where} each model's advantage lies
  (high-conflict trials for non-balanced populations). Scenario case
  studies demonstrated the nested Occam's razor effect: simpler
  populations are correctly attributed to simpler models.
\item
  \textbf{Model recovery:} The confusion matrix and calibration curves
  quantified the comparison procedure's reliability across many
  simulated populations, confirming appropriate asymmetric error
  patterns from the nested model structure.
\end{enumerate}

The key lesson: \textbf{the reparameterisation that solved the
single-agent identifiability problem is equally essential at the
multilevel level.} The hierarchical prior partially constrains the
scale, but it does not eliminate the ridge in each agent's conditional
likelihood. Only by separating allocation (\(\rho\)) from scaling
(\(\kappa\)) can we obtain meaningful individual-level parameter
estimates --- and by extension, meaningful population-level summaries of
how people vary.

\section{Mixed Strategies: When the Population Is
Heterogeneous}\label{mixed-strategies-when-the-population-is-heterogeneous}

Every analysis above assumed a \emph{homogeneous} population: all agents
were drawn from the same generative model. In practice, this assumption
is almost certainly wrong. A real experimental sample plausibly contains
people who use qualitatively different strategies --- some may integrate
evidence at face value (SBA-like), while others systematically
overweight social or direct evidence (WBA-like). What happens to our
pipeline when the population is a genuine mixture?

This section merges data from two generating models --- half the
participants behave as Simple Bayesian Agents, half as Weighted Bayesian
Agents with a strong social bias --- and examines whether the
hierarchical models and the comparison pipeline can still produce useful
inference.

Because no single hierarchical model can correctly represent a bimodal
population distribution, we should \emph{not} expect population-level
parameters to be meaningful. The scientific question shifts from ``what
does the average person do?'' to ``which strategy does \emph{each}
person use?'' This motivates a focus on \textbf{individual-level
parameter recovery} and \textbf{per-agent model comparison}, skipping
the usual population-level parameter interpretation.

\subsection{Generating the Mixed
Population}\label{generating-the-mixed-population}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2026}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{J\_mix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{40}  \CommentTok{\# 20 SBA + 20 WBA agents}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} SBA agents: strict Bayesian integration (wd = 1, ws = 1) {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{sba\_rhos   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{sba\_kappas }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-} WBA agents: strong social bias, higher total sensitivity {-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\# Draw from a population distribution to add realistic variation}
\NormalTok{wba\_raw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ MASS}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mvrnorm}\NormalTok{(}
  \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{mu    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{qlogis}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{)),   }\CommentTok{\# rho ≈ 0.25, kappa ≈ 2.5}
  \AttributeTok{Sigma =} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.08}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.08}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.12}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{wba\_rhos   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(wba\_raw[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{wba\_kappas }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(wba\_raw[, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{])}

\CommentTok{\# Combine and shuffle so agent IDs don\textquotesingle{}t reveal strategy}
\NormalTok{agent\_order   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(J\_mix)}
\NormalTok{all\_rhos      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(sba\_rhos, wba\_rhos)[agent\_order]}
\NormalTok{all\_kappas    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(sba\_kappas, wba\_kappas)[agent\_order]}
\NormalTok{true\_strategy }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{))[agent\_order]}

\CommentTok{\# Generate trial{-}level data}
\NormalTok{mixed\_stan\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_ml\_data}\NormalTok{(all\_rhos, all\_kappas,}
\NormalTok{                                     evidence\_combinations, }\AttributeTok{n\_reps =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Store ground truth for recovery plots}
\NormalTok{mixed\_truth }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{agent\_id       =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(J\_mix),}
  \AttributeTok{true\_rho       =}\NormalTok{ all\_rhos,}
  \AttributeTok{true\_kappa     =}\NormalTok{ all\_kappas,}
  \AttributeTok{true\_wd        =}\NormalTok{ all\_rhos }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ all\_kappas,}
  \AttributeTok{true\_ws        =}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ all\_rhos) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ all\_kappas,}
  \AttributeTok{true\_strategy  =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Mixed population: \%d agents (\%d SBA + \%d WBA)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{            J\_mix, }\FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(true\_strategy }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(true\_strategy }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Mixed population: 40 agents (20 SBA + 20 WBA)
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Total trials: \%d | Trials per agent: \%d}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{            mixed\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N, mixed\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ J\_mix))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Total trials: 2880 | Trials per agent: 72
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(mixed\_truth, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_wd, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_ws,}
                         \AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id), }\AttributeTok{nudge\_x =} \FloatTok{0.04}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{show.legend =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#984ea3"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{17}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Mixed Population: True Agent Parameters"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"SBA agents cluster at (1, 1); WBA agents are socially biased with higher total sensitivity"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x        =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[d]),}
    \AttributeTok{y        =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ w[s]),}
    \AttributeTok{colour   =} \StringTok{"Strategy"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \StringTok{"Strategy"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_mixed_population_viz-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The SBA agents form a tight cluster at \((w_d, w_s) = (1, 1)\) --- they
are all identical by construction. The WBA agents scatter around
\((w_d, w_s) \approx (0.6, 1.9)\), each with their own idiosyncratic
weights drawn from the population distribution. No single Gaussian
population on the \((\text{logit}(\rho), \log(\kappa))\) scale can
represent this bimodal structure.

\subsection{Fitting All Three Multilevel
Models}\label{fitting-all-three-multilevel-models}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_sba.rds"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_pba.rds"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_wba.rds"}\NormalTok{)))) \{}
  
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ mixed\_stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ mixed\_stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_ml\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ mixed\_stan\_data, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \CommentTok{\# Save each fit individually with $save\_object() so draws survive sessions}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_sba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_pba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_wba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_sba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_pba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit\_mix\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels/ch10\_fit\_mix\_wba.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== Mixed Population: Model Diagnostics ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Why Population Parameters Are
Misleading}\label{why-population-parameters-are-misleading}

Before turning to individual-level analysis, let's check what the
ML-WBA's population parameters say about this mixed population.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{draws\_mix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_mix\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{())}

\NormalTok{pop\_mix\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_mix\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mu[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"mu[2]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sigma[2]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Omega[1,2]"}\NormalTok{,}
                \StringTok{"pop\_rho"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"pop\_kappa"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== ML{-}WBA Population Parameter Estimates (Mixed Data) ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

\subsection{Individual Parameter
Recovery}\label{individual-parameter-recovery}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract individual posteriors}
\NormalTok{mix\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mixed\_truth }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{est\_rho   =} \FunctionTok{map\_dbl}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(draws\_mix[[}\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rho["}\NormalTok{, .x, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)]])),}
    \AttributeTok{est\_kappa =} \FunctionTok{map\_dbl}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(draws\_mix[[}\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"kappa["}\NormalTok{, .x, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)]])),}
    \AttributeTok{est\_wd    =} \FunctionTok{map\_dbl}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(draws\_mix[[}\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_direct["}\NormalTok{, .x, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)]])),}
    \AttributeTok{est\_ws    =} \FunctionTok{map\_dbl}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(draws\_mix[[}\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"weight\_social["}\NormalTok{, .x, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)]]))}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# ρ recovery}
\NormalTok{p\_mix\_rho }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(mix\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_rho, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ est\_rho,}
                                        \AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#984ea3"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovery: "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ rho }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" (relative allocation)"}\NormalTok{),}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ rho[j]),}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Estimated "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ rho[j]),}
       \AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"True Strategy"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# κ recovery}
\NormalTok{p\_mix\_kappa }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(mix\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_kappa, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ est\_kappa,}
                                          \AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#984ea3"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovery: "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa }\SpecialCharTok{*} \StringTok{" (total evidence scaling)"}\NormalTok{),}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa[j]),}
       \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Estimated "} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ kappa[j]),}
       \AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"True Strategy"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_mix\_rho }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ p\_mix\_kappa }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{plot\_layout}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{guides =} \StringTok{"collect"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\&}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_mixed_individual_recovery-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\subsection{Per-Agent Model
Comparison}\label{per-agent-model-comparison}

The population-level LOO comparison tells us which model best predicts
\emph{the aggregate dataset}. But the more informative question is:
which model best predicts \emph{each individual agent's} behaviour? We
decompose the total LOO into agent-level contributions.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Compute trial{-}level LOO for all three models}
\NormalTok{loo\_mix\_sba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fit\_mix\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{loo\_mix\_pba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fit\_mix\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{loo\_mix\_wba }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fit\_mix\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Population{-}level comparison (for reference)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== Population{-}Level Comparison (Mixed Data) ===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
=== Population-Level Comparison (Mixed Data) ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{loo\_compare}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ML{-}SBA"} \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_sba,}
                        \StringTok{"ML{-}PBA"} \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_pba,}
                        \StringTok{"ML{-}WBA"} \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_wba)))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
       elpd_diff se_diff
ML-WBA   0.0       0.0  
ML-PBA  -2.7       2.8  
ML-SBA -17.8       6.4  
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ml\_mix\_weights }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{loo\_model\_weights}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ML{-}SBA"} \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_sba, }\StringTok{"ML{-}PBA"} \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_pba, }\StringTok{"ML{-}WBA"} \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_wba),}
  \AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"stacking"}
\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Stacking weights:}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

Stacking weights:
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(ml\_mix\_weights, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Method: stacking
------
       weight
ML-SBA 0.055 
ML-PBA 0.170 
ML-WBA 0.775 
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Decompose into per{-}agent ELPD sums}
\NormalTok{agent\_elpd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{trial        =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(mixed\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{N),}
  \AttributeTok{agent\_id     =}\NormalTok{ mixed\_stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id,}
  \AttributeTok{elpd\_sba     =}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_sba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[, }\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{elpd\_pba     =}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_pba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[, }\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{],}
  \AttributeTok{elpd\_wba     =}\NormalTok{ loo\_mix\_wba}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise[, }\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_SBA =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(elpd\_sba),}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_PBA =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(elpd\_pba),}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_WBA =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(elpd\_wba),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups  =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(mixed\_truth }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, true\_strategy), }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"agent\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{best\_model =} \FunctionTok{case\_when}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      elpd\_SBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ elpd\_PBA }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ elpd\_SBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ elpd\_WBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{      elpd\_PBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ elpd\_SBA }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ elpd\_PBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ elpd\_WBA }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{,}
      \ConstantTok{TRUE} \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \StringTok{"WBA"}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \CommentTok{\# ELPD advantage of the best flexible model over the SBA baseline}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_advantage =} \FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(elpd\_PBA, elpd\_WBA) }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ elpd\_SBA}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Left: per{-}agent ELPD advantage coloured by true strategy}
\NormalTok{p\_agent\_advantage }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(agent\_elpd,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{reorder}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, elpd\_advantage),}
           \AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_advantage,}
           \AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_col}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#984ea3"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_flip}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Per{-}Agent ELPD: Best Flexible Model vs. SBA"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Positive = flexible model predicts better. Negative = SBA sufficient."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x        =} \StringTok{"Agent (sorted by advantage)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y        =} \StringTok{"ELPD Advantage (max(PBA, WBA) − SBA)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fill     =} \StringTok{"True Strategy"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{axis.text.y  =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Right: scatter of per{-}agent SBA vs WBA ELPD}
\NormalTok{p\_agent\_scatter }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(agent\_elpd,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_SBA, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ elpd\_WBA,}
           \AttributeTok{colour =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy, }\AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ best\_model)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#4daf4a"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#984ea3"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{15}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{17}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Agent{-}Level LOO: SBA vs. WBA"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Points above diagonal: WBA predicts better. Shape = winning model."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x        =} \StringTok{"Agent ELPD under ML{-}SBA"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y        =} \StringTok{"Agent ELPD under ML{-}WBA"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{colour   =} \StringTok{"True Strategy"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \StringTok{"Best Model"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_agent\_advantage }\SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ p\_agent\_scatter}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{10-BayesianModels_files/figure-pdf/ch10_mixed_agent_loo_viz-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The bar chart on the left reveals a clean separation. The WBA agents
(purple) consistently show positive ELPD advantages --- the flexible
model predicts their behaviour much better than the SBA. The SBA agents
(green) hover near zero or show slight negative advantages --- the SBA
is sufficient, and the flexible models' extra parameters represent
wasted complexity. The scatter plot on the right makes the same point
geometrically: WBA agents sit above the diagonal (ML-WBA predicts
better), while SBA agents sit on or below it.

\subsection{Individual-Level Confusion
Matrix}\label{individual-level-confusion-matrix}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Build the confusion matrix}
\NormalTok{mix\_confusion }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ agent\_elpd }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{count}\NormalTok{(true\_strategy, best\_model) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(true\_strategy) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{proportion =}\NormalTok{ n }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(n)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{true\_strategy =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_strategy, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{best\_model    =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(best\_model, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"PBA"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"WBA"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{complete}\NormalTok{(true\_strategy, best\_model, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{proportion =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Table}
\NormalTok{mix\_confusion }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{n) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ best\_model, }\AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ proportion) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{kable}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{digits =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
               \AttributeTok{caption =} \StringTok{"Individual{-}Level Strategy Classification: Mixed Population"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Heatmap}
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(mix\_confusion, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ best\_model, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ true\_strategy, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ proportion)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_tile}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{colour =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%d}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(\%s)"}\NormalTok{, n, scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{percent}\NormalTok{(proportion, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))),}
            \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fontface =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"mako"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{direction =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_discrete}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{limits =}\NormalTok{ rev) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Individual{-}Level Strategy Recovery: Mixed Population"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Rows = true generating strategy. Columns = model with highest per{-}agent ELPD."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x     =} \StringTok{"Assigned Model (Max Agent ELPD)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y     =} \StringTok{"True Strategy"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fill  =} \StringTok{"Classification}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Rate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{panel.grid =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{())}
\end{Highlighting}
