\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# ── Diagnostic issue investigation ───────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{backend\_diags  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{backend\_diagnostics}
\NormalTok{default\_diags  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{default\_diagnostics }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(sim\_id, max\_rhat, min\_ess\_to\_rank)}
\NormalTok{true\_params\_wide }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(sim\_id, variable, simulated\_value) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  tidyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ variable, }\AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value)}

\NormalTok{diag\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ backend\_diags }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(default\_diags,    }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"sim\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(true\_params\_wide, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"sim\_id"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{has\_issue =}\NormalTok{ n\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0} \SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ n\_max\_treedepth }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0} \SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ max\_rhat }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.01}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_diag\_c\_bias }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(diag\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ log\_c, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ bias, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ has\_issue)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Diagnostic Issues in Parameter Space"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Orange points: divergence, max treedepth, or Rhat \textgreater{} 1.01"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True log(c)"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"True Bias"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_diag\_w\_c }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(diag\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{w[1]}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ log\_c, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ has\_issue)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Attention vs Sensitivity"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True w[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"True log(c)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_diag\_c\_bias }\SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ p\_diag\_w\_c)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_sbc_single-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# ── Parameter recovery on SBC simulations ────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{recovery\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ sbc\_results\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats}

\NormalTok{p\_sbc\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(recovery\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{variable, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Parameter Recovery Across SBC Simulations"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Posterior mean vs True generated value"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True Value (simulated\_value)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Estimated Value (Posterior Mean)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_bw}\NormalTok{()}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sbc\_recovery)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_sbc_single-4.pdf}
\end{center}

When the true log(c) is above 0 the model computes similarity using
exponential decay: \texttt{exp(-c\ *\ distance)}. When c gets large,
similarity drops to exactly zero for anything except perfectly identical
stimuli, causing gradient collapse and HMC failures. The diagnostic
scatter plots above reveal that divergences and Rhat violations cluster
at high log(c) values, confirming that the prior mass above log(c) ≈ 2
(c ≈ 7--8) is unsupported by the sampler geometry.

However, calibration and parameter recovery look otherwise good.

\textbf{Practical constraint from SBC.} We treat c ≈ 12 as a hard
ceiling: a sensitivity this extreme makes stimuli that differ by any
measurable amount effectively identical, which is psychologically
implausible for the feature ranges used here. We therefore require that
the prior places negligible mass above c = 12, i.e., that the 99.85th
percentile (mean + 3 SD on the log scale) stays below log(12) ≈ 2.49.
For the single-subject model this is satisfied with
\texttt{Normal(log(2),\ 1.0)}, whose +3 SD upper bound is exp(0.69 +
3.0) ≈ 20 --- already at the boundary of acceptability. We carry this
constraint forward explicitly into the multilevel parameterisation.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Extending the GCM with Memory
Decay}\label{extending-the-gcm-with-memory-decay}

The canonical GCM weights every stored exemplar equally --- a perfect,
unbounded memory. That is pedagogically clean but psychologically
implausible. Indeed, many early experiments had participants being
extensively trained on a set of stimuli, repeated many times, before
they actually got tested, so that they would resemble better the model's
assumptions. Further, an unbounded memory becomes a real liability as
soon as the learning environment is non-stationary: if the category
structure changes halfway through the task, the unweighted sum of
exemplars anchors the agent to contradictions it has no mechanism to
discard. We add a single forgetting-rate parameter, \(\lambda\), that
downweights older exemplars by \(e^{-\lambda (t - t_e)}\) before
entering the similarity average:

\[\eta_{ie} = e^{-\lambda (i - t_e)} \cdot e^{-c\, d_{ie}}, \qquad m_{k,i} = \frac{\sum_{e \in C_k} \eta_{ie}}{\sum_{e \in C_k} e^{-\lambda(i - t_e)}}, \qquad p_{i} = \frac{\beta \cdot m_{1,i}}{\beta \cdot m_{1,i} + (1 - \beta) \cdot m_{0,i}}.\]

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \(\eta_{ie}\) (Activation/Similarity): The total strength of the
  connection between the current stimulus \(i\) and a stored exemplar
  \(e\). It combines how similar they are in ``feature space'' (spatial)
  and how recent the memory is (temporal).
\item
  \(i\): The current trial index (the ``now'').
\item
  \(t_e\): The trial index when exemplar \(e\) was first encountered and
  stored in memory.
\item
  \((i - t_e)\): The age of the exemplar---how many trials have passed
  since it was seen.
\item
  \(d_{ie}\): The psychological distance between the current stimulus
  \(i\) and the stored exemplar \(e\) in feature space.
\item
  \(c\): The sensitivity parameter. It controls how quickly similarity
  drops as distance increases. A high \(c\) means only very similar
  items count; a low \(c\) means the agent generalizes broadly.
\item
  \(\lambda\): The forgetting-rate (decay) parameter. It controls how
  quickly memories fade with time.
\item
  \(m_{k,i}\): The match (or evidence) for category \(k\). This is a
  weighted average of similarities for all exemplars belonging to that
  category.
\item
  \(C_k\): The set of all exemplars that the agent has previously
  assigned to category \(k\).
\item
  \(p_i\): The probability that the agent classifies the current
  stimulus \(i\) into Category 1.
\item
  \(\beta\): The bias parameter. It represents a baseline preference for
  Category 1 (\(\beta > 0.5\)) or Category 0 (\(\beta < 0.5\))
  regardless of the evidence.
\end{itemize}

The parameter \(\lambda\) governs the Temporal Decay term:
\(e^{-\lambda (i - t_e)}\).

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  The ``Memory Window'': Think of \(\lambda\) as controlling the size of
  the agent's moving window of attention.
\end{enumerate}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  If \(\lambda = 0\): The term \(e^{0} = 1\). The age of the memory has
  no effect. Every exemplar ever seen is weighted equally, representing
  a ``perfect,'' infinite memory.
\item
  If \(\lambda\) is large: The term \(e^{-\lambda \cdot age}\) drops to
  near-zero very quickly as age increases. The agent only ``remembers''
  or cares about the last few trials.
\item
  The Half-Life: You can calculate the ``half-life'' of a memory (the
  number of trials it takes for its influence to drop by half) as
  \(t_{1/2} = \frac{\ln(2)}{\lambda} \approx \frac{0.69}{\lambda}\). For
  example, if \(\lambda = 0.1\), a memory's influence is halved every
  \textasciitilde7 trials.
\end{itemize}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\setcounter{enumi}{1}
\tightlist
\item
  Effective Sample Size: The denominator in the \(m_{k,i}\) formula,
  \(\sum_{e \in C_k} e^{-\lambda(i - t_e)}\), is the decay-weighted
  effective sample size.
\end{enumerate}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Instead of just counting how many items are in Category \(k\) (which
  would be the raw count \(|C_k|\)), the model counts how many ``fresh''
  items are in Category \(k\).
\item
  If a category has 100 exemplars but they were all seen 500 trials ago,
  and \(\lambda\) is high, the ``effective count'' for that category
  will be near zero. This prevents old, potentially obsolete evidence
  from dominating the decision.
\end{itemize}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\setcounter{enumi}{2}
\tightlist
\item
  Why use it? \(\lambda\) is essential for non-stationary environments.
  If the rules of the task change (e.g., the category boundary shifts),
  an agent with \(\lambda=0\) will be stuck trying to reconcile new
  evidence with a massive mountain of old, contradictory evidence. An
  agent with a well-tuned \(\lambda\) can ``forget'' the old rules and
  adapt to the new ones by letting the old evidence fade away.
\end{enumerate}

\begin{tcolorbox}[enhanced jigsaw, toprule=.15mm, toptitle=1mm, bottomtitle=1mm, leftrule=.75mm, rightrule=.15mm, colframe=quarto-callout-note-color-frame, left=2mm, colback=white, titlerule=0mm, coltitle=black, colbacktitle=quarto-callout-note-color!10!white, opacityback=0, title=\textcolor{quarto-callout-note-color}{\faInfo}\hspace{0.5em}{Canonical scenario framework (Chapters 11--13)}, arc=.35mm, opacitybacktitle=0.6, breakable, bottomrule=.15mm]

All three categorization chapters test their non-stationarity extension
against the same three environmental scenarios, presented in the same
order. The ordering reflects \textbf{increasing assumption match}
between the scenario's data-generating process and the model's
structural commitments. The scenarios are defined here once; Chapters 12
and 13 adopt the same definitions and ordering.

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Scenario
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
What changes in the world
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Structural role
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
\textbf{Static Kruschke} & Nothing --- labels are fixed & Baseline: no
signal for the non-stationarity parameter \\
\textbf{Performance-contingent abrupt shifts} & Labels flip after a
streak of \(k\) correct responses & Misspecified: abrupt reversals that
no smooth-decay / Gaussian-drift / stochastic-mutation mechanism can
exactly represent \\
\textbf{Smooth drift} & Category centres follow a bivariate Gaussian
random walk & Assumption-matched (to varying degrees per model):
gradually stale evidence is what all three mechanisms are built to
handle \\
\end{longtable}

Comparing the three scenarios across chapters reveals how structural
differences between model families show up in their sensitivity to
non-stationarity. The synthesis is in the Trilogy Synthesis section at
the end of Chapter 13.

\end{tcolorbox}

We will test this extended model against the three scenarios:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Static Kruschke (baseline).} Fixed category labels, as in the
  canonical GCM analysis above. A low-\(\lambda\) model is correct for
  this data; we expect the posterior to be pulled toward the left tail
  of the \(\log\lambda\) prior and for the \(c\)/\(\lambda\) pair to be
  partially non-identified (both can make the similarity average
  narrower).
\item
  \textbf{Performance-contingent abrupt shifts.} After the agent reaches
  a streak of \(k\) consecutive correct responses, all category labels
  flip. The agent must unlearn the old mapping and relearn the new one.
  A high-\(\lambda\) model recovers fastest: it forgets the
  now-contradicted exemplars before they can anchor decisions. Note that
  these shifts are \emph{endogenous} to the agent's own behaviour --- a
  noisier agent triggers shifts less often and accumulates less
  information about \(\lambda\). Although this scenario is structurally
  misspecified (the decay GCM has no explicit abrupt-reversal
  mechanism), the post-flip recovery dynamics impose a strong constraint
  on \(\lambda\) that produces the clearest identifiability signal of
  the three scenarios.
\item
  \textbf{Smooth drift.} Category centres in feature space drift as an
  independent random walk. Labels are assigned trial-by-trial by
  proximity to the current (drifted) centres, so the category boundary
  migrates smoothly through the stimulus set. This is the
  \textbf{assumption-matched} scenario for the decay GCM: exponentially
  downweighting past exemplars is a defensible approximation to
  ``evidence collected under a different category mean is stale.''
  Moderate \(\lambda\) is appropriate here --- agents that forget too
  slowly lag behind the moving boundary; agents that forget too fast
  discard still-valid recent evidence.
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Assumption Match Across
Scenarios}\label{assumption-match-across-scenarios}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Scenario
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
What drifts in the world
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
What decay GCM assumes
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Match
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
Static & Nothing & Exponential forgetting (correct at \(\lambda \to 0\))
& \textbf{Information-starved}: \(\lambda\) weakly identified \\
Contingent shift & Abrupt label reversals & Smooth exponential
forgetting & \textbf{Misspecified}: no abrupt mechanism, but post-flip
recovery provides strong \(\lambda\) signal \\
Smooth drift & Gaussian centre drift & Smooth exponential downweighting
of stale exemplars & \textbf{Graceful degradation}: approximate but
reasonable match \\
\end{longtable}

An important subtlety emerges from this table: \emph{assumption match
and parameter identifiability do not always align}. The contingent-shift
scenario is more misspecified than smooth drift, yet it produces the
cleanest \(\lambda\) recovery --- because abrupt reversals impose strong
temporal constraints that only \(\lambda\) can explain. The drift
scenario recovers \(\lambda\) at the correct calibration rate (SBC
passes) but without the ``bonus'' identifiability that misspecification
accidentally provides. We will see both signatures in the validation
results below.

\subsection{Prior on the Decay
Parameter}\label{prior-on-the-decay-parameter}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Prior hyperparameters for log(lambda).}
\CommentTok{\# Normal({-}2, 1) gives median lambda ≈ 0.135 (half{-}life ≈ 5 trials).}
\CommentTok{\# ±2 SD covers lambda ∈ (0.018, 1.0), spanning "effectively no decay"}
\CommentTok{\# to "only the last trial or two matters".}
\NormalTok{LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_MEAN }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{2.0}
\NormalTok{LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_SD   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{1.0}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{R Simulator With Scenario
Logic}\label{r-simulator-with-scenario-logic}

The simulator loops over trials, computes the decay-weighted choice
probability, draws a response, and determines the \emph{observed}
feedback according to the scenario. Memory stores the trial index at
which each exemplar was encoded, so ages are exact at every step. For
the contingent-shift scenario, the label-flip state machine updates
\emph{after} the response is drawn (so the current trial's feedback uses
the label map that was in force at decision time).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{simulate\_decay\_scenario }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(w, c, lambda, bias, schedule,}
                                    \AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{),}
                                    \AttributeTok{streak\_target =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}
                                    \AttributeTok{drift\_traj    =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  scenario }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{match.arg}\NormalTok{(scenario)}
\NormalTok{  ntrials   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(schedule)}
\NormalTok{  obs\_mat   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(schedule[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)])}
\NormalTok{  base\_lab  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback}

\NormalTok{  memory\_obs   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\ConstantTok{NA\_real\_}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{nrow =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  memory\_cat   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  memory\_trial }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  label\_flip }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  streak     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L}

\NormalTok{  prob\_cat1         }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(ntrials)}
\NormalTok{  sim\_response      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(ntrials)}
\NormalTok{  observed\_feedback }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(ntrials)}
\NormalTok{  label\_flip\_trace  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{logical}\NormalTok{(ntrials)}

  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(ntrials)) \{}
\NormalTok{    current\_stim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(obs\_mat[i, ])}
\NormalTok{    n\_mem        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(memory\_obs)}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Decay{-}weighted *mean* similarity per category ───────────────────}
    \CommentTok{\# Numerator:   Σ (decay\_j · similarity\_j) over exemplars in that category.}
    \CommentTok{\# Denominator: Σ decay\_j over exemplars in that category  (effective N).}
    \CommentTok{\# Collapses to plain mean similarity when lambda = 0.}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (n\_mem }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0} \SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{any}\NormalTok{(memory\_cat }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{any}\NormalTok{(memory\_cat }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) \{}
\NormalTok{      p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ bias}
\NormalTok{    \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{      d     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sweep}\NormalTok{(memory\_obs, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, current\_stim, }\StringTok{"{-}"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%*\%}\NormalTok{ w)}
\NormalTok{      age   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ i }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ memory\_trial}
\NormalTok{      decay }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{lambda }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ age)}
\NormalTok{      sim   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{c }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ d)}
\NormalTok{      wt    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ decay }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ sim}
\NormalTok{      s1    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(wt[memory\_cat }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]);    s0 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(wt[memory\_cat }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{      w1    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(decay[memory\_cat }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]); w0 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(decay[memory\_cat }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{      m1    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (w1 }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1e{-}12}\NormalTok{) s1 }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ w1 }\ControlFlowTok{else} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{      m0    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (w0 }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1e{-}12}\NormalTok{) s0 }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ w0 }\ControlFlowTok{else} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{      num   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ bias }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ m1}
\NormalTok{      den   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ num }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ bias) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ m0}
\NormalTok{      p     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (den }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1e{-}12}\NormalTok{) num }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ den }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ bias}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{    p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1e{-}9}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{pmin}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FloatTok{1e{-}9}\NormalTok{, p))}
\NormalTok{    prob\_cat1[i]    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ p}
\NormalTok{    sim\_response[i] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p)}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Observed feedback depends on the scenario ───────────────────────}
\NormalTok{    fb }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{switch}\NormalTok{(scenario,}
      \AttributeTok{static           =}\NormalTok{ base\_lab[i],}
      \AttributeTok{contingent\_shift =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (label\_flip) }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_lab[i] }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ base\_lab[i],}
      \AttributeTok{drift            =}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{        d0 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(current\_stim }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ drift\_traj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mu0[i, ]))}
\NormalTok{        d1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(current\_stim }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ drift\_traj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mu1[i, ]))}
        \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(d1 }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ d0)}
\NormalTok{      \}}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    observed\_feedback[i] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fb}
\NormalTok{    label\_flip\_trace[i]  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ label\_flip}

    \CommentTok{\# Update the streak/flip state for the contingent scenario}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{      streak }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (sim\_response[i] }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ fb) streak }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L }\ControlFlowTok{else} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L}
      \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (streak }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ streak\_target) \{ label\_flip }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \SpecialCharTok{!}\NormalTok{label\_flip; streak }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L \}}
\NormalTok{    \}}

    \CommentTok{\# Add the current trial to memory}
\NormalTok{    memory\_obs   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbind}\NormalTok{(memory\_obs, current\_stim)}
\NormalTok{    memory\_cat   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(memory\_cat, fb)}
\NormalTok{    memory\_trial }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(memory\_trial, i)}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  schedule }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{prob\_cat1         =}\NormalTok{ prob\_cat1,}
      \AttributeTok{sim\_response      =}\NormalTok{ sim\_response,}
      \AttributeTok{observed\_feedback =}\NormalTok{ observed\_feedback,}
      \AttributeTok{label\_flip        =}\NormalTok{ label\_flip\_trace,}
      \AttributeTok{correct           =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(sim\_response }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ observed\_feedback)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Drift Trajectory
Generator}\label{drift-trajectory-generator}

The smooth-drift scenario needs a pre-computed trajectory of category
centres. Each centre follows an independent bivariate Gaussian random
walk with per-trial step SD \texttt{drift\_sigma}. Defaults place the
initial centres at the John K. Kruschke (1993) category means;
\texttt{drift\_sigma\ =\ 0.05} gives a cumulative SD of about 0.4 units
over 64 trials --- enough to migrate the boundary through roughly one
stimulus cell.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{make\_drift\_trajectory }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(ntrials, }\AttributeTok{drift\_sigma =} \FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(seed)}
\NormalTok{  mu0\_init }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1.75}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{3.25}\NormalTok{)   }\CommentTok{\# Kruschke cat{-}0 mean (height, position)}
\NormalTok{  mu1\_init }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{3.25}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.75}\NormalTok{)   }\CommentTok{\# Kruschke cat{-}1 mean}
\NormalTok{  d0 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(ntrials }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, drift\_sigma), }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, cumsum)}
\NormalTok{  d1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(ntrials }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, drift\_sigma), }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, cumsum)}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{mu0 =} \FunctionTok{sweep}\NormalTok{(d0, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, mu0\_init, }\StringTok{"+"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{mu1 =} \FunctionTok{sweep}\NormalTok{(d1, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, mu1\_init, }\StringTok{"+"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  The ``Ground Truth'' is a Random Walk The function
  make\_drift\_trajectory creates two paths (\(mu_0\) and \(mu_1\)).

  \begin{itemize}
  \item
    Initial State: On Trial 1, the categories start at the standard
    Kruschke positions (e.g., Cat 0 is at top-left, Cat 1 is at
    bottom-right).
  \item
    The Drift: Every single trial, both centers take a small random step
    in a random direction (North, South, East, or West). Because of the
    cumsum() (cumulative sum), these small steps add up.
  \item
    The Result: By Trial 64, the centers might have wandered quite far
    from where they started. The ``boundary'' between the
    categories---the line where you are equally likely to call something
    Cat 0 or Cat 1---is constantly shifting.
  \end{itemize}
\item
  How Exemplars are Generated When the simulation runs, it uses these
  drifting centers to create the stimuli the agent actually sees:
\item
  On Trial \(t\), the computer decides to show a ``Category 0''
  stimulus.
\item
  It looks up the current coordinates of the center: \(mu_{0,t}\).
\item
  It generates a stimulus near that point (usually by adding a bit of
  noise).
\item
  The agent sees this stimulus and is told: ``This is a Category 0.''
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Scenario Agent Wrappers}\label{scenario-agent-wrappers}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{simulate\_decay\_agent }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, scenario, w, c, lambda, bias,}
\NormalTok{                                 subject\_seed, }\AttributeTok{streak\_target =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}
                                 \AttributeTok{drift\_sigma =} \FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  schedule  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed)}
\NormalTok{  drift\_tr  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{make\_drift\_trajectory}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(schedule), drift\_sigma, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed)}
  \ControlFlowTok{else} \ConstantTok{NULL}

\NormalTok{  out }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_scenario}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{w =}\NormalTok{ w, }\AttributeTok{c =}\NormalTok{ c, }\AttributeTok{lambda =}\NormalTok{ lambda, }\AttributeTok{bias =}\NormalTok{ bias,}
    \AttributeTok{schedule      =}\NormalTok{ schedule,}
    \AttributeTok{scenario      =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{streak\_target =}\NormalTok{ streak\_target,}
    \AttributeTok{drift\_traj    =}\NormalTok{ drift\_tr}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  out }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{agent\_id       =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id,}
      \AttributeTok{scenario       =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
      \AttributeTok{w1\_true        =}\NormalTok{ w[}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{],}
      \AttributeTok{c\_true         =}\NormalTok{ c,}
      \AttributeTok{lambda\_true    =}\NormalTok{ lambda,}
      \AttributeTok{log\_c\_true     =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(c),}
      \AttributeTok{log\_lambda\_true =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(lambda),}
      \AttributeTok{bias\_true      =}\NormalTok{ bias}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cumulative\_accuracy =} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(correct) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Visualising the Three
Scenarios}\label{visualising-the-three-scenarios}

We simulate 10 agents per cell of a grid that crosses three \(\lambda\)
values with the three scenarios and plot cumulative accuracy. Static
data is where low \(\lambda\) wins. The contingent-shift data is where
moderate-to-high \(\lambda\) wins (faster forgetting speeds reversal
recovery, even though the model is misspecified for abrupt shifts). The
drift data rewards moderate \(\lambda\) tuned to the drift rate ---
agents that forget too slowly are anchored by obsolete exemplars; agents
that forget too fast discard still-valid recent evidence.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{decay\_viz\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch12\_decay\_scenario\_viz.csv"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(decay\_viz\_file)) \{}
\NormalTok{  viz\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{scenario    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{lambda\_true =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.02}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_id    =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{10}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{subject\_seed =} \DecValTok{10000} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{())}

\NormalTok{  viz\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id     =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id,}
         \AttributeTok{scenario     =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{scenario,}
         \AttributeTok{lambda       =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{lambda\_true,}
         \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{subject\_seed),}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, scenario, lambda, subject\_seed) \{}
      \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{agent\_id     =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id,}
        \AttributeTok{scenario     =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
        \AttributeTok{w            =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
        \AttributeTok{c            =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{lambda       =}\NormalTok{ lambda,}
        \AttributeTok{bias         =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{write\_csv}\NormalTok{(viz\_sim, decay\_viz\_file)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  viz\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(decay\_viz\_file, }\AttributeTok{show\_col\_types =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(viz\_sim }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
         \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }
                                 \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{),}
                                 \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Static Kruschke"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Performance{-}contingent flips"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Smooth drift"}\NormalTok{))),}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial\_within\_subject, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ cumulative\_accuracy,}
           \AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true), }\AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{interaction}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, lambda\_true))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun =}\NormalTok{ mean, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"line"}\NormalTok{,}
               \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true)), }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun.data =}\NormalTok{ mean\_se, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"ribbon"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{,}
               \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true), }\AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true)),}
               \AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{scenario, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{name =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(lambda)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option  =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{name =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(lambda)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Learning Curves Across the Three Scenarios"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Mean ± SE across 10 agents per cell; c = 2, bias = 0.5, "}\NormalTok{,}
                      \StringTok{"uniform attention"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Cumulative accuracy"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_decay_scenario_viz-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading the scenario panels.} In the static panel, accuracy
climbs monotonically to near-ceiling for all \(\lambda\), with the
lowest \(\lambda\) reaching the highest asymptote---unsurprising,
because any decay discards still-valid evidence. In the drift panel, the
curves plateau below ceiling; the plateau is higher for agents with a
\(\lambda\) tuned to the drift rate, allowing them to track the moving
boundary without being ``anchored'' by obsolete history.

In the contingent-shift panel, we would see a characteristic sawtooth
pattern if we were plotting individuals, but this is somewhat muted by
the averaging. High-\(\lambda\) agents rebound faster after a flip,
which means they reach the criterion earlier and flip more often.
Because these flips happen at different trial numbers for different
agents, averaging across them smooths out the sharp drops and recoveries
into a noisy plateau.

\textbf{The Alignment Critique.} To see the true effect of \(\lambda\)
on cognitive flexibility, we must align the data to the moment of the
shift. In non-stationary environments, averaging across absolute trial
numbers often masks the very process we are interested in---the speed of
adaptation.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Align contingent{-}shift data to the start of each new phase}
\NormalTok{reversal\_viz }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ viz\_sim }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, lambda\_true) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \CommentTok{\# Detect when the label mapping flipped}
    \AttributeTok{shift\_event =}\NormalTok{ label\_flip }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \FunctionTok{lag}\NormalTok{(label\_flip, }\AttributeTok{default =} \FunctionTok{first}\NormalTok{(label\_flip)),}
    \AttributeTok{phase\_id    =} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(shift\_event)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, lambda\_true, phase\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{trial\_in\_phase =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{phase\_accuracy =} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(correct) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ trial\_in\_phase}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \CommentTok{\# Focus on the first few reversals to see the learning dynamics}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(phase\_id }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, phase\_id }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, trial\_in\_phase }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=} \DecValTok{40}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(reversal\_viz, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial\_in\_phase, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ phase\_accuracy, }
                         \AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true), }\AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun =}\NormalTok{ mean, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"line"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun.data =}\NormalTok{ mean\_se, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"ribbon"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Phase"}\NormalTok{, phase\_id), }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{name =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(lambda)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Learning Performance Aligned to Category Shifts"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Cumulative accuracy reset at each reversal; contingent{-}shift scenario only"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trials Since Shift"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Cumulative Phase Accuracy"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_decay_reversal_alignment-1.pdf}
\end{center}

When aligned this way, the ``recovery advantage'' of high \(\lambda\)
becomes stark. While all agents collapse to chance (or below)
immediately after a shift, agents with higher decay rates
(\(\lambda = 0.6\)) shed their obsolete memory faster and return to high
accuracy significantly earlier than those with low decay.

\subsection{The Decay GCM in Stan}\label{the-decay-gcm-in-stan}

The Stan implementation adds \texttt{log\_lambda} as an unconstrained
scalar parameter and multiplies the similarity term by the decay weight
\texttt{exp(-lambda\ *\ age)} where
\texttt{age\ =\ i\ -\ past\_trial\_idx}. We also use a decay-weighted
mean similarity to ensure that the relative evidence for each category
is properly scaled as old exemplars fade.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{gcm\_decay\_single\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Generalized Context Model with Memory Decay — Single Subject}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_features;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total, N\_features] real obs;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} cat\_feedback;}

\StringTok{  vector[N\_features] prior\_w\_alpha;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_log\_c\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_log\_c\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_log\_lambda\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_log\_lambda\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_bias\_alpha;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_bias\_beta;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed data \{}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total, N\_total, N\_features] real abs\_diff;}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{    for (j in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      for (f in 1:N\_features) \{}
\StringTok{        abs\_diff[i, j, f] = abs(obs[i, f] {-} obs[j, f]);}
\StringTok{      \}}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  simplex[N\_features] w;}
\StringTok{  real log\_c;}
\StringTok{  real log\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} bias;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} c      = exp(log\_c);}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} lambda = exp(log\_lambda);}

\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=1e{-}9, upper=1{-}1e{-}9\textgreater{}[N\_total] prob\_cat1;}

\StringTok{  \{}
\StringTok{    array[N\_total] int memory\_trial\_idx;}
\StringTok{    array[N\_total] int memory\_cat;}
\StringTok{    int n\_mem = 0;}

\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total) \{}
\StringTok{      real p\_i;}
\StringTok{      int has\_cat0 = 0;}
\StringTok{      int has\_cat1 = 0;}

\StringTok{      for (k in 1:n\_mem) \{}
\StringTok{        if (memory\_cat[k] == 0) has\_cat0 = 1;}
\StringTok{        if (memory\_cat[k] == 1) has\_cat1 = 1;}
\StringTok{      \}}

\StringTok{      if (n\_mem == 0 || has\_cat0 == 0 || has\_cat1 == 0) \{}
\StringTok{        p\_i = bias;}
\StringTok{      \} else \{}
\StringTok{        real s0 = 0.0;   // sum of decay * similarity for cat 0}
\StringTok{        real s1 = 0.0;   // sum of decay * similarity for cat 1}
\StringTok{        real w0 = 0.0;   // sum of decay weights for cat 0 (effective N)}
\StringTok{        real w1 = 0.0;   // sum of decay weights for cat 1 (effective N)}
\StringTok{        for (e in 1:n\_mem) \{}
\StringTok{          real d = 0.0;}
\StringTok{          int past\_i = memory\_trial\_idx[e];}
\StringTok{          for (f in 1:N\_features)}
\StringTok{            d += w[f] * abs\_diff[i, past\_i, f];}
\StringTok{          real age          = i {-} past\_i;}
\StringTok{          real decay\_weight = exp({-}lambda * age);}
\StringTok{          real sim          = exp({-}c * d);}
\StringTok{          real weight       = decay\_weight * sim;}
\StringTok{          if (memory\_cat[e] == 1) \{ s1 += weight; w1 += decay\_weight; \}}
\StringTok{          else                    \{ s0 += weight; w0 += decay\_weight; \}}
\StringTok{        \}}
\StringTok{        real m1 = (w1 \textgreater{} 1e{-}12) ? s1 / w1 : 0.0;}
\StringTok{        real m0 = (w0 \textgreater{} 1e{-}12) ? s0 / w0 : 0.0;}
\StringTok{        real num = bias * m1;}
\StringTok{        real den = num + (1.0 {-} bias) * m0;}
\StringTok{        p\_i = (den \textgreater{} 1e{-}12) ? num / den : bias;}
\StringTok{      \}}

\StringTok{      prob\_cat1[i] = fmax(1e{-}9, fmin(1.0 {-} 1e{-}9, p\_i));}

\StringTok{      n\_mem += 1;}
\StringTok{      memory\_trial\_idx[n\_mem] = i;}
\StringTok{      memory\_cat[n\_mem] = cat\_feedback[i];}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  target += dirichlet\_lpdf(w | prior\_w\_alpha);}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(log\_c      | prior\_log\_c\_mu,      prior\_log\_c\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(log\_lambda | prior\_log\_lambda\_mu, prior\_log\_lambda\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  target += beta\_lpdf(bias         | prior\_bias\_alpha,    prior\_bias\_beta);}

\StringTok{  target += bernoulli\_lpmf(y | prob\_cat1);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  real lprior;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  if (run\_diagnostics) \{}
\StringTok{    for (i in 1:N\_total)}
\StringTok{      log\_lik[i] = bernoulli\_lpmf(y[i] | prob\_cat1[i]);}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  lprior =}
\StringTok{    dirichlet\_lpdf(w         | prior\_w\_alpha) +}
\StringTok{    normal\_lpdf(log\_c        | prior\_log\_c\_mu,      prior\_log\_c\_sigma) +}
\StringTok{    normal\_lpdf(log\_lambda   | prior\_log\_lambda\_mu, prior\_log\_lambda\_sigma) +}
\StringTok{    beta\_lpdf(bias           | prior\_bias\_alpha,    prior\_bias\_beta);}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\NormalTok{stan\_file\_gcm\_decay\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"stan/ch12\_gcm\_decay\_single.stan"}
\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(gcm\_decay\_single\_stan, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_decay\_single.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch12_gcm_decay_single.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_gcm\_decay\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_gcm\_decay\_single)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Fitting One Agent Per
Scenario}\label{fitting-one-agent-per-scenario}

We pick one representative agent per scenario with \(\lambda = 0.2\),
\(c = 2\), bias \(= 0.5\), and uniform attention, and fit the decay GCM
to each.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{decay\_fit\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_decay\_single\_fits.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{decay\_single\_fit\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{static           =} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{w =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{c =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{lambda =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bias =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =} \DecValTok{20001}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{contingent\_shift =} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{w =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{c =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{lambda =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bias =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =} \DecValTok{20003}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{drift            =} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{w =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{c =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{lambda =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bias =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =} \DecValTok{20002}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{stan\_data\_from\_agent }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_df) \{}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{N\_total               =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(agent\_df),}
    \AttributeTok{N\_features             =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
    \AttributeTok{y                     =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(agent\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response),}
    \AttributeTok{obs                   =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(agent\_df[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
    \AttributeTok{cat\_feedback          =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(agent\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{observed\_feedback),}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_w\_alpha         =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_log\_c\_mu        =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_log\_c\_sigma     =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_SD,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_log\_lambda\_mu   =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_log\_lambda\_sigma =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_SD,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_alpha      =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_bias\_beta       =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics       =} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(decay\_fit\_file)) \{}
\NormalTok{  decay\_fits }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ purrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{imap}\NormalTok{(decay\_single\_fit\_data, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_df, scen) \{}
\NormalTok{    dat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{stan\_data\_from\_agent}\NormalTok{(agent\_df)}
\NormalTok{    pf  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      mod\_gcm\_decay\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ dat, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
                                      \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_gcm\_decay\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ dat,}
      \AttributeTok{init            =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf)) pf }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{availableCores}\NormalTok{()),}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1500}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{,}
                         \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_decay\_single\_"}\NormalTok{, scen, }\StringTok{".rds"}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{    fit}
\NormalTok{  \})}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(decay\_fits, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(f) f}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{output\_files}\NormalTok{()), decay\_fit\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Decay GCM fits computed and saved.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  decay\_fits }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(decay\_single\_fit\_data), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
      \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{,}
                   \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_decay\_single\_"}\NormalTok{, scen, }\StringTok{".rds"}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{    \}),}
    \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(decay\_single\_fit\_data)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing decay GCM fits.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{MCMC Diagnostic Battery Across
Scenarios}\label{mcmc-diagnostic-battery-across-scenarios}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{    fit,}

\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  tbl }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen)}
\NormalTok{\})}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(decay\_diag\_tbls }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{        dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(scenario, metric, value, threshold, pass))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{walk}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(decay\_fits), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"=== Pair plot:"}\NormalTok{, scen, }\StringTok{"===}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(bayesplot}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mcmc\_pairs}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    decay\_fits[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"w[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{diag\_fun     =} \StringTok{"dens"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{off\_diag\_fun =} \StringTok{"hex"}
\NormalTok{  ))}
\NormalTok{\})}

\begin{verbatim}
=== Pair plot: static ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_decay_diagnostics-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{verbatim}
=== Pair plot: contingent_shift ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_decay_diagnostics-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{verbatim}
=== Pair plot: drift ===
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_decay_diagnostics-3.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{What to watch for in the pair plot.} A
\texttt{log\_c}/\texttt{log\_lambda} ridge is expected on static data
--- both narrow the effective similarity average, so the data
under-constrain their difference. On contingent-shift data, post-flip
reversal dynamics inject strong temporal evidence that tensions the two
parameters against each other, and the ridge should shrink markedly. On
drift data, the ridge also narrows (boundary tracking exercises
\(\lambda\)) but typically less sharply, consistent with the weaker
identifiability signal that assumption-matched tasks provide.

\subsection{Prior-Posterior Update Plots Across
Scenarios}\label{prior-posterior-update-plots-across-scenarios}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{decay\_pp\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ purrr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{imap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(decay\_fits, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit, scen) \{}
\NormalTok{  d }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"w[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{)))}
  \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen, }\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{,      }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{log\_c,      }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen, }\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{log\_lambda, }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen, }\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"w[1]"}\NormalTok{,       }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{w[1]}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{,     }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen, }\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{,       }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{bias,       }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}

\FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{n\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{4000}
\NormalTok{prior\_ref }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{,      }\AttributeTok{value =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_prior, LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_MEAN,      LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_SD),}
         \AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_prior, LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_SD),}
         \AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"w[1]"}\NormalTok{,       }\AttributeTok{value =} \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(n\_prior),         }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{parameter =} \StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{,       }\AttributeTok{value =} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(n\_prior, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),   }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  tidyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{crossing}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{truth\_lines }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{parameter =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"w[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{value     =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(prior\_ref, decay\_pp\_df }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} 
                   \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{)))),}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ source, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ source)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prior =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{posterior =} \StringTok{"\#006D4E"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Prior → Posterior Update: Decay GCM Across Scenarios"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red dashed line = true generating value used for the single{-}agent fit"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}

\subsection{One-Shot Parameter Recovery Across
Scenarios}\label{one-shot-parameter-recovery-across-scenarios}

We sweep a 3 × 3 grid of \((c, \lambda)\) truths, with 5 independent
simulated datasets per cell per scenario (135 fits total), and report
recovery scatters.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{decay\_recovery\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch12\_decay\_recovery\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{run\_decay\_recovery\_iter }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scenario, c\_true, lambda\_true, iteration) \{}
\NormalTok{  subject\_seed }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ iteration }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{10000} \SpecialCharTok{+}
                   \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(c\_true }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
                   \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
                   \ControlFlowTok{switch}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{static =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{drift =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{contingent\_shift =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  agent\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_id     =}\NormalTok{ iteration,}
    \AttributeTok{scenario     =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{w            =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{c            =}\NormalTok{ c\_true,}
    \AttributeTok{lambda       =}\NormalTok{ lambda\_true,}
    \AttributeTok{bias         =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  dat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{stan\_data\_from\_agent}\NormalTok{(agent\_df)}
\NormalTok{  pf  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_gcm\_decay\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ dat, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
                                    \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed),}
    \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_gcm\_decay\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ dat,}
      \AttributeTok{init            =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf)) pf }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{seed            =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed,}
      \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) \{ }\FunctionTok{message}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Fit error: "}\NormalTok{, e}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{message); }\ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{ \}}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit)) }\FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lambda"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  diag }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{scenario    =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{c\_true      =}\NormalTok{ c\_true,}
    \AttributeTok{lambda\_true =}\NormalTok{ lambda\_true,}
    \AttributeTok{iteration   =}\NormalTok{ iteration,}
    \AttributeTok{log\_c\_mean       =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mean[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{log\_lambda\_mean  =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mean[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{c\_mean           =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mean[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"c"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{lambda\_mean      =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mean[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"lambda"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{log\_c\_q5         =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{q5[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{log\_c\_q95        =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{q95[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{log\_lambda\_q5    =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{q5[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{log\_lambda\_q95   =}\NormalTok{ s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{q95[s}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{],}
    \AttributeTok{had\_divergence   =} \FunctionTok{any}\NormalTok{(diag}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{num\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(decay\_recovery\_file)) \{}
\NormalTok{  recovery\_plan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{scenario    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{c\_true      =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{5.0}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{lambda\_true =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.02}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{iteration   =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(multisession, }\AttributeTok{workers =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{availableCores}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  decay\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{future\_pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{scenario    =}\NormalTok{ recovery\_plan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{scenario,}
      \AttributeTok{c\_true      =}\NormalTok{ recovery\_plan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{c\_true,}
      \AttributeTok{lambda\_true =}\NormalTok{ recovery\_plan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{lambda\_true,}
      \AttributeTok{iteration   =}\NormalTok{ recovery\_plan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{iteration}
\NormalTok{    ),}
\NormalTok{    run\_decay\_recovery\_iter,}
    \AttributeTok{.options =} \FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{scheduling =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{plan}\NormalTok{(sequential)}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(decay\_recovery, decay\_recovery\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Decay recovery sweep complete.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  decay\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(decay\_recovery\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing decay recovery sweep.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}
