\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{decay\_recovery\_long }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ decay\_recovery }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(scenario, c\_true, lambda\_true, iteration, had\_divergence,}
\NormalTok{                log\_c\_mean, log\_lambda\_mean) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{cols      =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(log\_c\_mean, log\_lambda\_mean),}
    \AttributeTok{names\_to  =} \StringTok{"variable"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"estimated"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{parameter =} \FunctionTok{if\_else}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_c\_mean"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{truth     =} \FunctionTok{if\_else}\NormalTok{(parameter }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(c\_true), }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(lambda\_true)),}
    \AttributeTok{scenario  =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(decay\_recovery\_long, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ truth, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ estimated, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_grid}\NormalTok{(parameter }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ scenario, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{),}
                     \AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Had divergence"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"One{-}Shot Parameter Recovery for the Decay GCM"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Posterior mean vs true generating value; 5 iterations per (c, lambda) cell, 3 scenarios"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True value (log scale)"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Estimated value (posterior mean)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_decay_recovery_sweep-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Interpretation.} The \texttt{log\_c} row is near-diagonal across
all three scenarios --- the sensitivity parameter is well-identified
regardless of task structure. The \texttt{log\_lambda} row is the
diagnostic: scatter under static data (the decay parameter is weakly
identified, so posterior means regress toward the prior mean), tighter
under contingent-shift (post-flip recovery dynamics directly constrain
\(\lambda\)), and well-recovered but more diffuse under smooth drift
(the assumption-matched scenario recovers \(\lambda\) at the nominal
calibration rate, but without the ``bonus'' constraint the abrupt
reversals provide). As the assumption match table above predicts, better
assumption match does not automatically mean stronger identifiability
--- the misspecified contingent-shift task exercises the forgetting
mechanism more sharply than the matched drift task does.

\subsection{Simulation-Based Calibration Checks Across
Scenarios}\label{simulation-based-calibration-checks-across-scenarios}

SBC is the calibration test proper: draw parameters from their priors,
simulate data with those parameters, fit the model, and check that the
posterior ranks of the true parameter are uniform. We run a separate SBC
sweep for each scenario.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{make\_decay\_sbc\_generator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scenario) \{}
  \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
\NormalTok{      log\_c\_true      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_MEAN,      LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{      log\_lambda\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{      c\_true          }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(log\_c\_true)}
\NormalTok{      lambda\_true     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(log\_lambda\_true)}
\NormalTok{      w1\_true         }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      bias\_true       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbeta}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{      subject\_seed }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1e8}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      agent\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{agent\_id     =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{scenario     =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
        \AttributeTok{w            =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(w1\_true, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ w1\_true),}
        \AttributeTok{c            =}\NormalTok{ c\_true,}
        \AttributeTok{lambda       =}\NormalTok{ lambda\_true,}
        \AttributeTok{bias         =}\NormalTok{ bias\_true,}
        \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed}
\NormalTok{      )}

      \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{log\_c      =}\NormalTok{ log\_c\_true,}
          \AttributeTok{log\_lambda =}\NormalTok{ log\_lambda\_true,}
          \StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{w[1]}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}     \OtherTok{=}\NormalTok{ w1\_true,}
          \AttributeTok{bias       =}\NormalTok{ bias\_true}
\NormalTok{        ),}
        \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{stan\_data\_from\_agent}\NormalTok{(agent\_df)}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{run\_decay\_sbc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{n\_sims =} \DecValTok{200}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  cache\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_decay\_sbc\_"}\NormalTok{, scenario, }\StringTok{".rds"}\NormalTok{))}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\NormalTok{regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{\&\&} \FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(cache\_file)) \{}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded cached decay SBC for scenario:"}\NormalTok{, scenario, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(cache\_file))}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  gen }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_decay\_sbc\_generator}\NormalTok{(scenario)}
\NormalTok{  backend }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_gcm\_decay\_single,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup   =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{chains        =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta   =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh       =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  datasets }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(gen, }\AttributeTok{n\_sims =}\NormalTok{ n\_sims)}
\NormalTok{  res }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    datasets,}
\NormalTok{    backend,}
    \AttributeTok{cache\_mode     =} \StringTok{"results"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{cache\_location =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_decay\_sbc\_cache\_"}\NormalTok{, scenario)),}
    \AttributeTok{keep\_fits      =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(res, cache\_file)}
\NormalTok{  res}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{decay\_sbc\_static }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{run\_decay\_sbc}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{,           }\AttributeTok{n\_sims =} \DecValTok{200}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\NormalTok{  sbc\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stats }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scenario)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(variable }\SpecialCharTok{\%in\%} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"w[1]"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"bias"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{)))}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(decay\_sbc\_stats, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ simulated\_value, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"SBC{-}Level Parameter Recovery: Decay GCM"}\NormalTok{,}

\subsection{Discussion: Task Structure Governs
Identifiability}\label{discussion-task-structure-governs-identifiability}

Under static data, the \texttt{log\_lambda} parameter cannot really be
identified --- the posterior does not contain the truth at the nominal
rate because the data carry almost no information about forgetting. The
\texttt{log\_c} curve is well-behaved on the same data: the sensitivity
parameter is identified regardless. Under contingent-shift and drift
data, both parameters recover well, with contingent-shift providing the
sharpest \(\lambda\) constraint (abrupt reversals) and smooth drift
providing the best-matched but less forceful identifiability.

This is the identifiability argument as an empirical claim rather than
an algebraic one. The decay GCM's \emph{parameter} is always the same.
Its \emph{identifiability} is a joint property of the model and the
task: when the task exercises forgetting --- whether through a drifting
boundary or through performance-contingent reversals --- the model
recovers \(\lambda\) cleanly; when it does not, \(\lambda\) is
indistinguishable from a slightly wider similarity kernel.

A secondary observation emerges when we look closer at the calibration
checks: while parameter recovery (the scatter plot) looks excellent for
\texttt{log\_c} across all scenarios, the SBC rank histograms reveal a
subtle ``worry'' for \texttt{log\_c} specifically in the drift scenario.
This highlights a fundamental distinction in Bayesian validation:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{Point Recovery (The ``What''):} This asks if the posterior
  \emph{mean} is close to the truth. If your recovery plot is diagonal,
  your model is right ``on average.''
\item
  \textbf{Coverage Calibration (The ``How Sure''):} This asks if the
  posterior \emph{width} (the uncertainty) is honest. If the model is
  90\% confident, is it actually right 90\% of the time?
\end{itemize}

SBC is designed to catch the latter. In the drift scenario, the model is
right about the value of \texttt{log\_c} (good point recovery), but it
is slightly overconfident---it underestimates its own uncertainty. One
might wonder: \textbf{since the model has \(\lambda\), why doesn't the
decay parameter just ``map'' the drift and leave \(c\) alone?}

The answer lies in the \textbf{Smeared Memory} problem. Even with an
optimal \(\lambda\), the agent doesn't categorise based on only the
single last trial; they need a ``window'' of recent exemplars to be
effective. Because the boundary is drifting \emph{during} that window,
the exemplars in memory become spatially ``smeared'' --- a location that
was Category 0 five trials ago might be Category 1 now.

While \(\lambda\) can tell the model to trust recent trials more, it is
a blunt temporal instrument. It cannot resolve the spatial conflict that
arises when two different category labels occupy the same coordinates in
the recent memory window. The model's likelihood, having no parameter
for ``environmental volatility,'' is forced to explain this residual
friction using spatial sensitivity (\(c\)). It performs a form of
\textbf{attribution error}, interpreting this temporal volatility as
spatial overlap and settling on an overly precise estimate of \(c\).
Because the model is using the wrong ``tool'' to explain the noise, it
converges on a narrow posterior that doesn't cover the true value as
often as it should. If we only looked at the recovery plot, we would
miss this overconfidence entirely. This is why SBC is the ``gold
standard'' check: it ensures the model isn't just accurate, but also
appropriately humble about what it knows.

\textbf{How the three scenarios match the model's assumptions.} The
decay GCM assumes exemplars decay exponentially as a function of trial
age, with no separate mechanism for abrupt reversals or directed drift.
The \emph{static} scenario violates nothing but offers no signal for
\(\lambda\). The \emph{contingent-shift} scenario is a genuine
misspecification --- the true data-generating process has abrupt
reversals that no smooth decay can exactly reproduce --- yet it provides
the sharpest \(\lambda\) signal because post-flip recovery speed depends
specifically on how fast old, now-wrong exemplars are discounted. The
posterior predictive checks at scenario transitions (not plotted here)
should reveal systematic underperformance right after each flip; that
residual misfit is the handle for a future extension with an explicit
change-point mechanism. The \emph{smooth-drift} scenario is the
assumption-matched case: exponentially downweighting past exemplars is a
defensible approximation to ``evidence collected under a different
category mean is stale.'' SBC passes cleanly here, but \(\lambda\)
recovery is less sharp than under contingent-shift because drift does
not impose the stark temporal discontinuities that most strongly
constrain the forgetting rate.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{The \(c\)/\(\lambda\) Joint Posterior: Ridge
Geometry and Task
Resolution}{The c/\textbackslash lambda Joint Posterior: Ridge Geometry and Task Resolution}}\label{the-clambda-joint-posterior-ridge-geometry-and-task-resolution}

The pair plots from the single-agent scenario fits revealed a
\texttt{log\_c}/\texttt{log\_lambda} ridge on static data. Understanding
the geometry of this ridge --- and why task structure resolves it --- is
worth making explicit, because Chapter 12 faces a structurally similar
problem (\(q\)/\(r\) in the Kalman filter) and resolves it differently.

\textbf{Why does the ridge form?} Both \(c\) and \(\lambda\) narrow the
effective contribution of any given exemplar to the category mean:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  A high \(c\) shrinks contributions from spatially distant exemplars
  (similarity decays faster with distance).
\item
  A high \(\lambda\) shrinks contributions from temporally distant
  exemplars (similarity decays faster with trial age).
\end{itemize}

Under a stationary task with balanced categories, distance and age are
nearly uncorrelated --- older exemplars are not systematically farther
from the current stimulus. This means that increasing \(c\) and
decreasing \(\lambda\) (or vice versa) can produce nearly the same
observed choice probabilities, creating the elongated posterior ridge
you see in the pair plots.

\textbf{Why is the GCM ridge less severe than the Kalman filter's
\(q\)/\(r\) ridge?} The two parameters operate on orthogonal dimensions:
\(c\) operates on the spatial axis (feature distances), \(\lambda\) on
the temporal axis (trial age). Even under static data, choice
probabilities have some leverage on each independently --- \(c\) is
constrained by how performance differs across stimuli at varying spatial
distances, while \(\lambda\) is partially constrained by the
early-learning transient (the model must form initial memories before it
can categorize, and the learning rate is \(\lambda\)-dependent). In the
Kalman filter, \(q\) and \(r\) both scale the \emph{same} likelihood at
decision time (the multivariate normal variance at trial \(t\) is a
function of both), so their directions are more nearly collinear and the
ridge is typically more severe. Chapter 12 introduces an explicit
reparameterization (the SNR parameter \(\rho = q/r\)) to address this;
the GCM does not need it here.

\textbf{Task structure as the primary handle.} The SBC results above
confirm that the contingent-shift scenario cleanly resolves \(\lambda\)
without any reparameterization. The reversal dynamics create a temporal
structure that spatial sensitivity cannot account for: the \emph{time}
at which performance drops is determined by \(\lambda\) (how fast old,
now-wrong exemplars are forgotten) in a way that is independent of \(c\)
(which only governs how much nearby vs.~distant exemplars are weighted).
This is the key empirical signature of a parameter that is theoretically
identifiable but under-constrained by a particular task design: swap in
a task that exercises the mechanism, and identifiability follows.

\textbf{When would reparameterization be needed for the GCM?} If you
needed to run the decay GCM exclusively on static data, the ridge would
be a genuine sampling problem. In that case, a reparameterization could
help: write \(\log s = \log c + \log \lambda\) (the overall ``forgetting
rate'' in combined spatial-temporal units) and
\(\log d = \log c - \log \lambda\) (the relative weighting of spatial
vs.~temporal decay). The joint posterior of \((\log s, \log d)\) would
have a narrower marginal in the \(\log s\) direction (identified by
overall accuracy) and a wider marginal in the \(\log d\) direction (the
degenerate direction).

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{The Multilevel Decay GCM: Scaling to populations and changing
environments}\label{the-multilevel-decay-gcm-scaling-to-populations-and-changing-environments}

Having demonstrated that memory decay is essential for non-stationary
environments and that its parameters are identifiable given the right
task structure, we now combine these mechanisms into a full hierarchical
model. The \textbf{Multilevel Decay GCM} allows us to estimate
individual cognitive differences while sharing information across the
population to constrain our estimates. This is our ``Final Boss'' model:
population-aware, psychologically realistic, and robust to environmental
change.

\subsection{Mathematical Formulation: The Hierarchical
Architecture}\label{mathematical-formulation-the-hierarchical-architecture}

This model uses log-normal hierarchies for sensitivity and forgetting,
and logit-normal hierarchies for attention and response bias. With two
features, the simplex-valued attention vector \(\mathbf{w}_j\) is fully
determined by \(w_{j,1}\), so the natural simplex hierarchy (Dirichlet
around a population mean \(\bar{\mathbf{w}}\) with concentration
\(\kappa\)) collapses to a Beta on \(w_{j,1}\). We use a
\textbf{logit-normal} in its place --- equivalent in support and shape
to the Beta, but its non-centered parameterisation samples much more
cleanly than a Dirichlet hierarchy in NUTS, which is what
\texttt{ch12\_gcm\_ml\_decay.stan} implements.

\textbf{Population-level distributions (Priors):}

\[
\begin{aligned}
\bar{\mu}_{\text{logit}\,w_1}, \bar{\sigma}_{\text{logit}\,w_1} &\sim \text{Priors} \\
\bar{\mu}_{\log c}, \bar{\sigma}_{\log c} &\sim \text{Priors} \\
\bar{\mu}_{\log \lambda}, \bar{\sigma}_{\log \lambda} &\sim \text{Priors} \\
\bar{\mu}_{\text{logit}\,\beta}, \bar{\sigma}_{\text{logit}\,\beta} &\sim \text{Priors}
\end{aligned}
\]

\textbf{Individual-level parameters (for subject \(j\), with
\(w_{j,2} = 1 - w_{j,1}\)):}

\[
\begin{aligned}
\text{logit}(w_{j,1}) &\sim \text{Normal}(\bar{\mu}_{\text{logit}\,w_1}, \bar{\sigma}_{\text{logit}\,w_1}) \\
\log c_j &\sim \text{Normal}(\bar{\mu}_{\log c}, \bar{\sigma}_{\log c}) \\
\log \lambda_j &\sim \text{Normal}(\bar{\mu}_{\log \lambda}, \bar{\sigma}_{\log \lambda}) \\
\text{logit}(\beta_j) &\sim \text{Normal}(\bar{\mu}_{\text{logit}\,\beta}, \bar{\sigma}_{\text{logit}\,\beta})
\end{aligned}
\]

Each individual-level scalar uses a non-centered offset
(\texttt{z\ \textasciitilde{}\ N(0,1)}, with the actual parameter equal
to \texttt{pop\_mean\ +\ pop\_sd\ *\ z}) to avoid the funnel that direct
centred parameterisations produce when subject-level data are weakly
informative.

\textbf{Trial-level Generative Process (for trial \(i\) of subject
\(j\)):}

\[
\begin{aligned}
d_{iej} &= \sum_f w_{jf} |x_{if} - x_{ef}| \\
\eta_{iej} &= e^{-\lambda_j(i - t_e)} \cdot e^{-c_j d_{iej}} \\
m_{k,ij} &= \frac{\sum_{e \in C_k} \eta_{iej}}{\sum_{e \in C_k} e^{-\lambda_j(i - t_e)}} \\
p_{ij} &= \frac{\beta_j \cdot m_{1,ij}}{\beta_j \cdot m_{1,ij} + (1 - \beta_j) \cdot m_{0,ij}} \\
y_{ij} &\sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_{ij})
\end{aligned}
\]

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{Shrinkage:} The model uses the population means to ``pull''
  uncertain individual estimates toward the group average. This is
  especially useful for \(\lambda_j\) in scenarios where an individual's
  data might be locally uninformative.
\item
  \textbf{Effective Sample Size:} The denominator of \(m_{k,ij}\) is now
  the \textbf{decay-weighted effective number of exemplars}, ensuring
  that old, forgotten evidence doesn't inflate the mean similarity.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Simulating Data for the Multilevel Decay
GCM}\label{simulating-data-for-the-multilevel-decay-gcm}

We simulate three small populations of agents --- one per scenario
(static, smooth drift, performance-contingent shifts) --- using the
\textbf{same} population-level parameters across scenarios.
Subject-level parameters are drawn around the population means using the
logit-normal/log-normal hierarchies that match the Stan model exactly.
Because the simulator and the inference model now share a
parameterisation, recovery scatters can be read directly without
translating between Dirichlet concentration and logit SD.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{simulate\_multilevel\_decay\_gcm }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects, stimulus\_info, n\_blocks,}
\NormalTok{                                          scenario,}
\NormalTok{                                          base\_seed,}
\NormalTok{                                          pop\_logit\_w\_mean,    pop\_logit\_w\_sd,}
\NormalTok{                                          pop\_log\_c\_mean,      pop\_log\_c\_sd,}
\NormalTok{                                          pop\_log\_lambda\_mean, pop\_log\_lambda\_sd,}
\NormalTok{                                          pop\_logit\_bias\_mean, pop\_logit\_bias\_sd) \{}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(base\_seed)}
\NormalTok{  ind\_logit\_w    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects, pop\_logit\_w\_mean,    pop\_logit\_w\_sd)}
\NormalTok{  ind\_w1         }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(ind\_logit\_w)}
\NormalTok{  ind\_log\_c      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects, pop\_log\_c\_mean,      pop\_log\_c\_sd)}
\NormalTok{  ind\_log\_lambda }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects, pop\_log\_lambda\_mean, pop\_log\_lambda\_sd)}
\NormalTok{  ind\_logit\_bias }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects, pop\_logit\_bias\_mean, pop\_logit\_bias\_sd)}

\NormalTok{  true\_params }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_id        =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects),}
    \AttributeTok{scenario        =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{logit\_w\_true    =}\NormalTok{ ind\_logit\_w,}
    \AttributeTok{w1\_true         =}\NormalTok{ ind\_w1,}
    \AttributeTok{w2\_true         =} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ ind\_w1,}
    \AttributeTok{log\_c\_true      =}\NormalTok{ ind\_log\_c,}
    \AttributeTok{c\_true          =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(ind\_log\_c),}
    \AttributeTok{log\_lambda\_true =}\NormalTok{ ind\_log\_lambda,}
    \AttributeTok{lambda\_true     =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(ind\_log\_lambda),}
    \AttributeTok{logit\_bias\_true =}\NormalTok{ ind\_logit\_bias,}
    \AttributeTok{bias\_true       =} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(ind\_logit\_bias)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  all\_agent\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vector}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"list"}\NormalTok{, n\_subjects)}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (j }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects)) \{}
\NormalTok{    subject\_seed }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_seed }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{1000} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ j}
\NormalTok{    all\_agent\_data[[j]] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_decay\_agent}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{agent\_id     =}\NormalTok{ j,}
      \AttributeTok{scenario     =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
      \AttributeTok{w            =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(ind\_w1[j], }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ ind\_w1[j]),}
      \AttributeTok{c            =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(ind\_log\_c[j]),}
      \AttributeTok{lambda       =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(ind\_log\_lambda[j]),}
      \AttributeTok{bias         =} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(ind\_logit\_bias[j]),}
      \AttributeTok{subject\_seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data        =} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(all\_agent\_data),}
    \AttributeTok{true\_params =}\NormalTok{ true\_params}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{n\_subjects\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{10}
\NormalTok{pop\_params\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_w\_mean    =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,        }\CommentTok{\# median w1 ≈ 0.5}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_w\_sd      =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{,      }\CommentTok{\# ±2SD covers w1 ∈ (0.20, 0.80)}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_c\_mean      =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{), }\CommentTok{\# population median c = 2}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_c\_sd        =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_lambda\_mean =} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{), }\CommentTok{\# population median λ = 0.2}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_lambda\_sd   =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_bias\_mean =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_bias\_sd   =} \FloatTok{0.3}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{ml\_scenarios }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{ml\_decay\_files }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) }\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{data  =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_ml\_decay\_data\_"}\NormalTok{,  scen, }\StringTok{".csv"}\NormalTok{)),}
  \AttributeTok{truth =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_ml\_decay\_truth\_"}\NormalTok{, scen, }\StringTok{".csv"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios)}

\NormalTok{ml\_files\_exist }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{unlist}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_files, }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(.x}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{data, .x}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{truth)))))}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\NormalTok{ml\_files\_exist) \{}
\NormalTok{  ml\_decay\_sims }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
\NormalTok{    out }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_multilevel\_decay\_gcm}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{n\_subjects          =}\NormalTok{ n\_subjects\_sim,}
      \AttributeTok{stimulus\_info       =}\NormalTok{ stimulus\_info,}
      \AttributeTok{n\_blocks            =}\NormalTok{ n\_blocks,}
      \AttributeTok{scenario            =}\NormalTok{ scen,}
      \AttributeTok{base\_seed           =} \FunctionTok{match}\NormalTok{(scen, ml\_scenarios) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{1000} \SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{42}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_w\_mean    =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_logit\_w\_mean,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_w\_sd      =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_logit\_w\_sd,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_c\_mean      =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_log\_c\_mean,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_c\_sd        =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_log\_c\_sd,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_lambda\_mean =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_log\_lambda\_mean,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_log\_lambda\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_log\_lambda\_sd,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_bias\_mean =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_logit\_bias\_mean,}
      \AttributeTok{pop\_logit\_bias\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ pop\_params\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pop\_logit\_bias\_sd}
\NormalTok{    )}
    \FunctionTok{write\_csv}\NormalTok{(out}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{data,        ml\_decay\_files[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{data)}
    \FunctionTok{write\_csv}\NormalTok{(out}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{true\_params, ml\_decay\_files[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{truth)}
\NormalTok{    out}
\NormalTok{  \}) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  ml\_decay\_sims }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) }\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data        =} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_files[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{data,  }\AttributeTok{show\_col\_types =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{true\_params =} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_files[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{truth, }\AttributeTok{show\_col\_types =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{ml\_decay\_all }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen)}
\NormalTok{  ml\_decay\_sims[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{data }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ ml\_scenarios,}
                           \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Smooth drift"}\NormalTok{,}
                                      \StringTok{"Performance{-}contingent"}\NormalTok{)))}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_all,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial\_within\_subject, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ cumulative\_accuracy, }\AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey30"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun =}\NormalTok{ mean, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"line"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{group =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
               \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{scenario, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Multilevel Decay GCM: simulated learning curves across scenarios"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(n\_subjects\_sim, }\StringTok{" agents per scenario; thin grey = individuals, "}\NormalTok{,}
                      \StringTok{"thick blue = population mean"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Cumulative accuracy"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_simulate_multilevel_decay_data-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The static panel converges quickly to ceiling; smooth drift plateaus
below ceiling because the boundary keeps moving; performance-contingent
shifts produce a noisy plateau as different subjects flip at different
absolute trials and average over each other.

\subsection{Stan Implementation}\label{stan-implementation-2}

The Stan model implements the hierarchy described above, with three
architectural choices that matter for performance:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{The trial-by-trial likelihood lives in the \texttt{model}
  block, not in \texttt{transformed\ parameters}.} Memory is held in
  plain \texttt{int} arrays during the loop, so the autodiff tape never
  sees the per-trial scan. \texttt{prob\_cat1} and (optionally gated)
  \texttt{log\_lik} are recomputed once per saved draw in
  \texttt{generated\ quantities}.
\item
  \textbf{All hierarchies are non-centered} (logit-normal NCP for
  \texttt{w\_1}; standard z-offset for \texttt{log\_c},
  \texttt{log\_lambda}, and \texttt{logit\_bias}).
\item
  \textbf{Pairwise feature distances are precomputed in
  \texttt{transformed\ data}}, indexed by \texttt{stim\_id} instead of
  read from per-trial features.
\end{itemize}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{gcm\_ml\_decay\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Multilevel Decay Generalized Context Model}
\StringTok{// All individual parameters use non{-}centred parameterisation:}
\StringTok{//   {-} w\_1 via logit{-}normal NCP (eliminates Dirichlet funnel geometry)}
\StringTok{//   {-} log\_c, log\_lambda, logit\_bias via standard z{-}offset NCP}
\StringTok{// Sequential likelihood is computed directly in the model block (no}
\StringTok{// prob\_cat1 stored in transformed parameters), avoiding a large var{-}vector}
\StringTok{// on the autodiff tape. prob\_cat1 and log\_lik are recomputed once per saved}
\StringTok{// draw in generated quantities.}

\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_total;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_subjects;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_features;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} N\_unique\_stim;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} max\_trials\_per\_subject;}

\StringTok{  array[N\_subjects] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_total\textgreater{} subj\_start;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_subjects] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_total\textgreater{} subj\_end;}

\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=1, upper=N\_unique\_stim\textgreater{} stim\_id;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_unique\_stim, N\_features] real unique\_obs;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_total] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} cat\_feedback;}

\StringTok{  // Hyperparameters}
\StringTok{  real prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_sd\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_pop\_log\_c\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_log\_c\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_log\_c\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_mu;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_sigma;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} run\_diagnostics;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed data \{}
\StringTok{  array[N\_unique\_stim, N\_unique\_stim, N\_features] real stimulus\_abs\_diff;}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:N\_unique\_stim)}
\StringTok{    for (k in 1:N\_unique\_stim)}
\StringTok{      for (f in 1:N\_features)}
\StringTok{        stimulus\_abs\_diff[i, k, f] = abs(unique\_obs[i, f] {-} unique\_obs[k, f]);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  // Population attention weight: logit{-}normal NCP for w\_1 (w\_2 = 1 {-} w\_1)}
\StringTok{  real pop\_logit\_w\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} pop\_logit\_w\_sd;}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] z\_w;}

\StringTok{  // Population c, lambda, bias}
\StringTok{  real pop\_log\_c\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} pop\_log\_c\_sd;}
\StringTok{  real pop\_log\_lambda\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} pop\_log\_lambda\_sd;}
\StringTok{  real pop\_logit\_bias\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} pop\_logit\_bias\_sd;}

\StringTok{  // Non{-}centred individual offsets}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] z\_log\_c;}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] z\_log\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] z\_logit\_bias;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] subj\_log\_c;}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_subjects] subj\_log\_lambda;}
\StringTok{  vector\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{}[N\_subjects] subj\_bias;}
\StringTok{  array[N\_subjects] vector[N\_features] subj\_w;}

\StringTok{  for (j in 1:N\_subjects) \{}
\StringTok{    subj\_log\_c[j]      = pop\_log\_c\_mean + pop\_log\_c\_sd * z\_log\_c[j];}
\StringTok{    subj\_log\_lambda[j] = pop\_log\_lambda\_mean + pop\_log\_lambda\_sd * z\_log\_lambda[j];}
\StringTok{    subj\_bias[j]       = inv\_logit(pop\_logit\_bias\_mean + pop\_logit\_bias\_sd * z\_logit\_bias[j]);}
\StringTok{    real logit\_w       = pop\_logit\_w\_mean + pop\_logit\_w\_sd * z\_w[j];}
\StringTok{    subj\_w[j][1]       = inv\_logit(logit\_w);}
\StringTok{    subj\_w[j][2]       = 1.0 {-} subj\_w[j][1];}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  // Population priors}
\StringTok{  pop\_logit\_w\_mean    \textasciitilde{} normal(prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_mean, prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  pop\_logit\_w\_sd      \textasciitilde{} exponential(prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_sd\_lambda);}
\StringTok{  pop\_log\_c\_mean      \textasciitilde{} normal(prior\_pop\_log\_c\_mu, prior\_pop\_log\_c\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  pop\_log\_c\_sd        \textasciitilde{} exponential(prior\_pop\_log\_c\_lambda);}
\StringTok{  pop\_log\_lambda\_mean \textasciitilde{} normal(prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_mu, prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  pop\_log\_lambda\_sd   \textasciitilde{} exponential(prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_lambda);}
\StringTok{  pop\_logit\_bias\_mean \textasciitilde{} normal(prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_mu, prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_sigma);}
\StringTok{  pop\_logit\_bias\_sd   \textasciitilde{} exponential(prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_lambda);}

\StringTok{  // Non{-}centred priors}
\StringTok{  z\_w          \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_c      \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  z\_log\_lambda \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}
\StringTok{  z\_logit\_bias \textasciitilde{} std\_normal();}

\StringTok{  // Sequential likelihood — local workspace, one subject at a time}
\StringTok{  \{}
\StringTok{    array[max\_trials\_per\_subject] int mem\_stim;}
\StringTok{    array[max\_trials\_per\_subject] int mem\_cat;}
\StringTok{    array[max\_trials\_per\_subject] int mem\_trial;}

\StringTok{    for (j in 1:N\_subjects) \{}
\StringTok{      int n\_mem  = 0;}
\StringTok{      real c      = exp(subj\_log\_c[j]);}
\StringTok{      real lambda = exp(subj\_log\_lambda[j]);}
\StringTok{      real bias   = subj\_bias[j];}

\StringTok{      for (i in subj\_start[j]:subj\_end[j]) \{}
\StringTok{        real p\_i;}
\StringTok{        if (n\_mem == 0) \{}
\StringTok{          p\_i = bias;}
\StringTok{        \} else \{}
\StringTok{          real s1 = 0.0; real s0 = 0.0;}
\StringTok{          real w1 = 0.0; real w0 = 0.0;}
\StringTok{          int curr\_stim = stim\_id[i];}
\StringTok{          for (e in 1:n\_mem) \{}
\StringTok{            real dist = 0.0;}
\StringTok{            int past\_stim = mem\_stim[e];}
\StringTok{            for (f in 1:N\_features)}
\StringTok{              dist += subj\_w[j][f] * stimulus\_abs\_diff[curr\_stim, past\_stim, f];}
\StringTok{            real age   = (i {-} subj\_start[j] + 1.0) {-} mem\_trial[e];}
\StringTok{            real decay = exp({-}lambda * age);}
\StringTok{            real sim   = exp({-}c * dist);}
\StringTok{            real wt    = decay * sim;}
\StringTok{            if (mem\_cat[e] == 1) \{ s1 += wt; w1 += decay; \}}
\StringTok{            else                 \{ s0 += wt; w0 += decay; \}}
\StringTok{          \}}
\StringTok{          real m1  = (w1 \textgreater{} 1e{-}9) ? s1 / w1 : 0.0;}
\StringTok{          real m0  = (w0 \textgreater{} 1e{-}9) ? s0 / w0 : 0.0;}
\StringTok{          real num = bias * m1;}
\StringTok{          real den = num + (1.0 {-} bias) * m0;}
\StringTok{          p\_i = (den \textgreater{} 1e{-}9) ? num / den : bias;}
\StringTok{        \}}
\StringTok{        target += bernoulli\_lpmf(y[i] | fmax(1e{-}9, fmin(1.0 {-} 1e{-}9, p\_i)));}

\StringTok{        n\_mem += 1;}
\StringTok{        mem\_stim[n\_mem]  = stim\_id[i];}
\StringTok{        mem\_cat[n\_mem]   = cat\_feedback[i];}
\StringTok{        mem\_trial[n\_mem] = i {-} subj\_start[j] + 1;}
\StringTok{      \}}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  // Recompute once per saved draw (not on every leapfrog step).}
\StringTok{  // prob\_cat1 is always filled (needed for PPC); log\_lik only when run\_diagnostics=1.}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] prob\_cat1;}
\StringTok{  vector[N\_total] log\_lik = rep\_vector(0.0, N\_total);}

\StringTok{  \{}
\StringTok{    array[max\_trials\_per\_subject] int mem\_stim;}
\StringTok{    array[max\_trials\_per\_subject] int mem\_cat;}
\StringTok{    array[max\_trials\_per\_subject] int mem\_trial;}

\StringTok{    for (j in 1:N\_subjects) \{}
\StringTok{      int n\_mem  = 0;}
\StringTok{      real c      = exp(subj\_log\_c[j]);}
\StringTok{      real lambda = exp(subj\_log\_lambda[j]);}
\StringTok{      real bias   = subj\_bias[j];}

\StringTok{      for (i in subj\_start[j]:subj\_end[j]) \{}
\StringTok{        real p\_i;}
\StringTok{        if (n\_mem == 0) \{}
\StringTok{          p\_i = bias;}
\StringTok{        \} else \{}
\StringTok{          real s1 = 0.0; real s0 = 0.0;}
\StringTok{          real w1 = 0.0; real w0 = 0.0;}
\StringTok{          int curr\_stim = stim\_id[i];}
\StringTok{          for (e in 1:n\_mem) \{}
\StringTok{            real dist = 0.0;}
\StringTok{            int past\_stim = mem\_stim[e];}
\StringTok{            for (f in 1:N\_features)}
\StringTok{              dist += subj\_w[j][f] * stimulus\_abs\_diff[curr\_stim, past\_stim, f];}
\StringTok{            real age   = (i {-} subj\_start[j] + 1.0) {-} mem\_trial[e];}
\StringTok{            real decay = exp({-}lambda * age);}
\StringTok{            real sim   = exp({-}c * dist);}
\StringTok{            real wt    = decay * sim;}
\StringTok{            if (mem\_cat[e] == 1) \{ s1 += wt; w1 += decay; \}}
\StringTok{            else                 \{ s0 += wt; w0 += decay; \}}
\StringTok{          \}}
\StringTok{          real m1  = (w1 \textgreater{} 1e{-}9) ? s1 / w1 : 0.0;}
\StringTok{          real m0  = (w0 \textgreater{} 1e{-}9) ? s0 / w0 : 0.0;}
\StringTok{          real num = bias * m1;}
\StringTok{          real den = num + (1.0 {-} bias) * m0;}
\StringTok{          p\_i = (den \textgreater{} 1e{-}9) ? num / den : bias;}
\StringTok{        \}}
\StringTok{        real p\_clamp  = fmax(1e{-}9, fmin(1.0 {-} 1e{-}9, p\_i));}
\StringTok{        prob\_cat1[i]  = p\_clamp;}
\StringTok{        if (run\_diagnostics)}
\StringTok{          log\_lik[i]  = bernoulli\_lpmf(y[i] | p\_clamp);}

\StringTok{        n\_mem += 1;}
\StringTok{        mem\_stim[n\_mem]  = stim\_id[i];}
\StringTok{        mem\_cat[n\_mem]   = cat\_feedback[i];}
\StringTok{        mem\_trial[n\_mem] = i {-} subj\_start[j] + 1;}
\StringTok{      \}}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\NormalTok{stan\_file\_gcm\_ml\_decay }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"stan/ch12\_gcm\_ml\_decay.stan"}
\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(gcm\_ml\_decay\_stan, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_ml\_decay.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch12_gcm_ml_decay.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_gcm\_ml\_decay       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_gcm\_ml\_decay)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Building the Stan Data
List}\label{building-the-stan-data-list}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{build\_ml\_stan\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(ml\_data, stimulus\_info, }\AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L) \{}
\NormalTok{  ml\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_data }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, trial\_within\_subject) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{row =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{())}

\NormalTok{  unique\_stim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ stimulus\_info }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(stimulus) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{unique\_idx =} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{())}
\NormalTok{  stim\_lookup }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(unique\_stim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{unique\_idx, unique\_stim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stimulus)}

\NormalTok{  subj\_idx }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_data }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{start =} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(row), }\AttributeTok{end =} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(row), }\AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(agent\_id)}

  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{N\_total                =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ml\_data),}
    \AttributeTok{N\_subjects             =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(subj\_idx),}
    \AttributeTok{N\_features             =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
    \AttributeTok{N\_unique\_stim          =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(unique\_stim),}
    \AttributeTok{max\_trials\_per\_subject =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(subj\_idx}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{end }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ subj\_idx}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{start }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L)),}

    \AttributeTok{subj\_start             =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(subj\_idx}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{start),}
    \AttributeTok{subj\_end               =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(subj\_idx}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{end),}

    \AttributeTok{stim\_id                =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(stim\_lookup[}\FunctionTok{as.character}\NormalTok{(ml\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stimulus\_id)]),}
    \AttributeTok{y                      =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(ml\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response),}
    \AttributeTok{unique\_obs             =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(unique\_stim[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
    \AttributeTok{cat\_feedback           =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(ml\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{observed\_feedback),}

    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_mean      =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_sigma     =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_logit\_w\_sd\_lambda =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_log\_c\_mu          =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_log\_c\_sigma       =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_SD,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_log\_c\_lambda      =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_mu     =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_sigma  =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_SD,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_log\_lambda\_lambda =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_mu     =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_sigma  =} \FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_pop\_logit\_bias\_lambda =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics             =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(run\_diagnostics)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{Exponential(2)} priors on the population SDs
(\texttt{*\_lambda\ =\ 2}) have median 0.35 and 95th percentile ≈ 1.5
--- wide enough to allow real between-subject variation, tight enough to
keep NUTS off the worst part of the funnel when only 10 subjects are
observed. We keep \texttt{run\_diagnostics\ =\ 0} for the main fits
because we will not run LFO at the multilevel level here; flip it to
\texttt{1} only when computing per-trial \texttt{log\_lik} is needed.

\subsection{Fitting the Multilevel Decay GCM Across
Scenarios}\label{fitting-the-multilevel-decay-gcm-across-scenarios}

We compile once and fit each scenario in turn. With 10 subjects × 64
trials × 4 chains × 2000 iterations, expect each fit to take a few
minutes on a modern laptop.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ml\_decay\_fit\_files }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen)}
  \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch12\_gcm\_ml\_decay\_fit\_"}\NormalTok{, scen, }\StringTok{".rds"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{unlist}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_fit\_files)))) \{}
\NormalTok{  ml\_decay\_fits }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Fitting multilevel decay GCM:"}\NormalTok{, scen, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    dat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_ml\_stan\_data}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_sims[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{data, stimulus\_info,}
                              \AttributeTok{run\_diagnostics =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L)}
\NormalTok{    fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_gcm\_ml\_decay}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ dat,}
      \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{700} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{match}\NormalTok{(scen, ml\_scenarios),}
      \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{availableCores}\NormalTok{()),}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{max\_treedepth   =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{200}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{init            =} \FloatTok{0.5}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_fit\_files[[scen]])}
\NormalTok{    fit}
\NormalTok{  \}) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Multilevel decay GCM fits computed and saved.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  ml\_decay\_fits }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen)}
    \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_fit\_files[[scen]])}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{setNames}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing multilevel decay GCM fits.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing multilevel decay GCM fits.
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{MCMC Diagnostic Battery}\label{mcmc-diagnostic-battery-1}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ml\_decay\_diag }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
  \FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary\_table}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    ml\_decay\_fits[[scen]],}
    \AttributeTok{params =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"pop\_logit\_w\_mean"}\NormalTok{,    }\StringTok{"pop\_log\_c\_mean"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"pop\_log\_lambda\_mean"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"pop\_logit\_bias\_mean"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"pop\_logit\_w\_sd"}\NormalTok{,      }\StringTok{"pop\_log\_c\_sd"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"pop\_log\_lambda\_sd"}\NormalTok{,   }\StringTok{"pop\_logit\_bias\_sd"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen)}
\NormalTok{\})}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_diag }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{        dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(scenario, metric, value, threshold, pass))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 18 x 5
   scenario         metric                          value threshold pass 
   <chr>            <chr>                           <dbl> <chr>     <lgl>
 1 static           Divergences (zero tolerance)   0      == 0      TRUE 
 2 static           Max rank-normalised R-hat      2.84   < 1.01    FALSE
 3 static           Min bulk ESS                   4.65   > 400     FALSE
 4 static           Min tail ESS                  13.3    > 400     FALSE
 5 static           Min E-BFMI                     1.12   > 0.2     TRUE 
 6 static           Max MCSE / posterior SD        0.488  < 0.05    FALSE
 7 contingent_shift Divergences (zero tolerance)   0      == 0      TRUE 
 8 contingent_shift Max rank-normalised R-hat      2.93   < 1.01    FALSE
 9 contingent_shift Min bulk ESS                   4.59   > 400     FALSE
10 contingent_shift Min tail ESS                  11.2    > 400     FALSE
11 contingent_shift Min E-BFMI                     0.971  > 0.2     TRUE 
12 contingent_shift Max MCSE / posterior SD        0.492  < 0.05    FALSE
13 drift            Divergences (zero tolerance)   0      == 0      TRUE 
14 drift            Max rank-normalised R-hat      1.00   < 1.01    TRUE 
15 drift            Min bulk ESS                 944.     > 400     TRUE 
16 drift            Min tail ESS                 493.     > 400     TRUE 
17 drift            Min E-BFMI                     0.694  > 0.2     TRUE 
18 drift            Max MCSE / posterior SD        0.0302 < 0.05    TRUE 
\end{verbatim}

If the model is healthy you will see no divergences, R̂ \textless{} 1.01
across all population parameters, and ESS comfortably above 400 for the
population means. Subject-level scalars typically have somewhat lower
ESS, especially for \texttt{log\_lambda} under the static scenario ---
the data carry little information about forgetting when the boundary
never moves, so the posterior is partly pinned by the prior and the
partial pooling toward the population mean.

\subsection{Population-Level Recovery}\label{population-level-recovery}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{pop\_truth }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{with}\NormalTok{(pop\_params\_sim, }\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"pop\_logit\_w\_mean"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"pop\_log\_c\_mean"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"pop\_log\_lambda\_mean"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"pop\_logit\_bias\_mean"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{truth    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(pop\_logit\_w\_mean, pop\_log\_c\_mean,}
\NormalTok{               pop\_log\_lambda\_mean, pop\_logit\_bias\_mean)}
\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Extract all posterior draws for the population means}
\NormalTok{pop\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
  \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(ml\_decay\_fits[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(pop\_truth}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{all\_of}\NormalTok{(pop\_truth}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable), }
                 \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"variable"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen)}
\NormalTok{\}) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ ml\_scenarios,}
                           \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Drift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Contingent shift"}\NormalTok{)),}
         \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(variable, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ pop\_truth}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable))}

\CommentTok{\# Precompute prior densities for overlay}
\NormalTok{prior\_params }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(pop\_truth}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ pop\_truth}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{variable),}
  \AttributeTok{mu    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_MEAN, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{sigma =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, LOG\_C\_PRIOR\_SD, LOG\_LAMBDA\_PRIOR\_SD, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{prior\_density }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ prior\_params }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(variable) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{reframe}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(mu }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FloatTok{3.5}\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{sigma, mu }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{3.5}\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{sigma, }\AttributeTok{length.out =} \DecValTok{200}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{dnorm}\NormalTok{(x, mu, sigma)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Prior distribution}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ prior\_density, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ x, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ y), }
            \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Posterior distributions per scenario}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ pop\_draws, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ scenario), }
               \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Simulation truth}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ pop\_truth, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ truth), }
             \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{variable, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Population{-}level parameter recovery"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Posterior densities vs Simulation Truth (red dashed) and Prior (black dotted)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Parameter value (transformed scale)"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{11-CategorizationModelsExemplars_files/figure-pdf/ch12_ml_decay_population_recovery-1.pdf}

\subsection{Subject-Level Recovery and
Shrinkage}\label{subject-level-recovery-and-shrinkage}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{subj\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(ml\_scenarios, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(scen) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_decay\_fits[[scen]]}
\NormalTok{  truth }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ml\_decay\_sims[[scen]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{true\_params}

\NormalTok{  s }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"subj\_log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"subj\_log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{param    =} \FunctionTok{if\_else}\NormalTok{(stringr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{str\_detect}\NormalTok{(variable, }\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{),}
                         \StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{agent\_id =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(stringr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{str\_extract}\NormalTok{(variable, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, param, }\AttributeTok{est\_mean =}\NormalTok{ mean, }\AttributeTok{est\_q5 =}\NormalTok{ q5, }\AttributeTok{est\_q95 =}\NormalTok{ q95)}

\NormalTok{  truth\_long }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ truth }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
\NormalTok{    dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, log\_c\_true, log\_lambda\_true) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(log\_c\_true, log\_lambda\_true),}
                 \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"truth"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{param =} \FunctionTok{if\_else}\NormalTok{(param }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"log\_c\_true"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_c"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_lambda"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{  s }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(truth\_long, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"agent\_id"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =}\NormalTok{ scen)}
\NormalTok{\}) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scenario =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scenario, }\AttributeTok{levels =}\NormalTok{ ml\_scenarios,}
                           \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Drift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Contingent shift"}\NormalTok{)))}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(subj\_recovery, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ truth, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ est\_mean, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ scenario)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_pointrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ est\_q5, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ est\_q95), }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_grid}\NormalTok{(param }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ scenario, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Subject{-}level parameter recovery (with partial pooling)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Posterior mean ± 90\% CI vs simulation truth"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True value"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior estimate"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{)}
