    \AttributeTok{arrow =} \FunctionTok{arrow}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{type =} \StringTok{"closed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{length =} \FunctionTok{unit}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.08}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"inches"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{angle =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ final\_prototypes,}
             \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ feature2\_mean, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ feature1\_mean, }\AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(category)),}
             \AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{shape =} \DecValTok{18}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_path}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ ellipse\_data,}
            \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ feature2\_mean, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ feature1\_mean,}
                \AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ category, }\AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(category)),}
            \AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"0"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"1"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Category"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_shape\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"0"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"1"} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{17}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Category"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Prototype Learning Trajectory (Kalman Filter, r = 1.0)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Prototypes start at (2.5, 2.5). Dashed lines show path; ellipses show final 68\% uncertainty."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Position Feature"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Height Feature"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{range}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{position, prototype\_trajectory}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{feature2\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{range}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{height,   prototype\_trajectory}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{feature1\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_track_prototypes-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Interpretation}: The dashed lines show the path the prototypes
took during learning (starting from (2.5, 2.5), the center of the
feature space). The final prototype locations are marked with diamonds,
and the ellipses represent the model's final uncertainty (68\%
confidence) about the true prototype location for each category. We see
the prototypes moving towards the center of their respective category
members and uncertainty shrinking over time --- exactly the incremental,
variance-reducing Bayesian update the Kalman filter implements. The
shrinking ellipses indicate that once the agent has seen enough
exemplars, the covariances are small enough that further learning is
negligible, and the prototypes are effectively frozen.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Prior Predictive Check}\label{prior-predictive-check-3}

Before fitting any data, we verify that the prior on \texttt{log\_r}
generates only scientifically plausible learning trajectories. We draw
\(S = 500\) values from \(\log r \sim \text{Normal}(0, 1)\) --- implying
\(r \in (0.13, 7.4)\) at \(\pm 2\) SD --- simulate one agent per draw,
and plot the envelope.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_ppc\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{500}

\NormalTok{ppc\_log\_r  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_ppc\_samples, LOG\_R\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_R\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{ppc\_log\_q  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_ppc\_samples, LOG\_Q\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_Q\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{ppc\_r      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(ppc\_log\_r)}
\NormalTok{ppc\_q      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(ppc\_log\_q)}
\NormalTok{ppc\_sched  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{999}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{prior\_pred\_curves }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_ppc\_samples), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(s) \{}
\NormalTok{  res }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{prototype\_kalman}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{r\_value   =}\NormalTok{ ppc\_r[s],}
    \AttributeTok{q\_value   =}\NormalTok{ ppc\_q[s],}
    \AttributeTok{obs       =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(ppc\_sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
    \AttributeTok{cat\_one   =}\NormalTok{ ppc\_sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
    \AttributeTok{initial\_mu =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{sample\_id =}\NormalTok{ s,}
    \AttributeTok{trial     =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ppc\_sched)),}
    \AttributeTok{correct   =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(res}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ ppc\_sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cum\_acc =} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(correct) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{())}
\NormalTok{\})}

\NormalTok{ppc\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ prior\_pred\_curves }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(trial) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{q05 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(cum\_acc, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{q25 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(cum\_acc, }\FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{q50 =} \FunctionTok{median}\NormalTok{(cum\_acc),}
    \AttributeTok{q75 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(cum\_acc, }\FloatTok{0.75}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{q95 =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(cum\_acc, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(ppc\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q05, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95), }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.20}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q25, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q75), }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.40}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ q50), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#006D4E"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey40"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_y\_continuous}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Prior Predictive Check: Kalman Filter Prototype Model"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Ribbons: 50\% and 90\% prior predictive intervals}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Priors: log(r) \textasciitilde{} Normal(0, 1), log(q) \textasciitilde{} Normal({-}2, 1)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Cumulative Accuracy"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_prior_predictive_check-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The prior predictive envelope spans chance performance (uninformative
\(r\) draws) through near-ceiling accuracy (informative \(r\) draws),
without implying impossible behavior such as sustained below-chance
performance. This is the plausibility standard we require before
proceeding to inference.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{The Prototype Model in Stan}\label{the-prototype-model-in-stan}

Simulations help us understand the model, but we want to fit it to
experimental data to estimate its parameters. The primary free parameter
is the observation noise variance \texttt{r\_value}. We implement the
Kalman filter prototype model in Stan to estimate \texttt{r\_value} (on
the log scale) from data.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Why the Kalman state lives in
\texttt{transformed\ parameters}}{Why the Kalman state lives in transformed parameters}}\label{why-the-kalman-state-lives-in-transformed-parameters}

The prototype model has two parameters --- \texttt{log\_r} and
\texttt{log\_q}. Given both and the observed data (stimuli and
feedback), the \emph{entire} Kalman filter state is deterministic:
prototype means, prototype covariances, and the per-trial choice
probability \(p_i\) all follow algebraically from \texttt{log\_r} and
the trial sequence. Whenever a quantity depends on a parameter but is
otherwise deterministic, it belongs in \texttt{transformed\ parameters}
(evaluated once per leapfrog step) rather than
\texttt{transformed\ data} (which is for parameter-free preprocessing)
or \texttt{model\{\}} (which would force a recomputation in
\texttt{generated\ quantities}). We follow that rule here, and
\texttt{log\_lik{[}i{]}} in \texttt{generated\ quantities} becomes a
one-line lookup of \texttt{p{[}i{]}}.

\begin{quote}
\textbf{A note on architectural symmetry with Chapter 11.} The Chapter
11 GCM's choice probabilities are \emph{also} deterministic given the
parameters and the data, and they \emph{also} belong in
\texttt{transformed\ parameters} for the same reason. We do the same
here: \texttt{prob\_cat1{[}i{]}} is a transformed parameter, the model
block reduces to a single vectorised
\texttt{bernoulli\_lpmf(y\ \textbar{}\ prob\_cat1)}, and
\texttt{log\_lik{[}i{]}} is a one-line lookup.
\end{quote}

The same path-dependence concern from Ch. 11 still applies: trial
\(t\)'s choice probability depends on all previous stimuli through the
Kalman state, so standard PSIS-LOO is invalid. Unlike previous drafts,
we no longer treat that as a caveat to repeat --- the LFO-CV machinery
from Chapter 11 is generalised to the Kalman case in §``Leave-Future-Out
Cross-Validation'' below, and the comparison against exact
one-step-ahead refits is reported in this chapter rather than deferred
to a hypothetical future supplement.

\begin{quote}
\textbf{\texttt{reduce\_sum} and parallelization}: As with the GCM, the
Kalman prototype model cannot use \texttt{reduce\_sum} because
successive Kalman steps are causally chained --- trial \(t\)'s
contribution to the likelihood depends on the filter state from trials
\(1, \ldots, t-1\). The entire filter must be computed sequentially.
With two parameters (\texttt{log\_r}, \texttt{log\_q}) the posterior
geometry is 2D, and Pathfinder initialization is still reliable because
both parameters are unconstrained scalars with prior-dominated tails.
\end{quote}

\subsection{Stan Model}\label{stan-model}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{prototype\_single\_stan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{// Kalman Filter Prototype Model — Single Subject, with Process Noise}
\StringTok{// Key design:}
\StringTok{//   log\_r and log\_q are the two parameters (both unconstrained).}
\StringTok{//   r\_value = exp(log\_r): observation noise variance}
\StringTok{//   q\_value = exp(log\_q): process noise / prototype drift rate}
\StringTok{//   Kalman loop structure: Predict (add Q) → Decide → Update.}
\StringTok{//   The full filter is in transformed parameters so log\_lik in generated}
\StringTok{//   quantities reads p[i] directly without re{-}running the filter.}

\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} ntrials;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} nfeatures;}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} cat\_one;}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials, nfeatures] real obs;}

\StringTok{  vector[nfeatures] initial\_mu\_cat0;}
\StringTok{  vector[nfeatures] initial\_mu\_cat1;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} initial\_sigma\_diag;}

\StringTok{  real prior\_logr\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_logr\_sd;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_logq\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_logq\_sd;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real log\_r;}
\StringTok{  real log\_q;}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} r\_value = exp(log\_r);}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} q\_value = exp(log\_q);}

\StringTok{  array[ntrials] real\textless{}lower=1e{-}9, upper=1{-}1e{-}9\textgreater{} p;}

\StringTok{  \{}
\StringTok{    vector[nfeatures] mu\_cat0 = initial\_mu\_cat0;}
\StringTok{    vector[nfeatures] mu\_cat1 = initial\_mu\_cat1;}
\StringTok{    matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] sigma\_cat0 =}
\StringTok{      diag\_matrix(rep\_vector(initial\_sigma\_diag, nfeatures));}
\StringTok{    matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] sigma\_cat1 =}
\StringTok{      diag\_matrix(rep\_vector(initial\_sigma\_diag, nfeatures));}
\StringTok{    matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] r\_matrix =}
\StringTok{      diag\_matrix(rep\_vector(r\_value, nfeatures));}
\StringTok{    matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] q\_matrix =}
\StringTok{      diag\_matrix(rep\_vector(q\_value, nfeatures));}
\StringTok{    matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] I\_mat =}
\StringTok{      diag\_matrix(rep\_vector(1.0, nfeatures));}

\StringTok{    for (i in 1:ntrials) \{}
\StringTok{      vector[nfeatures] x = to\_vector(obs[i]);}

\StringTok{      // ── Prediction step: add process noise to both categories ──────────}
\StringTok{      sigma\_cat0 = sigma\_cat0 + q\_matrix;}
\StringTok{      sigma\_cat1 = sigma\_cat1 + q\_matrix;}

\StringTok{      // ── Decision ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\StringTok{      matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] cov0 = sigma\_cat0 + r\_matrix;}
\StringTok{      matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] cov1 = sigma\_cat1 + r\_matrix;}

\StringTok{      real log\_p0 = multi\_normal\_lpdf(x | mu\_cat0, cov0);}
\StringTok{      real log\_p1 = multi\_normal\_lpdf(x | mu\_cat1, cov1);}
\StringTok{      real prob1  = exp(log\_p1 {-} log\_sum\_exp(log\_p0, log\_p1));}

\StringTok{      p[i] = fmax(1e{-}9, fmin(1 {-} 1e{-}9, prob1));}

\StringTok{      // ── Update (measurement update for the correct category only) ────────}
\StringTok{      if (cat\_one[i] == 1) \{}
\StringTok{        vector[nfeatures] innov = x {-} mu\_cat1;}
\StringTok{        matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] S  = sigma\_cat1 + r\_matrix;}
\StringTok{        matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] K  = mdivide\_right\_spd(sigma\_cat1, S);}
\StringTok{        matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] IK = I\_mat {-} K;}
\StringTok{        mu\_cat1    = mu\_cat1 + K * innov;}
\StringTok{        sigma\_cat1 = IK * sigma\_cat1 * IK\textquotesingle{} + K * r\_matrix * K\textquotesingle{};}
\StringTok{        sigma\_cat1 = 0.5 * (sigma\_cat1 + sigma\_cat1\textquotesingle{});}
\StringTok{      \} else \{}
\StringTok{        vector[nfeatures] innov = x {-} mu\_cat0;}
\StringTok{        matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] S  = sigma\_cat0 + r\_matrix;}
\StringTok{        matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] K  = mdivide\_right\_spd(sigma\_cat0, S);}
\StringTok{        matrix[nfeatures, nfeatures] IK = I\_mat {-} K;}
\StringTok{        mu\_cat0    = mu\_cat0 + K * innov;}
\StringTok{        sigma\_cat0 = IK * sigma\_cat0 * IK\textquotesingle{} + K * r\_matrix * K\textquotesingle{};}
\StringTok{        sigma\_cat0 = 0.5 * (sigma\_cat0 + sigma\_cat0\textquotesingle{});}
\StringTok{      \}}
\StringTok{    \}}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(log\_r | prior\_logr\_mean, prior\_logr\_sd);}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(log\_q | prior\_logq\_mean, prior\_logq\_sd);}
\StringTok{  target += bernoulli\_lpmf(y | p);}
\StringTok{\}}

\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  vector[ntrials] log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  real lprior;}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:ntrials)}
\StringTok{    log\_lik[i] = bernoulli\_lpmf(y[i] | p[i]);}
\StringTok{  lprior = normal\_lpdf(log\_r | prior\_logr\_mean, prior\_logr\_sd) +}
\StringTok{           normal\_lpdf(log\_q | prior\_logq\_mean, prior\_logq\_sd);}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}

\NormalTok{stan\_file\_proto\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch13\_prototype\_single.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(prototype\_single\_stan, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{),}
                \AttributeTok{basename =} \StringTok{"ch13\_prototype\_single.stan"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
[1] "/Users/au209589/Dropbox/Teaching/AdvancedCognitiveModeling23_book/stan/ch13_prototype_single.stan"
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{mod\_prototype\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(stan\_file\_proto\_single)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Stan model compiled successfully.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Stan model compiled successfully.
\end{verbatim}

\textbf{Key aspects of the Stan implementation:}

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{Parameters block}: Two scalars \texttt{log\_r} and
  \texttt{log\_q}. Positivity constraints are handled by \texttt{exp()}
  in \texttt{transformed\ parameters}.
\item
  \textbf{Prediction step}: \texttt{sigma\_cat0} and
  \texttt{sigma\_cat1} each have \texttt{q\_matrix} added before the
  decision. With \texttt{q\_value\ =\ 0} this recovers the static-target
  filter.
\item
  \textbf{Transformed parameters}: Runs the full Kalman filter
  trial-by-trial, updating the two prototype distributions and recording
  \texttt{p{[}i{]}} --- the probability of choosing Category 1 on trial
  \(i\) --- before each trial's update. The update happens on \emph{all}
  trials (including the last), consistent with the R simulation.
\item
  \textbf{Model block}: Priors on \texttt{log\_r} and \texttt{log\_q}
  plus a vectorised Bernoulli likelihood using the pre-computed
  \texttt{p} vector. One line.
\item
  \textbf{Generated quantities}: \texttt{log\_lik{[}i{]}} reads directly
  from \texttt{p{[}i{]}} --- no re-computation of the Kalman filter
  required.
\end{itemize}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Fitting the Model to Simulated
Data}\label{fitting-the-model-to-simulated-data}

We use Pathfinder initialization before HMC sampling:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Select one simulated agent with r\_value = 2.0, q\_value = 0.1}
\NormalTok{agent\_to\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ prototype\_responses }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(r\_value\_true }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{, q\_value\_true }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, agent\_id }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{stopifnot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(agent\_to\_fit) }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ total\_trials)}

\NormalTok{proto\_data\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{ntrials            =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(agent\_to\_fit),}
  \AttributeTok{nfeatures          =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
  \AttributeTok{cat\_one            =}\NormalTok{ agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
  \AttributeTok{y                  =}\NormalTok{ agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response,}
  \AttributeTok{obs                =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(agent\_to\_fit[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
  \AttributeTok{initial\_mu\_cat0    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{initial\_mu\_cat1    =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{initial\_sigma\_diag =} \FloatTok{10.0}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_logr\_mean    =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_R\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_logr\_sd      =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_R\_PRIOR\_SD,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_logq\_mean    =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_Q\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
  \AttributeTok{prior\_logq\_sd      =}\NormalTok{ LOG\_Q\_PRIOR\_SD}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{fit\_filepath\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simmodels"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch13\_proto\_single\_fit.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(fit\_filepath\_single)) \{}
\NormalTok{  pf\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_prototype\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  fit\_proto\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_prototype\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single,}
    \AttributeTok{init            =}\NormalTok{ pf\_single,}
    \AttributeTok{seed            =} \DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{chains          =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{availableCores}\NormalTok{()),}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =} \DecValTok{1500}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{300}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save\_object}\NormalTok{(fit\_filepath\_single)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Single{-}agent prototype fit computed and saved.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_proto\_single }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(fit\_filepath\_single)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing single{-}agent prototype fit.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Loaded existing single-agent prototype fit.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
\NormalTok{  param\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r\_value"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"q\_value"}\NormalTok{))}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(param\_summary)}

\NormalTok{  true\_r     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{r\_value\_true[}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{  true\_log\_r }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(true\_r)}
\NormalTok{  true\_q     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{q\_value\_true[}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{  true\_log\_q }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(true\_q)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{True log\_r ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(true\_log\_r, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"  True r ="}\NormalTok{, true\_r, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True log\_q ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(true\_log\_q, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"  True q ="}\NormalTok{, true\_q, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 4 x 10
  variable    mean  median    sd   mad      q5     q95  rhat ess_bulk ess_tail
  <chr>      <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
1 log_r     0.0948  0.0971 0.548 0.527 -0.808   0.991   1.00    1841.    1906.
2 r_value   1.28    1.10   0.749 0.565  0.446   2.69    1.00    1841.    1906.
3 log_q    -1.22   -1.12   0.817 0.776 -2.70   -0.0641  1.00    2026.    1926.
4 q_value   0.390   0.328  0.280 0.244  0.0670  0.938   1.00    2026.    1926.

True log_r = 0.693   True r = 2 
True log_q = -2.303   True q = 0.1 
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{MCMC Diagnostic Battery}\label{mcmc-diagnostic-battery-2}

Before reading anything off the posterior, we curate the diagnostic
table. Earlier drafts of this chapter ran \texttt{cmdstan\_diagnose()}
silently and moved on. The point of running diagnostics is \emph{seeing}
them --- even when (as here, with two parameters) they are nearly
trivial. The \texttt{diagnostic\_summary\_table()} helper defined in the
setup chunk is the canonical version shared across Chapters 11, 12, 13,
and 15. It returns a one-row tibble and emits a warning if any of the
six thresholds are breached.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
\NormalTok{  diag\_tbl }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary\_table}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    fit\_proto\_single,}
    \AttributeTok{params =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r\_value"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"q\_value"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(diag\_tbl)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 6 x 4
  metric                           value threshold pass 
  <chr>                            <dbl> <chr>     <lgl>
1 Divergences (zero tolerance)    0      == 0      TRUE 
2 Max rank-normalised R-hat       1      < 1.01    TRUE 
3 Min bulk ESS                 1841      > 400     TRUE 
4 Min tail ESS                 1906      > 400     TRUE 
5 Min E-BFMI                      0.977  > 0.2     TRUE 
6 Max MCSE / posterior SD         0.0233 < 0.05    TRUE 
\end{verbatim}

With two parameters, \texttt{mcmc\_pairs} is informative for detecting
posterior correlations between \texttt{log\_r} and \texttt{log\_q}. The
trace plot grid plus rank histograms remain the primary MCMC
diagnostics.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
\NormalTok{  p\_trace }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ bayesplot}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mcmc\_trace}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{facet\_args =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Trace plots — should look like hairy caterpillars"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  p\_rank }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ bayesplot}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mcmc\_rank\_overlay}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{facet\_args =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Rank histograms — should be uniform across chains"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  p\_pairs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ bayesplot}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{mcmc\_pairs}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{diag\_fun     =} \StringTok{"dens"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{off\_diag\_fun =} \StringTok{"scatter"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{np           =} \FunctionTok{nuts\_params}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_trace }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_rank)}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_pairs)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_trace_and_rank-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_trace_and_rank-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\subsection{Posterior Predictive
Check}\label{posterior-predictive-check}

The posterior predictive check asks: does the fitted model, when run
forward with parameters drawn from the posterior, produce data that look
like the observed data? Because \texttt{p} is already in
\texttt{transformed\ parameters}, we can read posterior draws of the
choice probability directly and use them to generate posterior
predictive responses.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Posterior draws of p[i] (one row per draw, one column per trial)}
\NormalTok{  p\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"p"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  n\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(p\_draws)}
\NormalTok{  n\_obs   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ncol}\NormalTok{(p\_draws)}

  \CommentTok{\# Generate posterior predictive responses}
\NormalTok{  yrep }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(n\_draws }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ n\_obs, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{as.vector}\NormalTok{(p\_draws)),}
                 \AttributeTok{nrow =}\NormalTok{ n\_draws, }\AttributeTok{ncol =}\NormalTok{ n\_obs)}

  \CommentTok{\# Cumulative{-}accuracy ribbons vs. observed}
\NormalTok{  obs\_cum\_acc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback) }\SpecialCharTok{/}
                 \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_obs)}
                 
  \CommentTok{\# Use "==" directly and add 0 to coerce to numeric without losing matrix dims}
\NormalTok{  yrep\_correct }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sweep}\NormalTok{(yrep, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback), }\StringTok{"=="}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{0}
  
\NormalTok{  yrep\_cum\_acc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{t}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(yrep\_correct, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(r) }\FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(r) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(r)))}

\NormalTok{  cum\_acc\_summary }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{trial =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_obs),}
    \AttributeTok{q05   =} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(yrep\_cum\_acc, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, quantile, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{q25   =} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(yrep\_cum\_acc, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, quantile, }\FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{q50   =} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(yrep\_cum\_acc, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, quantile, }\FloatTok{0.50}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{q75   =} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(yrep\_cum\_acc, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, quantile, }\FloatTok{0.75}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{q95   =} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(yrep\_cum\_acc, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, quantile, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{obs   =}\NormalTok{ obs\_cum\_acc}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(cum\_acc\_summary, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q05, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q95), }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#56B4E9"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.20}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ q25, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ q75), }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#56B4E9"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.40}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ q50), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ obs), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Posterior Predictive Check: Cumulative Accuracy"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Blue: posterior predictive (50\% and 90\% intervals + median)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Orange: observed"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Cumulative accuracy"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_ppc-1.pdf}
\end{center}

The observed cumulative-accuracy curve should sit comfortably inside the
posterior predictive band. If it drifts to the edge, the model is
missing structure that the data carry --- the most likely candidate
being a separate decisional-noise term that the single \(r\) parameter
cannot capture.

\subsection{Prior--Posterior Update
Plot}\label{priorposterior-update-plot-1}

Did the data teach the model anything? We need an explicit overlay of
prior and posterior densities, on both the \emph{computational scale}
(\texttt{log\_r}, where the prior was defined) and the
\emph{interpretable scale} (\texttt{r}, where the cognitive
interpretation lives). If posterior and prior overlap substantially, the
data were uninformative for that parameter. If posterior is narrow but
centred far from the truth, the model is confidently wrong.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
\NormalTok{  draws\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r\_value"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"q\_value"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  n\_prior    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(draws\_df)}
\NormalTok{  prior\_logr }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_prior, LOG\_R\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_R\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{  prior\_r    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(prior\_logr)}
\NormalTok{  prior\_logq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_prior, LOG\_Q\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOG\_Q\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{  prior\_q    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(prior\_logq)}

\NormalTok{  prior\_post\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ prior\_logr,    }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{log\_r, }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"r"}\NormalTok{,     }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ prior\_r,        }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"r"}\NormalTok{,     }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{r\_value, }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ prior\_logq,    }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{log\_q, }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"q"}\NormalTok{,     }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ prior\_q,        }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"prior"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \StringTok{"q"}\NormalTok{,     }\AttributeTok{value =}\NormalTok{ draws\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{q\_value, }\AttributeTok{source =} \StringTok{"posterior"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{scale =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(scale, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"q"}\NormalTok{)))}

\NormalTok{  true\_lines }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{scale =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"q"}\NormalTok{),}
                   \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_q"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"q"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{value =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(true\_log\_r, true\_r, true\_log\_q, true\_q)}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(prior\_post\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ source, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ source)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ true\_lines,}
               \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ value),}
               \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ scale, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prior =} \StringTok{"\#999999"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{posterior =} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prior =} \StringTok{"\#666666"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{posterior =} \StringTok{"\#006D4E"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Prior → Posterior Update: Kalman Prototype Model (r and q)"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red dashed line = true generating value"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_prior_posterior_update-1.pdf}
\end{center}

We expect the posterior on the interpretable scale to be substantially
narrower than the prior and to overlap the true value. The \texttt{r}
panel uses a heavy-tailed prior (the exponential of a Gaussian) so the
prior density is wide and right-skewed; the posterior should look like a
clearly localised peak by comparison. \emph{Information gain is what
this plot makes visual} --- our preferred way of saying ``the data were
informative.''

\subsection{Randomised LOO-PIT
Calibration}\label{randomised-loo-pit-calibration}

For continuous outcomes the preferred calibration check is LOO-PIT. For
binary outcomes the equivalent is the \emph{randomised} LOO-PIT
(Säilynoja et al.~2022), which avoids the degenerate two-bin
distribution that plain LOO-PIT produces on Bernoulli data. Chapter 11
implements this for the GCM; we apply the same construction here.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# PSIS{-}LOO weights from log\_lik}
\NormalTok{  log\_lik\_mat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# FIX: Add save\_psis = TRUE so the weight matrix is retained}
\NormalTok{  loo\_obj     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(log\_lik\_mat, }\AttributeTok{cores =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{save\_psis =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  log\_w       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{weights.importance\_sampling}\NormalTok{(loo\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{psis\_object, }
                                                  \AttributeTok{normalize =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }
                                                  \AttributeTok{log =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# Posterior draws of choice probability}
\NormalTok{  p\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_proto\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"p"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# Per{-}observation LOO predictive probability of cat 1}
\NormalTok{  loo\_p\_cat1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{ncol}\NormalTok{(p\_draws)), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
    \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(log\_w[, i]) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_draws[, i])}
\NormalTok{  \}, }\FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{  y\_obs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ agent\_to\_fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response}

  \CommentTok{\# Randomised PIT for binary y:}
  \CommentTok{\#   if y == 1: U \textasciitilde{} Uniform(1 {-} p, 1)}
  \CommentTok{\#   if y == 0: U \textasciitilde{} Uniform(0, 1 {-} p)}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2026}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  rloo\_pit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(y\_obs), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{    p\_i }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo\_p\_cat1[i]}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (y\_obs[i] }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_i, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_i)}
\NormalTok{  \}, }\FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{  p\_pit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rloo\_pit =}\NormalTok{ rloo\_pit), }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ rloo\_pit)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(density)), }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{,}
                   \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#56B4E9"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Randomised LOO{-}PIT: Kalman Prototype Model"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"If the model is calibrated, the histogram should look uniform on [0, 1]"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Randomised PIT value"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{))}
    
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_pit)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_loo_pit_randomized-1.pdf}
\end{center}

A roughly uniform histogram is the calibration target. Systematic
deviations (a U-shape, a peak in the middle, or skew) flag specific
failure modes: U-shape means the posterior is overconfident (the model
thinks it knows the answer better than it does), a central hump means
underconfident, and a one-sided skew means there is a directional bias
the model has not captured.

\subsection{LOO with Pareto-k as a Diagnostic, Not a Predictive
Score}\label{loo-with-pareto-k-as-a-diagnostic-not-a-predictive-score}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_proto\_single)) \{}
\NormalTok{  loo\_proto }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(log\_lik\_mat, }\AttributeTok{cores =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(loo\_proto)}
  \FunctionTok{plot}\NormalTok{(loo\_proto, }\AttributeTok{label\_points =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{main =} \StringTok{"PSIS{-}LOO Pareto{-}k: Kalman Prototype Model"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

Computed from 6000 by 64 log-likelihood matrix.

         Estimate  SE
elpd_loo    -38.8 3.5
p_loo         1.1 0.2
looic        77.6 7.0
------
MCSE of elpd_loo is NA.
MCSE and ESS estimates assume independent draws (r_eff=1).

Pareto k diagnostic values:
                         Count Pct.    Min. ESS
(-Inf, 0.7]   (good)     63    98.4%   4987    
   (0.7, 1]   (bad)       0     0.0%   <NA>    
   (1, Inf)   (very bad)  1     1.6%   <NA>    
See help('pareto-k-diagnostic') for details.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_loo_single-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{quote}
\textbf{⚠️ Reframing PSIS-LOO for sequential models.} The Kalman filter
is path-dependent: trial \(t\)'s choice probability depends on the
entire filter state from trials \(1, \ldots, t-1\). Standard PSIS-LOO is
\emph{not} a valid measure of out-of-sample predictive accuracy here ---
leaving out trial \(t\) implicitly conditions on a counterfactual filter
state that the model never actually saw. We compute it anyway, for two
reasons: (1) the \textbf{Pareto-\(\hat{k}\) values are still informative
as a localisation diagnostic} --- high \(\hat{k}\) at specific trials
flags observations the model cannot easily reconstruct from the rest,
which can reveal where the structural assumptions are stressed; (2)
compatibility with downstream tooling. The actual predictive evaluation
is done by LFO-CV in the next subsection. The numeric ELPD reported by
\texttt{loo()} should not be taken as a generalisation estimate.
\end{quote}

\subsection{Leave-Future-Out Cross-Validation: Inheriting and
Generalising the Chapter 11
Implementation}\label{leave-future-out-cross-validation-inheriting-and-generalising-the-chapter-11-implementation}

For sequential models, LOO is invalid and LFO-CV is the correct
alternative (Bürkner, Gabry, \& Vehtari 2020). Chapter 11 implements
PSIS-LFO for the GCM as \texttt{psis\_lfo\_gcm()}. The Kalman prototype
model has the same path-dependence structure but a much cheaper
per-trial cost, so LFO-CV is even more tractable here. Below is the same
algorithm specialised to the prototype model and saved as
\texttt{psis\_lfo\_kalman()}. The two functions share an interface so
Chapter 15 can call either through a common front-end.

The algorithm (Bürkner et al.~2020 Algorithm 1):

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  Choose a minimum training window \(L\) (we use \(L = 16\), two blocks
  of the Kruschke task).
\item
  Fit the model once on \(y_{1:L}\). This is the \emph{reference fit}.
\item
  For each \(t = L+1, \ldots, T\):

  \begin{enumerate}
  \def\labelenumii{\alph{enumii}.}
  \tightlist
  \item
    Use the reference fit's posterior draws \(\theta^{(s)}\) to compute
    the importance ratio for re-weighting \(\theta\) from
    \(p(\theta \mid y_{1:t-1})\) relative to the reference.
  \item
    Smooth the ratios with PSIS, check Pareto \(\hat{k}\).
  \item
    If \(\hat{k} \leq 0.7\), accept the ratios and use them to evaluate
    \(\log p(y_t \mid y_{1:t-1})\).
  \item
    If \(\hat{k} > 0.7\), \textbf{refit} the model on \(y_{1:t-1}\),
    update the reference, and continue.
  \end{enumerate}
\item
  Return pointwise ELPD, the refit history, and the \(\hat{k}\)
  trajectory.
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# psis\_lfo\_kalman(): Leave{-}future{-}out CV for the Kalman prototype model.}
\CommentTok{\# Mirrors psis\_lfo\_gcm() from Chapter 11. Returns pointwise ELPD across the}
\CommentTok{\# evaluation window, the indices at which a refit was triggered, and the}
\CommentTok{\# Pareto{-}k trajectory.}
\NormalTok{psis\_lfo\_kalman }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(stan\_data,}
\NormalTok{                            stan\_model,}
                            \AttributeTok{L           =} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{,    }\CommentTok{\# min training window}
                            \AttributeTok{k\_thresh    =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{,   }\CommentTok{\# PSIS{-}k refit threshold}
                            \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{,}
                            \AttributeTok{iter\_sample =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
                            \AttributeTok{chains      =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
                            \AttributeTok{seed        =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  T\_total }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ stan\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ntrials}

  \CommentTok{\# Helper: build a stan\_data list restricted to trials 1:t}
\NormalTok{  truncate\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(d, t) \{}
\NormalTok{    out }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ d}
\NormalTok{    out}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ntrials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ t}
\NormalTok{    out}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{cat\_one }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{cat\_one[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{t]}
\NormalTok{    out}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{t]}
\NormalTok{    out}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ d}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{t, , drop }\OtherTok{=} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{    out}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# Helper: fit on y\_\{1:t\}}
\NormalTok{  refit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(t) \{}
\NormalTok{    d\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{truncate\_data}\NormalTok{(stan\_data, t)}
\NormalTok{    pf  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      stan\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ d\_t, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
                            \AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ seed }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ t),}
      \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    init\_val }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf)) pf }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FloatTok{0.5}
\NormalTok{    stan\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data            =}\NormalTok{ d\_t,}
      \AttributeTok{init            =}\NormalTok{ init\_val,}
      \AttributeTok{seed            =}\NormalTok{ seed }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ t,}
      \AttributeTok{chains          =}\NormalTok{ chains,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =}\NormalTok{ chains,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup     =}\NormalTok{ iter\_warmup,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_sampling   =}\NormalTok{ iter\_sample,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh         =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{adapt\_delta     =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# Helper: given a fitted model, evaluate log\_lik at trial t}
  \CommentTok{\# using cmdstanr\textquotesingle{}s $generate\_quantities() so we don\textquotesingle{}t re{-}MCMC.}
  \CommentTok{\# Helper: given a fitted model, evaluate log\_lik at trial t}
\NormalTok{  eval\_log\_lik\_at }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(fit\_ref, t) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Build the "extended" data that includes trial t in the likelihood}
\NormalTok{    d\_ext }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{truncate\_data}\NormalTok{(stan\_data, t)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# FIX: Call generate\_quantities on stan\_model, not fit\_ref}
\NormalTok{    gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ stan\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{generate\_quantities}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{fitted\_params =}\NormalTok{ fit\_ref,}
      \AttributeTok{data          =}\NormalTok{ d\_ext,}
      \AttributeTok{seed          =}\NormalTok{ seed }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ t,}
      \AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =}\NormalTok{ chains}
\NormalTok{    )}
    
    \CommentTok{\# log\_lik[t] for each posterior draw}
\NormalTok{    ll\_mat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    ll\_mat[, t]}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \CommentTok{\# ── Initial fit on the minimum window ─────────────────────────────────────}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Initial LFO fit on trials 1 to"}\NormalTok{, L, }\StringTok{"...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  fit\_ref }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{refit}\NormalTok{(L)}
\NormalTok{  ref\_window }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ L}

  \CommentTok{\# Storage}
\NormalTok{  pointwise\_elpd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\ConstantTok{NA\_real\_}\NormalTok{, T\_total)}
\NormalTok{  k\_hat\_traj     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\ConstantTok{NA\_real\_}\NormalTok{, T\_total)}
\NormalTok{  refit\_at       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# ── Sequential evaluation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{  log\_ratios\_accum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)  }\CommentTok{\# log p(y\_\{ref+1:t{-}1\} | theta) accumulation}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (t }\ControlFlowTok{in}\NormalTok{ (L }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{T\_total) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Build importance ratios for theta\_ref vs theta\_\{1:t{-}1\}}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(log\_ratios\_accum) }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{      log\_w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{ndraws}\NormalTok{(fit\_ref}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{()))}
\NormalTok{    \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{      log\_w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ log\_ratios\_accum  }\CommentTok{\# raw log ratios; psis() handles normalization}
\NormalTok{    \}}

    \CommentTok{\# PSIS smoothing}
\NormalTok{    psis\_obj }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{psis}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{log\_ratios =} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(log\_w, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{r\_eff =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    k\_hat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(psis\_obj)) loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{pareto\_k\_values}\NormalTok{(psis\_obj) }\ControlFlowTok{else} \ConstantTok{Inf}
\NormalTok{    k\_hat\_traj[t] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ k\_hat}

    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.finite}\NormalTok{(k\_hat) }\SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ k\_hat }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ k\_thresh) \{}
      \CommentTok{\# Refit on y\_\{1:t{-}1\}}
      \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"  trial"}\NormalTok{, t, }\StringTok{": k\_hat ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(k\_hat, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"→ refitting}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      fit\_ref }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{refit}\NormalTok{(t }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ref\_window }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ t }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{      refit\_at }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(refit\_at, t)}
\NormalTok{      log\_ratios\_accum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      log\_w }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, posterior}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{ndraws}\NormalTok{(fit\_ref}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{()))}
\NormalTok{      psis\_obj }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{psis}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{log\_ratios =} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(log\_w, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{r\_eff =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    \}}

    \CommentTok{\# Evaluate log p(y\_t | y\_\{1:t{-}1\}, theta) for each draw}
\NormalTok{    ll\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{eval\_log\_lik\_at}\NormalTok{(fit\_ref, t)}

    \CommentTok{\# Importance{-}weighted estimate of log p(y\_t | y\_\{1:t{-}1\})}
\NormalTok{    log\_w\_norm }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.vector}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{weights}\NormalTok{(psis\_obj, }\AttributeTok{normalize =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{log =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    pointwise\_elpd[t] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ matrixStats}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{logSumExp}\NormalTok{(log\_w\_norm }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ll\_t)}

    \CommentTok{\# Accumulate log p(y\_t | theta) for the next iteration\textquotesingle{}s importance ratio}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(log\_ratios\_accum) }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{      log\_ratios\_accum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ll\_t}
\NormalTok{    \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{      log\_ratios\_accum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ log\_ratios\_accum }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ ll\_t}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{pointwise\_elpd =}\NormalTok{ pointwise\_elpd,}
    \AttributeTok{refit\_at       =}\NormalTok{ refit\_at,}
    \AttributeTok{k\_hat\_traj     =}\NormalTok{ k\_hat\_traj,}
    \AttributeTok{L              =}\NormalTok{ L,}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_lfo       =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(pointwise\_elpd, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{n\_refits       =} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(refit\_at)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{quote}
\textbf{Note on the importance-ratio accumulation.} The trick that makes
PSIS-LFO efficient is that the importance ratio for
\(p(\theta \mid y_{1:t-1})\) vs the reference \(p(\theta \mid y_{1:L})\)
is a \emph{running product}: for each new trial \(t\) we just multiply
the existing per-draw weight by \(p(y_{t-1} \mid \theta)\). We never
need to refit so long as the smoothed Pareto-\(\hat{k}\) stays below the
threshold. When it crosses 0.7, we refit and reset the running product.
The accumulation in \texttt{log\_ratios\_accum} above implements this
--- every time we evaluate \texttt{log\_lik{[}t{]}}, we add it to the
cumulative log-ratio for the next iteration.
\end{quote}

We now run this on the same single-agent fit, and validate it against
\emph{exact} one-step-ahead refits at every 8th trial. If PSIS-LFO is
doing its job, the two ELPD curves should agree closely.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lfo\_cache\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch13\_lfo\_kalman\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(lfo\_cache\_file)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Running PSIS{-}LFO for the prototype model...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  lfo\_psis }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{psis\_lfo\_kalman}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{stan\_data  =}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single,}
    \AttributeTok{stan\_model =}\NormalTok{ mod\_prototype\_single,}
    \AttributeTok{L          =} \DecValTok{16}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{k\_thresh   =} \FloatTok{0.7}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \CommentTok{\# ── Exact one{-}step{-}ahead refits at every 8th trial ────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{  exact\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{24}\NormalTok{, total\_trials, }\AttributeTok{by =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  exact\_elpd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(exact\_t))}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (k }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(exact\_t)) \{}
\NormalTok{    t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ exact\_t[k]}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"  exact refit at t ="}\NormalTok{, t, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    d\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}
\NormalTok{    d\_t}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ntrials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ t }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}
\NormalTok{    d\_t}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{cat\_one }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{cat\_one[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{(t }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)]}
\NormalTok{    d\_t}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{(t }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)]}
\NormalTok{    d\_t}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{(t }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), , drop }\OtherTok{=} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{]}

\NormalTok{    pf  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{      mod\_prototype\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ d\_t, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    init\_val }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf)) pf }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FloatTok{0.5}
\NormalTok{    fit\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_prototype\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ d\_t, }\AttributeTok{init =}\NormalTok{ init\_val, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{    )}

    \CommentTok{\# Evaluate log\_lik at trial t}
\NormalTok{    d\_ext }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}
\NormalTok{    d\_ext}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ntrials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ t}
\NormalTok{    d\_ext}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{cat\_one }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{cat\_one[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{t]}
\NormalTok{    d\_ext}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{y[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{t]}
\NormalTok{    d\_ext}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ proto\_data\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{obs[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{t, , drop }\OtherTok{=} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{]}
    
    \CommentTok{\# ── THE FIX: Called on mod\_prototype\_single instead of fit\_t ──}
\NormalTok{    gq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_prototype\_single}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{generate\_quantities}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fitted\_params =}\NormalTok{ fit\_t, }\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ d\_ext)}
    
\NormalTok{    ll\_mat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ gq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    exact\_elpd[k] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ matrixStats}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{logSumExp}\NormalTok{(ll\_mat[, t]) }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(ll\_mat))}
\NormalTok{  \}}

  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{lfo\_psis =}\NormalTok{ lfo\_psis,}
               \AttributeTok{exact\_t =}\NormalTok{ exact\_t, }\AttributeTok{exact\_elpd =}\NormalTok{ exact\_elpd),}
\NormalTok{          lfo\_cache\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"LFO results computed and saved.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  cached }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(lfo\_cache\_file)}
\NormalTok{  lfo\_psis }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cached}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{lfo\_psis}
\NormalTok{  exact\_t }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cached}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{exact\_t}
\NormalTok{  exact\_elpd }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cached}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{exact\_elpd}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded existing LFO results.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Loaded existing LFO results.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lfo\_psis"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"PSIS{-}LFO sum ELPD:"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{elpd\_lfo, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Number of refits triggered:"}\NormalTok{, lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{n\_refits,}
      \StringTok{"out of"}\NormalTok{, total\_trials }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{L, }\StringTok{"trials}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# Plot: PSIS{-}LFO pointwise vs exact refits}
\NormalTok{  comp\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{trial    =} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise\_elpd),}
    \AttributeTok{psis\_elpd =}\NormalTok{ lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pointwise\_elpd}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  exact\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{trial =}\NormalTok{ exact\_t, }\AttributeTok{exact\_elpd =}\NormalTok{ exact\_elpd)}

\NormalTok{  p\_lfo }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(comp\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ psis\_elpd)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ exact\_df,}
               \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ exact\_elpd),}
               \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"PSIS{-}LFO vs. Exact One{-}Step{-}Ahead Refits"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Blue line: PSIS{-}LFO pointwise ELPD; orange points: exact refits at every 8th trial"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"log p(y\_t | y\_\{1:t{-}1\})"}
\NormalTok{    )}

  \CommentTok{\# Plot: Pareto{-}k trajectory and refit triggers}
\NormalTok{  k\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{trial =} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k\_hat\_traj),}
                 \AttributeTok{k\_hat =}\NormalTok{ lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k\_hat\_traj)}
\NormalTok{  refit\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{trial =}\NormalTok{ lfo\_psis}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{refit\_at)}

\NormalTok{  p\_k }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(k\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ k\_hat)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ refit\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =}\NormalTok{ trial),}
               \AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Pareto{-}k trajectory and refit triggers"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Dashed red: k = 0.7 threshold; dotted vertical lines = refits"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Pareto k"}
\NormalTok{    )}

  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_lfo }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_k)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
PSIS-LFO sum ELPD: -32.25 
Number of refits triggered: 1 out of 48 trials
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{12-CategorizationModelsPrototypes_files/figure-pdf/ch13_lfo_run-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading the output.} The blue line is the PSIS-LFO pointwise
ELPD; the orange points are exact one-step-ahead refits. They should sit
on top of each other (modulo Monte Carlo noise from the small chain
count). The Pareto-\(\hat{k}\) trajectory should mostly stay below 0.7,
with refits triggered only occasionally. If the two curves diverge
systematically, PSIS-LFO is failing for this model and the exact refit
pipeline (slow but unambiguous) is the fallback. \textbf{This is the
LFO-CV implementation that Chapter 15's model comparison will use.}
Chapter 13's marginalised mixture is conditionally exchangeable and does
not need it; Chapter 11's GCM has its own canonical implementation; this
chapter inherits the algorithm and applies it to the Kalman case. The
repeated LFO-CV caveat from earlier drafts is now discharged.

\subsection{Prior Sensitivity
Analysis}\label{prior-sensitivity-analysis-2}

  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(ps)}

\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
