                                             \AttributeTok{log =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# Compute LOO predictive probability of cat 1 from Model B}
  \CommentTok{\# For this we need per{-}trial p\_cat1 under each posterior draw and rule.}
  \CommentTok{\# Easiest: sample rule per draw, compute p\_cat1 = (1 {-} eps if rule says 1 else eps),}
  \CommentTok{\# then average using LOO weights.}
\NormalTok{  eps\_draws\_full }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{))}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob}
\NormalTok{  n\_draws\_full   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(eps\_draws\_full)}
\NormalTok{  n\_obs          }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(fit\_schedule)}

  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  rule\_idx\_per\_draw }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{L, n\_draws\_full, }\AttributeTok{replace =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# Per (draw, trial) p\_cat1}
\NormalTok{  p\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{nrow =}\NormalTok{ n\_draws\_full, }\AttributeTok{ncol =}\NormalTok{ n\_obs)}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (s }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_draws\_full)) \{}
\NormalTok{    r }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ rule\_idx\_per\_draw[s]}
\NormalTok{    pred\_row }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ rule\_preds[r, ]}
\NormalTok{    p\_draws[s, ] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pred\_row }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{,}
                            \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ eps\_draws\_full[s],}
\NormalTok{                            eps\_draws\_full[s])}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  loo\_p\_cat1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_obs), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
    \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(log\_w[, i]) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_draws[, i])}
\NormalTok{  \}, }\FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{  y\_obs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(fit\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response)}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2026}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  rloo\_pit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(y\_obs), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{    p\_i }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo\_p\_cat1[i]}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (y\_obs[i] }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_i, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ p\_i)}
\NormalTok{  \}, }\FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rloo\_pit =}\NormalTok{ rloo\_pit), }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ rloo\_pit)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(density)), }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{,}
                   \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#E69F00"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"white"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Randomised LOO{-}PIT (Model B)"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"If Model B is calibrated for this data, the histogram should look uniform on [0, 1]"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Randomised PIT value"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{coord\_cartesian}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_loo_pit_randomised-1.pdf}
\end{center}

A roughly uniform histogram says Model B is calibrated \emph{for the
proxy task} (predicting individual binary responses given the posterior
over \(\varepsilon\) and the rule mixture). It does \emph{not} say Model
B is calibrated as a model of the cognitive process --- that would
require the NPE pipeline to validate Model A directly.

\subsection{LOO with Pareto-k as a Misspecification
Diagnostic}\label{loo-with-pareto-k-as-a-misspecification-diagnostic}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan)) \{}
\NormalTok{  loo\_rule }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(log\_lik\_mat, }\AttributeTok{cores =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(loo\_rule)}
  \FunctionTok{plot}\NormalTok{(loo\_rule, }\AttributeTok{label\_points =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{main =} \StringTok{"PSIS{-}LOO Pareto{-}k: Model B"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}

Computed from 6000 by 64 log-likelihood matrix.

         Estimate  SE
elpd_loo    -32.6 3.1
p_loo         0.2 0.0
looic        65.2 6.3
------
MCSE of elpd_loo is 0.0.
MCSE and ESS estimates assume independent draws (r_eff=1).

All Pareto k estimates are good (k < 0.7).
See help('pareto-k-diagnostic') for details.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_loo_rule-1.pdf}
\end{center}

Unlike Chapters 11 and 12, here the LOO ELPD is \textbf{technically
valid} (Model B is conditionally exchangeable --- see next subsection).
But the Pareto-\(\hat{k}\) values are still informative as a
\emph{misspecification diagnostic}: when Model B is misspecified
relative to the true generating process (Model A), \(\hat{k}\) values
are likely to be elevated on the trials where the proxy fails most
badly. Plotting \(\hat{k}\) against trial number and stimulus identity
localises the misspecification.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan)) \{}
\NormalTok{  k\_values }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ loo\_rule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{diagnostics}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{pareto\_k}
\NormalTok{  k\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{trial      =} \FunctionTok{seq\_along}\NormalTok{(k\_values),}
    \AttributeTok{k\_hat      =}\NormalTok{ k\_values,}
    \AttributeTok{stimulus   =}\NormalTok{ fit\_schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{stimulus\_id,}
    \AttributeTok{height     =}\NormalTok{ fit\_schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{height,}
    \AttributeTok{position   =}\NormalTok{ fit\_schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{position,}
    \AttributeTok{true\_cat   =}\NormalTok{ fit\_schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
    \AttributeTok{obs\_y      =}\NormalTok{ fit\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  p\_trial }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(k\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ k\_hat)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(true\_cat)), }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.0}\NormalTok{),}
               \AttributeTok{linetype =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"dotted"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"solid"}\NormalTok{),}
               \AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"0"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"1"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{),}
                       \AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"True cat"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Pareto{-}k by trial"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red lines: 0.5 (monitor), 0.7 (problematic), 1.0 (failed)"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Pareto k"}
\NormalTok{    )}

\NormalTok{  p\_stim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(k\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(stimulus), }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ k\_hat)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_boxplot}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#E69F00"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_jitter}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Pareto{-}k by stimulus identity"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"If a particular stimulus has consistently elevated k\_hat,}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{that stimulus is where the rule set fails"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Stimulus ID"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Pareto k"}
\NormalTok{    )}

  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_trial }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_stim)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_pareto_k_by_trial_and_stimulus-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading the Pareto-k plots as a misspecification diagnostic.} If
the high-\(\hat{k}\) values cluster on the \emph{boundary stimuli} of
the Kruschke set --- the ones whose true category cannot be determined
by any single rule from the candidate set --- that is direct evidence
that the rule set is missing the relevant boundary structure. If the
high-\(\hat{k}\) values cluster at \emph{late trials}, that is a signal
that Model A's particle filter has converged to a rule the candidate set
does not contain, and Model B is using \(\varepsilon\) inflation to
absorb the systematic late-trial errors. In both cases, the diagnostic
does not just say ``the model is wrong'' --- it says \textbf{where the
model is wrong}, which is much more useful.

\subsection{LFO-CV Does Not Apply
Here}\label{lfo-cv-does-not-apply-here}

\begin{quote}
\textbf{A back-reference, not a caveat to repeat.} Chapters 11 and 12
implement LFO-CV via \texttt{psis\_lfo\_gcm()} and
\texttt{psis\_lfo\_kalman()} because the GCM and the Kalman prototype
are path-dependent: trial \(t\)'s likelihood depends on the entire
history of trials \(1, \ldots, t-1\) through the memory or filter state.
\textbf{Model B has no such path-dependence.} All trials are
conditionally independent given \(\varepsilon\) and the rule mixture:
leaving out trial \(t\) does not affect the likelihood of any other
trial. Standard PSIS-LOO is therefore \emph{exactly correct} for Model B
and the LFO-CV machinery is unnecessary. We say this once here so the
reader does not encounter the same caveat for the third time after
seeing it twice in the previous chapters.

A subtler point: when Chapter 15 compares Model B against the GCM
(Chapter 11) and the Kalman prototype (Chapter 12), the comparison
should be reported under each model's \emph{correct} validation tool ---
LFO-CV for the path-dependent ones, standard LOO for Model B. ELPD
differences across these tools are not directly commensurable on a fine
scale, but they are commensurable enough to detect large effects. The
bigger concern in Chapter 15 is the cross-architecture mismatch (Model B
isn't even fitting the cognitive theory), and that concern is what the
NPE subsection below resolves.
\end{quote}

\subsection{Prior Sensitivity
Analysis}\label{prior-sensitivity-analysis-3}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan)) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Get all log\_lik variable names (log\_lik[1], log\_lik[2], ...)}
\NormalTok{  all\_params }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{metadata}\NormalTok{()}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_params}
\NormalTok{  log\_lik\_vars }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ all\_params[}\FunctionTok{grep}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\^{}log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, all\_params)]}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Compute sensitivity}
\NormalTok{  ps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_sensitivity}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan, }\AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{log\_lik =}\NormalTok{ log\_lik\_vars)}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(ps)}
  
  \CommentTok{\# Try plotting if log\_lik can be found automatically by create\_priorsense\_data}
  \FunctionTok{try}\NormalTok{(priorsense}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{powerscale\_plot\_dens}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan, }\AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Sensitivity based on cjs_dist
Prior selection: all priors
Likelihood selection: all data

   variable prior likelihood                     diagnosis
 error_prob 0.212      0.051 potential prior-data conflict
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_prior_sensitivity-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\texttt{powerscale\_sensitivity()} reports a sensitivity index \(\psi\)
for each parameter. \textbf{One important limitation:} it perturbs only
the \emph{explicit} prior on \texttt{logit\_error\_prob}. It cannot
detect sensitivity to the \emph{implicit} prior over the rule set, which
is the much bigger source of structural bias for Model B. Low \(\psi\)
here means Model B is robust to the parametric prior on \(\varepsilon\)
--- it does \emph{not} mean Model B is robust to the choice of candidate
rules. The next subsection addresses that.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Parameter Recovery (Model B)}\label{parameter-recovery-model-b}

We test recovery systematically across the plausible range of
\texttt{error\_prob}, with multiple independent datasets per true value,
each with its own randomized stimulus schedule. \textbf{Caveat upfront:}
the data are generated by Model A, fitted by Model B. If Model A
converges to a rule that is not in Model B's candidate set, recovery
will be biased. This is the structural-bias signature again.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{run\_rule\_recovery }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(true\_error\_prob, iteration,}
\NormalTok{                              stimulus\_info, n\_blocks) \{}
\NormalTok{  subject\_seed }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ iteration }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{1000} \SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(true\_error\_prob }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  sched        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed)}

\NormalTok{  sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{n\_particles =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED,}
    \AttributeTok{error\_prob  =}\NormalTok{ true\_error\_prob,}
    \AttributeTok{obs         =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
    \AttributeTok{cat\_one     =}\NormalTok{ sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
    \AttributeTok{max\_dims    =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS,}
    \AttributeTok{quiet       =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  stan\_dat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{ntrials                =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(sched),}
    \AttributeTok{nfeatures              =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
    \AttributeTok{y                      =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response),}
    \AttributeTok{obs                    =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_mean =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
    \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  pf }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{pathfinder}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_dat, }\AttributeTok{num\_paths =} \DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}
                              \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed),}
    \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) }\ConstantTok{NULL}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  init\_val }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(pf)) pf }\ControlFlowTok{else} \FloatTok{0.5}

\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_dat, }\AttributeTok{init =}\NormalTok{ init\_val,}
      \AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) \{ }\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Fit error:"}\NormalTok{, e}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{message, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{); }\ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{ \}}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit)) }\FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(}\ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  draws\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"df"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{true\_error\_prob   =}\NormalTok{ true\_error\_prob,}
    \AttributeTok{iteration         =}\NormalTok{ iteration,}
    \AttributeTok{estimated\_eps\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(draws\_eps}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob),}
    \AttributeTok{est\_lower\_95      =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws\_eps}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{est\_upper\_95      =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws\_eps}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{had\_divergence    =} \FunctionTok{any}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{diagnostic\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{num\_divergent }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{eps\_values\_to\_test }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{n\_recovery\_iters   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{5}   \CommentTok{\# increase to 20–50 for publication{-}quality recovery}

\NormalTok{recovery\_file\_rule }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_rule\_recovery\_results.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(recovery\_file\_rule)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Running parameter recovery for Model B...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  recovery\_plan }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{true\_error\_prob =}\NormalTok{ eps\_values\_to\_test,}
    \AttributeTok{iteration       =} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_recovery\_iters)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  rule\_recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{future\_pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{true\_error\_prob =}\NormalTok{ recovery\_plan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{true\_error\_prob,}
         \AttributeTok{iteration       =}\NormalTok{ recovery\_plan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{iteration),}
\NormalTok{    run\_rule\_recovery,}
    \AttributeTok{stimulus\_info =}\NormalTok{ stimulus\_info,}
    \AttributeTok{n\_blocks      =}\NormalTok{ n\_blocks,}
    \AttributeTok{.options =} \FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(rule\_recovery\_results, recovery\_file\_rule)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovery complete and saved.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  rule\_recovery\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(recovery\_file\_rule)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Recovery results loaded.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Recovery results loaded.
\end{verbatim}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(rule\_recovery\_results,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ true\_error\_prob, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ estimated\_eps\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ est\_lower\_95, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ est\_upper\_95,}
                    \AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence),}
                \AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.005}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ had\_divergence), }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FALSE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"TRUE"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#D55E00"}\NormalTok{),}
                     \AttributeTok{name =} \StringTok{"Had divergence"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"Parameter Recovery: Model B (error\_prob)"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Points: posterior mean; bars: 95\% CrI; n="}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{                      n\_recovery\_iters,}
                      \StringTok{" datasets per true value.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
                      \StringTok{"Data generated by Model A; fitted by Model B."}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"True error\_prob"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Estimated error\_prob"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_parameter_recovery-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Discussion of Recovery Results.} Recovery of
\texttt{error\_prob} depends on whether Model B's candidate rule set
contains rules that match what Model A converges to on each replicate.
When the converged rule is in the candidate set, recovery is good. When
it is not, Model B compensates by inflating \(\varepsilon\) to absorb
the systematic misclassifications, and the posterior mean shifts up ---
the points drift above the diagonal. This drift is the
\emph{quantitative} version of the structural-bias warning.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Discharging the Post-Hoc Rule
Selection}\label{discharging-the-post-hoc-rule-selection}

Phase 0 Q5 surfaced the fact that Model B's candidate rules are picked
by inspecting the stimulus layout, which makes them empirical, not
prior. We discharge this in two complementary ways.

\subsection{Option 1: Sensitivity to the Candidate Rule
Set}\label{option-1-sensitivity-to-the-candidate-rule-set}

We define three alternative candidate rule sets and refit Model B on the
same data:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \textbf{Set A} --- the 7 hand-picked rules (the ones in the Stan code
  above: 5 flat + 2 nested).
\item
  \textbf{Set B} --- 5 rules generated \emph{blind} by running Model A
  on a separately-simulated training dataset and taking its top-weighted
  rules at the end of training. The training data is generated with
  \(\varepsilon = 0.1\) on a different schedule than the fitting data.
  This is the ``honest'' version of the candidate set.
\item
  \textbf{Set C} --- 5 rules deliberately chosen to be \emph{bad} (rules
  that disagree with most stimuli), as a stress test.
\end{itemize}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Helper to build a Stan model from a list of (predictor function) rules}
\NormalTok{build\_stan\_with\_rules }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(rule\_predictors, name\_suffix) \{}
\NormalTok{  R }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(rule\_predictors)}
\NormalTok{  rule\_lines }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"    int pred\%d = \%s;"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(R), rule\_predictors),}
    \AttributeTok{collapse =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  lp\_lines\_model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"    lp[\%d] = bernoulli\_lpmf(y[i] | pred\%d == 1 ? 1 {-} error\_prob : error\_prob);"}\NormalTok{,}
            \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(R), }\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(R)),}
    \AttributeTok{collapse =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  stan\_code }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}
\StringTok{data \{}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} ntrials;}
\StringTok{  int\textless{}lower=1\textgreater{} nfeatures;}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials] int\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} y;}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials, nfeatures] real obs;}
\StringTok{  real prior\_logit\_error\_mean;}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0\textgreater{} prior\_logit\_error\_sd;}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{parameters \{ real logit\_error\_prob; \}}
\StringTok{transformed parameters \{}
\StringTok{  real\textless{}lower=0, upper=1\textgreater{} error\_prob = inv\_logit(logit\_error\_prob);}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials] real lp\_marginal;}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:ntrials) \{}
\StringTok{    real h = obs[i, 1];}
\StringTok{    real p = obs[i, 2];}
\StringTok{\%s}
\StringTok{    int R = \%d;}
\StringTok{    vector[R] lp;}
\StringTok{\%s}
\StringTok{    lp\_marginal[i] = log\_sum\_exp(lp) {-} log(R);}
\StringTok{  \}}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{model \{}
\StringTok{  target += normal\_lpdf(logit\_error\_prob | prior\_logit\_error\_mean, prior\_logit\_error\_sd);}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:ntrials) target += lp\_marginal[i];}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{generated quantities \{}
\StringTok{  array[ntrials] real log\_lik;}
\StringTok{  for (i in 1:ntrials) log\_lik[i] = lp\_marginal[i];}
\StringTok{  real lprior = normal\_lpdf(logit\_error\_prob | prior\_logit\_error\_mean, prior\_logit\_error\_sd);}
\StringTok{\}}
\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, rule\_lines, R, lp\_lines\_model)}

\NormalTok{  fname }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"stan"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch14\_rule\_\%s.stan"}\NormalTok{,  name\_suffix))}
  \FunctionTok{write\_stan\_file}\NormalTok{(stan\_code, }\AttributeTok{dir =} \StringTok{"stan/"}\NormalTok{,}
                  \AttributeTok{basename =} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"ch14\_rule\_\%s.stan"}\NormalTok{, name\_suffix))}
  \FunctionTok{cmdstan\_model}\NormalTok{(fname, }\AttributeTok{force\_recompile =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Set A: hand{-}picked rules — 5 flat + 2 nested (replicates Model B exactly)}
\NormalTok{rules\_A }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"h \textgreater{} 2.5 ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"p \textless{} 2.5 ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"(h \textgreater{} 2.0 \&\& p \textgreater{} 3.0) ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"(h \textless{} 2.0 || p \textless{} 2.0) ? 0 : 1"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"(h \textgreater{} 2.5 \&\& p \textless{} 3.5) ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"(h \textgreater{} 3.5 || (h \textgreater{} 1.5 \&\& p \textless{} 1.5)) ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,}
  \StringTok{"(h \textgreater{} 1.5 \&\& (p \textless{} 2.5 || h \textgreater{} 3.5)) ? 1 : 0"}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Set B: blind rules from a fresh Model A run on a separate seed}
\NormalTok{get\_blind\_rules }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  blind\_sched }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{50000} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ seed)}
\NormalTok{  obs\_b   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(blind\_sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)])}
\NormalTok{  cat\_b   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ blind\_sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback}
\NormalTok{  res     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{track\_rule\_learning}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{n\_particles =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED, }\AttributeTok{error\_prob =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{obs =}\NormalTok{ obs\_b, }\AttributeTok{cat\_one =}\NormalTok{ cat\_b, }\AttributeTok{max\_dims =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS, }\AttributeTok{top\_k =} \DecValTok{5}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \CommentTok{\# Take the top 5 distinct rules at the final trial}
\NormalTok{  final }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ res }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(trial }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(trial)) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{distinct}\NormalTok{(dimension1, operation1, threshold1,}
\NormalTok{             dimension2, operation2, threshold2,}
\NormalTok{             logic, prediction, }\AttributeTok{.keep\_all =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{head}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{  pred\_strings }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{character}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(final))}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (k }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(final))) \{}
\NormalTok{    r }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ final[k, ]}
\NormalTok{    feat1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{dimension1 }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\StringTok{"h"} \ControlFlowTok{else} \StringTok{"p"}
\NormalTok{    cond1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%s \%s \%.2f"}\NormalTok{, feat1, r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{operation1, r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{threshold1)}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{dimension2)) \{}
\NormalTok{      pred\_strings[k] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"(\%s) ? \%d : \%d"}\NormalTok{, cond1, r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prediction, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prediction)}
\NormalTok{    \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{      feat2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{dimension2 }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\StringTok{"h"} \ControlFlowTok{else} \StringTok{"p"}
\NormalTok{      cond2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%s \%s \%.2f"}\NormalTok{, feat2, r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{operation2, r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{threshold2)}
\NormalTok{      logic\_op }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{logic }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"AND"}\NormalTok{) }\StringTok{"\&\&"} \ControlFlowTok{else} \StringTok{"||"}
\NormalTok{      pred\_strings[k] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"(\%s \%s \%s) ? \%d : \%d"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{                                  cond1, logic\_op, cond2,}
\NormalTok{                                  r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prediction, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ r}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{prediction)}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{  pred\_strings}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{rules\_B }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{get\_blind\_rules}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Set C: deliberately bad rules}
\NormalTok{rules\_C }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"h \textgreater{} 4.5 ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,        }\CommentTok{\# virtually never fires}
  \StringTok{"p \textgreater{} 4.5 ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,        }\CommentTok{\# virtually never fires}
  \StringTok{"h \textless{} 0.5 ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,        }\CommentTok{\# virtually never fires}
  \StringTok{"(h \textgreater{} 0.5 \&\& p \textgreater{} 0.5) ? 1 : 0"}\NormalTok{,  }\CommentTok{\# always fires}
  \StringTok{"(h \textgreater{} 0.5 || p \textgreater{} 0.5) ? 1 : 0"}   \CommentTok{\# always fires}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Compile and fit each}
\NormalTok{mod\_A }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_stan\_with\_rules}\NormalTok{(rules\_A, }\StringTok{"setA"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{mod\_B }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_stan\_with\_rules}\NormalTok{(rules\_B, }\StringTok{"setB"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{mod\_C }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{build\_stan\_with\_rules}\NormalTok{(rules\_C, }\StringTok{"setC"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{fit\_with\_rules }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(model, label) \{}
\NormalTok{  fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ rule\_data\_single, }\AttributeTok{init =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{  draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{))}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{rule\_set     =}\NormalTok{ label,}
    \AttributeTok{posterior\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob),}
    \AttributeTok{posterior\_lo =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{posterior\_hi =} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{elpd\_loo     =}\NormalTok{ loo}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{loo}\NormalTok{(fit}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"log\_lik"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{format =} \StringTok{"matrix"}\NormalTok{))}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{estimates[}\StringTok{"elpd\_loo"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Estimate"}\NormalTok{]}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{rule\_set\_sensitivity\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_rule\_set\_sensitivity.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(rule\_set\_sensitivity\_file)) \{}
\NormalTok{  rule\_set\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{fit\_with\_rules}\NormalTok{(mod\_A, }\StringTok{"A: hand{-}picked"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{fit\_with\_rules}\NormalTok{(mod\_B, }\StringTok{"B: blind from Model A"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{fit\_with\_rules}\NormalTok{(mod\_C, }\StringTok{"C: deliberately bad"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(rule\_set\_results, rule\_set\_sensitivity\_file)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  rule\_set\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(rule\_set\_sensitivity\_file)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"rule\_set\_results"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(rule\_set\_results)}

  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(rule\_set\_results,}
         \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ rule\_set, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ posterior\_mean)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_pointrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ posterior\_lo, }\AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ posterior\_hi)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Sensitivity of Model B\textquotesingle{}s ε posterior to candidate rule set"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red dashed: true ε = 0.1 used to generate the data"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Posterior mean ± 95\% CrI for ε"}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{coord\_flip}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 3 x 5
  rule_set              posterior_mean posterior_lo posterior_hi elpd_loo
  <chr>                          <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>    <dbl>
1 A: hand-picked                0.0777       0.0120        0.204    -32.5
2 B: blind from Model A         0.159        0.0551        0.303    -36.9
3 C: deliberately bad           0.622        0.139         0.944    -44.6
\end{verbatim}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_rule_set_sensitivity-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading the rule-set sensitivity.} If the three posteriors
agree, the candidate-set choice is innocuous. If they disagree --- which
is the expected outcome --- the gap quantifies how much of Model B's
estimated \(\varepsilon\) is \emph{cognitive} (genuinely tracking the
agent's error rate) vs \emph{structural} (compensating for missing
rules). Set C should produce a wildly inflated \(\varepsilon\) and a
much worse ELPD, providing the upper bound on how much rule-set choice
can move the posterior.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Sensitivity to \texttt{max\_dims} (Rule
Grammar
Depth)}{Sensitivity to max\_dims (Rule Grammar Depth)}}\label{sensitivity-to-max_dims-rule-grammar-depth}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{max\_dims\_results\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_max\_dims\_sensitivity.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(max\_dims\_results\_file)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Sweeping max\_dims...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  max\_dims\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(md) \{}
    \FunctionTok{tryCatch}\NormalTok{(\{}
\NormalTok{      sched }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{42}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      sim\_md }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{n\_particles =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED,}
        \AttributeTok{error\_prob  =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{obs         =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
        \AttributeTok{cat\_one     =}\NormalTok{ sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
        \AttributeTok{max\_dims    =}\NormalTok{ md}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{      cum\_acc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(sim\_md}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback)}
      \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{max\_dims =}\NormalTok{ md, }\AttributeTok{mean\_accuracy =}\NormalTok{ cum\_acc)}
\NormalTok{    \}, }\AttributeTok{error =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(e) \{}
      \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{max\_dims =}\NormalTok{ md, }\AttributeTok{mean\_accuracy =} \ConstantTok{NA\_real\_}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    \})}
\NormalTok{  \})}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(max\_dims\_results, max\_dims\_results\_file)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  max\_dims\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(max\_dims\_results\_file)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"max\_dims\_results"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(max\_dims\_results)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Note: max\_dims = 3 will fall back to 2 in our 2{-}feature task.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"The point of this sweep is to make the structural choice visible,}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"not to discover anything new.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 3 x 2
  max_dims mean_accuracy
     <dbl>         <dbl>
1        1         0.625
2        2         0.766
3        3         0.766
Note: max_dims = 3 will fall back to 2 in our 2-feature task.
 The point of this sweep is to make the structural choice visible,
 not to discover anything new.
\end{verbatim}

\texttt{max\_dims} is a structural commitment of the rule grammar. In
our 2-feature task it can be 1 or 2 (3 is meaningless). In a
higher-dimensional task it would be a genuine free parameter and should
be either fixed with explicit cognitive justification or inferred via
NPE.

\subsection{Sensitivity to Resampling
Threshold}\label{sensitivity-to-resampling-threshold}

Phase 0 Q4 committed to \texttt{RESAMPLE\_THRESHOLD\ =\ 0.5}. This
controls how aggressively the particle filter reorganises its hypothesis
set: lower values allow more particle degeneracy before resampling;
higher values trigger resampling earlier. The cognitive interpretation
is something like ``how quickly the learner abandons low-performing
hypotheses.''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{resample\_sensitivity\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_resample\_threshold\_sensitivity.rds"}\NormalTok{)}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(resample\_sensitivity\_file)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Sweeping resampling threshold...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  resample\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.25}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.50}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.75}\NormalTok{), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(rt) \{}
\NormalTok{    accs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(rep) \{}
\NormalTok{      sched }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{42000} \SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ rep)}
\NormalTok{      sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{n\_particles               =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED,}
        \AttributeTok{error\_prob                =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{obs                       =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
        \AttributeTok{cat\_one                   =}\NormalTok{ sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
        \AttributeTok{max\_dims                  =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS,}
        \AttributeTok{resample\_threshold\_factor =}\NormalTok{ rt,}
        \AttributeTok{quiet                     =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{      )}
      \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback)}
\NormalTok{    \}, }\FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{resample\_threshold =}\NormalTok{ rt,}
           \AttributeTok{mean\_accuracy =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(accs), }\AttributeTok{sd\_accuracy =} \FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(accs))}
\NormalTok{  \})}
  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(resample\_results, resample\_sensitivity\_file)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  resample\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(resample\_sensitivity\_file)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"resample\_results"}\NormalTok{)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(resample\_results)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"If mean accuracy varies substantially across thresholds,}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"the resampling threshold is a consequential structural choice.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"If it is roughly stable, the choice is innocuous for this task.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
# A tibble: 3 x 3
  resample_threshold mean_accuracy sd_accuracy
               <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>
1               0.25         0.825      0.0960
2               0.5          0.839      0.0621
3               0.75         0.817      0.109 
If mean accuracy varies substantially across thresholds,
 the resampling threshold is a consequential structural choice.
 If it is roughly stable, the choice is innocuous for this task.
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{Option 2: Treat Set A as a Sensitivity Condition, Not a
Canonical
Model}\label{option-2-treat-set-a-as-a-sensitivity-condition-not-a-canonical-model}

Rather than use Set A as \emph{the} Model B and add Set B as a
``robustness check,'' the honest framing is to report all three sets as
variants of Model B with no privileged status. The reader sees three
posteriors instead of one and decides for themselves how much of the
variation is structural. This is the framing we adopt for the chapter 15
model comparison, where Model B is reported as a \emph{family} of
proxies indexed by the candidate set rather than as a single point
estimate.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Calibration: Two Different Checks for Two Different
Things}\label{calibration-two-different-checks-for-two-different-things}

Earlier drafts of this chapter ran one block under the header
``Simulation-Based Calibration (SBC)'' that generated data with the
particle filter (Model A) and fitted Model B. \textbf{That is not SBC.}
Talts--Vehtari SBC requires the data to be drawn from the \emph{same}
generative model that is then fitted, so that the joint \((\theta, y)\)
distribution of the inference model is what gets calibrated. When the
generative and inferential models differ, what you are running is closer
to a robustness check than a calibration check, and labelling it SBC is
misleading.

We split the misnamed block into two correctly-labelled checks: (1) a
\textbf{Cross-Model Calibration Check} (data from Model A, fitted by
Model B) and (2) a \textbf{Real SBC for Model B} (data from Model B
itself, fitted by Model B). The first is a robustness check; the second
is the actual Talts--Vehtari calibration test.

\subsection{Cross-Model Calibration Check (NOT
SBC)}\label{cross-model-calibration-check-not-sbc}

\textbf{Procedure.} For each iteration \(i = 1, \ldots, S\): draw
\(\varepsilon_i\) from the prior, simulate one dataset with Model A (the
particle filter) using \(\varepsilon_i\), fit Model B to that dataset,
record the rank of \(\varepsilon_i\) within Model B's posterior. Compute
the rank ECDF.

\textbf{What a flat ECDF means here.} It means: under the specific
misspecification of Model B for data from Model A, \(\varepsilon\)
happens to be recovered with the right \emph{marginal} coverage. It does
\textbf{not} mean Model B is calibrated in the Talts--Vehtari sense,
because the joint \((\theta, y)\) distribution of the inference model is
not the joint distribution of the data-generating process.

\textbf{Why we run it anyway.} Because it answers a useful practical
question: ``when I fit Model B to data that came from a richer
generative process (Model A), are the resulting credible intervals
approximately well-calibrated for the \emph{cognitive} parameter
\(\varepsilon\)?'' The answer to that question is informative even
though it is not what SBC is technically supposed to answer.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_cross\_iters }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{500}   \CommentTok{\# use \textgreater{}= 1000 for production}

\NormalTok{cross\_generator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}
  \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
\NormalTok{    logit\_eps\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{    eps\_true       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(logit\_eps\_true)}

\NormalTok{    sched }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1e8}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
    \CommentTok{\# }\AlertTok{NOTE}\CommentTok{: data drawn from Model A (the particle filter)}
\NormalTok{    sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{n\_particles =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED,}
      \AttributeTok{error\_prob  =}\NormalTok{ eps\_true,}
      \AttributeTok{obs         =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
      \AttributeTok{cat\_one     =}\NormalTok{ sched}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback,}
      \AttributeTok{max\_dims    =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS,}
      \AttributeTok{quiet       =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{    )}

    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{logit\_error\_prob =}\NormalTok{ logit\_eps\_true),}
      \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{ntrials                =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(sched),}
        \AttributeTok{nfeatures              =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
        \AttributeTok{y                      =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response),}
        \AttributeTok{obs                    =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_mean =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{cross\_backend }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  mod\_rule\_stan,}
  \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{cross\_datasets }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  cross\_generator,}
  \AttributeTok{n\_sims =}\NormalTok{ n\_cross\_iters}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{cross\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  cross\_datasets, cross\_backend,}
  \AttributeTok{cache\_mode     =} \StringTok{"results"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{cache\_location =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_cross\_model\_check\_cache"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{keep\_fits      =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Plotted as rank ECDF, but with explicit re{-}labelling in the title}
\FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf\_diff}\NormalTok{(cross\_results) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"CROSS{-}MODEL CALIBRATION CHECK (not SBC)"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Data: Model A. Fit: Model B. Flat ≠ calibrated, only marginally well{-}covered."}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_cross_model_check-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(cross\_results) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Cross{-}model rank histogram (not SBC)"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_cross_model_check-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading this plot.} A flat curve here means Model B's
\(\varepsilon\) posterior covers Model A's true generating
\(\varepsilon\) at approximately the right marginal rate. This is
\emph{useful} --- it tells you Model B is at least not catastrophically
wrong as a tool for estimating cognitive error rates. But it does not
say Model B's inference engine is calibrated in the Talts--Vehtari
sense, because the data-generating distribution is not the inference
model's joint distribution.

\subsection{Real SBC for Model B}\label{real-sbc-for-model-b}

For a proper Talts--Vehtari SBC of Model B, the generator must use Model
B itself: draw \(\varepsilon\) from the prior, draw a rule index from
\(\{1, \ldots, 5\}\) uniformly (mirroring Model B's mixture prior), and
generate Bernoulli responses accordingly. Then fit Model B and check the
rank distribution.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_real\_sbc\_iters }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{500}   \CommentTok{\# use \textgreater{}= 1000 for production}

\NormalTok{real\_sbc\_generator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}
  \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
\NormalTok{    logit\_eps\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{    eps\_true       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(logit\_eps\_true)}

    \CommentTok{\# Use the SAME generator as Model B\textquotesingle{}s mixture: draw a rule, generate Bernoulli responses}
\NormalTok{    rule\_idx }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{L, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    sched    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1e8}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    obs\_mat  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)])}
\NormalTok{    n\_trials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(obs\_mat)}

\NormalTok{    rule\_pred\_for\_trial }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{      h }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_mat[i, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]; p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_mat[i, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{]}
      \ControlFlowTok{switch}\NormalTok{(rule\_idx,}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{),}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{),}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{2.0} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{3.0}\NormalTok{),}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{!}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.0} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{)),}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{2.5} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{),}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{3.5} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ (h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.5} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)),}
             \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.5} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ (p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.5} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{    preds  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_trials), rule\_pred\_for\_trial, }\FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    p\_cat1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(preds }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ eps\_true, eps\_true)}
\NormalTok{    y      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(n\_trials, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_cat1)}

    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{logit\_error\_prob =}\NormalTok{ logit\_eps\_true),}
      \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{ntrials                =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials,}
        \AttributeTok{nfeatures              =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
        \AttributeTok{y                      =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(y),}
        \AttributeTok{obs                    =}\NormalTok{ obs\_mat,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_mean =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
        \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{real\_sbc\_backend }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  mod\_rule\_stan,}
  \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{real\_sbc\_datasets }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  real\_sbc\_generator,}
  \AttributeTok{n\_sims =}\NormalTok{ n\_real\_sbc\_iters}
\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{real\_sbc\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  real\_sbc\_datasets, real\_sbc\_backend,}
  \AttributeTok{cache\_mode     =} \StringTok{"results"}\NormalTok{,}
  \AttributeTok{cache\_location =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_real\_sbc\_cache"}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{keep\_fits      =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf\_diff}\NormalTok{(real\_sbc\_results) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"REAL SBC for Model B (Talts–Vehtari)"}\NormalTok{,}
          \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Data: Model B\textquotesingle{}s own mixture generator. Fit: Model B."}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_real_sbc_model_B-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(real\_sbc\_results) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Real SBC rank histogram for Model B"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_real_sbc_model_B-2.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading this plot.} This is the actual calibration check. A flat
ECDF here means Stan correctly inverts Model B (Model B's inference
engine is calibrated in the Talts--Vehtari sense). Systematic deviations
would indicate a miscalibration in the Stan implementation itself --- a
sign error, an off-by-one in the marginalisation, or a
prior-on-\texttt{logit\_error\_prob} mismatch. \textbf{This is the only
diagnostic in the chapter that tests the Stan engine on its own terms.}
The cross-model check above tests something else --- it tests how Model
B behaves when applied to data from a richer process --- and that
something else is informative but it is not SBC.

\subsection{Posterior SBC for Model B (Phase
6c)}\label{posterior-sbc-for-model-b-phase-6c}

The persona's Phase 6c calls for \textbf{Posterior SBC (PSBC)}: instead
of drawing true parameters from the prior, draw them from the
\emph{fitted posterior}. This tests calibration in the specific
high-probability region the actual data force us into. A sampler can be
globally unbiased (prior SBC passes) yet struggle locally (PSBC fails)
or vice versa.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_psbc\_iters }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{500}  \CommentTok{\# use \textgreater{}= 1000 for production}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan)) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# Draw true parameters from the fitted posterior, not the prior}
\NormalTok{  fitted\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"logit\_error\_prob"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  fitted\_logit\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fitted\_draws}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{logit\_error\_prob}

\NormalTok{  psbc\_generator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_generator\_function}\NormalTok{(}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{() \{}
      \CommentTok{\# Draw from the POSTERIOR, not the prior}
\NormalTok{      logit\_eps\_true }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(fitted\_logit\_eps, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      eps\_true       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(logit\_eps\_true)}

      \CommentTok{\# Generate from Model B\textquotesingle{}s own mixture (same as real SBC; 7 rules)}
\NormalTok{      rule\_idx }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{L, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      sched    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \FunctionTok{sample.int}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1e8}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      obs\_mat  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)])}
\NormalTok{      n\_trials }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(obs\_mat)}

\NormalTok{      rule\_pred\_for\_trial }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(j) \{}
\NormalTok{        h }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_mat[j, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]; p }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ obs\_mat[j, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{]}
        \ControlFlowTok{switch}\NormalTok{(rule\_idx,}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{),}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.5}\NormalTok{),}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{2.0} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{3.0}\NormalTok{),}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{!}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.0} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{)),}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{2.5} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{),}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{3.5} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ (h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.5} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)),}
               \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{1.5} \SpecialCharTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ (p }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{2.5} \SpecialCharTok{||}\NormalTok{ h }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{3.5}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{      \}}
\NormalTok{      preds  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_trials), rule\_pred\_for\_trial, }\FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{      p\_cat1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(preds }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ eps\_true, eps\_true)}
\NormalTok{      y      }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(n\_trials, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p\_cat1)}

      \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{variables =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{logit\_error\_prob =}\NormalTok{ logit\_eps\_true),}
        \AttributeTok{generated =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{ntrials                =}\NormalTok{ n\_trials,}
          \AttributeTok{nfeatures              =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
          \AttributeTok{y                      =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(y),}
          \AttributeTok{obs                    =}\NormalTok{ obs\_mat,}
          \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_mean =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
          \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  psbc\_backend }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{SBC\_backend\_cmdstan\_sample}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    mod\_rule\_stan,}
    \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  psbc\_datasets }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_datasets}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    psbc\_generator,}
    \AttributeTok{n\_sims =}\NormalTok{ n\_psbc\_iters}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  psbc\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{compute\_SBC}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    psbc\_datasets, psbc\_backend,}
    \AttributeTok{cache\_mode     =} \StringTok{"results"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{cache\_location =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_psbc\_cache"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{keep\_fits      =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{plot\_ecdf\_diff}\NormalTok{(psbc\_results) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"POSTERIOR SBC for Model B (Phase 6c)"}\NormalTok{,}
            \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"True ε drawn from FITTED posterior, not the prior.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{Tests local calibration in the high{-}probability region."}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{plot\_rank\_hist}\NormalTok{(psbc\_results) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{ggtitle}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Posterior SBC rank histogram for Model B"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{13-CategorizationModelsRules_files/figure-pdf/ch14_psbc_model_B-1.pdf}
\end{center}

\textbf{Reading this plot.} A flat ECDF here means Model B is calibrated
\emph{locally} around the posterior region that the actual data
identified. If prior SBC passes but PSBC fails, the sampler is globally
unbiased but locally struggles --- a sign of geometry issues in the
high-posterior region. If both pass, the Stan implementation is sound in
both senses.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Neural Posterior Estimation for Model
A}\label{neural-posterior-estimation-for-model-a}

This is the section that does what earlier drafts of this material
avoided with a weak tool: it fits the actual cognitive theory rather
than a tractable proxy. Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) --- a modern
simulation-based inference method --- trains a conditional normalising
flow to approximate the posterior \(p(\theta \mid y)\) from simulated
\((\theta, y)\) pairs, without requiring a tractable likelihood or
hand-crafted summary statistics. The Deistler et al.~(2025) tutorial and
the BayesFlow paper (Radev et al.~2023) provide the methodological
background; we give a working implementation here.

\textbf{Why NPE instead of rejection ABC?} Earlier drafts used rejection
ABC with three hand-crafted summary statistics. That approach had three
problems:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Schedule mismatch bug.} Each ABC simulation drew a \emph{new}
  random schedule, injecting trial-order noise on top of parameter
  signal. The particle filter is path-dependent, so the learning slope
  and per-stimulus accuracy are functions of \emph{which stimuli
  appeared when}. The correct conditional simulator holds the schedule
  fixed at the observed schedule and re-randomises only the particle
  initialisation and Bernoulli draws.
\item
  \textbf{Summary statistic insufficiency.} The three summaries (block-1
  vs block-final accuracy, per-stimulus accuracy vector, lag-1
  autocorrelation) were reasonable guesses, but guesses. NPE with a
  learned embedding network discovers the relevant features of the
  response sequence end-to-end from the training loss, eliminating the
  need to defend ``why these three and not others?''
\item
  \textbf{No amortization.} Rejection ABC pays the full simulation
  budget per subject. NPE pays it once during training. After training,
  inference on any new subject takes milliseconds. This is what makes
  the Chapter 15 comparison across all three cognitive theories
  executable.
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Setting Up the Python
Environment}\label{setting-up-the-python-environment}

We use the \texttt{sbi} package (Tejero-Cantero et al.~2020; Boelts et
al.~2024) via \texttt{reticulate}. The key components are: a prior, a
simulator wrapper, and an inference network (a normalising flow with a
learned embedding network).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# ── Python environment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\CommentTok{\# Requires: pip install sbi torch numpy}
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (run\_intensive\_checks) \{}
  \FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(reticulate)}
\NormalTok{  reticulate}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{use\_virtualenv}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"r{-}reticulate"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{required =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{py\_available}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{initialize =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)) \{}
    \FunctionTok{stop}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Python not available. Install Python and: pip install sbi torch numpy"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  sbi   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{import}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"sbi"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  torch }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{import}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"torch"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  np    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{import}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"numpy"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  utils }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{import}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"sbi.utils"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  infer }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{import}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"sbi.inference"}\NormalTok{)}

  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"sbi version:"}\NormalTok{, sbi}\SpecialCharTok{$}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{\_\_version\_\_}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"torch version:"}\NormalTok{, torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{\_\_version\_\_}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Skipping NPE setup (run\_intensive\_checks is FALSE).}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{verbatim}
Skipping NPE setup (run_intensive_checks is FALSE).
\end{verbatim}

\subsection{The Simulator Wrapper}\label{the-simulator-wrapper}

The simulator must take a parameter vector \(\theta\) and return the
observed data \(y\). \textbf{Critically, the schedule is held fixed} ---
we condition on the observed trial order and only re-randomise the
particle filter's internal stochasticity (particle initialisation and
Bernoulli response noise). This fixes the schedule-mismatch bug from
earlier drafts.

We infer two parameters jointly:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \(\varepsilon\) --- error probability, on the logit scale for
  unconstrained inference
\item
  \(\log(N_{\text{particles}})\) --- working-memory bound, on the log
  scale
\end{itemize}

The data representation for NPE is the full per-trial response vector
\(y \in \{0, 1\}^T\), concatenated with the per-trial stimulus features
and feedback. The embedding network learns what to attend to.
