\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# The observed schedule — used for ALL simulations (schedule{-}fix)}
\NormalTok{npe\_schedule }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fit\_schedule}
\NormalTok{npe\_obs\_mat  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(npe\_schedule[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)])}
\NormalTok{npe\_cat\_one  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ npe\_schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback}
\NormalTok{n\_trials\_npe }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(npe\_schedule)}

\CommentTok{\# Build the context: [height, position, feedback] per trial}
\CommentTok{\# This is constant across simulations because the schedule is fixed}
\NormalTok{context\_per\_trial }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(npe\_obs\_mat, npe\_cat\_one)  }\CommentTok{\# (n\_trials, 3)}

\CommentTok{\# R{-}side simulator: theta → response vector}
\CommentTok{\# theta[1] = logit(eps), theta[2] = log(N\_particles)}
\NormalTok{simulate\_model\_a }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(theta\_vec) \{}
\NormalTok{  eps     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(theta\_vec[}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{  n\_part  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{L, }\FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(theta\_vec[}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{]))))}

\NormalTok{  sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{n\_particles               =}\NormalTok{ n\_part,}
    \AttributeTok{error\_prob                =}\NormalTok{ eps,}
    \AttributeTok{obs                       =}\NormalTok{ npe\_obs\_mat,}
    \AttributeTok{cat\_one                   =}\NormalTok{ npe\_cat\_one,}
    \AttributeTok{max\_dims                  =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS,}
    \AttributeTok{resample\_threshold\_factor =}\NormalTok{ RESAMPLE\_THRESHOLD,}
    \AttributeTok{quiet                     =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{  )}
  \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Test it}
\NormalTok{test\_theta }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{qlogis}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{test\_y     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{simulate\_model\_a}\NormalTok{(test\_theta)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Test simulation: eps ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(test\_theta[}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]), }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{),}
    \StringTok{", N\_particles ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(test\_theta[}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{])),}
    \StringTok{", mean response ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(test\_y), }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Generating the Training
Dataset}\label{generating-the-training-dataset}

NPE training requires a dataset of \((\theta, x)\) pairs where \(x\)
includes both the response vector and the stimulus context. We generate
this dataset in R (leveraging \texttt{furrr} parallelism) and pass it to
Python as tensors.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_train\_sims   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{20000}   \CommentTok{\# 20k sims; increase to 50k for production}
\NormalTok{npe\_cache\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_npe\_training\_data.rds"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(npe\_cache\_file)) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Generating NPE training dataset ("}\NormalTok{, n\_train\_sims, }\StringTok{" simulations)...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2026}\NormalTok{)}

  \CommentTok{\# Draw parameters from the prior}
\NormalTok{  train\_logit\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_train\_sims, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{  train\_log\_npart }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(n\_train\_sims, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{300}\NormalTok{))  }\CommentTok{\# log{-}uniform over [20, 300]}
\NormalTok{  train\_thetas    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(train\_logit\_eps, train\_log\_npart)}

  \CommentTok{\# Simulate in parallel — schedule is FIXED (the bug fix)}
\NormalTok{  train\_responses }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{future\_map}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_train\_sims),}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) }\FunctionTok{simulate\_model\_a}\NormalTok{(train\_thetas[i, ]),}
    \AttributeTok{.options =} \FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{.progress =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  train\_y\_mat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(rbind, train\_responses)}

  \FunctionTok{saveRDS}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{thetas =}\NormalTok{ train\_thetas, }\AttributeTok{responses =}\NormalTok{ train\_y\_mat),}
\NormalTok{          npe\_cache\_file)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Training data saved:"}\NormalTok{, n\_train\_sims, }\StringTok{"simulations.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  cached\_train }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readRDS}\NormalTok{(npe\_cache\_file)}
\NormalTok{  train\_thetas }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cached\_train}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{thetas}
\NormalTok{  train\_y\_mat  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cached\_train}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{responses}
\NormalTok{  n\_train\_sims }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(train\_thetas)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded"}\NormalTok{, n\_train\_sims, }\StringTok{"cached training simulations.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Training the NPE Inference
Network}\label{training-the-npe-inference-network}

We use SNPE with a neural spline flow. The embedding network is a small
MLP that processes the concatenation of
\texttt{{[}response,\ height,\ position,\ feedback{]}} per trial into a
learned summary vector.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (run\_intensive\_checks) \{}
\NormalTok{  npe\_model\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_npe\_trained\_posterior.pkl"}\NormalTok{)}
  
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(npe\_model\_file)) \{}
    \CommentTok{\# Convert to PyTorch tensors}
\NormalTok{    theta\_tensor }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(train\_thetas, }\AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}
  
    \CommentTok{\# Build data tensor: concatenate [response\_t, height\_t, position\_t, feedback\_t]}
    \CommentTok{\# Shape: (n\_sims, n\_trials * 4)}
\NormalTok{    x\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_train\_sims), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{      resp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ train\_y\_mat[i, ]}
      \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{t}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(resp, context\_per\_trial)))}
\NormalTok{    \})}
\NormalTok{    x\_mat    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(rbind, x\_list)}
\NormalTok{    x\_tensor }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(x\_mat, }\AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}
  
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"theta shape:"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(theta\_tensor}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{shape, }\AttributeTok{collapse =} \StringTok{" x "}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"x shape:    "}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(x\_tensor}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{shape, }\AttributeTok{collapse =} \StringTok{" x "}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  
    \CommentTok{\# Define prior bounds for sbi}
\NormalTok{    prior\_low  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{3} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD,}
                                  \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)),}
                                \AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}
\NormalTok{    prior\_high }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{3} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD,}
                                  \FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{300}\NormalTok{)),}
                                \AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}
\NormalTok{    sbi\_prior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ utils}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{BoxUniform}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{low =}\NormalTok{ prior\_low, }\AttributeTok{high =}\NormalTok{ prior\_high)}
  
    \CommentTok{\# Build and train the NPE}
\NormalTok{    inference\_obj }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ infer}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{SNPE}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{prior             =}\NormalTok{ sbi\_prior,}
      \AttributeTok{density\_estimator =} \StringTok{"nsf"}   \CommentTok{\# neural spline flow}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    inference\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{append\_simulations}\NormalTok{(theta\_tensor, x\_tensor)}
  
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Training NPE (this takes a few minutes on CPU)...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    density\_estimator }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ inference\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{train}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{training\_batch\_size =} \DecValTok{256}\NormalTok{L,}
      \AttributeTok{learning\_rate       =} \FloatTok{5e{-}4}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{show\_train\_summary  =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{    )}
  
    \CommentTok{\# Build the posterior object}
\NormalTok{    npe\_posterior }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ inference\_obj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{build\_posterior}\NormalTok{(density\_estimator)}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"NPE training complete.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  
    \CommentTok{\# Save the trained model state}
\NormalTok{    torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{save}\NormalTok{(density\_estimator}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{state\_dict}\NormalTok{(), npe\_model\_file)}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Trained NPE saved to"}\NormalTok{, npe\_model\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Pre{-}trained NPE found at"}\NormalTok{, npe\_model\_file, }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"To use it, rebuild the sbi inference object and load the state dict:}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"  inference\_obj$build\_posterior() {-}\textgreater{} npe\_posterior}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"  (see sbi docs for state\_dict loading; requires same network architecture)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Performing Inference on the Observed
Data}\label{performing-inference-on-the-observed-data}

After training, inference is instantaneous: evaluate the trained network
at the observed data \(x_{\text{obs}}\).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Build the observed data tensor (same format as training)}
\NormalTok{obs\_responses }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(fit\_sim}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response)}
\NormalTok{obs\_x\_vec     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{t}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(obs\_responses, context\_per\_trial)))}
\NormalTok{obs\_x\_tensor  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(obs\_x\_vec, }\AttributeTok{nrow =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}

\CommentTok{\# Sample from the NPE posterior}
\NormalTok{n\_npe\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{10000}\NormalTok{L}
\NormalTok{npe\_samples   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ npe\_posterior}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{shape =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(n\_npe\_samples),}
  \AttributeTok{x     =}\NormalTok{ obs\_x\_tensor}
\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{npe\_draws }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(npe\_samples}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{numpy}\NormalTok{())}
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(npe\_draws) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"logit\_eps"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_n\_particles"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{npe\_eps    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(npe\_draws[, }\StringTok{"logit\_eps"}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{npe\_n\_part }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(npe\_draws[, }\StringTok{"log\_n\_particles"}\NormalTok{])}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"NPE posterior for ε:           mean ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(npe\_eps), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{),}
    \StringTok{"  95\% CI = ["}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{","}\NormalTok{,}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"]}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"NPE posterior for N\_particles: mean ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(npe\_n\_part), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
    \StringTok{"  95\% CI = ["}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_n\_part, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{","}\NormalTok{,}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_n\_part, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"]}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{eps =}\NormalTok{ npe\_eps), }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ eps)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(density)),}
                 \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{40}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"NPE Posterior for ε (Model A)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red dashed: true generating ε = 0.1"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"ε"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_npart }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{n\_part =}\NormalTok{ npe\_n\_part), }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ n\_part)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(density)),}
                 \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{40}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"NPE Posterior for N\_particles (Model A)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red dashed: true generating N\_particles = 100"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"N\_particles"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_eps }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_npart)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\textbf{Reading the NPE posterior.} Two things to check: (1) both
posteriors should contain the true generating values, and (2)
\(N_{\text{particles}}\) should show a meaningful posterior --- if it is
prior-like, the data are uninformative about working-memory capacity for
this task length. A narrow posterior on \(N_{\text{particles}}\) would
mean the task is diagnostic for this cognitive parameter, which is a
substantive finding.

\subsection{NPE Validation: SBC + Expected Coverage (Phase
6)}\label{npe-validation-sbc-expected-coverage-phase-6}

The persona's Phase 6 requires calibration checks. For NPE, the standard
diagnostics are marginal SBC (Talts et al.~2018) and expected coverage
(Hermans et al.~2022). Because NPE is amortized, running 1000
calibration checks takes seconds --- each requires only a forward pass
through the trained network.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{n\_cal }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{L}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Running NPE calibration diagnostics ("}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(n\_cal), }\StringTok{" checks)...}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Generate calibration set: draw theta from prior, simulate x}
\FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7777}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{cal\_logit\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(n\_cal, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN, LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD)}
\NormalTok{cal\_log\_npart }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(n\_cal, }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{), }\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{300}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{cal\_thetas    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(cal\_logit\_eps, cal\_log\_npart)}

\NormalTok{cal\_responses }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{future\_map}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_cal),}
  \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) }\FunctionTok{simulate\_model\_a}\NormalTok{(cal\_thetas[i, ]),}
  \AttributeTok{.options =} \FunctionTok{furrr\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{.progress =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{cal\_y\_mat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(rbind, cal\_responses)}

\CommentTok{\# Build calibration data tensors}
\NormalTok{cal\_x\_list }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_cal), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{  resp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cal\_y\_mat[i, ]}
  \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{t}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(resp, context\_per\_trial)))}
\NormalTok{\})}
\NormalTok{cal\_x\_mat    }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{do.call}\NormalTok{(rbind, cal\_x\_list)}
\NormalTok{cal\_x\_tensor }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(cal\_x\_mat, }\AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}

\CommentTok{\# ── Marginal SBC ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{n\_post\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{L}
\NormalTok{sbc\_ranks }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L, }\AttributeTok{nrow =}\NormalTok{ n\_cal, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(sbc\_ranks) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"logit\_eps"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"log\_n\_particles"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_cal)) \{}
\NormalTok{  post\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ npe\_posterior}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{shape =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(n\_post\_samples),}
    \AttributeTok{x     =}\NormalTok{ cal\_x\_tensor[i }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{unsqueeze}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L)}
\NormalTok{  )}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{numpy}\NormalTok{()}

  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (j }\ControlFlowTok{in} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{    sbc\_ranks[i, j] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(post\_samples[, j] }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ cal\_thetas[i, j])}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{sbc\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_tibble}\NormalTok{(sbc\_ranks) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"parameter"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"rank"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{p\_sbc }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(sbc\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ rank)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(density)), }\AttributeTok{bins =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{,}
                 \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{parameter, }\AttributeTok{scales =} \StringTok{"free\_x"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Marginal SBC Rank Histograms (NPE, Model A)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Uniform = calibrated. U{-}shape = overconfident. Hump = underconfident."}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Rank"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Count"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# ── Expected Coverage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{coverage\_levels }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{seq}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{empirical\_coverage }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(coverage\_levels, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(alpha) \{}
\NormalTok{  covered }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{vapply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_cal), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
\NormalTok{    post\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ npe\_posterior}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{shape =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(n\_post\_samples),}
      \AttributeTok{x     =}\NormalTok{ cal\_x\_tensor[i }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{unsqueeze}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L)}
\NormalTok{    )}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{numpy}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{    q\_lo }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(post\_samples[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{], (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ alpha) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    q\_hi }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(post\_samples[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{], }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ alpha) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    cal\_thetas[i, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ q\_lo }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ cal\_thetas[i, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=}\NormalTok{ q\_hi}
\NormalTok{  \}, }\FunctionTok{logical}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}
  \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(covered)}
\NormalTok{\}, }\FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{coverage\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{nominal =}\NormalTok{ coverage\_levels, }\AttributeTok{empirical =}\NormalTok{ empirical\_coverage)}

\NormalTok{p\_coverage }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(coverage\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ nominal, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ empirical)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_abline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{slope =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{intercept =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"grey50"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_ribbon}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ nominal }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(nominal }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ nominal) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ n\_cal),}
                  \AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ nominal }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(nominal }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ nominal) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ n\_cal)),}
              \AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"grey80"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"\#0072B2"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Expected Coverage (NPE, Model A)"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Points on diagonal = well{-}calibrated. Grey band = 2SE."}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Nominal coverage"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Empirical coverage"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{coord\_equal}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xlim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{ylim =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(p\_sbc }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ p\_coverage)}

\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{{-}{-}{-} Coverage summary {-}{-}{-}}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"50\% nominal coverage: empirical ="}\NormalTok{,}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(coverage\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{empirical[coverage\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{nominal }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FloatTok{0.50}\NormalTok{], }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"95\% nominal coverage: empirical ="}\NormalTok{,}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(coverage\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{empirical[coverage\_df}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{nominal }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{], }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\textbf{Reading the validation.} The SBC rank histograms should be
uniform within sampling noise. The coverage plot should track the
diagonal within the grey 2-SE band. If the SBC histogram is U-shaped,
the posterior is overconfident --- increase training data or use a more
expressive flow. If it is humped, the posterior is underconfident. Both
checks benefit enormously from amortization: each of the 1000
calibration checks requires only a forward pass.

\subsection{The Main Empirical Result: NPE vs Stan
Posteriors}\label{the-main-empirical-result-npe-vs-stan-posteriors}

Here is the comparison the chapter has been building toward. We fit the
same observed dataset under both Model A (via NPE) and Model B (via HMC)
and overlay the two posteriors on \(\varepsilon\). \textbf{The gap
between them is the structural bias you accept when you replace the
cognitive theory with a tractable proxy.}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"npe\_eps"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\&\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan)) \{}
\NormalTok{  stan\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{))}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob}

\NormalTok{  comparison\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{eps =}\NormalTok{ npe\_eps, }\AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"Model A (NPE, particle filter)"}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{eps =}\NormalTok{ stan\_eps, }\AttributeTok{method =} \StringTok{"Model B (HMC, marginalised mixture)"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(comparison\_df, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ eps, }\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ method, }\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ method)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_density}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.4}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_vline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{xintercept =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"red"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{linetype =} \StringTok{"dashed"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Model A (NPE, particle filter)"}      \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#009E73"}\NormalTok{,}
                                  \StringTok{"Model B (HMC, marginalised mixture)"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#E69F00"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_manual}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Model A (NPE, particle filter)"}      \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#006D4E"}\NormalTok{,}
                                   \StringTok{"Model B (HMC, marginalised mixture)"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"\#B07D00"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{title    =} \StringTok{"NPE vs HMC: Posterior on ε for Model A vs Model B"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Red dashed: true generating ε = 0.1}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{The gap between these distributions IS the structural bias of the proxy."}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"ε"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Density"}
\NormalTok{    )}

  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{{-}{-}{-} Posterior summaries {-}{-}{-}}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Model A (NPE) posterior:    mean ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(npe\_eps), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{),}
      \StringTok{"  95\% CI = ["}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{","}\NormalTok{,}
                       \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"]}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Model B (HMC) posterior:    mean ="}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(stan\_eps), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{),}
      \StringTok{"  95\% CI = ["}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(stan\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{","}\NormalTok{,}
                       \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(stan\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"]}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"True generating ε:           0.1}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}

\subsection{Demonstrating That the Gap Tracks Rule-Set
Mismatch}\label{demonstrating-that-the-gap-tracks-rule-set-mismatch}

The headline claim --- ``the gap is structural bias, not inference
noise'' --- requires an experiment. We generate datasets from multiple
seeds and compare the NPE-vs-Stan gap. The gap should be small when
Model A converges to a rule that is in Model B's candidate set, and
large when it does not.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{exists}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"npe\_posterior"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\&\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.null}\NormalTok{(mod\_rule\_stan)) \{}
\NormalTok{  gap\_seeds }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{42}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{123}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{99}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{55}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  gap\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(gap\_seeds, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(s) \{}
\NormalTok{    sched\_g }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ s)}
\NormalTok{    sim\_g   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{n\_particles =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED, }\AttributeTok{error\_prob =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{obs =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched\_g[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
      \AttributeTok{cat\_one =}\NormalTok{ sched\_g}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback, }\AttributeTok{max\_dims =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS, }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{    )}

    \CommentTok{\# NPE inference (amortized — instant)}
\NormalTok{    obs\_g\_vec }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{t}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(sim\_g}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response),}
                                     \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched\_g[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
\NormalTok{                                     sched\_g}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback)))}
\NormalTok{    obs\_g\_tensor }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{tensor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(obs\_g\_vec, }\AttributeTok{nrow =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{dtype =}\NormalTok{ torch}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{float32)}
\NormalTok{    npe\_g\_samples }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ npe\_posterior}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{5000}\NormalTok{L), }\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ obs\_g\_tensor)}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{numpy}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{    npe\_g\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plogis}\NormalTok{(npe\_g\_samples[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{])}

    \CommentTok{\# Stan inference}
\NormalTok{    stan\_dat\_g }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{ntrials =} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(sched\_g), }\AttributeTok{nfeatures =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{L,}
      \AttributeTok{y =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(sim\_g}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sim\_response),}
      \AttributeTok{obs =} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(sched\_g[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)]),}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_mean =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_MEAN,}
      \AttributeTok{prior\_logit\_error\_sd   =}\NormalTok{ LOGIT\_ERROR\_PRIOR\_SD}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    fit\_g }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mod\_rule\_stan}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{sample}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ stan\_dat\_g, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ s, }\AttributeTok{chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{parallel\_chains =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{iter\_warmup =} \DecValTok{800}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{iter\_sampling =} \DecValTok{1000}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{refresh =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{adapt\_delta =} \FloatTok{0.9}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    stan\_g\_eps }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as\_draws\_df}\NormalTok{(fit\_g}\SpecialCharTok{$}\FunctionTok{draws}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"error\_prob"}\NormalTok{))}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{error\_prob}

    \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ s,}
           \AttributeTok{npe\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(npe\_g\_eps), }\AttributeTok{stan\_mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(stan\_g\_eps),}
           \AttributeTok{gap =} \FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(npe\_g\_eps) }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(stan\_g\_eps)),}
           \AttributeTok{npe\_covers\_truth  =}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.1} \SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_g\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.1} \SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(npe\_g\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{)),}
           \AttributeTok{stan\_covers\_truth =}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.1} \SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(stan\_g\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.025}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\&}\NormalTok{ (}\FloatTok{0.1} \SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=} \FunctionTok{quantile}\NormalTok{(stan\_g\_eps, }\FloatTok{0.975}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{  \})}

  \FunctionTok{print}\NormalTok{(gap\_results)}

  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(gap\_results, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(seed), }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ gap)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{geom\_col}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#E69F00"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"NPE{-}vs{-}Stan Gap Across Different Datasets"}\NormalTok{,}
         \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Large gap = converged rule NOT in Model B\textquotesingle{}s set. Small gap = converged rule IS in set."}\NormalTok{,}
         \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Schedule seed"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"|NPE mean ε − Stan mean ε|"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\textbf{Reading the gap demonstration.} If the gap varies across seeds
--- small for some, large for others --- the structural bias is driven
by rule-set mismatch, not inference noise. This is the expected outcome
and the chapter's main empirical contribution.

\subsection{Amortization Payoff for Chapter
15}\label{amortization-payoff-for-chapter-15}

Because the NPE network is trained once, inference on any new subject's
data takes milliseconds. For the Chapter 15 comparison across all three
cognitive theories (GCM, Kalman prototype, rule particle filter), each
model gets one trained NPE network, and the full multi-subject
comparison runs in seconds. The code pattern:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# After training, inference on N subjects is trivial:}
\CommentTok{\# for each subject s:}
\CommentTok{\#   x\_s \textless{}{-} build\_data\_tensor(subject\_responses\_s, subject\_schedule\_s)}
\CommentTok{\#   posterior\_s \textless{}{-} npe\_posterior$sample(10000L, x = x\_s)}
\CommentTok{\# Total time: \textasciitilde{}N * 0.1 seconds}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

This is the methodological payoff of using NPE instead of ABC: the
Chapter 15 comparison becomes both honest (fitting the cognitive theory
directly) and actually executable (amortized inference scales to many
subjects).

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Extending the Particle Filter with Rule
Mutation}\label{extending-the-particle-filter-with-rule-mutation}

The particle filter as defined so far has no mechanism for entertaining
a rule that was not in the initial candidate set. Resampling purges
low-weight particles but cannot \emph{create} new rule variants, so the
filter's effective hypothesis space shrinks monotonically across trials.
On a stationary task like Kruschke (1993) this is fine --- the filter
converges on whichever small subset of initial particles happens to
match the data. But on a task where the ``correct'' rule changes over
time, the filter has no way to recover: all the rules that would fit the
new regime were likely eliminated in the first ten or twenty trials.

The fix is a \textbf{rule-mutation step}: at each trial, each particle
independently regenerates with probability \(\mu\), redrawn from the
rule grammar. This is the particle filter analogue of the process noise
\(q\) in the Kalman prototype (Ch. 12) and the recency weight
\(\lambda\) in the decay GCM (Ch. 11) --- a single parameter that
injects hypothesis turnover.

The three chapters now share a common shape:

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Chapter
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Model
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Non-stationarity parameter
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Mechanism
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
Ch. 11 (decay GCM) & Exemplar store & \(\lambda\) (forgetting rate) &
Exponentially downweight old exemplars \\
Ch. 12 (Kalman prototype) & Running mean + covariance & \(q\) (process
noise) & Re-inflate uncertainty each trial \\
Ch. 13 (rule filter, below) & Particle distribution over rules & \(\mu\)
(mutation rate) & Randomly regenerate a fraction of particles each
trial \\
\end{longtable}

\subsection{Mutation Mechanism}\label{mutation-mechanism}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# At each trial, each particle independently has probability mutation\_prob}
\CommentTok{\# of being replaced by a freshly drawn rule. This keeps the hypothesis space}
\CommentTok{\# from collapsing and allows the filter to track changes in the correct rule.}
\NormalTok{mutate\_particles }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(particles, mutation\_prob, n\_features,}
\NormalTok{                             max\_dims, feature\_range) \{}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (mutation\_prob }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}=} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) }\FunctionTok{return}\NormalTok{(particles)}
\NormalTok{  n }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(particles)}
\NormalTok{  to\_mutate }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{which}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{runif}\NormalTok{(n) }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ mutation\_prob)}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in}\NormalTok{ to\_mutate) \{}
\NormalTok{    particles[[i]] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{generate\_random\_rule}\NormalTok{(n\_features, max\_dims, feature\_range)}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{  particles}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Scenario-Aware Particle
Filter}\label{scenario-aware-particle-filter}

We extend \texttt{rule\_particle\_filter} to take a
\texttt{mutation\_prob} argument and a \texttt{scenario} switch that
controls how the true feedback sequence is produced. The scenarios
follow the canonical framework from Chapter 11: static Kruschke,
performance-contingent abrupt shifts, and smooth drift (in that order).
Note that contingent-shift flips are endogenous to the agent's own
accuracy --- a noisier agent triggers fewer flips and accumulates less
information about \(\mu\) (see Chapter 11 for discussion).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{make\_drift\_trajectory }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(ntrials, }\AttributeTok{drift\_sigma =} \FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{seed =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) \{}
  \FunctionTok{set.seed}\NormalTok{(seed)}
\NormalTok{  mu0\_init }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1.75}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{3.25}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  mu1\_init }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{3.25}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{1.75}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  d0 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(ntrials }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, drift\_sigma), }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, cumsum)}
\NormalTok{  d1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{apply}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{rnorm}\NormalTok{(ntrials }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, drift\_sigma), }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, cumsum)}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{mu0 =} \FunctionTok{sweep}\NormalTok{(d0, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, mu0\_init, }\StringTok{"+"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{mu1 =} \FunctionTok{sweep}\NormalTok{(d1, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, mu1\_init, }\StringTok{"+"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{rule\_particle\_filter\_scenario }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(schedule,}
\NormalTok{                                          error\_prob,}
                                          \AttributeTok{mutation\_prob              =} \FloatTok{0.0}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{n\_particles                =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED,}
                                          \AttributeTok{max\_dims                   =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS,}
                                          \AttributeTok{resample\_threshold\_factor  =}\NormalTok{ RESAMPLE\_THRESHOLD,}
                                          \AttributeTok{scenario                   =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{,}
                                                                          \StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{,}
                                                                          \StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{),}
                                          \AttributeTok{streak\_target              =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{drift\_traj                 =} \ConstantTok{NULL}\NormalTok{,}
                                          \AttributeTok{quiet                      =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  scenario   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{match.arg}\NormalTok{(scenario)}
\NormalTok{  n\_trials   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(schedule)}
\NormalTok{  obs        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(schedule[, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"height"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"position"}\NormalTok{)])}
\NormalTok{  base\_lab   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ schedule}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{category\_feedback}
\NormalTok{  n\_features }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ncol}\NormalTok{(obs)}
\NormalTok{  fr         }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{range}\NormalTok{(obs[, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]), }\FunctionTok{range}\NormalTok{(obs[, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{]))}

\NormalTok{  ps        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{initialize\_particles}\NormalTok{(n\_particles, n\_features, max\_dims, fr)}
\NormalTok{  particles }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ps}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{particles}
\NormalTok{  weights   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ps}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weights}

\NormalTok{  label\_flip         }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  streak             }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L}

\NormalTok{  response\_probs     }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{numeric}\NormalTok{(n\_trials)}
\NormalTok{  sim\_response       }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(n\_trials)}
\NormalTok{  observed\_feedback  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{integer}\NormalTok{(n\_trials)}
\NormalTok{  label\_flip\_trace   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{logical}\NormalTok{(n\_trials)}

  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \FunctionTok{seq\_len}\NormalTok{(n\_trials)) \{}
\NormalTok{    stim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(obs[i, ])}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Mutation step ─────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{    particles }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mutate\_particles}\NormalTok{(particles, mutation\_prob,}
\NormalTok{                                  n\_features, max\_dims, fr)}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Prediction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{    preds        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(particles, evaluate\_rule, }\AttributeTok{stimulus =}\NormalTok{ stim)}
\NormalTok{    p\_cat1\_rules }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(preds }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ error\_prob, error\_prob)}
\NormalTok{    p            }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(weights }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ p\_cat1\_rules)}
\NormalTok{    p            }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{1e{-}9}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{pmin}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FloatTok{1e{-}9}\NormalTok{, p))}
\NormalTok{    response\_probs[i] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ p}
\NormalTok{    sim\_response[i]   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbinom}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, p)}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Observed feedback depends on scenario ─────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{    fb }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{switch}\NormalTok{(scenario,}
      \AttributeTok{static           =}\NormalTok{ base\_lab[i],}
      \AttributeTok{contingent\_shift =} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (label\_flip) }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ base\_lab[i] }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ base\_lab[i],}
      \AttributeTok{drift            =}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{        d0 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(stim }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ drift\_traj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mu0[i, ]))}
\NormalTok{        d1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{abs}\NormalTok{(stim }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ drift\_traj}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mu1[i, ]))}
        \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(d1 }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ d0)}
\NormalTok{      \}}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{    observed\_feedback[i] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ fb}
\NormalTok{    label\_flip\_trace[i]  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ label\_flip}

    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{      streak }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (sim\_response[i] }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ fb) streak }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{L }\ControlFlowTok{else} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L}
      \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (streak }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}=}\NormalTok{ streak\_target) \{ label\_flip }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \SpecialCharTok{!}\NormalTok{label\_flip; streak }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{L \}}
\NormalTok{    \}}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Weight update ────────────────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{    weights }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{update\_weights}\NormalTok{(particles, weights, stim, fb, error\_prob)}

    \CommentTok{\# ── Resample if ESS is low ───────────────────────────────────────}
\NormalTok{    ess }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(weights}\SpecialCharTok{\^{}}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (ess }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ n\_particles }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ resample\_threshold\_factor) \{}
\NormalTok{      rs        }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{resample\_particles}\NormalTok{(particles, weights)}
\NormalTok{      particles }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ rs}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{particles}
\NormalTok{      weights   }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ rs}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{weights}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}

\NormalTok{  schedule }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{prob\_cat1         =}\NormalTok{ response\_probs,}
      \AttributeTok{sim\_response      =}\NormalTok{ sim\_response,}
      \AttributeTok{observed\_feedback =}\NormalTok{ observed\_feedback,}
      \AttributeTok{label\_flip        =}\NormalTok{ label\_flip\_trace,}
      \AttributeTok{correct           =} \FunctionTok{as.integer}\NormalTok{(sim\_response }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ observed\_feedback)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Agent Wrapper}\label{agent-wrapper}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{simulate\_rule\_agent\_scenario }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, scenario,}
\NormalTok{                                         error\_prob, mutation\_prob,}
\NormalTok{                                         subject\_seed,}
                                         \AttributeTok{n\_particles   =}\NormalTok{ N\_PARTICLES\_FIXED,}
                                         \AttributeTok{max\_dims      =}\NormalTok{ MAX\_RULE\_DIMS,}
                                         \AttributeTok{streak\_target =} \DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}
                                         \AttributeTok{drift\_sigma   =} \FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{  schedule }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{make\_subject\_schedule}\NormalTok{(stimulus\_info, n\_blocks, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed)}
\NormalTok{  drift\_tr }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (scenario }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{)}
    \FunctionTok{make\_drift\_trajectory}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(schedule), drift\_sigma, }\AttributeTok{seed =}\NormalTok{ subject\_seed)}
  \ControlFlowTok{else} \ConstantTok{NULL}

\NormalTok{  out }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rule\_particle\_filter\_scenario}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{schedule                  =}\NormalTok{ schedule,}
    \AttributeTok{error\_prob                =}\NormalTok{ error\_prob,}
    \AttributeTok{mutation\_prob             =}\NormalTok{ mutation\_prob,}
    \AttributeTok{n\_particles               =}\NormalTok{ n\_particles,}
    \AttributeTok{max\_dims                  =}\NormalTok{ max\_dims,}
    \AttributeTok{scenario                  =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
    \AttributeTok{streak\_target             =}\NormalTok{ streak\_target,}
    \AttributeTok{drift\_traj                =}\NormalTok{ drift\_tr,}
    \AttributeTok{resample\_threshold\_factor =}\NormalTok{ RESAMPLE\_THRESHOLD}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  out }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{agent\_id           =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id,}
      \AttributeTok{scenario           =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
      \AttributeTok{error\_prob\_true    =}\NormalTok{ error\_prob,}
      \AttributeTok{mutation\_prob\_true =}\NormalTok{ mutation\_prob}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(agent\_id) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cumulative\_accuracy =} \FunctionTok{cumsum}\NormalTok{(correct) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{row\_number}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}
    \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{tcolorbox}[enhanced jigsaw, toprule=.15mm, toptitle=1mm, bottomtitle=1mm, leftrule=.75mm, rightrule=.15mm, colframe=quarto-callout-note-color-frame, left=2mm, colback=white, titlerule=0mm, coltitle=black, colbacktitle=quarto-callout-note-color!10!white, opacityback=0, title=\textcolor{quarto-callout-note-color}{\faInfo}\hspace{0.5em}{Unified scenario framework across the three chapters}, arc=.35mm, opacitybacktitle=0.6, breakable, bottomrule=.15mm]

All three chapters use the same three scenarios in the same order
(static → contingent shift → drift), reflecting \textbf{increasing
assumption match} between the scenario's data-generating process and the
model's structural commitments. The canonical definition and rationale
are in Chapter 11. What differs across chapters is \emph{how well} the
assumption-matched scenario actually matches:

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 6\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Chapter
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Model
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Contingent shift
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Smooth drift
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
Ch. 11 --- Decay GCM & Exemplar memory with forgetting & Misspecified
(no abrupt mechanism); strong \(\lambda\) signal via post-flip recovery
& Graceful degradation; approximate match \\
Ch. 12 --- Kalman prototype & Gaussian-drift prototype tracker &
Misspecified; filter smooths the reversal & \textbf{Exact match}:
Gaussian drift is the model's native story \\
Ch. 13 --- Rule particle filter & Bayesian particle filter + mutation &
Approximate; mutation lets the filter recover & Approximate; mutation
supplies fresh candidates but does not track direction \\
\end{longtable}

Readers should compare the learning-curve panels across chapters to see
how each model's structural commitments show up in its sensitivity to
the two non-stationary scenarios. The Kalman prototype is uniquely
well-matched to smooth drift; the decay GCM and the rule filter are both
general-purpose in ways that make them approximately suitable for either
non-stationary scenario, for different mechanistic reasons.

\end{tcolorbox}

\subsection{Visualising the Three
Scenarios}\label{visualising-the-three-scenarios-2}

We simulate 8 agents per cell across a 3 × 3 grid of scenarios ×
mutation rates, at fixed \(\varepsilon = 0.1\) and
\(N_{\text{particles}} = 100\). As in Chapters 11 and 12, we expect
mutation to \emph{hurt} on static data (it purges rules that already
match) and \emph{help} on non-stationary data (it injects fresh
candidates the filter can reweight when the environment changes).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{rule\_scenario\_viz\_file }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"simdata"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"ch14\_rule\_scenario\_viz.csv"}\NormalTok{)}

\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (regenerate\_simulations }\SpecialCharTok{||} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{file.exists}\NormalTok{(rule\_scenario\_viz\_file)) \{}
\NormalTok{  viz\_grid }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand\_grid}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{scenario      =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"static"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"contingent\_shift"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"drift"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{mutation\_prob =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{agent\_id      =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{|\textgreater{}}

\NormalTok{  rule\_viz\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{agent\_id      =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{agent\_id,}
         \AttributeTok{scenario      =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{scenario,}
         \AttributeTok{mutation\_prob =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mutation\_prob,}
         \AttributeTok{subject\_seed  =}\NormalTok{ viz\_grid}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{subject\_seed),}
    \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(agent\_id, scenario, mutation\_prob, subject\_seed) \{}
      \FunctionTok{simulate\_rule\_agent\_scenario}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{agent\_id      =}\NormalTok{ agent\_id,}
        \AttributeTok{scenario      =}\NormalTok{ scenario,}
        \AttributeTok{error\_prob    =} \FloatTok{0.1}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{mutation\_prob =}\NormalTok{ mutation\_prob,}

\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    \}}

\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  rule\_viz\_sim }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(rule\_scenario\_viz\_file, }\AttributeTok{show\_col\_types =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(rule\_viz\_sim,}
       \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ trial\_within\_subject, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ cumulative\_accuracy,}
           \AttributeTok{color =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(mutation\_prob\_true))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun =}\NormalTok{ mean, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"line"}\NormalTok{,}
               \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(mutation\_prob\_true)), }\AttributeTok{linewidth =} \FloatTok{1.1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{stat\_summary}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fun.data =}\NormalTok{ mean\_se, }\AttributeTok{geom =} \StringTok{"ribbon"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.15}\NormalTok{,}
               \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(mutation\_prob\_true),}
                   \AttributeTok{group =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(mutation\_prob\_true)),}
               \AttributeTok{color =} \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{scenario, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{labeller =} \FunctionTok{as\_labeller}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
               \AttributeTok{static           =} \StringTok{"Static Kruschke"}\NormalTok{,}
               \AttributeTok{contingent\_shift =} \StringTok{"Performance{-}contingent flips"}\NormalTok{,}
               \AttributeTok{drift            =} \StringTok{"Smooth drift"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_color\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{name =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(mu)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{option  =} \StringTok{"plasma"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{name =} \FunctionTok{expression}\NormalTok{(mu)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Mean ± SE across 8 agents per cell; ε = 0.1, N\_particles = 100"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Trial"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Cumulative accuracy"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
